items.techniques: Template Tracing, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Curve Sewing, Clipping Curves, Turning and Stuffing, Oversewing, Topstitching, Gathering, Embroidery, Dart Construction, Collar Construction, Pocket Construction, Snap Fastener Installation
items.materialNeeded:
Plain cotton fabric
Printed cotton fabric
Embroidery thread
Fibre filling
Lightweight quilt wadding
Snap fasteners.

steps.title: Sew and Prepare the Limbs
steps.content: Cut the arm and leg pieces from plain cotton fabric. Pair each limb right sides together and sew around the edges, leaving the flat top open and a 1.18 inches turning gap along the back edge. Turn right side out, clip curves where needed, and press.

tip.text: Use a turning tool or chopstick to gently push out the ends of the arms and legs for smooth shaping.

steps.title: Position the Limbs on the Body
steps.content: Arrange the finished arms and legs on the right side of one body piece. Align the leg seams so they run centrally and angle the limbs inward toward the body. Tack securely in place within the seam allowance.

tip.text: Check that both arms and both legs are positioned symmetrically before tacking, as uneven placement is difficult to correct later.

steps.title: Construct the Main Body
steps.content: Place the second body piece on top with right sides together, ensuring all limbs are tucked inside. Stitch around the body perimeter, leaving a 1.18 inches opening along one side. Clip curves, turn right side out, and pull the limbs fully into position.

tip.text: Keep limbs well away from the stitching line to avoid accidentally sewing them into the body seam.

steps.title: Stuff and Close the Body
steps.content: Fill the body firmly with fibre filling, paying particular attention to the neck area so it remains stable and upright. Stuff the limbs to a medium firmness through their side openings. Close all turning gaps with neat oversewing stitches.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually in small amounts to avoid lumps and achieve an even shape.

steps.title: Make the Ears
steps.content: Cut two pairs of ear pieces from plain fabric and layer them with lightweight quilt wadding. Stitch around the curved edges, leaving the straight edge open. Clip curves, turn right side out, and press flat.

tip.text: Trim excess seam allowance around the curves before turning to reduce bulk inside the ears.

steps.title: Construct the Anteater Head
steps.content: Sew the front head sections together from the top of the head around the long snout to the base of the neck. Stitch the head gusset, matching center seams carefully. Turn right side out and lightly tack the ear positions before assembly.

tip.text: Misaligned center seams can cause the snout to twist, so pin thoroughly before sewing.

steps.title: Stuff and Embroider the Head
steps.content: Firmly stuff the head, making sure the long snout is filled all the way to the tip. Embroider small black eyes on both sides of the face and stitch a simple mouth at the end of the snout.

tip.text: Use a chopstick or knitting needle to push small amounts of filling into the tip of the snout for a smooth finish.

steps.title: Attach the Head to the Body
steps.content: Place the completed head over the neck section of the body. Match the center seams and secure with strong oversewing stitches using doubled thread. Add additional stuffing if needed before fully closing the seam.

tip.text: The head joint experiences the most stress during handling, so reinforce the stitching thoroughly.

steps.title: Create the Pocket
steps.content: Fold the pocket piece right sides together and stitch around the edges, leaving a small turning gap. Turn, press, and topstitch along the upper edge. Position the pocket on the front playsuit panel and stitch in place.

tip.text: Press the pocket carefully before attaching to achieve crisp, professional-looking edges.

steps.title: Construct the Playsuit Body
steps.content: Cut front and back playsuit pieces from both outer fabric and lining fabric. Sew the outer and lining sections separately, joining shoulder seams and center fronts as indicated. Position the lining and outer fabric right sides together.

tip.text: Keep track of lining and outer fabric pieces to avoid accidentally mixing left and right sections.

steps.title: Make and Attach the Collar
steps.content: Sew collar pieces right sides together around the outer edge. Clip curves, turn right side out, and press. Zigzag or finish the inner raw edge and tack the collar to the neckline of the outer playsuit.

tip.text: Press the collar thoroughly before attachment to help it sit flat around the neckline.

steps.title: Assemble the Playsuit
steps.content: Stitch armhole seams from armhole to armpit. Clip curves and turn through one shoulder opening. Match and stitch side seams and inseams for both outer fabric and lining. Turn the garment right side out and press.

tip.text: Clip curved armhole seams carefully without cutting through the stitching line.

steps.title: Finish the Legs and Back Opening
steps.content: Turn and stitch the leg hems on both outer fabric and lining. Topstitch neatly around the hems. Sew the back crotch seam as indicated and hand stitch any remaining openings closed.

tip.text: A narrow topstitch close to the edge helps keep the lining from rolling outward.

steps.title: Install Snap Fasteners and Dress the Toy
steps.content: Attach snap fasteners to the overlapping back sections of the playsuit. Fit the finished garment onto the anteater, ensuring the tail opening remains clear and all closures align properly.

tip.text: Test the snap placement before setting them permanently to ensure the back opening closes evenly.

items.name: Lion Plush
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Curve Sewing, Clipping Curves, Stuffing, Oversewing, Embroidery, Appliqué, Topstitching, Gathering, Elastic Casing Construction, Pocket Construction, Garment Sewing, Snap Fastener Installation
items.materialNeeded:
Plain cotton fabric
Printed cotton fabric
Brown felt
Brown embroidery thread
Fibre filling
Lightweight quilt wadding
Narrow elastic
Snap fasteners
Pliers.

steps.title: Prepare and Sew the Limbs
steps.content: Cut the arm and leg pieces from plain cotton fabric. Pair matching pieces right sides together and sew around each limb, leaving the flat top open and a 1.18 inches turning gap along the back edge. Turn right side out, clip curves, and press.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances before turning to help the limbs keep a smooth rounded shape.

steps.title: Position the Limbs on the Body
steps.content: Arrange the completed arms and legs on one body piece. Flatten the legs so the seam runs down the center and angle them toward the body. Pin and tack the limbs securely in place within the seam allowance.

tip.text: Uneven limb placement will be very noticeable once stuffed, so check alignment before sewing.

steps.title: Construct the Lion Body
steps.content: Place the second body piece on top with right sides together and all limbs tucked inside. Stitch around the body, leaving a 1.18 inches turning opening on one straight section. Turn right side out and pull all limbs fully into position.

tip.text: Keep the limbs clear of the seam line to avoid accidentally stitching them into the body seam.

steps.title: Stuff and Close the Body
steps.content: Firmly stuff the body with fibre filling, paying extra attention to the neck area to prevent drooping. Stuff the limbs to medium firmness and close all openings neatly using oversewing stitches.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually in small amounts for a smoother finish with fewer lumps.

steps.title: Make the Lion Ears
steps.content: Cut four ear pieces from plain fabric and layer them with lightweight quilt wadding. Sew around the curved edges, leaving the straight edge open. Clip curves, turn right side out, and press.

tip.text: Press the ears carefully after turning to help them hold their shape during assembly.

steps.title: Construct the Head
steps.content: Sew the front head pieces and head gusset together, matching all center seams carefully. Add the back head piece and stitch the darts as marked. Attach the ears at the indicated positions.

tip.text: Misaligned center seams can make the face appear crooked, so pin thoroughly before stitching.

steps.title: Create and Attach the Mane
steps.content: Cut a single mane piece from lightweight fabric. Layer multiple shades as directed and stitch decorative feather-like lines across the mane sections. Carefully cut around each layer and attach the mane around the head, matching center seams.

tip.text: Take care not to cut through the stitching lines when trimming the layered mane sections.

steps.title: Stuff and Embroider the Head
steps.content: Turn the head right side out and firmly stuff. Embroider round eyes using brown embroidery thread. Create the nose and mouth details as shown and shape the muzzle with additional stitching if required.

tip.text: Mark eye positions lightly with a fabric marker before embroidering for better symmetry.

steps.title: Attach the Head to the Body
steps.content: Place the finished head over the neck section of the body. Secure using strong oversewing stitches with doubled thread. Add more stuffing if needed before completely closing the seam.

tip.text: Reinforce the neck seam well because it supports the weight of the finished head.

steps.title: Sew the T-Shirt Body
steps.content: Cut front and back shirt pieces from printed fabric. Stitch shoulder seams, underarm seams, and side seams. Clip curves where necessary and turn right side out.

tip.text: Avoid stretching the neckline while sewing, as it can distort the shirt shape.

steps.title: Finish the T-Shirt Neckline and Sleeves
steps.content: Cut contrast binding strips and apply them to the neckline and armholes. Fold the binding to the inside and slip-stitch neatly in place. Press all seams flat.

tip.text: Press the binding before slip-stitching to achieve a clean professional finish.

steps.title: Create and Attach the Pocket
steps.content: Cut a small pocket from contrasting fabric. Sew around the edges, leaving a turning gap. Turn right side out, press, and oversew the pocket onto the shirt front.

tip.text: Choose a centered motif on the pocket fabric for a more polished appearance.

steps.title: Install Back Closures
steps.content: Mark the snap positions on the shirt back. Attach snap fasteners using pliers or by sewing them securely in place so the shirt closes neatly at the back.

tip.text: Check alignment before fixing the snaps permanently to prevent a twisted closure.

steps.title: Sew the Trouser Legs
steps.content: Cut the trouser pieces and stitch the outer leg seams. Prepare the cuff pieces and attach them to the leg openings. Finish seam allowances with zigzag stitching if desired.

tip.text: Ensure both cuffs are attached at the same height before stitching permanently.

steps.title: Assemble the Trousers
steps.content: Fold each leg right sides together and sew the inside leg seams. Turn one leg right side out and place it inside the other leg. Stitch around the crotch seam and turn the trousers right side out.

tip.text: Pin the crotch curves carefully to prevent puckering in this high-stress seam.

steps.title: Create the Elastic Waistband
steps.content: Fold the top edge to form a waistband casing and topstitch, leaving a small opening. Thread narrow elastic through the casing using a safety pin, adjust the fit, and close the opening.

tip.text: Do not twist the elastic inside the casing before joining the ends.

steps.title: Dress and Finish the Lion
steps.content: Press the completed shirt and trousers. Dress the lion, adjust the mane, and ensure all clothing closures and seams are secure.

tip.text: Give the mane a final press and gentle finger-shaping to enhance its layered effect.

items.name: Clown Doll
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Stuffing, Appliqué, Felt Embellishment, Gathering, Slip Stitching, Topstitching, Crochet Chain Stitch, Crochet Slip Stitch, Crochet Double Crochet, Eyelet Insertion, Decorative Embellishment
items.materialNeeded:
Plain cotton fabric
Assorted printed cotton fabrics
Toy filling
Quilt wadding
Felt (assorted colours)
Pom poms (large and small)
DK cotton yarn
3mm crochet hook
Flat elastic (12mm wide)
Metal eyelets (10mm)
Matching sewing thread.

steps.title: Create the Body
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut two mirrored body pieces from folded fabric and two sets of arm and leg pieces using contrasting prints. Sew the arms and legs separately along their seam lines and press. Sew the body pieces together, leaving the neck opening. Fold the neck seam allowance inward and tack in place before stuffing the body firmly.

tip.text: Make sure the body pieces are mirrored correctly before sewing or the clown shape may become uneven.

steps.title: Make and Attach the Head
steps.content: Cut two head pieces from plain fabric. Sew around the head, leaving the neck opening. Turn right side out and stuff firmly. Slip the neck into the body opening and hand stitch securely, adding additional stuffing to the neck area if needed before closing.

tip.text: Do not overstuff the neck area or it may be difficult to attach the head neatly.

steps.title: Create the Ruffled Collar
steps.content: Cut a fabric strip measuring 1.97 inches x 11.81 inches. Fold and glue the short ends, then fold lengthwise with right sides together and stitch the long edge. Turn right side out, thread elastic through the tube and tie securely to create a gathered collar. Slip the collar over the clown's head.

tip.text: Use a safety pin attached to the elastic to make threading through the fabric tube much easier.

steps.title: Add the Facial Features
steps.content: Cut two 0.79 inches felt circles for cheeks, a 1.57 inches curved felt piece for the mouth, and small felt stars for the eyes. Oversew the cheeks to the lower face and position the mouth so it slightly overlaps them. Attach the stars above the cheeks and sew a large red pom pom to the center as the nose. Add smaller pom poms down the center front of the body.

tip.text: Pin all facial features in place first and step back to check symmetry before sewing.

steps.title: Construct the Hat
steps.content: Cut two hat pieces from patterned fabric. Sew along the curved edge, turn right side out, and press. Topstitch 5mm from the seam. Fold right sides together and stitch the back seam. Reinforce with a row of zigzag stitching and press. Fold the front edge upward to form the brim.

tip.text: If the cone point is not clipped before turning, the hat tip may appear bulky.

steps.title: Crochet the Hat Trim
steps.content: Using a 3mm crochet hook and yellow DK cotton yarn, crochet a chain of 34 stitches. Check the fit around the clown's head and adjust if needed. Join the chain into a loop and continue working chained loops around the brim to create a decorative border.

tip.text: Check the circumference before finishing the crochet trim, as it will be difficult to resize later.

steps.title: Attach the Hat
steps.content: Slip the finished hat over the clown's head. Add a small amount of stuffing inside the hat so it stands upright. Hand stitch the hat securely to the head and attach decorative pom poms to the front.

tip.text: A little stuffing inside the cone helps the hat keep its shape without becoming heavy.

steps.title: Crochet Wrist and Ankle Cuffs
steps.content: Create small crocheted loop trims for both wrists and ankles using the same yarn and stitch pattern used on the hat trim. Slip the finished cuffs over the limbs and secure with a few hand stitches.

tip.text: Make the cuffs before attaching them so both pairs can be matched for size.

steps.title: Finish and Embellish
steps.content: Check all seams and embellishments. Secure any loose stitches, adjust stuffing if necessary, and ensure the pom poms, felt details, collar, cuffs, and hat are firmly attached. Charlie the Clown is now complete.

tip.text: For toys intended for young children, replace small detachable embellishments with embroidered details where possible.

items.name: Ballerina Doll
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Stuffing, Slip Stitching, Gathering, Backstitch Embroidery, French Knot Embroidery, Hair Wrapping, Braiding, Tulle Gathering, Decorative Trim Application, Fabric Glue Application
items.materialNeeded:
Skin-tone woven fabric or jersey
Cream fabric
Pale pink cotton lace
Pale pink lining fabric
Pink net (tulle)
Brown felt (small scrap)
Toy filling
Diamanté trim
Beige yarn
Pink embroidery thread
White embroidery thread.

steps.title: Cut Out the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut one pair of heads, two pairs of arms, two pairs of legs, two pairs of shoes, one pair of body pieces, and the skirt and knicker pieces from the specified fabrics. Apply a 3mm seam allowance to the shoe pieces as instructed.

tip.text: Transfer all template markings before cutting to avoid confusion during assembly.

steps.title: Make the Legs and Shoes
steps.content: Attach the shoe pieces to the base of each leg. Stitch around the feet and legs, leaving the top open. Turn right side out, lightly stuff the legs, flatten the tops, and sew them closed. Add a strip of pink lace just above each shoe.

tip.text: Keep the stuffing light in the legs so they remain flexible and easier to position.

steps.title: Construct the Arms
steps.content: Sew each pair of arm pieces together, leaving the top edge open. Turn right side out and lightly stuff. Close the upper edges and prepare them for insertion into the body seam.

tip.text: Avoid overstuffing the arms or they may become bulky when sewn into the body.

steps.title: Assemble the Body
steps.content: Position the completed arms between the body pieces according to the template markings. Stitch around the body, trapping the arms in the seam and leaving the neck opening. Turn right side out.

tip.text: Double-check arm placement before sewing, as they cannot be repositioned once enclosed in the seam.

steps.title: Create and Attach the Head
steps.content: Sew the head pieces together, leaving the neck opening. Clip curves, turn right side out, and lightly stuff. Attach the head to the body through the neck opening and secure with stitching.

tip.text: Keep the head slightly softer than the body for a more natural doll appearance.

steps.title: Stuff and Close the Doll
steps.content: Firmly stuff the body while maintaining shape. Close the body opening with hand slip stitches, ensuring seam allowances are hidden.

tip.text: Uneven stuffing can make the doll lean or sit awkwardly.

steps.title: Create the Hair and Bun
steps.content: Wrap beige yarn around cardboard, stitch the wrapped yarn onto the head, and gradually build coverage. Braid two 7.87 inches yarn lengths, wrap them into a bun, and stitch securely to the top of the head. Add diamanté trim around the bun and parting.

tip.text: Anchor the yarn rows evenly from side seam to side seam to achieve full hair coverage.

steps.title: Embroider the Face
steps.content: Cut small oval eye shapes from brown felt. Create French knot details with white embroidery thread and attach the eyes to the face. Embroider the mouth using pink embroidery thread and backstitch.

tip.text: Mark facial feature positions lightly before stitching to maintain symmetry.

steps.title: Make the Knickers
steps.content: Cut a 2.76 inches x 11.57 inches rectangle from pink lining fabric. Fold, sew, and create the leg openings according to the template. Turn right side out and dress the doll with the finished knickers.

tip.text: Press seams before turning for a cleaner and more professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Tulle Skirt
steps.content: Cut three pieces of pink net, including one waistband section and a long gathered skirt strip. Gather the skirt edge, attach it to the waistband lining, sew the waistband closed, and turn right side out.

tip.text: Gather the tulle evenly to avoid bulky areas around the waistband.

steps.title: Dress the Ballerina
steps.content: Pull the completed skirt over the knickers and position it at the waist. Attach two lace shoulder straps between the front and back waistband sections to complete the ballet costume.

tip.text: Adjust the skirt gathers before securing the straps so the fullness is distributed evenly.

items.name: Builder Doll
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Stuffing, Turning and Pressing, Facing Construction, Topstitching, Slip Stitching, Felt Appliqué, Soft Toy Construction, Decorative Assembly, Hot Glue Application
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton jersey fabric
Ivory fabric
Red gingham fabric
Chambray fabric
Black felt
Yellow felt
Rust felt
Mustard felt
Pink felt
Toy filling
Wadding
Polystyrene ball (2.36 inches diameter)
Hot glue gun
Pale blue ribbon
Silver faux leather
Brown felt strip
Matching sewing thread.

steps.title: Cut Out All Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut the dungarees, dungaree facings, shirt piece, and accessory pieces from the specified fabrics. Prepare felt pieces for the toolbelt and tools.

tip.text: Transfer all template markings before cutting to ensure accurate assembly later.

steps.title: Construct the Dungarees
steps.content: Sew the curved edges of the dungaree pieces together to create the center front and center back seams. Join the straight side seams and turn right side out.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances before turning to reduce bulk and improve shaping.

steps.title: Attach the Dungaree Facing and Straps
steps.content: Position two 2.36 inches lengths of pale blue ribbon at the upper corners of the front dungarees. Place the facing inside the top edge, matching curves, and stitch in place. Turn the facing inward and topstitch neatly.

tip.text: Ensure the ribbon straps are not twisted before sewing them into the facing seam.

steps.title: Finish the Leg Openings
steps.content: Fold and edge stitch a 5mm hem around each leg opening of the dungarees. Match the inner leg seams and stitch them together. Turn the garment right side out and press.

tip.text: Press each seam after sewing to achieve a crisp and professional finish.

steps.title: Make the Shirt
steps.content: Fold and stitch 5mm hems at the sleeve ends. Fold the shirt along the shoulder line and sew the side seams and sleeves. Hem the center back opening and neckline before turning right side out.

tip.text: Keep seam allowances narrow around the neckline to prevent puckering.

steps.title: Dress the Doll
steps.content: Place the completed shirt onto the doll. Pull the dungarees over the shirt and position the ribbon straps correctly at the back. Hand stitch the straps securely in place.

tip.text: Try the clothing on the doll before permanently securing the straps.

steps.title: Create the Toolbelt
steps.content: Measure the doll's waist and cut a felt strip to size. Cut a buckle from silver faux leather and stitch it to the felt strip. Wrap the belt around the doll and hand stitch the ends together at the back.

tip.text: Use small hidden stitches when attaching the belt for a cleaner appearance.

steps.title: Make the Spanner
steps.content: Cut two spanner shapes from silver faux leather. Place wrong sides together and stitch around the edges. Use the template to achieve the correct silhouette.

tip.text: Cut both layers together to keep the spanner pieces perfectly aligned.

steps.title: Make the Hammer
steps.content: Cut the hammer head from silver faux leather and a 0.79 inches x 1.97 inches strip from brown felt for the handle. Stitch the hammer head pieces together and attach the felt handle securely.

tip.text: Reinforce the handle attachment because this accessory is handled frequently.

steps.title: Construct the Doll Body
steps.content: Sew the body, arms, and legs according to the template instructions. Insert the 2.36 inches polystyrene ball into the head area, add toy filling and wadding, then close all openings securely.

tip.text: Distribute stuffing evenly around the polystyrene ball to avoid visible lumps.

steps.title: Add Facial Features and Details
steps.content: Create the facial features from felt and embroidery as indicated by the pattern. Attach hair, facial details, and any decorative elements before dressing the doll.

tip.text: Pin all facial elements temporarily first to check placement and expression.

steps.title: Attach Accessories and Finish
steps.content: Place the completed toolbelt around the doll's waist and attach the hammer and spanner. Check all seams, clothing, and embellishments before completing the builder doll.

tip.text: Secure all small accessories firmly if the doll will be handled by children.

items.name: Claus Dolls
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Stuffing, Slip Stitching, Embroidery, Appliqué, Decorative Trim Attachment, Gathering, Soft Toy Construction, Topstitching, Turning and Pressing
items.materialNeeded:
Assorted Christmas print cotton fabrics
Pale pink fabric
Red fabric
Green fabric
White faux fur
Toy stuffing
Lace trim
Ric rac braid
Decorative ribbon
Small buttons
Small pom poms
Embroidery thread
Metallic ribbon
Vintage-style decorative button
Matching sewing thread.

steps.title: Cut the Body Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut two head sections, two patterned body sections, and four arm pieces. Stitch small darts at the bottom of each body piece and sew a dart into each head section.

tip.text: Mark all dart positions before cutting to avoid losing important shaping references.

steps.title: Assemble and Stuff the Body
steps.content: Pair the body sections and stitch around the edges, leaving a 1.57 inches opening at the bottom between the darts. Turn right side out, shape carefully, stuff firmly, and close the opening with slip stitches.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually and distribute it evenly to create a smooth rounded shape.

steps.title: Make the Arms
steps.content: Sew the arm pieces together in pairs, leaving a small opening along the upper back edge. Turn, stuff lightly, fold in the seam allowances, and close with slip stitches.

tip.text: Avoid overstuffing the arms or they may become difficult to position neatly against the body.

steps.title: Decorate the Arms
steps.content: Cut lengths of white ric rac or decorative lace and hand sew around each wrist. Add small decorative buttons above the wrist trims.

tip.text: Use matching thread for trims so stitches remain nearly invisible.

steps.title: Create the Apron
steps.content: Cut an apron shape from patterned cotton. Turn under a narrow hem around the edges and secure with slip stitches. Position and stitch the apron onto the front of Mrs Claus.

tip.text: Press the apron edges before attaching to achieve a crisp finish.

steps.title: Decorate Mrs Claus
steps.content: Wrap lace around the waist and neck area, securing with slip stitches. Add decorative buttons and trims to complete the festive appearance.

tip.text: Layer different trims and ribbons to create a richer handmade Christmas look.

steps.title: Decorate Santa's Coat
steps.content: Hand sew white ric rac around the neckline, down the center front, and around the lower body edge. Add small black buttons evenly spaced along the front opening.

tip.text: Measure button spacing before sewing to keep the coat symmetrical.

steps.title: Add Belt and Buckle
steps.content: Wrap metallic ribbon around Santa's waist and secure it neatly. Attach a vintage-style decorative button to serve as the belt buckle.

tip.text: Position the buckle slightly above the body midpoint for a more natural appearance.

steps.title: Attach the Arms
steps.content: Pin the completed arms to the front body so the tops resemble shoulders. Secure them with a ladder stitch and reinforce the attachment points.

tip.text: Use strong doubled thread because the arm joints receive the most handling.

steps.title: Create the Face
steps.content: Sketch facial features lightly with a soft pencil. Embroider eyes and smiles using embroidery thread. Add soft pink blush to the cheeks. For Santa, cut a beard shape from faux fur and hand sew it in place.

tip.text: Test facial placement with temporary marks before embroidering permanently.

steps.title: Make the Santa Hat
steps.content: Cut a hat from cotton fabric and sew a strip of white faux fur along the lower edge. Fold the hat right sides together, stitch into a cone shape, turn right side out, and place over the head.

tip.text: Trim seam allowances at the hat tip to reduce bulk when turning.

steps.title: Add Pom Pom and Final Details
steps.content: Attach a small pom pom to the tip of the hat. Check all trims, buttons, embroidery, and accessories before completing the Santa and Mrs Claus dolls.

tip.text: Secure all small embellishments firmly if the dolls will be displayed around children.

items.name: Heart Pocket Pillow
items.materialNeeded:
Patterned cotton fabric, two fat quarters.
Plain cotton fabric, two fat quarters.
Pom-pom trim, 2 meters.
Adhesive webbing, 11.97 inches x 7.87 inches.
Lightweight iron-on interfacing, 8.66 inches x 8.66 inches.
Red cotton fabric, four strips measuring 3.54 inches x 17.72 inches.
Cushion stuffing, 500 g.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins.
Paper templates.
Iron.
Fabric scissors.
items.techniques: Pattern Matching, Curved Seam Sewing, Applique, Topstitching, Gathering and Pinning Trim, Cushion Construction, Turning and Stuffing, Edge Finishing

steps.title: Prepare and Cut the Fabric
steps.content: Print and cut the heart templates. Cut two large heart pieces from patterned cotton, two medium heart pieces from plain cotton, one small heart piece from patterned cotton, and four red cotton strips measuring 3.54 inches x 17.72 inches. Apply lightweight interfacing and adhesive webbing to the fabrics as indicated by the pattern.

tip.text: Align patterned fabrics carefully before cutting so the design remains balanced across the finished cushion.

steps.title: Create the Cushion Piping Border
steps.content: Join the red cotton strips end to end to create a continuous strip. Fold the strip lengthwise and wrap it around the pom-pom trim, pinning or tacking it securely in place.

tip.text: Joining the strips with mismatched edges can create bulky sections that are difficult to sew smoothly.

steps.title: Sew the Medium Heart Pocket
steps.content: Place the two medium heart pieces right sides together and sew around the curved edge, leaving a small opening for turning. Clip the seam allowance around curves, turn right side out, and press flat.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances carefully without cutting into the stitching line.

steps.title: Create the Heart Applique
steps.content: Apply adhesive webbing to the back of the small patterned heart. Position it in the center of the medium heart and fuse it in place with an iron.

tip.text: Check the placement before fusing permanently, as repositioning after pressing is difficult.

steps.title: Attach Decorative Pom-Pom Trim
steps.content: Sew pom-pom trim around the edge of the small applique heart. Stitch close to the trim tape to keep the pom-poms evenly spaced.

tip.text: Allowing the trim to twist while sewing can make the pom-poms sit unevenly.

steps.title: Create the Pocket Front
steps.content: Place one patterned large heart flat with the right side facing up. Position the completed medium heart on top and pin it in place. Stitch around the lower edge only, leaving the upper edge open to form a pocket.

tip.text: Double-check that the pocket opening faces upward before sewing.

steps.title: Attach the Outer Pom-Pom Trim
steps.content: Pin the prepared pom-pom trim around the edge of the large heart front piece with the pom-poms facing inward. Tack the trim in place around the entire perimeter.

tip.text: Keep the trim away from the seam allowance at tight curves to prevent bunching.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion
steps.content: Place the second large heart piece right sides together with the front piece. Pin securely and sew around the entire cushion edge, leaving an opening large enough for turning and stuffing.

tip.text: Forgetting to leave a turning gap will require unpicking stitches later.

steps.title: Clip and Turn the Cushion
steps.content: Trim excess seam allowance and clip all curved sections, especially around the top heart indentation and rounded edges. Turn the cushion right side out through the opening.

tip.text: Do not cut through the stitching when clipping curves or the seam may split after stuffing.

steps.title: Stuff the Cushion
steps.content: Fill the cushion evenly with cushion stuffing, working the filling into the heart curves and corners until the shape is full and symmetrical.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually and massage it into place to avoid lumps.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Hand stitch the turning opening closed using a ladder stitch or slip stitch for an invisible finish.

tip.text: Use matching thread and small stitches to make the closure nearly invisible.

steps.title: Final Shaping and Inspection
steps.content: Fluff the cushion, reshape the heart curves, and check that all seams, trim, and pocket edges are secure. Remove loose threads and make any final adjustments.

tip.text: Uneven stuffing distribution can make one side of the heart appear larger than the other.

items.name: Nordic Horse Pillows
items.finishedSize:
13 inches x 13 inches 
 13 inches x 14 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Red cotton print fabric
Blue cotton print fabric
Tan cotton fabric
Cream cotton fabric
Plain cotton fabric for cushion backing
33.15 inches (15 inch) square cushion pad
Toy stuffing
Ribbon trim
Embroidery thread
Tracing paper
Iron-on applique webbing (optional)
Sewing thread
items.sewingTechniques:
Raw-edge applique
Machine applique
Hand embroidery
Topstitching
Decorative ribbon insertion
Stuffed soft toy construction
Cushion assembly
Slip stitching
Turning and pressing
Seam clipping

steps.title: Cut the Cushion and Horse Pieces
steps.content: Print the templates at actual size and cut out all pattern pieces. Cut the square cushion front, cushion backing, horse applique pieces, and horse toy body pieces from the specified fabrics. Transfer all template markings before sewing.

tip.text: Label each applique piece after cutting to avoid confusion when assembling the decorative horse motifs.

steps.title: Prepare the Applique Motifs
steps.content: Apply fusible webbing to the reverse side of the applique fabrics if using. Trace the horse design pieces, cut them out accurately, and position them onto the cushion front following the template layout.

tip.text: Cutting directly on the traced line can make applique pieces too small. Leave the exact shape intact when trimming.

steps.title: Stitch the Horse Applique
steps.content: Secure the applique pieces to the cushion front using a narrow zigzag stitch, satin stitch, or decorative machine embroidery stitch. Work slowly around curves and points for a neat finish.

tip.text: Pulling the fabric while stitching curves can distort the applique shape and cause puckering.

steps.title: Add Ribbon and Embroidered Details
steps.content: Attach decorative ribbon trims to the horse design and embellish with embroidery details such as hearts, borders, and traditional Scandinavian-inspired motifs. Stitch all embellishments securely.

tip.text: Use an embroidery hoop when adding decorative stitching to keep the fabric smooth and prevent wrinkles.

steps.title: Construct the Square Cushion Front
steps.content: Complete all applique and embroidery work on the cushion front. Press the panel carefully from the reverse side using a pressing cloth to protect decorative stitching.

tip.text: Pressing directly onto applique stitching can flatten decorative details and damage delicate trims.

steps.title: Assemble the Square Cushion
steps.content: Place the cushion front and backing right sides together. Stitch around the perimeter, leaving an opening for turning. Clip corners, turn right side out, and press carefully.

tip.text: Failing to clip the corners before turning will result in bulky, rounded cushion corners.

steps.title: Insert Cushion Pad and Close Opening
steps.content: Insert the cushion pad through the opening. Adjust the corners and edges until evenly filled. Hand sew the opening closed using an invisible ladder stitch.

tip.text: Push the cushion pad firmly into each corner before closing the opening for a crisp professional finish.

steps.title: Sew the Horse Ornament Front and Back
steps.content: Place the horse body pieces right sides together and stitch around the shape, leaving a turning gap. Clip curved seam allowances and trim excess fabric around tight corners.

tip.text: Do not clip through the stitching line when trimming curves, as this can weaken the seams.

steps.title: Turn and Stuff the Horse
steps.content: Turn the horse right side out through the opening. Carefully shape the legs, ears, and curves before stuffing evenly with toy filling until firm but not overfilled.

tip.text: Overstuffing can distort the horse shape and place unnecessary stress on the seams.

steps.title: Finish the Horse Cushion
steps.content: Close the turning opening with hand stitching. Add any final embroidery details, decorative trims, or embellishments. Press lightly and inspect all seams before displaying.

tip.text: Use matching thread for the closing stitches so the seam becomes virtually invisible.

items.name: Seahorse Pillow Duo
items.finishedSize:
Cushion: 12 inches x 22 inches
Seahorse Decoration: 6 inches x 9 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Coral print cotton fabric (15.75 inches x fabric width)
Pink cotton fabric (15.75 inches x fabric width)
Plain pink cotton fabric
Cream cotton fabric
Toy stuffing
Polyester wadding
Buttons (optional)
Black embroidery thread
Orange ribbon (12 inches)
Pink ric-rac trim
Pink sequins
Pink beads
Matching sewing thread
Air-erasable fabric marker
Paper templates
items.sewingTechniques:
Pattern cutting
Machine sewing
Stuffing soft toys
Topstitching
Hand sewing
Trim insertion
Gathering
Curved seam sewing
Turning and shaping
Decorative embellishment

steps.title: Cut Out the Seahorse Pieces
steps.content: Print the templates at actual size and cut out all pattern pieces. Cut two seahorse body pieces from the main fabric. For the cushion version, also cut front, back, and gusset pieces as indicated on the template.

tip.text: Transfer all notch and opening markings before removing the paper template to make assembly easier.

steps.title: Prepare Decorative Features
steps.content: Cut ribbon, ric-rac trim, and decorative embellishments to length. Position trims along the seahorse body where indicated and baste in place before assembly.

tip.text: Skipping the basting step can cause trims to shift while sewing the body seams.

steps.title: Sew the Seahorse Body
steps.content: Place the two body pieces right sides together. Stitch around the entire seahorse shape, leaving the marked opening for turning and stuffing. Sew carefully around the curved head and curled tail sections.

tip.text: Use a shorter stitch length around tight curves to prevent jagged seam lines and improve durability.

steps.title: Clip Curves and Turn Right Side Out
steps.content: Trim seam allowances and clip into curved areas around the neck, head, and tail. Turn the seahorse right side out through the opening and gently shape all curves using a blunt turning tool.

tip.text: Do not cut through the stitching when clipping curves, as this can cause seam failure after stuffing.

steps.title: Add Facial Details
steps.content: Create the eye using embroidery, a bead, or a button depending on the intended user. Stitch securely and add any additional decorative details before stuffing.

tip.text: Avoid buttons and beads when making the toy for young children. Use embroidered details instead.

steps.title: Stuff the Seahorse
steps.content: Fill the seahorse evenly with toy stuffing, starting with the nose and working toward the body and tail. Shape the curved tail carefully as you stuff.

tip.text: Add stuffing in small amounts rather than large handfuls to achieve a smooth, even shape without lumps.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Hand stitch the turning opening closed using a ladder stitch or slip stitch. Ensure the seam is neat and nearly invisible from the outside.

tip.text: Use thread that matches the fabric exactly to hide the closing stitches.

steps.title: Create the Cushion Cover
steps.content: For the cushion version, sew the front and back cushion panels together, inserting any gusset sections as indicated. Leave an opening for inserting the cushion pad.

tip.text: Failing to match seam intersections accurately can result in a twisted or uneven cushion shape.

steps.title: Insert Cushion Pad and Finish
steps.content: Insert the cushion pad into the completed cover and close the opening with hand stitching or the chosen closure method. Press lightly if needed and reshape the finished cushion.

tip.text: Gently massage the cushion corners after inserting the pad to distribute filling evenly and improve the finished appearance.

items.name: Boat Pillow Set
items.finishedSize:
Boat Cushion: 16 x 16 inches
Whale Card: 5 x 7 inches
items.materialNeeded:
White cotton fabric for cushion front and back
Red striped cotton fabric scraps
Blue cotton fabric scraps
Blue gingham fabric scraps
Iron-on fusible webbing
Embroidery thread (blue)
AC6 card blank (5 x 7 inches)
General sewing thread
Printer paper for templates
Pins
Fabric scissors
Iron and ironing board
Sewing machine
items.sewingTechniques:
Raw-edge applique
Machine applique stitching
Topstitching
Decorative machine stitching
Basic cushion construction
Turning and pressing seams
Hand finishing
Fusible web applique

steps.title: Print and Prepare Templates
steps.content: Download, print, and cut out the boat and whale templates at actual size. Separate each applique section and label the fabric pieces according to the pattern.

tip.text: Cut paper templates accurately along the outline to ensure clean, matching applique shapes.

steps.title: Prepare Applique Fabrics
steps.content: Apply fusible webbing to the wrong side of the red striped, blue, and gingham fabrics. Trace the template shapes onto the paper backing and cut them out roughly.

tip.text: Do not remove the paper backing until all applique pieces have been cut and checked for placement.

steps.title: Create the Boat Motif
steps.content: Position the boat hull, mast, sails, and name lettering on the cushion front following the template layout. Fuse each piece in place with an iron.

tip.text: Misaligned sails or lettering can make the design appear unbalanced. Check placement before fusing permanently.

steps.title: Stitch the Applique Design
steps.content: Using blue thread, machine stitch around all applique shapes. Add decorative stitching around the boat, sails, and lettering to secure the raw edges and enhance the design.

tip.text: Use a slightly shorter stitch length when sewing around tight curves for smoother applique edges.

steps.title: Construct the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the cushion front and back pieces right sides together. Sew around the edges, leaving an opening for turning. Clip corners, turn right side out, and press flat.

tip.text: Trim excess fabric from the corners before turning to reduce bulk and create sharp corners.

steps.title: Insert Cushion Pad and Finish
steps.content: Insert the cushion pad through the opening. Hand stitch or machine stitch the opening closed to complete the nautical cushion.

tip.text: Use a ladder stitch for an almost invisible hand-finished closure.

steps.title: Prepare the Whale Card Applique
steps.content: Trace the whale template onto fusible webbing, fuse it to blue fabric, and cut out the whale shape. Remove the backing paper and position the applique onto the front of the card blank.

tip.text: Applying the whale too close to the card edge can make stitching difficult and reduce durability.

steps.title: Finish the Whale Greeting Card
steps.content: Fuse the whale in place and stitch around the edge using a decorative or straight stitch. Trim any loose threads and add a personal greeting inside the card.

tip.text: Use coordinating thread colors to make the applique stand out while maintaining a neat finish.

items.name: Butterfly Garden Pillow
items.finishedSize:
Cushion: 16.5 x 16.5 inches
Butterfly: 12.5 x 4.5 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Assorted cotton fabrics (fat quarters)
Pink floral cotton fabric
Striped cotton fabric
Lightweight quilt wadding
Fusible applique web
Fiber filling (toy stuffing)
Cushion pad, 16 x 16 inches
Matching sewing thread
Embroidery thread
Pins
Fabric scissors
Iron and ironing board
Sewing machine
Hand sewing needle
Printer paper for templates
items.sewingTechniques:
Strip piecing
Patchwork construction
Machine applique
Fusible web applique
Topstitching
Stuffed soft toy construction
Hand stitching
Turning and stuffing shaped pieces
Decorative embroidery
Cushion cover assembly

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Front Panels
steps.content: Print and cut the cushion templates. Cut a 12.5-inch square floral center panel and prepare six striped fabric strips measuring approximately 2.5 inches wide. Sew the strips around the center panel one side at a time, pressing the seams after each addition.

tip.text: Press seams after every strip is attached to keep the cushion front flat and prevent distortion.

steps.title: Create the Framed Cushion Design
steps.content: Continue adding striped borders around the floral center until the completed cushion front measures approximately 16.5 inches square. Trim excess fabric and square up all edges.

tip.text: Failing to square the panel before continuing can result in uneven cushion seams later.

steps.title: Prepare Butterfly Applique Pieces
steps.content: Trace the butterfly templates onto fusible applique web and fuse them to selected patterned fabrics. Cut out the butterfly shapes accurately and remove the backing paper.

tip.text: Use contrasting fabric prints for different butterflies to create more visual interest on the cushion.

steps.title: Apply the Butterfly Decorations
steps.content: Arrange the butterfly appliques across the cushion front and fuse them into place. Machine stitch around each butterfly using a small zigzag stitch or decorative applique stitch to secure the raw edges.

tip.text: Do not stretch the fabric while stitching around curves, or the butterflies may pucker.

steps.title: Construct the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the cushion front and back pieces right sides together. Sew around all four edges, leaving an opening large enough for the cushion pad. Clip corners, turn right side out, and press.

tip.text: Trim the seam allowance diagonally at each corner before turning to achieve crisp corners.

steps.title: Insert Cushion Pad and Finish
steps.content: Insert the cushion pad through the opening and close the gap using a ladder stitch or a neat machine seam.

tip.text: Overfilling the cushion can distort the applique design and cause seam stress.

steps.title: Cut Butterfly Body and Wings
steps.content: Using the butterfly template, cut the wing sections from contrasting fabrics and cut two body pieces from coordinating fabric. Prepare matching wing pairs according to the pattern layout.

tip.text: Keep left and right wing pairs together after cutting to avoid mixing mirrored pieces.

steps.title: Assemble the Butterfly Wings
steps.content: Sew the wing sections together following the template arrangement. Press all seams flat and trim the completed wings to match the template shape.

tip.text: Uneven seam allowances can cause the wing pairs to become mismatched.

steps.title: Sew and Stuff the Butterfly Body
steps.content: Place the butterfly body pieces right sides together and stitch around the edge, leaving a turning opening. Turn right side out, stuff firmly with fiber filling, and close the opening by hand.

tip.text: Avoid overstuffing the body, as it may make attaching the wings difficult.

steps.title: Attach the Wings
steps.content: Layer the upper and lower wings according to the pattern and secure them at the center. Stitch the wings firmly to the stuffed butterfly body.

tip.text: Hand-baste the wing layers together first to prevent shifting during final assembly.

steps.title: Add Facial Details
steps.content: Embroider the butterfly face and attach antennae using embroidery thread. Shape the body slightly with a few hand stitches if desired.

tip.text: Sketch the face on paper first to test the expression before stitching onto the finished butterfly.

steps.title: Complete the Butterfly Decoration
steps.content: Check all seams and embellishments, trim loose threads, and gently reshape the wings. The butterfly can be displayed separately or used as a decorative accessory alongside the cushion.

tip.text: Lightly steam the wings after construction to help them hold their shape neatly.

items.name: Cutwork Flower Pillow
items.materialNeeded:
Wool felt, 16 inches square.
Cotton fabric, 18 inches square.
Cushion pad, 16 inches square.
Matching sewing thread.
Embroidery thread (optional).
Air-erasable fabric marker.
Hand sewing needle.
Fabric scissors.
Pins or fabric clips.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Cutwork, Hand Sewing, Oversewing Stitch, Cushion Construction, Edge Stitching, Applique

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Front
steps.content: Print and cut the flower template. Cut a 16-inch square from felt and press it flat. Position the template on the right side of the felt and trace the flower outline and inner petal openings using an air-erasable fabric marker.

tip.text: Press the felt before marking to ensure the template sits flat and the traced lines remain accurate.

steps.title: Mark the Cutwork Design
steps.content: Transfer all inner petal shapes that will be removed to create the decorative cutwork effect. Check that all petals are evenly spaced before cutting.

tip.text: Skipping a final layout check can result in uneven petal spacing and an unbalanced flower design.

steps.title: Cut Out the Petal Openings
steps.content: Carefully cut along the marked inner petal lines using sharp embroidery scissors. Remove each petal section while keeping the outer flower shape intact.

tip.text: Avoid pulling or stretching the felt while cutting, as this can distort the finished flower shape.

steps.title: Secure the Raw Edges
steps.content: Using matching thread, work an oversewing stitch around each cut edge to strengthen the openings and create a neat decorative finish.

tip.text: Keep your stitches evenly spaced to achieve a professional-looking cutwork finish.

steps.title: Prepare the Contrast Layer
steps.content: Cut a cotton backing square slightly larger than the felt front. Place the cotton behind the cutwork panel so the contrasting colour shows through the flower openings.

tip.text: Bright contrasting colours create a stronger visual effect through the cutwork petals.

steps.title: Attach the Decorative Front
steps.content: Pin the felt and cotton layers together. Stitch around the outer flower design and around the panel edges to secure both layers permanently.

tip.text: Insufficient pinning may allow the cotton backing to shift and become misaligned.

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Back
steps.content: Cut a matching back panel to the same size as the completed front. Press all pieces flat and trim any uneven edges.

tip.text: Square and even panels make final assembly much easier and improve the finished appearance.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the front and back panels right sides together. Stitch around all four sides using a 0.39 inches seam allowance, leaving an opening large enough for turning and inserting the cushion pad.

tip.text: Do not forget the turning gap, otherwise the cushion cover cannot be turned right side out.

steps.title: Turn and Insert the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Trim bulky seam allowances, turn the cover right side out, and carefully push out the corners. Insert the cushion pad and adjust the filling evenly.

tip.text: Use a blunt point turner rather than scissors to avoid accidentally piercing the fabric.

steps.title: Close and Finish
steps.content: Hand stitch the opening closed using an invisible ladder stitch. Smooth the cushion and shape the flower design so the contrast fabric is evenly visible through the cutwork openings.

tip.text: A ladder stitch creates a nearly invisible closure and gives the cushion a clean professional finish.

items.name: Craft Pocket Pillow
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabric for cushion front, 17 inches square.
Coordinating cotton fabric for cushion back, 17 inches square.
Assorted cotton fabric scraps for appliqué pocket details.
Medium-weight fusible interfacing, 16 inches square.
Cushion pad, 16 inches square.
Matching sewing thread.
Embroidery thread for decorative lettering.
Fabric marker or pencil.
Buttons or decorative embellishments (optional).
Pins or fabric clips.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Machine Sewing, Decorative Topstitching, Embroidery, Pocket Construction, Cushion Assembly

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Panels
steps.content: Cut the front and back cushion panels to size. Apply fusible interfacing to the wrong side of the front panel to provide additional stability for the appliqué and pocket features.

tip.text: Fuse the interfacing before marking any design placements to prevent distortion later.

steps.title: Create the Envelope Pocket
steps.content: Cut fabric pieces for the decorative envelope-style pocket. Fold and press the top edge, then stitch it in place before assembling the pocket sections.

tip.text: Skipping pressing at this stage can cause uneven pocket edges and inaccurate alignment.

steps.title: Construct the Pocket Front
steps.content: Layer the pocket fabrics according to the template design and stitch them together. Add decorative trims or contrasting fabrics if desired.

tip.text: Use coordinating prints from the main fabric collection to create a cohesive finished look.

steps.title: Add Decorative Appliqué Details
steps.content: Cut and position appliqué shapes such as labels, tags, sewing-themed motifs, or decorative patches. Secure them using a narrow zigzag stitch or appliqué stitch.

tip.text: Reduce sewing speed when stitching around small appliqué curves to maintain smooth edges.

steps.title: Embroider the Lettering
steps.content: Transfer the wording or handwritten-style message onto the fabric and embroider it using coordinating embroidery thread.

tip.text: Use a removable fabric marker so all visible guide lines can be erased after stitching.

steps.title: Attach the Pocket to the Cushion Front
steps.content: Position the completed pocket on the front cushion panel and stitch around the sides and bottom edges, leaving the opening accessible.

tip.text: Check pocket placement carefully before sewing, as repositioning may leave visible needle marks.

steps.title: Add Decorative Topstitching
steps.content: Topstitch around the pocket edges and decorative sections to enhance the handmade appearance and improve durability.

tip.text: Lengthen the stitch slightly for cleaner, more professional-looking topstitching.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the front and back panels right sides together. Stitch around all four sides, leaving a turning gap along one edge.

tip.text: Avoid stitching the turning opening closed, or the cushion pad cannot be inserted.

steps.title: Turn and Press
steps.content: Trim the corners, turn the cushion cover right side out, and carefully push out all corners. Press the cushion cover flat.

tip.text: Use a blunt point turner rather than scissors to avoid damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Insert Cushion Pad and Finish
steps.content: Insert the cushion pad, adjust the filling evenly, and close the opening using an invisible ladder stitch or machine stitch close to the edge.

tip.text: Fluff the cushion insert before closing the opening to achieve a fuller, more polished finish.

items.name: Cat Applique Pillow
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, plain cotton, 19.69 inches x fabric width.
Fabric, blue cotton, 19.69 inches x fabric width.
Fabric, lining, 19.69 inches x fabric width.
Fabric, butterfly print, 19.69 inches x fabric width.
Fabric, meadow print, 19.69 inches x fabric width.
Fabric, ditsy print, scraps.
Fabric, cross stitch print, scraps.
Felt, cream, scraps.
Felt, blue, scraps.
Thread, coordinating.
Buttons, assorted.
Cotton lace trim, white.
Fusible webbing.
Double-sided tape.
Stuffing or cushion pad.
Concealed zip, blue, 16 inches (40.39 inches).
Alphabet block stamps, mini.
Fabric paint, black.
Sponge.
Palette.
Picture frame.
items.techniques: Fabric applique, Fusible applique, Machine stitching, Topstitching, Zip insertion, Button sewing, Decorative embellishment, Hand tacking, Fabric painting, Zigzag stitching

steps.title: Cut the Cushion Panels
steps.content: Print and cut the templates. Cut a back panel measuring 43.1.97 inches x 42.36 inches from blue cotton and a center panel measuring 25.1.97 inches x 42.36 inches. Prepare all remaining fabric pieces according to the template markings.

tip.text: Press all fabrics before cutting to ensure accurate measurements and clean panel assembly.

steps.title: Create the Cat Appliques
steps.content: Apply fusible webbing to the wrong side of the butterfly and meadow print fabrics. Transfer the cat templates, cut out the shapes and fuse them onto the center panel. Machine stitch neatly around all applique edges to secure them.

tip.text: Leaving gaps between stitches around applique edges may cause the fabric to lift after repeated use.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zip
steps.content: Cut the upper and lower cushion sections. Join the zip between the center panel and adjoining panel using a 1.1.97 inches seam allowance. Press the seams flat and stitch as close to the zipper teeth as possible. Secure the second side of the zip and test its operation.

tip.text: Always test the zip before closing the cushion seams to avoid having to unpick completed seams later.

steps.title: Create the Sentiment Panel
steps.content: Use a sponge and fabric paint to apply your chosen wording with mini alphabet stamps onto cross-stitch print fabric. Heat-set the paint according to the manufacturer's instructions. Layer the printed fabric onto decorative felt rosettes and stitch it to the center panel.

tip.text: Practice the stamped wording on scrap fabric first to achieve even paint coverage and spacing.

steps.title: Make Felt Rosettes and Decorative Details
steps.content: Cut cream and blue felt circles and layer them to create rosettes. Sew buttons through the center of each rosette. Arrange additional buttons around the cat motifs and stitch them securely. Add white cotton lace trim to the upper and lower edges of the center panel.

tip.text: Ensure all buttons are sewn securely, especially if the cushion may be displayed where children can access it.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the front and back panels right sides together. Pin and machine stitch around the perimeter using a 1.1.97 inches seam allowance. Trim seam allowances where necessary and turn the cover right side out through the zip opening.

tip.text: Clip the corners diagonally before turning to achieve crisp, professional-looking corners.

steps.title: Insert the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Insert the cushion pad through the concealed zip opening. Smooth the corners and check that all embellishments are securely attached. Close the zip to complete the project.

tip.text: Overstuffing the cushion can distort the applique design and make the cover difficult to zip closed.

items.name: Leaf Pillow Set
items.finishedSize: Cushion: 12 x 12 inches; Hottie Cover: 8 x 13 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Cream wool fabric, 1m width.
White poly cotton fabric.
Concealed zip, 7 inches.
Beads and sequins, assorted.
Hand dyes: rosewood red, flamingo pink, woodland brown, terracotta brown.
Salt.
Toy stuffing.
Orange embroidery thread.
Leaf templates.
Fabric glue or pins.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Fabric dyeing, Applique, Machine sewing, Zip insertion, Embellishment, Blanket stitch, Cushion construction

steps.title: Cut and Dye the Cushion Fabrics
steps.content: Cut one 30.39 inches square for the cushion front, one 12.76 inches x 30.39 inches back piece and one 7.87 inches x 30.39 inches back piece from cream wool fabric. Prepare dye baths using Flamingo Pink, Rosewood Red, Woodland Brown and Terracotta Brown dyes. Dye the cushion pieces and several additional wool rectangles that will be used for the leaf appliqués. Allow all fabric pieces to dry completely.

tip.text: Do not sew dyed fabric until it is completely dry, as moisture can distort seams and transfer colour.

steps.title: Create and Attach the Leaf Appliqués
steps.content: Use the downloaded templates to cut nine leaf shapes from the dyed wool rectangles. Arrange the leaves on the cushion front until satisfied with the composition. Pin or lightly glue them in place, then machine stitch around each leaf to secure.

tip.text: Combine several dye shades across the leaves to create a more realistic autumn effect.

steps.title: Add Decorative Embellishments
steps.content: Sew coordinating beads and sequins onto selected leaves to add texture and detail. Distribute embellishments evenly across the design.

tip.text: Overloading one area with beads can make the cushion front bulky and difficult to sew.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zip
steps.content: Join the two cushion back sections with the concealed zip, using a 1.1.97 inches seam allowance. Press the zip area flat after stitching.

tip.text: Pressing before and after zip insertion helps achieve a professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the cushion front and completed back right sides together. Pin all edges and machine stitch with a 0.39 inches seam allowance. Clip the corners and trim excess bulk before turning.

tip.text: Avoid trimming too close to the stitching line, as this may weaken the seams.

steps.title: Create the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Cut two 30.39 inches squares from white poly cotton fabric. Stitch around the edges, leaving a small opening. Turn right side out, fill with toy stuffing and hand sew the opening closed.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually and distribute it evenly to avoid lumps.

steps.title: Finish the Cushion
steps.content: Turn the cushion cover right side out, insert the cushion pad through the zip opening and adjust the filling evenly inside the cover.

tip.text: Check that all embellishments are securely attached before regular use.

steps.title: Cut and Dye the Hottie Cover Pieces
steps.content: Cut the front and back pieces for the hot water bottle cover from cream wool fabric using the template. Prepare a Terracotta Brown dye bath and colour the cover pieces. Allow them to dry thoroughly.

tip.text: Rinse dyed wool thoroughly to prevent excess dye from transferring during use.

steps.title: Prepare the Leaf Decorations
steps.content: Cut six leaf shapes from leftover dyed wool. Arrange them on the front cover piece and pin or glue them temporarily in place.

tip.text: Take a photo of the arrangement before sewing so you can easily recreate the layout.

steps.title: Stitch the Appliqués
steps.content: Machine stitch around the edges of each leaf using matching or contrasting thread. Ensure all edges are securely attached.

tip.text: Pulling the wool while stitching may distort the shape of the appliqués.

steps.title: Decorate the Back Flaps
steps.content: Finish the edges of the back opening flaps with orange blanket stitch embroidery. Keep stitches evenly spaced for a decorative finish.

tip.text: Use embroidery thread of consistent tension to create uniform blanket stitches.

steps.title: Assemble the Hottie Cover
steps.content: Place the front and back pieces right sides together and stitch around the outer edges according to the template. Turn right side out and press carefully.

tip.text: Do not accidentally stitch the opening closed, or the hot water bottle will not fit inside.

steps.title: Finish the Hottie Cover
steps.content: Insert the hot water bottle into the finished cover and adjust the fabric so it sits smoothly around the bottle.

tip.text: Always ensure the hot water bottle is properly sealed before placing it inside the fabric cover.

items.name: Round Floor Pouf
items.materialNeeded:
Patterned cotton fabrics, eight coordinating prints.
White cotton fabric, 2m.
Polystyrene beans.
Zip, 11.81 inches.
Zip, 23.62 inches.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Iron and ironing board.
Funnel for filling.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Zip Insertion, Straight Stitch Sewing, Topstitching, Seam Matching, Pressing, Soft Furnishing Construction

steps.title: Create the Inner Pouffe Ring
steps.content: Download and print the pattern pieces. Cut eight 22.36 inches x 30.79 inches rectangles from white cotton fabric. Insert a 11.81 inches zip between two rectangles along one long edge. Sew the remaining rectangles together to create a continuous ring.

tip.text: Press every seam open immediately after stitching to reduce bulk and improve the final shape.

steps.title: Construct the Inner Top Panel
steps.content: Cut eight triangular panels from white cotton. Stitch the triangles together in pairs, then join the pairs into semicircles. Sew the semicircles together to form a complete octagonal top panel.

tip.text: Failing to align triangle points accurately can cause the top panel to become uneven.

steps.title: Assemble the Inner Beanbag
steps.content: Pin the top panel to the side ring with right sides together, matching seam lines carefully. Stitch around the edge. Construct the base panel in the same way and attach it to the ring, leaving the zip partially open.

tip.text: Always leave the zipper open before sewing the final seam so the beanbag can be turned right side out.

steps.title: Fill the Inner Beanbag
steps.content: Turn the beanbag right side out. Fill it with polystyrene beans through the zipper opening until comfortably full. Close the zipper securely.

tip.text: Polystyrene beads can scatter easily. Fill the beanbag in a controlled area and avoid overfilling.

steps.title: Create the Patchwork Top Cover
steps.content: Cut triangular sections from the eight patterned fabrics. Mix the prints and stitch sections together into full-sized triangles. Sew them into pairs, then semicircles, and finally into a complete octagonal top panel.

tip.text: Lay out all fabric pieces before sewing to achieve a balanced colour distribution.

steps.title: Make the Patchwork Side Panels
steps.content: Cut rectangular sections from each fabric print. Combine and stitch them together to form eight multi-coloured side panels. Join the panels together into a ring and press all seams flat.

tip.text: Skipping seam pressing can cause the patchwork sections to twist and distort.

steps.title: Install the Base Zip
steps.content: Construct the base from patchwork triangles in the same way as the top panel. Press under 0.39 inches along the straight edges. Attach a 23.62 inches zip between the folded edges and topstitch securely.

tip.text: Test the zip operation before final topstitching to ensure smooth opening and closing.

steps.title: Assemble the Outer Cover
steps.content: Attach the patchwork top panel to the side ring with right sides together. Stitch around the perimeter and press the seams. Repeat for the base panel, ensuring the zipper remains partially open.

tip.text: Mismatched seam intersections can make the finished pouffe appear uneven.

steps.title: Insert the Inner and Finish
steps.content: Turn the cover right side out through the zipper opening. Insert the filled beanbag inner. Adjust the filling so the seams align neatly and zip the cover closed.

tip.text: Massage the filling into the corners and edges for a smooth, professional finish.

items.name: Woodland Fox Pillow
items.materialNeeded:
Tweed fabric, blue, 19.69 inches.
Tweed fabric, beige, 19.69 inches.
Printed cotton fabric, orange floral.
Printed cotton fabric, orange.
Printed cotton fabric, blue floral.
Printed cotton fabric, red spot.
Printed cotton fabric, yellow spot.
Printed cotton fabric, blue spot.
Printed cotton fabric, green print.
Cream lining fabric, 19.69 inches.
Fusible webbing, 19.84 inches.
Zip, 11.81 inches.
Polyester fibre filling or cushion pad.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Carbon paper.
Pins.
Iron and ironing board.
items.techniques: Applique, Fusible Webbing Application, Zip Insertion, Straight Stitch Sewing, Edge Stitching, Pattern Matching, Cushion Construction

steps.title: Create the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Cut two 30.79 inches x 16.54 inches rectangles from cream lining fabric. Place right sides together and machine stitch around the edges, leaving a 3.94 inches opening for turning. Turn right side out, fill with fibre stuffing, and hand sew the opening closed.

tip.text: Distribute the filling evenly into the corners before closing the opening for a smooth cushion shape.

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Cut one 30.79 inches x 16.54 inches front panel from blue tweed fabric and two 12.76 inches x 16.54 inches back panels. Match the tweed pattern carefully so the checks align across the finished cushion cover.

tip.text: Ignoring pattern matching can make the zipper seam highly visible in the finished cushion.

steps.title: Insert the Back Zipper
steps.content: Place the two back panels right sides together and stitch 2.36 inches from each end along the long edge. Press the seam open and insert the 11.81 inches zipper into the opening. Topstitch securely on both sides of the zipper.

tip.text: Press the seam open before installing the zipper to avoid puckering around the zipper area.

steps.title: Cut the Woodland Appliqué Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the woodland animal template. Cut the fox body and face strips from orange floral fabric, the face, body strip and backing pieces from orange print fabric, the head from pink fabric, the eyes, ears and nose from red spot fabric, and the leaf from yellow spot fabric. Apply fusible webbing to the reverse side of all appliqué pieces.

tip.text: Label the appliqué pieces after cutting to keep track of their placement during assembly.

steps.title: Arrange the Appliqué Design
steps.content: Remove the backing paper from the fusible webbing and position the fox and leaf pieces in the center of the tweed front panel. Cover with a pressing cloth and iron to fuse all elements in place.

tip.text: Placing appliqué pieces too close to the seam allowance may cause parts of the design to be caught in the cushion seams.

steps.title: Stitch Around the Appliqué
steps.content: Using coordinating thread, machine stitch around each appliqué shape to secure it permanently. Trim thread ends neatly and pull loose thread tails to the back of the work before knotting.

tip.text: Use a shorter stitch length when sewing around curved appliqué sections for smoother edges.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Open the zipper halfway. Place the front and back cover pieces right sides together and pin around all four sides. Machine stitch using a consistent seam allowance and trim the corners.

tip.text: Always leave the zipper partially open before sewing the cover closed, otherwise turning the cushion right side out will be difficult.

steps.title: Finish the Cushion
steps.content: Turn the cover right side out through the zipper opening and press carefully. Insert the finished cushion pad and close the zipper to complete the woodland cushion.

tip.text: Push the corners out gently with a blunt tool to create crisp, professional-looking edges.

items.name: Tree Applique Pillow
items.materialNeeded:
Mustard cotton fabric.
Plain linen fabric.
Cloud and floral printed fabric.
Various coloured fabric scraps.
Coordinating ribbon, approximately 18 inches.
Assorted buttons.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Fusible interfacing.
Square cushion pad, 17.5 x 17.5 inches.
Tissue paper.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Pins.
Iron and ironing board.
5mm seam allowance used unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Machine Sewing, Topstitching, Decorative Stitching, Envelope Cushion Construction, Pressing, Hand Finishing

steps.title: Cut the Cushion Front and Appliqué Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut a 44.1.97 inches square from plain linen fabric for the cushion front. Using the templates, cut the tree trunk, branches, clouds, leaves and decorative shapes from assorted printed fabrics. Apply fusible interfacing to the back of all appliqué pieces.

tip.text: Apply interfacing before cutting detailed shapes to prevent distortion and fraying.

steps.title: Arrange and Stitch the Appliqué Design
steps.content: Trace the layout onto tissue paper. Position the tree, clouds and decorative pieces on the cushion front, leaving space for the lettering. Pin everything in place and machine stitch around each shape. Press from the reverse side when finished.

tip.text: Do not place appliqué pieces inside the seam allowance area or they may be partially hidden after assembly.

steps.title: Add the Fabric Lettering
steps.content: Trace the lettering guide onto tissue paper. Position the fabric letters on the cushion front and stitch around each letter twice. Carefully remove the tissue paper and press the cushion front from the reverse side.

tip.text: Pull tissue paper away slowly to avoid stretching or breaking decorative stitches.

steps.title: Create Decorative Embellishments
steps.content: Stitch decorative birds and details around the appliqué scene. Add any extra decorative stitching shown in the design. Press the completed front panel flat.

tip.text: Use contrasting thread colours to make decorative stitching stand out.

steps.title: Add the Side Border Strips
steps.content: Cut two mustard cotton strips measuring approximately 2.1.97 inches x 44.1.97 inches. Stitch one strip to each side of the completed front panel using a narrow seam allowance. Trim excess fabric and press the seams open.

tip.text: Uneven seam allowances can cause the cushion front to become slightly skewed.

steps.title: Prepare the Envelope Back Panels
steps.content: Cut the required mustard cotton and floral print fabric pieces for the cushion back. Hem the overlapping edges and attach the coordinating ribbon as shown. Press all folds and seams flat.

tip.text: Press each hem before stitching to achieve cleaner and more accurate edges.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the cushion front right side up. Layer the back panels right side down, overlapping them to create the envelope opening. Align all raw edges, pin securely and stitch around the entire cushion using a 5mm seam allowance.

tip.text: Ensure the envelope overlap faces inward before sewing or the opening will be on the outside.

steps.title: Turn and Finish the Cushion
steps.content: Clip the corners to reduce bulk and turn the cushion cover right side out through the envelope opening. Carefully push out the corners, press the cover flat and insert the cushion pad. Tie the ribbon into a decorative bow if desired.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to shape corners neatly without damaging the fabric.

items.name: Bicycle Pillow Set
items.finishedSize: Cushion: 11.81 inches x 16.54 inches; Wallet: 11.1.97 inches x 9.84 inches (open)
items.materialNeeded:
Bike print fabric.
Grey fabric.
Jade fabric.
Natural linen.
Ribbon.
Cushion pad, 11.81 inches x 16.54 inches.
Pelmet interfacing.
Black thread.
Cream thread.
Bicycle template.
Pins.
Iron.
Sewing machine.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Sewing, Topstitching, Applique, Cushion Making, Wallet Construction

steps.title: Cut the Cushion Front Panels
steps.content: Cut a 41.18 inches strip of jade bike-print fabric, a 3.94 inches x 41.18 inches piece of linen, and a matching strip of grey bike-print fabric. Arrange the three sections to create the cushion front.

tip.text: Check the direction of the bicycle print before cutting to ensure all motifs face the same way.

steps.title: Join the Front Sections
steps.content: Place the fabric sections right sides together and stitch them into one panel. Press all seam allowances open on the reverse side.

tip.text: Poorly pressed seams can create bulky ridges that show through on the finished cushion.

steps.title: Add Decorative Ribbon
steps.content: Pin ribbon along both the upper and lower edges of the linen section and stitch into place. Press from the reverse side.

tip.text: Uneven ribbon placement can make the central panel appear crooked.

steps.title: Stitch the Bicycle Design
steps.content: Transfer the bicycle motif onto tissue paper and position it on the linen panel. Stitch over the design using black thread. Remove the tissue paper and embroider the wording as desired.

tip.text: Use a shorter stitch length when sewing around curved sections of the bicycle.

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Back
steps.content: Cut two grey fabric sections for the cushion back. Insert ribbon between the layers and sew with a 5mm seam allowance. Press and topstitch neatly.

tip.text: Ensure ribbon ends are secured before assembling the back pieces.

steps.title: Create the Overlap Closure
steps.content: Cut a jade fabric section and hem one edge by folding it twice. Attach a ribbon tie approximately 3.54 inches from the hemmed edge and press flat.

tip.text: Press each fold before stitching for a cleaner finish.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the cushion front right side up. Layer the back sections over the front, ensuring the overlap closure is positioned correctly. Stitch around all edges, trim corners, turn right side out and insert the cushion pad.

tip.text: Check that no ribbon is caught inside the seam before sewing around the cover.

steps.title: Cut Wallet Pieces
steps.content: Cut one grey and one jade fabric piece measuring 11.1.97 inches x 9.84 inches. Stitch a decorative ribbon across the grey piece and press.

tip.text: Placing the ribbon too high may interfere with the wallet fold.

steps.title: Construct the Wallet Body
steps.content: Place both wallet pieces right sides together and stitch around the edges, leaving an opening for turning. Trim corners, turn right side out and press.

tip.text: Trim seam allowances carefully to reduce bulk at the corners.

steps.title: Create the Wallet Folds
steps.content: Fold the wallet in half and press a centre crease. Fold each end inward by 1.97 inches and press again to form the future card pockets.

tip.text: Use a ruler to ensure both pocket folds are identical.

steps.title: Insert Interfacing
steps.content: Cut two pieces of pelmet interfacing and place them inside the wallet on either side of the centre fold. Pin securely.

tip.text: Keep a small gap between interfacing pieces to allow the wallet to fold smoothly.

steps.title: Topstitch the Wallet
