steps.content: Topstitch around the entire wallet using black thread. Add a decorative folded ribbon above the fabric band if desired.

tip.text: Lengthen the stitch slightly when topstitching for a professional appearance.

steps.title: Form the Card Pockets
steps.content: Fold up both ends of the wallet to create pockets. Hand sew using ladder stitch so the stitches remain hidden and secure.

tip.text: Visible hand stitches can detract from the clean finish of the wallet.

items.name: Ice Cream Pillows
items.finishedSize: Ice cream cushion and ice lolly cushion (various sizes based on template)
items.materialNeeded:
Pink spotted cotton fabric, 81.97 inches x 17.87 inches.
Dark pink patterned fabric, 9.84 inches x 11.81 inches.
Yellow striped fabric, 15.75 inches x 25.59 inches.
Cream lining fabric, 31.50 inches x 111.81 inches.
Ric rac trim, pink, 17.72 inches.
Wide ric rac trim, dark pink, 1m.
Assorted buttons.
Red felt ball.
Felt flowers.
Polyester fibre filling.
Foam pad (for ice lolly cushion).
Beige fabric for lolly stick.
Cardboard tube or stiff insert.
Matching sewing thread.
Tracing paper.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Sewing, Cushion Construction, Applique, Decorative Trims, Stuffing

steps.title: Prepare the Ice Cream Template Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut the full ice cream shape from yellow striped fabric. Transfer the decorative cone lines and stitch over them using dark thread.

tip.text: Mark all template lines before cutting smaller decorative pieces to improve accuracy.

steps.title: Create the Ice Cream Scoops
steps.content: Cut the large scoop from pink spotted fabric and the smaller scoop from dark pink patterned fabric. Position the large scoop onto the cone section and stitch along the decorative edge. Attach the smaller scoop on top.

tip.text: Misaligned scoop edges can make the finished cushion appear uneven.

steps.title: Add Decorative Trims
steps.content: Sew wide pink ric rac across the join between the cone and scoop. Add narrow ric rac between the large and small scoop sections. Attach buttons evenly and sew felt flowers onto the trim.

tip.text: Pin trims in place before sewing to keep curves smooth and even.

steps.title: Construct the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Prepare the back sections by hemming the edges. Overlap the back pieces to create an envelope opening. Stitch the front and back together with right sides facing.

tip.text: Ensure the envelope overlap is deep enough to prevent the cushion insert from showing.

steps.title: Make the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Cut matching pieces from cream lining fabric. Sew around the shape leaving an opening for turning and stuffing. Turn right side out, fill with fibre stuffing, and close the opening.

tip.text: Distribute stuffing evenly into corners and curves for a professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the Ice Cream Cushion
steps.content: Insert the stuffed cushion pad into the cover. Sew a red felt ball to the top of the ice cream to represent a cherry.

tip.text: Overstuffing can distort the decorative applique details.

steps.title: Prepare the Ice Lolly Front
steps.content: Trim a foam pad into a rounded ice lolly shape. Cut spotted, patterned brown and plain brown fabric sections. Join the fabrics together and press seams flat.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when ironing patterned cottons to avoid shine marks.

steps.title: Decorate the Ice Lolly
steps.content: Sew ric rac trim across the colour-change seams. Add buttons and decorative details evenly across the front panel.

tip.text: Uneven button spacing can make the design appear unbalanced.

steps.title: Create the Back Panel
steps.content: Assemble the back panel using matching fabrics. Add ric rac trim and hem exposed edges before pressing all seams flat.

tip.text: Keep seam allowances consistent to ensure the front and back pieces match correctly.

steps.title: Construct the Cushion Body
steps.content: Join the side, top and base sections together. Attach the front panel, then sew the back panel into place, leaving an opening for turning.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances before turning for smoother edges.

steps.title: Insert the Foam Pad
steps.content: Turn the cover right side out and press. Insert the prepared foam pad into the cushion body and close the opening securely.

tip.text: Check that the foam fills the corners properly before sealing the opening.

steps.title: Make the Lolly Stick
steps.content: Cut two stick pieces from beige fabric. Stitch together, turn right side out and lightly stuff. Insert a cardboard tube or stiff support and close the opening.

tip.text: A firm insert helps the lolly stick maintain its shape during use.

steps.title: Attach the Stick and Finish
steps.content: Secure the stuffed stick to the base of the ice lolly cushion with strong hand stitches. Check all seams and decorative details before use.

tip.text: Reinforce the attachment point carefully, as it receives the most handling.

items.name: Stitch Cat Pillow
items.finishedSize: Cat-shaped cushion, approximately 7.87 inches x 11.81 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Grey wool fabric, 15.75 inches x 23.62 inches.
Embroidery needle.
Embroidery threads, assorted colours.
Embroidery hoop.
Polyester fibre filling.
Buttons, 2.
Sewing needle.
Matching sewing thread.
Fabric marker or transfer pencil.
1.0.79 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Embroidery, Running Stitch, Satin Stitch, Back Stitch, French Knots, Cushion Construction, Hand Sewing

steps.title: Transfer the Cat Design
steps.content: Download and print the template. Transfer the cat outline onto a piece of wool fabric measuring at least 7.87 inches x 11.81 inches. Place the fabric in an embroidery hoop and secure it tightly before stitching.

tip.text: Keep the fabric evenly tensioned in the hoop to prevent puckering during embroidery.

steps.title: Outline the Cat Shape
steps.content: Using orange embroidery thread, work running stitches around the entire outline of the cat. Continue around the body, tail, head and ears until the full silhouette is defined.

tip.text: Uneven stitch lengths can make the outline appear irregular.

steps.title: Embroider the Facial Features
steps.content: Outline the lower face using running stitch. Fill the triangular nose with black satin stitch. Add whiskers and the mouth using black back stitch. Create French knots for decorative facial details.

tip.text: Use short, neat satin stitches to create a smooth and solid nose shape.

steps.title: Decorate the Ears
steps.content: Fill the inner ears with yellow satin stitch. Outline each ear using rows of running stitch in contrasting colours following the triangular ear shape.

tip.text: Avoid pulling the thread too tightly, as this can distort the ear shape.

steps.title: Create Decorative Body Embroidery
steps.content: Use the remaining guide lines to decorate the cat with circles, spirals, zigzags, straight lines and curved motifs. Experiment with different thread colours and stitch patterns to create a unique design.

tip.text: Mix contrasting thread colours to make decorative motifs stand out against the grey fabric.

steps.title: Attach the Eyes
steps.content: Sew two buttons onto the face to create the cat's eyes. Position them evenly before stitching securely in place.

tip.text: Misaligned eyes can noticeably affect the cat's expression.

steps.title: Prepare the Back Piece
steps.content: Remove the embroidered fabric from the hoop and press lightly from the reverse side. Place it right sides together with a second piece of matching fabric.

tip.text: Always press embroidery from the reverse side to avoid flattening the stitches.

steps.title: Cut Out the Cushion Shape
steps.content: Cut around the cat shape through both fabric layers, leaving approximately 1.1.97 inches outside the embroidered outline for seam allowance.

tip.text: Use sharp embroidery scissors for smoother curved edges.

steps.title: Sew the Cushion Together
steps.content: Stitch around the cat shape using a 1.0.79 inches seam allowance. Leave a 1.97 inches opening along one straight section for turning and stuffing.

tip.text: Do not sew the turning gap closed at this stage.

steps.title: Turn and Stuff the Cushion
steps.content: Clip curved seam allowances carefully without cutting the stitches. Turn the cat right side out and fill evenly with fibre stuffing until plump.

tip.text: Insufficient stuffing can cause the cat shape to lose definition.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Fold the seam allowance of the opening inward and close the gap using small slip stitches. Secure the thread and trim any excess.

tip.text: Invisible slip stitches create a neat and professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the Cushion
steps.content: Check all embroidery details, remove any visible transfer marks and gently shape the cushion by hand. The embroidered cat cushion is now complete.

tip.text: Avoid excessive washing to preserve the decorative embroidery work.

items.name: Draped Front Jacket
items.materialNeeded:
Faux suede fabric, 1.7m (59.06 inches wide).
Matching thread.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Waterfall Front Construction, Sleeve Insertion, Understitching, Gathering, Hemming

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Main Pieces
steps.content: Cut out all pattern pieces according to the cutting guide. Neaten the centre-front edges, upper front edges and side edges. With right sides together, sew the front and back pieces together at the shoulder seams. Press the seams open, ensuring the extra length on the back shoulder seam is positioned towards the neckline.

tip.text: Lay all pattern pieces in the same nap direction when working with faux suede to avoid colour shading differences.

steps.title: Attach the Back Neck Facing
steps.content: With right sides together, pin and sew the back neck facing to the back neckline. Clip into the neckline curve where required and understitch the facing to keep it rolled to the inside. Fold the facing into position and stitch along the shoulder seam lines to secure it neatly.

tip.text: Skipping understitching may cause the facing to roll outward and become visible from the right side.

steps.title: Construct and Insert the Sleeves
steps.content: Sew the underarm seams of the sleeves with right sides together and neaten the raw edges. Stitch two rows of gathering stitches around each sleeve head. Match the sleeve notches with the armhole markings and gently ease the sleeve into the armhole before stitching. Neaten the armhole seam allowances and press.

tip.text: Avoid pulling the gathering threads too tightly or puckers may form around the sleeve cap.

steps.title: Finish the Side Seams
steps.content: With right sides together, match the underarm seams and side seams of the jacket. Stitch continuously from the sleeve cuff to the lower hem. Neaten the seam allowances and press them towards the back of the garment.

tip.text: Pin the underarm seam intersection first to keep all seams aligned accurately.

steps.title: Create the Waterfall Front
steps.content: Carefully press the waterfall front edges into their natural drape. Check that both front panels fall evenly and mirror each other. Adjust and press again if necessary before hemming.

tip.text: Uneven pressing can cause one waterfall front edge to hang longer than the other.

steps.title: Hem the Sleeves
steps.content: Neaten the sleeve cuff edges. Turn up a 2.1.97 inches hem allowance, press firmly and stitch in place. Repeat for the second sleeve, ensuring both cuffs are identical in depth.

tip.text: Use plenty of steam and a pressing cloth when hemming faux suede for a crisp finish.

steps.title: Hem the Jacket
steps.content: Neaten the lower edge of the jacket. Turn up a 2.1.97 inches hem along the entire lower edge and press carefully. Stitch the hem evenly, maintaining a consistent distance from the folded edge.

tip.text: Check the garment length on a dress form or while wearing it before permanently stitching the hem.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Give the completed jacket a final press, shaping the waterfall front and smoothing all seams. Remove any visible markings and inspect the garment for loose threads before wearing.

tip.text: Allow the jacket to cool completely after pressing so the waterfall folds keep their shape.

items.name: Cozy Throw-On Poncho
items.materialNeeded:
Knitted fabric, 1.7m (1.5m wide).
Jersey fabric, 11.81 inches.
Grosgrain ribbon, 6.2m.
Matching thread.
Pins or clips.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Sewing Knits, Neckband Construction, Edge Binding, Mitred Corners

steps.title: Prepare the Poncho and Neckband
steps.content: Cut out all pieces according to the cutting guide. Fold the poncho in half lengthwise and locate the neckline markings. Sew the two short ends of the neckband together with right sides facing to form a loop. Turn the neckband right side out and pin the seam to the centre-back neckline of the poncho. Match the centre-front of the neckband to the centre-front neckline and pin the remaining section evenly before stitching in place.

tip.text: Use clips instead of pins on knitted fabrics to avoid stretching or leaving visible holes.

steps.title: Finish the Neckline
steps.content: Fold one long edge of the neckband under by 1.1.97 inches and press. Position the folded edge over the neckline seam allowance, enclosing the raw edges inside the band. Slip stitch the folded edge neatly to the neckline to create a clean finish.

tip.text: Avoid stretching the neckband while sewing or the neckline may become distorted.

steps.title: Attach the Grosgrain Ribbon
steps.content: Pin the grosgrain ribbon along the right side of all four outer edges of the poncho, covering approximately 0.39 inches of the fabric edge. Edge stitch the ribbon in place, sewing carefully to each corner. Leave the needle down at every corner, lift the presser foot, rotate the poncho and continue stitching along the next edge.

tip.text: Press the ribbon before attaching it to help it lie flat and achieve a more professional finish.

steps.title: Create Mitred Corners and Finish
steps.content: Trim excess fabric bulk from the corners. Fold the ribbon around to the wrong side of the poncho and form neat mitred corners. Pin securely and edge stitch along the ribbon, enclosing all raw edges inside the binding. Give the poncho a final press to complete the reversible finish.

tip.text: Trim corner bulk carefully to avoid cutting into the stitching line.

items.name: Folded Fabric Basket
items.techniques: Straight Stitching, Turning, Topstitching, Pressing, Folding, Button Attachment
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabric
Plain cotton fabric
Matching sewing thread
4 buttons
Fabric marker or chalk
Pins or sewing clips

steps.title: Cut and Sew the Fabric Square
steps.content: Cut one square from printed fabric and one square from plain fabric. For the large size, cut both squares at 11.8" x 11.8". Place the fabrics right sides together and sew around the edges using a 0.2-inch seam allowance, leaving an opening on one side for turning. Trim the corners, turn right side out, fold the raw edges of the opening inward, press, and topstitch around the entire square.

tip.text: Trim the corners diagonally before turning to reduce bulk and create crisp, sharp corners.

steps.title: Create the Fold Lines
steps.content: Fold the fabric square in half vertically and horizontally, pressing lightly to create two center crease lines. On the outside of the basket, mark the halfway point between the center and outer edge along each crease. Fold each corner diagonally from mark to mark and pin in place. Topstitch approximately 0.04 inch from the folded edge to create permanent creases.

tip.text: Inaccurate marking can cause the basket sides to become uneven, so measure each fold carefully before stitching.

steps.title: Form the Basket Shape
steps.content: Pinch one side of the square along the original pressed crease and pin a line from the corner of the base to the outer edge. Repeat on all four sides to form the basket walls. Topstitch along the pinned lines and flatten the corner pockets that are created.

tip.text: Press the folds before topstitching to help the basket hold its shape more neatly.

steps.title: Secure the Corner Flaps
steps.content: Fold down the corner flaps between the pockets. Sew a button through the flap and all fabric layers beneath it to secure the basket shape permanently. Repeat for all four corners to complete the origami box.

tip.text: Ensure the button placement is centered before sewing, as uneven placement can distort the shape of the finished box.

items.name: Wall Pocket Organizer
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Fusible Interfacing Application, Straight Stitching, Curved Edge Binding, Zigzag Stitching, Topstitching, Bias Binding, Hand Stitching
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabric
Coordinating cotton fabric
White cotton fabric
Lightweight fusible interfacing
Binding fabric strips (2" wide)
Matching sewing thread
Pins or sewing clips

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Fabric
steps.content: Cut one background panel measuring 11.8" x 13.8" from printed cotton fabric. Fuse a matching piece of lightweight interfacing to the wrong side. Using the pocket templates, cut all pocket pieces from coordinating fabrics and fuse interfacing to the back of each pocket piece. Cut several binding strips measuring 2" wide from a darker coordinating fabric.

tip.text: Fuse the interfacing before cutting curved pocket edges to help maintain accurate shapes and prevent distortion.

steps.title: Bind the Pocket Curves
steps.content: Place a binding strip right sides together along the curved top edge of a pocket. Sew using a 0.2-inch seam allowance, easing the strip smoothly around the curve. Fold the binding over the raw edge, tuck under approximately 0.1 inch, and topstitch in place. Trim the excess binding. Repeat for all pockets.

tip.text: Avoid stretching the curved pocket edge while sewing, as this can cause rippling and uneven pocket openings.

steps.title: Attach the Pockets
steps.content: Arrange the pockets on the background panel according to the desired layout. Allow the top edges to flare slightly to create extra storage space. Pin in place and secure each pocket using a narrow zigzag stitch around the three straight sides.

tip.text: If the pockets are pulled flat while sewing, they may lose capacity and become difficult to use.

steps.title: Add Decorative Binding Strips
steps.content: Cut additional 2" binding strips. Apply them along the lower edges of the center pockets to cover the zigzag stitching. Fold the binding over the raw edge, tuck the seam allowance underneath, and topstitch neatly. Add another binding strip vertically down the center of the organizer to cover the remaining exposed stitching.

tip.text: Press the binding before topstitching to create crisp edges and a professional finish.

steps.title: Create Hanging Loops
steps.content: Cut two fabric strips measuring 0.8" x 4.7". Fold the long edges inward, fold each strip in half lengthwise, and topstitch close to both folded edges to create narrow hanging loops.

tip.text: Use a longer stitch length when topstitching narrow loops to achieve straighter, more even results.

steps.title: Attach the Hanging Loops
steps.content: Fold each loop in half and position them 0.4 inch from the top corners of the organizer with the loops facing downward. Secure them temporarily with a few stitches.

tip.text: Check that both loops are positioned at the same height before stitching to ensure the organizer hangs evenly.

steps.title: Add the Back Panel
steps.content: Cut a piece of white cotton measuring 11.8" x 13.8". Place it against the back of the organizer to cover all construction stitching and pin securely around the edges.

tip.text: Smooth the back panel carefully before pinning to prevent wrinkles from being trapped inside the finished organizer.

steps.title: Bind the Outer Edges
steps.content: Finish the entire outer edge of the organizer using 2" wide binding strips. Miter or overlap the corners neatly and fold all raw ends under before stitching. Topstitch around the perimeter to secure the binding.

tip.text: Take extra care at the corners to avoid bulky folds that can make the binding difficult to stitch evenly.

steps.title: Finish the Organizer
steps.content: Lift the hanging loops into an upright position and secure them with a few small hand stitches. Give the organizer a final press and complete the project.

tip.text: Press the finished organizer from the back side to avoid flattening the pockets and preserve their storage capacity.

items.name: Appliqué Toy Bin
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Fusible Interfacing Application, Fusible Fleece Application, Appliqué, Free-Motion Embroidery, Satin Stitching, Straight Stitching, Box Construction, Turning, Topstitching
items.materialNeeded:
Medium-weight cotton fabric for outer shell
Lightweight cotton fabric for lining and appliqué motifs
Lightweight fusible interfacing
Fusible fleece
Fabric stabilizer
Matching sewing thread
Pins or sewing clips
Printed motif templates

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Cut one square base and four side panels from outer fabric, lining fabric, and fusible fleece. For the small tub, cut one 6.7" square and four 6.7" x 7.9" rectangles. For the large tub, cut one 7.9" square and four 7.9" x 9.4" rectangles.

tip.text: Label the outer, lining, and fleece pieces immediately after cutting to avoid mixing them up during assembly.

steps.title: Prepare the Appliqué Motifs
steps.content: Trace the motif templates onto the paper side of fusible interfacing. Roughly cut out the shapes and fuse them to the wrong side of lightweight cotton fabric. Trim neatly around the motif outlines. Position the motifs in the center of the outer side panels, remove the backing paper, and press them in place.

tip.text: Make sure all motifs are centered before fusing, as repositioning them afterward may damage the fabric.

steps.title: Embroider the Motifs
steps.content: Outline each appliqué motif using free-motion embroidery, satin stitching, or hand embroidery. If using free-motion machine embroidery, lower the feed dogs, attach a free-motion foot, and place stabilizer beneath the fabric while stitching. Remove the stabilizer after completing the embroidery.

tip.text: Keep your stitching speed steady during free-motion embroidery to prevent uneven outlines and puckering.

steps.title: Attach the Fusible Fleece
steps.content: Fuse the fleece to the wrong side of all outer fabric pieces according to the manufacturer's instructions.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when fusing fleece to avoid scorching delicate fabrics.

steps.title: Construct the Outer Container
steps.content: Place two outer side panels right sides together and sew along one long edge using a narrow seam allowance. Press the seam open. Continue adding the remaining side panels until a tube is formed. Attach the square base to the bottom edge of the side panel tube with right sides together. Trim seam allowances and clip the corners.

tip.text: Clip the corners carefully after sewing the base to reduce bulk and help the box hold a crisp shape.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Repeat the same construction process with the lining pieces. Leave a 4" opening in one side of the base seam for turning later.

tip.text: Do not forget the turning opening, otherwise the project cannot be turned right side out after assembly.

steps.title: Join the Outer and Lining
steps.content: Turn the lining right side out. Place it inside the outer container so that right sides are facing each other. Match the raw top edges and sew completely around the top opening.

tip.text: Match the side seams carefully before sewing to keep the finished toy box symmetrical.

steps.title: Turn and Close the Opening
steps.content: Turn the entire container right side out through the opening left in the lining base. Fold the seam allowances inward and stitch the opening closed.

tip.text: Push out the base corners fully after turning to achieve a clean box shape.

steps.title: Finish the Toy Box
steps.content: Push the lining inside the container and press the top edge neatly. Topstitch around the entire upper edge. Fold the top edge down to create a decorative cuff and complete the project.

tip.text: Press the cuff thoroughly before topstitching to help the toy box stand upright and maintain its shape.

items.name: Hanging Fabric Basket
items.materialNeeded:
Assorted cotton fabrics
Lightweight cotton lining fabric
Wadding
Pink bias binding
Decorative woven ribbon
Plastic boning (3.5-inch)
Matching sewing thread
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Dart Sewing, Quilting, Straight Stitching, Circular Base Construction, Bag Construction, Lining Assembly, Turning, Boning Insertion, Topstitching, Bias Binding Application

steps.title: Prepare and Cut the Fabric
steps.content: Print the basket template and transfer it onto the fabric. Cut one outer basket panel, one lining panel, and one matching wadding panel. Transfer all dart markings onto the wrong side of the fabric pieces.

tip.text: Use a washable fabric marker to transfer dart markings accurately for a cleaner finished shape.

steps.title: Sew the Darts
steps.content: Fold each dart with right sides together and stitch from the raw edge toward the dart point. Repeat for all darts on both the outer fabric and lining pieces. Press the darts outward.

tip.text: Do not backstitch at the dart point, as this can create puckers and add unnecessary bulk.

steps.title: Quilt the Lining
steps.content: Trim the wadding around the dart areas and pin it to the wrong side of the lining. Stitch decorative quilting lines across the lining to secure the layers together.

tip.text: Start quilting from the center and work outward to prevent shifting and wrinkles in the wadding.

steps.title: Prepare the Circular Base
steps.content: Cut one fabric circle and one matching wadding circle measuring approximately 5.5 inches in diameter. Layer them together and secure with pins or clips.

tip.text: Use a circular template to keep the base perfectly round and easier to attach later.

steps.title: Construct the Outer Basket
steps.content: Fold the outer basket panel right sides together and sew the side seam. Attach the circular base to the lower edge of the basket body, easing the fabric evenly around the curve.

tip.text: Clip the curved seam allowances carefully without cutting through the stitching line to help the base sit flat.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Repeat the same process with the quilted lining piece. Sew the side seam and attach the circular base to complete the lining.

tip.text: Distribute the fabric evenly around the circular base to avoid puckers and uneven shaping.

steps.title: Join the Basket and Lining
steps.content: Place the outer basket right side out inside the lining, which should remain wrong side out. Align the side seams and top edges, then sew around the upper edge, leaving an opening near the back seam for turning.

tip.text: Remember to leave a turning opening, otherwise the basket cannot be turned right side out.

steps.title: Turn Right Side Out
steps.content: Pull the basket through the opening. Push the lining inside the basket and shape the top edge neatly.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to shape the curves and seams without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Add the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Fold a piece of ribbon into a loop and insert the raw ends between the lining and outer fabric at the back seam. Stitch securely to the outer basket.

tip.text: Reinforce the hanging loop with several rows of stitching to support the weight of the filled basket.

steps.title: Create the Boning Channel
steps.content: Stitch two vertical lines approximately 2 inches apart on either side of the back seam. Join them at the bottom with a horizontal line to form a channel.

tip.text: Mark the channel lines before sewing to keep the boning centered and straight.

steps.title: Insert the Boning
steps.content: Insert a 3.5-inch piece of plastic boning into the channel to help the basket hold its shape and prevent it from tipping forward.

tip.text: Ensure the boning is fully enclosed and the ends are smooth to prevent wear on the fabric over time.

steps.title: Finish the Top Edge
steps.content: Fold the remaining raw edges inward and topstitch around the entire opening. Apply bias binding around the top edge if desired for additional durability and decoration.

tip.text: Press the top edge thoroughly before topstitching for a crisp, professional finish.

items.name: Stuffed Patchwork Pots
items.finishedSize: Approximately 3.1 inches diameter x 3.1 inches height
items.materialNeeded:
Assorted printed cotton fabric scraps
Coordinating sewing thread
Polyester fiberfill
Turquoise felt
Thin card stock
Adhesive tape
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Straight Stitching, Tube Construction, Hand Basting, Stuffing, Slip Stitching, Oversewing

steps.title: Cut the Patchwork Pieces
steps.content: Print and cut out the template. Cut 8 patchwork sections from assorted cotton fabrics, adding a 0.2-inch seam allowance around each piece.

tip.text: Arrange the fabric pieces before sewing to create a balanced color distribution around the pot.

steps.title: Join the Patchwork Panel
steps.content: Place the patchwork pieces right sides together and sew the long edges to form a panel. Do not sew the first piece to the last piece yet. Press all seams open.

tip.text: Uneven seam allowances can cause the patchwork panel to twist and prevent the pot from forming a smooth cylinder.

steps.title: Create the Inner Lining Tube
steps.content: Cut a rectangle of coordinating fabric measuring 3.1 inches x 6.7 inches. Fold it in half with right sides together and sew the short edges to form a tube.

tip.text: Press the seam allowance open to reduce bulk inside the finished pincushion pot.

steps.title: Finish the Raw Edges
steps.content: Turn under 0.2 inch along both raw edges of the lining tube and baste them in place.

tip.text: Keep the folded edges consistent to achieve a neat and professional top edge.

steps.title: Shape the Inner Support
steps.content: While the lining tube is inside out, slide it over a cylindrical object such as a hairspray can. Cut a strip of thin card approximately 2.6 inches wide and long enough to wrap around the inside twice. Secure the card with tape.

tip.text: Make sure the card overlaps securely so it does not shift while stuffing the pot.

steps.title: Assemble the Pot Body
steps.content: Keeping the card and lining on the cylinder, pull the patchwork section over the outside so the patchwork is right side out and the lining remains inside.

tip.text: Check that the patchwork seams align vertically before stuffing to prevent a crooked finished shape.

steps.title: Stuff the Pot
steps.content: Firmly stuff polyester fiberfill between the outer patchwork layer and the inner lining layer until the pot feels evenly padded.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually and distribute it evenly around the pot to maintain a smooth rounded shape.

steps.title: Close the Side Seam
steps.content: Slide the pot slightly off the cylinder. Align the remaining open patchwork edges and close them using oversewing stitches.

tip.text: Keep the stuffing away from the seam area while stitching to ensure the seam closes neatly.

steps.title: Finish the Top Edge
steps.content: Oversew the folded top edges of the lining and outer fabric together. Add additional stuffing if necessary before completely closing the opening.

tip.text: Understuffing can cause the pot to collapse, while overstuffing may distort the circular shape.

steps.title: Attach the Felt Base
steps.content: Cut a felt circle approximately 3.1 inches in diameter. Position it on the bottom of the pot and secure it with oversewing stitches around the edge.

tip.text: Pin the felt base evenly before stitching to keep it centered on the finished pincushion pot.

steps.title: Complete the Project
steps.content: Remove the cylinder form and reshape the pincushion pot. Adjust the stuffing as needed and smooth the sides to complete the project.

tip.text: Roll the finished pot gently between your hands to distribute the stuffing evenly and create a polished finish.

items.name: Teddy Drawstring Bag
items.materialNeeded:
Red spotted linen fabric, 20.5" x 29"
Lining fabric, 20.5" x 29"
Grey linen fabric for teddy applique
Blue spotted linen fabric for pyjama applique
Black felt scrap for nose
White cotton tape for drawstring
Fusible webbing
Black embroidery thread
Grey embroidery thread
White sewing thread
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Fusible Applique, Machine Applique, Free-Motion Embroidery, Straight Stitch Sewing, Bag Construction, Lining Construction, Turning, Topstitching, Drawstring Channel Construction, Drawstring Insertion, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Main Fabric and Lining
steps.content: Cut one piece of red spotted fabric measuring 20.5" x 29" and one lining piece of the same size. Fold the main fabric in half lengthwise and press to create a center reference line.

tip.text: Press the fold accurately because it will help position the applique design evenly on the front of the bag.

steps.title: Prepare the Applique Pieces
steps.content: Trace the teddy bear and pyjama templates onto fusible webbing. Roughly cut around each shape and fuse them to the wrong side of the grey and blue fabrics. Cut out all applique pieces accurately along the template lines.

tip.text: Leaving excess fabric around applique pieces can make the finished design look uneven and bulky.

steps.title: Fuse the Applique Design
steps.content: Arrange the teddy bear and pyjama pieces on one half of the red fabric front. Position the teddy approximately 0.4" away from the center fold to allow for seam allowances. Fuse the pieces in place with an iron.

tip.text: Check the placement carefully before pressing permanently because repositioning fused applique can be difficult.

steps.title: Machine Applique the Teddy and Pyjamas
steps.content: Using grey thread, machine applique around the teddy and pyjama shapes to secure them permanently to the bag front.

tip.text: Sew slowly around curves and corners to create smooth applique edges.

steps.title: Add Embroidered Details
steps.content: Attach a quilting or free-motion foot and use black thread to embroider facial details and decorative lines onto the pyjamas. Cut a small nose from black felt and stitch it securely in place.

tip.text: Reduce sewing speed during free-motion embroidery to maintain smooth, controlled stitching.

steps.title: Join the Main Fabric and Lining
steps.content: Place the top edge of the lining and main fabric right sides together. Sew across the top edge and press the seam allowances open.

tip.text: Pressing the seam open helps reduce bulk and creates a cleaner finished opening.

steps.title: Fold and Assemble the Bag
steps.content: Fold the entire project lengthwise with right sides together, matching the main fabric and lining sections. Pin all layers securely.

tip.text: Misaligned side seams can cause the lining and outer bag to twist after turning.

steps.title: Sew the Side and Bottom Seams
steps.content: Starting at the folded bottom corner of the outer bag, sew along the bottom edge, up the side seam, continue onto the lining section, and sew the lining bottom edge. Leave a 2" to 4" opening in the lining bottom seam for turning.

tip.text: Do not forget the turning opening in the lining, or the bag cannot be turned right side out.

steps.title: Turn the Bag Right Side Out
steps.content: Pull the entire bag through the opening left in the lining. Carefully shape the corners and seams until everything is fully turned.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to push out corners without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Close the Lining Opening
steps.content: Fold the seam allowances inward and stitch the lining opening closed. Press the lining flat.

tip.text: Press the opening before stitching to create a nearly invisible closure.

steps.title: Insert the Lining
steps.content: Push the lining inside the outer bag and press the upper edge neatly so the lining sits smoothly inside.

tip.text: Twisted lining layers can create wrinkles and prevent the drawstring channel from lying flat.

steps.title: Create the Drawstring Channel
steps.content: Measure 4" down from the top edge of the bag and stitch completely around the bag to form a drawstring channel.

tip.text: Keep the channel width consistent so the drawstring moves smoothly around the bag.

steps.title: Open Access Points
steps.content: Carefully cut small openings through the seam allowance at the channel access points to allow the drawstring to pass through.

tip.text: Only cut through the seam allowance and avoid cutting into the stitching line.

steps.title: Insert the Drawstring
steps.content: Attach a safety pin to one end of the cotton tape and thread it through the entire channel until it returns to the opening.

tip.text: Secure the free end of the tape while threading to prevent it from slipping inside the channel.

steps.title: Finish the Bag
steps.content: Tie a knot in the ends of the cotton tape to prevent it from pulling through the channel. Press the bag flat and adjust the gathers evenly to complete the project.

tip.text: Distribute the gathers evenly around the opening for a neat and professional finish.

items.name: Makeup Zip Pouch
items.materialNeeded:
Blush cotton fabric
Plain lilac cotton fabric
Lilac spot cotton fabric
Blue fabric scraps
Dark pink fabric scraps
Yellow fabric scraps
Silver glitter felt
Lightweight fusible interfacing
Wadding
5.91 inches zipper
Embroidery thread
Sewing thread
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Fusible Applique, Machine Applique, Free-Motion Embroidery, Quilting, Zipper Installation, Lining Construction, Boxed Corners, Bag Construction, Topstitching, Turning, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Panels
steps.content: Cut one blush fabric panel measuring 4.9" x 10.2", one lilac spot panel measuring 1.6" x 10.2", and one plain lilac panel measuring 3.3" x 10.2". Place the pieces right sides together and sew them into a single front panel. Press the seams open.

tip.text: Press each seam immediately after sewing to reduce bulk and create a smooth foundation for the applique.

steps.title: Prepare the Applique Pieces
steps.content: Trace all makeup-themed template pieces onto the selected fabrics using the reference image as a color guide. Apply lightweight fusible interfacing to the back of each piece except the glitter felt pieces. Cut out all shapes accurately.

tip.text: Cutting too far outside the template lines can make the applique design appear uneven after stitching.

steps.title: Attach the Applique Design
steps.content: Arrange the applique pieces on the front panel as desired. Fuse or pin them in place and machine stitch around each shape to secure. Add the decorative text using hand stitching or machine embroidery and remove any transfer paper or tissue used for marking.

tip.text: Pin all applique pieces before stitching to check the overall composition and spacing.

steps.title: Quilt the Front Panel
steps.content: Use the completed front panel as a template to cut two pieces of wadding. Place one piece of wadding behind the front panel and quilt around each applique shape to secure the layers together.

tip.text: Quilting close to the applique edges helps stabilize the fabric and prevents shifting during bag assembly.

steps.title: Prepare the Zipper Ends
steps.content: Cut two fabric pieces measuring 1.4" x 4.3". Fold a 0.2" flap to the wrong side of each short end and press. Fold each piece in half and stitch one piece to each end of the zipper to extend and finish the zipper ends.

tip.text: Uneven zipper tabs can cause the zipper to sit crooked when attached to the bag.

steps.title: Attach the First Side of the Zipper
steps.content: Place the front panel right side up on top of a piece of wadding. Position the zipper right side down along the upper edge. Place one lining piece right side down on top. Pin and sew through all layers using a zipper foot.

tip.text: Keep the zipper teeth clear of the needle path to avoid breaking needles or damaging the zipper.

steps.title: Attach the Second Side of the Zipper
steps.content: Layer the remaining wadding, back fabric right side up, zipper right side down, and remaining lining piece right side down. Pin and sew along the zipper edge. Turn both sides right side out and topstitch along the zipper.

tip.text: Topstitch close to the zipper edge to prevent fabric from catching in the zipper during use.

steps.title: Open the Zipper and Trim the Corners
steps.content: Open the zipper halfway. Flatten the bag so the lining pieces are together and the outer pieces are together. Cut a 1.4" square from each bottom corner of both the outer fabric and lining.

tip.text: Always open the zipper before sewing the pouch closed or turning the bag later will be difficult.

steps.title: Sew the Bag Body
steps.content: Place the outer fabrics right sides together and the lining fabrics right sides together. Sew around the outer edges using a 0.2" seam allowance. Leave a 3.1" opening in the bottom of the lining for turning.

tip.text: Do not sew across the boxed-corner cutouts, as they will be shaped in the next step.

steps.title: Create the Boxed Corners
steps.content: Match the side seam to the bottom seam at each cut corner to form a boxed corner. Pin securely and sew across each opening. Trim excess fabric from the corners.

tip.text: Align the seams precisely before stitching to create symmetrical boxed corners.

steps.title: Turn and Finish the Bag
steps.content: Turn the bag right side out through the opening in the lining. Push out all corners and shape the bag carefully. Fold the lining opening seam allowances inward and stitch it closed. Push the lining inside the bag and press the finished pouch.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to shape the corners without stretching or damaging the fabric.

items.name: Unicorn Storage Basket
items.materialNeeded:
Patterned cotton fabric for outer body
Cotton fabric for lining
Glitter felt
Printed cotton fabric
Fusible webbing
Wadding
Plastic doll eyes
Embroidery thread
Multi-colored yarn
Matching sewing thread
Pins or sewing clips
Fabric scissors
Iron and ironing board
items.techniques: Template Cutting, Fusible Applique, Machine Applique, Embroidery, Soft Toy Construction, Turning, Stuffing, Pleat Formation, Decorative Stitching, Hemming, Bag Construction, Boxed Corners, Lining Construction, Topstitching, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Template Pieces
steps.content: Print and assemble the templates. Apply fusible webbing to the back of the glitter felt and printed cotton fabrics. Cut two nostrils, one scalloped nose piece, two inner ears, two outer ears, one horn, one head piece, one outer front body piece, one outer back body piece, one lining front body piece, and one lining main body piece.

tip.text: Label each cut piece immediately after cutting to avoid confusion during assembly.

steps.title: Create the Unicorn Face Applique
steps.content: Fuse the inner ear pieces onto the outer ears. Fuse the nostrils onto the scalloped nose piece. Position and fuse the completed nose applique onto the head piece near the lower edge.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when fusing glitter felt to prevent damage from excessive heat.

steps.title: Add Facial Details
steps.content: Attach the doll eyes securely to the head piece. Embroider the mouth and any additional facial details using embroidery thread.

tip.text: Avoid placing the eyes too close together, as it can distort the unicorn's facial proportions.

steps.title: Construct the Horn and Ears
steps.content: Fold the horn piece right sides together and sew along the long edges, leaving the bottom open. Turn right side out and lightly stuff with wadding. Create a small pleat at the base of each ear and secure with a few stitches.

tip.text: Use only a small amount of stuffing so the horn remains soft and easy to sew into the seam.

steps.title: Attach the Horn and Ears
steps.content: Place the head piece right side up. Position the horn at the center of the top edge with the tip pointing downward. Place one ear on each side of the horn. Baste all pieces in place.

tip.text: Ensure the ears and horn point inward toward the fabric so they will face outward after turning.

steps.title: Join the Head and Back Body
steps.content: Place the outer back body piece right sides together with the head piece. Stitch along the top edge, securing the horn and ears within the seam. Remove any temporary basting stitches.

tip.text: Backstitch over the horn and ear bases to reinforce these high-stress areas.

steps.title: Add the Unicorn Mane
steps.content: Thread strands of multi-colored yarn around the horn area to create a decorative mane. Wrap embroidery thread around the horn in a spiral pattern and secure at both ends.

tip.text: Mix several yarn colors and lengths to create a fuller, more playful mane.

steps.title: Prepare the Outer Pocket
steps.content: Create a 0.4" hem along the top edge of the outer front body piece. Place the pocket piece onto the lower portion of the outer body with right sides facing outward. Stitch around the sides and bottom, leaving the hemmed top edge open.

tip.text: Do not accidentally stitch the pocket opening closed.

steps.title: Create the Outer Storage Body
steps.content: Sew the outer body pieces together according to the template shape. Box both lower corners by measuring approximately 2.4" from each corner point, stitching across, and trimming the excess fabric. Turn the outer body right side out.

tip.text: Measure both boxed corners carefully so the organizer stands evenly.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Hem the upper edge of the lining front body piece by 0.4". Sew it onto the lower section of the lining body to create the lining pocket. Stitch around the sides and bottom, leaving the top open. Box the lining corners the same way as the outer body but leave the lining wrong side out.

tip.text: Do not turn the lining right side out before inserting it into the organizer.

steps.title: Insert Wadding and Assemble
steps.content: Place the lining inside the outer organizer. Match the pocket openings and baste them together. Cut a slightly smaller head-shaped piece of wadding and insert it between the outer fabric and lining at the unicorn head section.

tip.text: Trim the wadding slightly smaller than the template to reduce seam bulk.

steps.title: Finish the Organizer
steps.content: Fold the raw edges of both the lining and outer fabric inward. Press neatly, pin together, and topstitch all around the opening to secure the layers and complete the unicorn storage organizer.

tip.text: Check that all raw edges are fully enclosed before topstitching to prevent fraying during use.

items.name: Felt Glasses Sleeve
items.materialNeeded:
Violet felt
Lavender felt
Aqua felt
Primrose felt
Cream felt
Matching sewing thread
Ribbon, 13"
2 buttons
Embroidery thread
Pins or sewing clips
Fabric scissors
Pinking shears
Hand sewing needle
Template printout
items.techniques: Template Cutting, Felt Applique, Hand Applique, Oversewing, Decorative Embroidery, Button Attachment, Topstitching, Hemming, Soft Case Construction, Edge Finishing

steps.title: Cut the Main Felt Pieces
steps.content: Cut two violet felt rectangles measuring 4" x 4.75" and two lavender felt rectangles measuring 2.4" x 4". Pair one small rectangle with one large rectangle for the front and back sections.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors to keep the felt edges clean and smooth.

steps.title: Join the Front and Back Panels
steps.content: Pin a long edge of each lavender rectangle to a short edge of each violet rectangle. Sew using a seam allowance of approximately 0.4". Press the seams flat and topstitch along both joined seams.

tip.text: Topstitch close to the seam line to help the felt lie flat and prevent bulky edges.

steps.title: Shape the Case Opening
steps.content: With the lavender section positioned at the top of the front panel, cut a smooth semi-circular notch at the center of the upper edge to create the opening for the case.

tip.text: Avoid cutting the notch too deep or it may weaken the opening edge.

steps.title: Prepare the Owl Applique Pieces
steps.content: Using the template, cut a pale pink circle for the owl body, aqua pieces for the head and wings, primrose pieces for the feet, beak, moon, and stars, and cream circles for the eyes.

tip.text: Keep all applique pieces organized by color before beginning assembly.

steps.title: Attach the Owl Body
steps.content: Position the owl body on the lower front section of the case. Stitch around the edges using matching thread and an oversewing stitch. Tuck the feet beneath the lower edge of the body while sewing.

tip.text: Pin the applique pieces in place before stitching to maintain accurate positioning.

steps.title: Add the Owl Features
steps.content: Stitch the owl head, wings, beak, and eye pieces onto the body. Secure each piece with small, even oversewing stitches around the edges.

tip.text: Large uneven stitches can make the applique look untidy and less secure.

steps.title: Attach Buttons and Embroider Details
steps.content: Sew one button onto each eye using violet thread. Embroider eyebrows and any decorative facial details with embroidery thread.

tip.text: Ensure the buttons are sewn securely so they do not loosen with regular use.

steps.title: Decorate the Upper Panel
steps.content: Stitch the moon and star applique pieces onto the upper lavender section of the front panel using matching thread and oversewing stitches.

tip.text: Arrange the moon and stars before sewing to create a balanced composition.

steps.title: Create the Ribbon Closure
steps.content: Attach one end of the ribbon to the center top of the back panel. Cut a lavender felt rectangle measuring 0.6" x 0.8" and stitch it over the ribbon end to conceal and reinforce the attachment point.

tip.text: Stitch several times through the ribbon attachment area for added durability.

steps.title: Make the Ribbon Tab
steps.content: Cut two lavender felt circles approximately 0.8" in diameter. Sandwich the free ribbon end between the circles and oversew around the edges to create a decorative pull tab.

tip.text: Keep the ribbon centered between the circles before stitching.

steps.title: Assemble the Case
steps.content: Place the front and back pieces wrong sides together. Stitch around both long sides and across the bottom edge approximately 0.3" from the edge using an oversewing stitch.

tip.text: Do not sew across the top opening or the case will not be usable.

steps.title: Finish the Edges
steps.content: Trim the outer edges carefully with pinking shears to create a decorative finish and reduce fraying. Inspect all seams and trim any loose threads.

tip.text: Trim slowly with pinking shears to maintain an even decorative edge around the entire case.

items.name: Pom-Pom Fabric Pot
items.materialNeeded:
Outer fabric (17.7 x 19.7 inches)
Lining fabric (17.7 x 19.7 inches)
Medium-weight fusible interfacing (17.7 x 19.7 inches)
Pom-pom trim (39.4 inches)
Plastic canvas mesh or sturdy card (3.5 x 7.9 inches)
Fabric glue
Matching sewing thread
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Fusible Interfacing Application, Straight Stitching, Box Corner Construction, Turning, Slip Stitching, Topstitching, Trim Attachment

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Pieces
steps.content: Print and cut out the template. Cut two outer fabric pieces, two lining pieces, and two interfacing pieces using the template. Fuse the interfacing to the wrong side of the outer fabric pieces according to the manufacturer's instructions.

tip.text: Allow the interfacing to cool completely before handling to ensure a strong bond with the fabric.

steps.title: Sew the Outer Fabric Body
steps.content: Place the outer fabric pieces right sides together. Match the edges marked A and sew using a 0.4-inch seam allowance. Then align the edges marked B and C and sew to form the pot shape.

tip.text: Carefully match the template markings before sewing, as misaligned seams can distort the final shape of the pot.

steps.title: Create the Boxed Corners
steps.content: With the outer fabric still wrong side out, press the seams flat. Pull apart the cut-out corner sections to form boxed corners and stitch across each opening using a 0.4-inch seam allowance. Turn the outer fabric right side out.

tip.text: Flatten the corner seams evenly before stitching to create a neat, symmetrical base.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Repeat the same construction process with the lining pieces. Sew the side seams, form the boxed corners, and leave the lining wrong side out.

tip.text: Press each seam after sewing to reduce bulk and improve the final finish.

steps.title: Insert the Base Support
steps.content: Cut a piece of plastic canvas mesh or sturdy card slightly smaller than the base of the pot. Place it inside the base of the outer fabric and secure it with a few hand stitches or fabric glue.

tip.text: Make sure the base insert is smaller than the seam allowances so it does not interfere with assembly.

steps.title: Join the Outer and Lining
steps.content: Place the outer pot inside the lining with right sides facing each other. Align the top edges and pin around the opening. Sew around the top edge, leaving a 4-inch opening for turning.

tip.text: Do not forget to leave the turning gap, otherwise the project cannot be turned right side out.

steps.title: Turn and Shape the Pot
steps.content: Turn the entire project right side out through the opening. Push the lining inside the outer fabric and carefully shape all corners and edges.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to push out the corners without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Press the top edge flat and remove any wrinkles. Close the turning gap neatly using a slip stitch.

tip.text: Hand stitch with matching thread so the closure becomes nearly invisible.

steps.title: Topstitch the Upper Edge
steps.content: Topstitch around the entire top edge of the pot to secure the layers and create a crisp finish.

tip.text: Use a slightly longer stitch length when topstitching for a cleaner decorative finish.

steps.title: Attach the Pom-Pom Trim
steps.content: Hand sew or glue the pom-pom trim around the upper edge of the pot. Fold the top edge of the pot downward to reveal the lining and complete the project.

tip.text: If using fabric glue, allow it to dry completely before folding or handling the pot.

items.name: Patchwork Pumpkin Pin Cushions
items.finishedSize:
Large: 5.1 inches diameter
Small: 3.9 inches diameter
items.materialNeeded:
Assorted cotton fabric scraps
Green felt
Green embroidery thread
Toy stuffing (polyester fiberfill)
Decorative ribbon
Brown felt
Green button
Matching sewing thread
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Segment Piecing, Turning, Stuffing, Sculpting with Thread, Felt Appliqué, Decorative Embellishment

steps.title: Cut the Pumpkin Segments
steps.content: Download and print the pumpkin template. Trace the pumpkin segment template onto eight fabric pieces using a variety of coordinating fabrics. Cut out all eight segments for one pumpkin.

tip.text: Arrange the fabric colors before sewing to create a balanced patchwork effect around the pumpkin.

steps.title: Sew the Segment Pairs
steps.content: Place two pumpkin segments right sides together and stitch along one curved edge using a 0.25-inch seam allowance. Finger-press the seam open. Repeat until four pairs are completed.

tip.text: Matching the curved edges accurately is important. Misaligned points can distort the pumpkin shape.

steps.title: Create the Pumpkin Halves
steps.content: Join two pairs together to create one half of the pumpkin containing four segments. Repeat to create the second half. Press all seams open.

tip.text: Press seams after each joining step to reduce bulk and create smoother curves.

steps.title: Assemble the Pumpkin Body
steps.content: Place both pumpkin halves right sides together and stitch around the outer edge, leaving approximately a 0.8-inch opening at the base for turning and stuffing.

tip.text: Do not sew the turning gap closed. The opening is needed for stuffing later.

steps.title: Turn and Stuff
steps.content: Turn the pumpkin right side out through the opening. Carefully shape the curves and fill firmly with toy stuffing until the pumpkin is evenly rounded.

tip.text: Understuffing can cause the pumpkin to collapse, while overstuffing may strain the seams.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Hand sew the opening closed using small ladder stitches or whip stitches.

tip.text: Use matching thread and small stitches for an almost invisible finish.

steps.title: Sculpt the Pumpkin Segments
steps.content: Thread a long needle with doubled green embroidery thread. Insert the needle through the center top of the pumpkin and pull it through the bottom. Wrap the thread around the pumpkin between each segment, pulling slightly to create the characteristic pumpkin grooves. Repeat around all eight sections.

tip.text: Pull the thread firmly but not excessively, or the stuffing may become distorted.

steps.title: Create the Base Flower
steps.content: Cut the small flower shape from green felt. Sew a green button in the center and attach the felt flower to the base of the pumpkin, stitching through the entire pumpkin for stability.

tip.text: Stitch through the center of the pumpkin to help maintain the sculpted shape.

steps.title: Make Decorative Ribbon Curls
steps.content: Wrap ribbon around a cylindrical object and secure it. Apply craft glue and allow it to dry completely. Remove the curled ribbon and trim to the desired length.

tip.text: Allow the glue to dry fully before removing the ribbon to preserve the spiral shape.

steps.title: Attach the Ribbon Decoration
steps.content: Hand sew two curled ribbon pieces to the top of the pumpkin near the stem position.

tip.text: Position the ribbon curls before sewing to ensure they complement the pumpkin shape.

steps.title: Create the Stem
steps.content: Cut a piece of brown felt measuring approximately 1.4 inches x 2 inches. Fold it in half lengthwise, sew along the side and top edge, then stuff firmly to create the stem.

tip.text: Stuff the stem firmly so it remains upright when attached.

steps.title: Make and Attach the Leaf
steps.content: Cut a leaf shape from green felt. Make a slit at the center, insert the finished stem through the opening, and sew the stem securely to the leaf.

tip.text: Secure the stem with several stitches to prevent it from loosening during use.

steps.title: Finish the Pumpkin
steps.content: Position the felt leaf and stem assembly on top of the pumpkin and hand sew it securely in place. Adjust the ribbon curls and complete the pincushion.

tip.text: Take a few extra stitches through the center of the pumpkin to keep the embellishments firmly attached.

items.name: Fox Toy Basket
items.finishedSize: Approximately 10 inches diameter x 12 inches height
items.materialNeeded:
Orange cotton fabric
Taupe cotton fabric
White patterned cotton fabric
Quilt batting
Fusible adhesive web
Polyester thread
2 large round buttons
1 square button
items.techniques: Appliqué, Fusible Adhesive Application, Zigzag Stitching, Straight Stitching, Bag Construction, Circular Base Attachment, Topstitching, Slip Stitching
items.keyTechniques: Appliqué, Circular Base Construction, Zigzag Stitching

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut the orange outer body pieces, taupe lining pieces, circular base pieces, ear pieces, eye appliqué, and quilt batting according to the pattern. Fuse quilt batting to the orange body and base pieces.

tip.text: Label each pattern piece after cutting to avoid confusion during assembly.

steps.title: Quilt the Outer Fabric
steps.content: Place each orange outer fabric piece on its matching quilt batting piece. Secure the layers together by zigzag stitching around the entire edge.

tip.text: Do not stretch the fabric while stitching or the outer tote may become distorted.

steps.title: Prepare the Appliqué Pieces
steps.content: Trace the eye pieces and inner ear pieces onto fusible adhesive web. Fuse them to the wrong side of the white fabric and carefully cut them out.

tip.text: Allow the fused pieces to cool before cutting for cleaner and more accurate edges.

steps.title: Construct the Fox Ears
steps.content: Cut two mirrored pairs of outer ears from orange fabric and one pair from batting. Baste one fabric ear layer to the batting, fuse the inner ear appliqué in the center, and secure it with a zigzag stitch.

tip.text: Center the inner ear appliqué carefully to keep both ears symmetrical.

steps.title: Sew and Turn the Ears
steps.content: Place the remaining ear pieces right sides together over the prepared ears. Stitch around the curved edges, leaving the bottom edge open. Trim the seam allowance, turn right side out, and press flat.

tip.text: Trim the seam allowance around curves before turning to reduce bulk and improve the ear shape.

steps.title: Attach the Fox Face
steps.content: Find the center of the padded orange body panel. Fuse the eye appliqué piece in place and secure it with a zigzag stitch around the edge.

tip.text: Use a matching thread color for the appliqué stitching to achieve a cleaner finish.

steps.title: Attach the Ears
steps.content: Position the completed ears along the top edge of the orange body panel, matching the raw edges. Baste them temporarily in place.

tip.text: Check that both ears angle outward evenly before stitching permanently.

steps.title: Join the Outer and Lining Top Edge
steps.content: Place the taupe lining panel over the orange outer panel with right sides together. Stitch along the top edge, securing the ears between the layers.

tip.text: Make sure the ears are fully tucked inside the seam allowance to prevent accidentally stitching through the visible ear section.

steps.title: Form the Tote Body
steps.content: Open the joined fabric layers and fold them in the opposite direction so the outer fabrics face together and the lining fabrics face together. Stitch the side seams, leaving a 2.75-inch opening in the lining seam for turning later.

tip.text: Press the top seam before sewing the side seams to keep the tote body aligned.

steps.title: Attach the Outer Base
steps.content: Pin the orange circular base to the bottom edge of the outer tote. Match opposite points first, then quarter points, before stitching around the entire circle.

tip.text: Distribute the fabric evenly around the circle to avoid puckers or gathers.

steps.title: Attach the Lining Base
steps.content: Repeat the same process with the taupe lining circle, stitching it to the lining section of the tote.

tip.text: Clip small notches into the seam allowance if needed to help the circular base sit smoothly.

steps.title: Turn the Tote Right Side Out
steps.content: Pull the entire tote through the opening left in the lining. Carefully shape the base and side seams until the tote stands evenly.

tip.text: Pull gently while turning to avoid stressing the circular seam allowances.

steps.title: Close the Lining Opening
steps.content: Fold the raw edges of the lining opening inward and close it neatly using a slip stitch.

tip.text: Use small hand stitches for an almost invisible closure.

steps.title: Topstitch the Tote Opening
steps.content: Push the lining inside the tote. Press the top edge and topstitch around the opening to keep the lining in place and create a clean finish.

tip.text: A slightly longer stitch length often produces neater topstitching on thicker layers.

steps.title: Add the Fox Face Details
steps.content: Sew two large round buttons onto the eye appliqué sections and attach a square button at the center as the fox nose. Complete the project.

tip.text: If the tote is intended for young children, replace buttons with embroidered details for added safety.

items.name: Floral Gift Duo
items.finishedSize: Glasses case: approx. 7.1 inches x 3.5 inches; Nail scissors pouch: approx. 3.9 inches x 2.4 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric: floral print, 7.9 inches square; small print, 7.9 inches square.
Felt: thick felt, 15.7 inches square; dark orange, orange, yellow, blue, red and green scraps.
Sequins: orange.
Seed beads.
Embroidery thread, coordinating.
Fusible webbing.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Embroidery scissors.
0.4 inches seam allowance used where stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Embroidery, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Beadwork

steps.title: Prepare the Glasses Case Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the template. Cut the glasses case shape from thick felt and a mirrored lining piece from green floral fabric. Cut small squares of dark orange, orange, yellow and green felt and apply fusible webbing to the reverse side of each piece.

tip.text: Fuse the webbing before cutting detailed motifs to prevent distortion.

steps.title: Cut and Arrange the Flower Motifs
steps.content: Trace the flower, oval and stem motifs onto the appropriate felt colours and cut them out carefully. Arrange the stem and flower pieces on the front of the glasses case and press them into position.

tip.text: Check motif placement before pressing, as fused pieces are difficult to reposition.

steps.title: Decorate the Flower Design
steps.content: Stitch around the flower motifs using straight stitch and zigzag stitch. Layer the oval shapes to create the flower centre and embellish with orange sequins and seed beads. Add extra decorative stitching around the design.

tip.text: Mix hand embroidery with machine stitching for added texture and detail.

steps.title: Add the Embroidered Message
steps.content: Write 'Mum' or a personalised message onto the fabric with an air-erasable pen. Embroider over the lettering using backstitch and coordinating embroidery thread.

tip.text: Test the pen on a fabric scrap first to ensure the markings disappear completely.

steps.title: Construct the Glasses Case
steps.content: Place the felt outer and floral lining right sides together. Stitch around the curved edges, leaving the opening edge free. Turn right side out, press carefully and tuck the lining inside. Fold the lining edge under and secure with neat hand stitches.

tip.text: Trim bulky seam allowances around curves before turning for a smoother finish.

steps.title: Prepare the Nail Scissors Pouch
steps.content: Cut the pouch front and back pieces from green wool felt and matching lining fabric. Apply fusible webbing to blue, green and red felt scraps and transfer the flower template shapes.

tip.text: Keep appliqué pieces organised in colour groups to speed up assembly.

steps.title: Decorate the Pouch Front
steps.content: Fuse the flower and stem motifs onto the front pouch section. Secure them using zigzag and straight stitching, then embellish with sequins and beads around the flower centre and outer edges.

tip.text: Avoid placing bulky beads too close to seam allowances.

steps.title: Assemble the Pouch
steps.content: With right sides together, sew the front and back pouch pieces. Turn right side out and press. Stitch the lining separately, insert it into the pouch and fold the raw edges inward. Hand stitch the lining securely into place.

tip.text: Ensure the lining sits flat inside the pouch before securing it permanently.

steps.title: Finish the Gift Set
steps.content: Inspect all stitching, trim loose threads and give both projects a final press. Pair the glasses case and scissors pouch together to create a coordinated handmade Mother's Day gift set.

tip.text: Matching embroidery colours across both projects creates a professional coordinated look.

items.name: Snow Globe Sweater
items.finishedSize: Adult jumper with custom snow globe appliqué
items.materialNeeded:
Paper globe templates.
Scissors.
Felt or other non-fraying fabric in two colours.
Fabric marker pen.
Sewing machine with darning foot.
A4 plastic craft sheet.
Selection of Christmas-themed appliqué pieces.
Bean-bag filling.
Glitter.
Dress pins.
items.techniques: Applique, Machine Sewing, Decorative Embellishment

steps.title: Prepare the Jumper
steps.content: Iron the Christmas appliqué pieces centrally onto the jumper according to the desired design layout.

tip.text: Arrange all appliqué elements before ironing to ensure the composition is balanced.

steps.title: Create the Globe Template
steps.content: Using an A4 plastic craft sheet and the snow globe template, cut out the snow globe shape, enlarging the template if required to fit the design.

tip.text: A template that is too small may not fully cover the appliqué scene.

steps.title: Cut the Felt Pieces
steps.content: Cut the outer globe circle and the globe base from felt using the prepared template as a guide.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors to achieve smooth, accurate curves.

steps.title: Add the Snow Effect
steps.content: Place a small amount of bean-bag filling along with glitter, stars, or other festive embellishments over the appliqué design.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling the globe or the contents may restrict movement inside the design.

steps.title: Assemble the Globe
steps.content: Position the plastic craft sheet over the embellishments. Place the felt globe ring on top, followed by the felt base, ensuring all layers align correctly.

tip.text: Misaligned layers can leave parts of the plastic sheet exposed.

steps.title: Pin Everything in Place
steps.content: Secure all layers with dress pins, keeping the decorative filling evenly distributed within the globe area.

tip.text: Use plenty of pins around curves to prevent shifting during sewing.

steps.title: Sew the Snow Globe
steps.content: Fit a darning foot to your sewing machine and sew around the outer edge of the felt globe using a straight stitch close to the edge to secure all layers.

tip.text: Sew slowly around curved sections to maintain an even seam and avoid puckering.

items.name: Bunny Bow Scrunchie
items.finishedSize: One adult or child-sized scrunchie with removable bunny ears
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric, assorted fat quarters.
Round elastic cord.
Fabric glue.
items.techniques: Basic Sewing, Gathering, Slip Stitching, Knot Tying

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Choose a cotton fat quarter and cut the required strips for the scrunchie band and bunny ear tie according to the template measurements.

tip.text: Lightweight cotton creates softer gathers and is easier to tie into neat bunny ears.

steps.title: Sew the Scrunchie Tube
steps.content: Fold the scrunchie strip lengthwise with right sides together and stitch along the long edge to create a fabric tube.

tip.text: Keep the seam allowance consistent to ensure the scrunchie gathers evenly.

steps.title: Turn and Insert Elastic
steps.content: Turn the tube right side out and thread the round elastic through the casing. Secure the elastic ends together with a strong knot.

tip.text: Hold both ends of the elastic firmly while threading to prevent it slipping back into the tube.

steps.title: Close the Scrunchie
steps.content: Tuck the raw fabric edges inside and close the opening using slip stitches or a small amount of fabric glue.

tip.text: Hand stitching gives a cleaner finish than glue and creates a more durable scrunchie.

steps.title: Create the Bunny Ears
steps.content: Cut and sew the bunny ear piece, then turn it right side out and press it flat. Shape the pointed ends neatly.

tip.text: Trim excess seam allowance at the points before turning to reduce bulk.

steps.title: Tie the Bunny Ears
steps.content: Wrap the ear piece around the scrunchie and tie it into an overhand knot, positioning the ears upright.

tip.text: Tighten the knot gradually while shaping it with your fingers to keep it neat and symmetrical.

steps.title: Adjust and Finish
steps.content: Arrange the gathers evenly around the scrunchie and adjust the ears until both sides match. Trim any loose threads.

tip.text: Avoid pulling the knot too tightly at once, as it can twist the fabric and distort the bunny ears.

items.name: Mum & Baby Animals
items.finishedSize: Mum: approx. 11.8 inches tall, Baby: approx. 5.9 inches tall
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric: plain and patterned prints.
Embroidery thread.
Toy stuffing.
Lightweight quilt wadding.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Sewing machine.
Hand sewing needle.
0.4 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Toy Making, Stuffing, Embroidery, Hand Sewing

steps.title: Cut Out the Body Pieces
steps.content: Print and cut out the templates. Trace all body, arm, leg, ear and pocket pieces onto the chosen fabrics. Cut out the pieces carefully, transferring any markings needed for assembly.

tip.text: Keep pattern pieces grouped together to avoid mixing up mum and baby sizes.

steps.title: Make the Head and Body
steps.content: Fold the body fabric right sides together and stitch around the traced outline, leaving the turning gap marked on the pattern. Clip curves where necessary and turn right side out.

tip.text: Forgetting to leave the turning gap will make stuffing impossible later.

steps.title: Create the Arms and Legs
steps.content: Sew each pair of arm and leg pieces together with right sides facing. Leave a small opening for turning. Turn the pieces right side out and press flat.

tip.text: A chopstick or turning tool helps push out narrow limbs neatly.

steps.title: Make the Ears
steps.content: Pair the ear fabrics with right sides together and stitch around the edges. Trim the seams, turn right side out and press before attaching them to the head.

tip.text: Bulky seam allowances can make the ears look uneven after turning.

steps.title: Attach Limbs and Ears
steps.content: Position the arms, legs and ears on the body according to the markings. Pin securely and stitch in place before completing the body seams.

tip.text: Check that both sides are symmetrical before sewing permanently.

steps.title: Stuff the Toy
steps.content: Fill the body, arms and legs firmly with toy stuffing. Shape the limbs as you work and ensure the stuffing is distributed evenly throughout the toy.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually in small amounts for a smoother finish.

steps.title: Close the Openings
steps.content: Fold in the seam allowances around each opening and close them using neat slip stitches. Secure all thread ends firmly.

tip.text: Large hand stitches may remain visible on the finished toy.

steps.title: Embroider the Face
steps.content: Using embroidery thread, stitch the eyes, nose, whiskers and mouth onto the face. Keep the features centered and evenly spaced.

tip.text: Draw the facial features lightly with a removable fabric marker before stitching.

steps.title: Add the Pocket or Apron Detail
steps.content: Create the decorative tummy pocket or apron piece from contrasting fabric. Attach it securely to the front of the toy using small machine or hand stitches.

tip.text: Make sure decorative pieces are firmly attached if the toy will be handled frequently.

steps.title: Make the Baby Animal
steps.content: Repeat the same construction process using the smaller pattern pieces to create the matching baby bear or bunny companion.

tip.text: Use coordinating fabrics so the mum and baby animals look like a matching set.

steps.title: Finish and Shape
steps.content: Check all seams, reshape the stuffing if necessary and trim any loose threads. Gently press the fabric if needed and make final adjustments to the facial features.

tip.text: Avoid overheating stuffed toys when pressing, as synthetic fillings can be damaged by excessive heat.

items.name: Hanging Heart Garland
items.materialNeeded:
Floral fabric, three different prints.
Plain cotton fabric, three colours.
White nylon cord.
Bamboo garden cane, 18.5 inches.
Toy filling.
Pinking shears.
Tapestry needle.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Hand Sewing, Stuffing, Decorative Hanging Construction

steps.title: Prepare the Heart Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the heart template. Cut out 30 heart pieces to make 15 stuffed hearts, using a mixture of floral and plain fabrics. Cut two matching heart shapes for each ornament.

tip.text: Mix prints and solid fabrics evenly throughout the project to create a balanced design.

steps.title: Sew the Hearts
steps.content: Place matching heart pieces right sides together and pin around the edges. Sew around each heart, leaving a 3–1.6 inches opening for turning and stuffing.

tip.text: Leaving too small an opening can make turning and stuffing difficult.

steps.title: Clip and Turn
steps.content: Trim the seam allowances and clip curves where necessary. Turn each heart right side out through the opening and gently shape the curves with a blunt tool.

tip.text: Use a knitting needle or chopstick carefully to push out the heart point neatly.

steps.title: Stuff the Hearts
steps.content: Fill each heart with toy stuffing until plump but not overly firm. Ensure the stuffing is distributed evenly throughout the shape.

tip.text: Overstuffing may distort the heart shape and strain the seams.

steps.title: Close the Openings
steps.content: Hand sew the openings closed using small ladder stitches. Repeat the process until all 15 hearts are completed.

tip.text: Matching thread colour will help hide the closing stitches.

steps.title: Prepare the Hanging Cords
steps.content: Cut three lengths of white cord. Thread a tapestry needle and pass the cord through the bottom point of a heart, bringing the needle out at the top centre. Repeat to create three vertical strands of hearts.

tip.text: Uneven spacing between hearts can make the finished wall hanging look unbalanced.

steps.title: Secure the Hearts
steps.content: Tie knots above and below each heart to keep them in position on the cord. Continue threading and securing hearts until all strands are assembled.

tip.text: Measure the spacing before knotting to ensure all strands hang evenly.

steps.title: Attach to the Bamboo Cane
steps.content: Drill or create small holes near each end and the centre of the bamboo cane. Thread the three heart strands through the holes and secure them with knots.

tip.text: Check that all knots are tight enough to support the weight of the stuffed hearts.

steps.title: Create the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Cut an additional piece of cord and tie it to both ends of the bamboo cane to form a hanging loop.

tip.text: Adjust the loop length to suit the wall space where the decoration will be displayed.

steps.title: Finish the Wall Hanging
steps.content: Trim excess cord, check the alignment of the hearts, and make final adjustments to spacing and positioning. Hang the decoration and enjoy your finished project.

tip.text: Keep the wall hanging away from direct sunlight to help preserve fabric colours.

items.name: Felt Tree Trio
items.finishedSize: Tall tree: 8.7 inches high; Medium tree: 6.3 inches high; Small tree: 4.7 inches high
items.materialNeeded:
