items.techniques: Dressmaking, A-Line Skirt Construction, Pocket Construction, Dart Sewing, Concealed Zipper Insertion, Petersham Waist Finish, Edge Stitching, Understitching, Seam Finishing, Hemming, Hand Sewing, Pressing

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Pocket Pieces
steps.content: Cut the front skirt, back skirt and contrast pocket pieces. Fold the top pocket facings along the fold line and stitch the corner nearest the centre front on both pockets. Trim corner bulk and turn the facings to the wrong side.

tip.text: Trim seam allowances diagonally at the corners to create crisp pocket edges after turning.

steps.title: Understitch the Pocket Facings
steps.content: Lengthen the stitch length slightly and sew a row of understitching approximately 0.6 inches from the cut edge. Pull the stitching line gently so the pocket edge rolls neatly to the inside. Press thoroughly.

tip.text: Skipping pressing at this stage can cause pocket edges to roll outward later.

steps.title: Attach the Pockets
steps.content: Position the pockets on the front skirt according to the pattern notches. Pin in place and edge-stitch around the sides and lower edge, leaving the pocket opening free. Neaten the pocket edges together with the skirt side seams.

tip.text: Verify both pockets are mirrored and positioned evenly before stitching.

steps.title: Sew the Back Darts
steps.content: Transfer dart markings and stitch the darts on the back skirt. Press the darts toward the centre back.

tip.text: Tie thread tails at the dart tips instead of backstitching to avoid puckering.

steps.title: Construct the Skirt Body
steps.content: Sew the centre-back seam from the hem up to the zipper opening mark. Reinforce the top of the seam. Join the front and back skirt sections at the side seams and press all seam allowances open.

tip.text: Do not accidentally stitch into the zipper opening area.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zipper
steps.content: Press the seam allowances at the zipper opening. Position the concealed zipper face down with the teeth aligned to the crease line and stitch in place using a concealed zipper foot or regular zipper foot.

tip.text: Check that the waistline aligns perfectly across both sides of the zipper before final stitching.

steps.title: Finish the Centre Back Opening
steps.content: Secure the upper ends of the zipper and test the closure. Pull the zipper slider through and close the opening. Press the centre-back seam neatly.

tip.text: Press around the zipper using a pressing cloth to avoid damaging the zipper tape.

steps.title: Apply the Petersham Waist Finish
steps.content: Stitch the inside edge of the curved Petersham ribbon to the waist edge of the skirt. Fold the longer outer edge over the seam allowance and edge-stitch from the right side to secure.

tip.text: Keep the Petersham smooth and evenly curved to avoid a wavy waistline.

steps.title: Finish the Petersham Ends
steps.content: Trim excess Petersham above the zipper. Fold the ends neatly back and tuck them inside the waistband edge. Hand stitch the folded ends and secure them to the side seams.

tip.text: Hand stitching creates a cleaner finish than machine stitching in this area.

steps.title: Hem the Skirt
steps.content: Neaten the lower edge of the skirt. Press up a 1.6 inch hem and hand stitch in place to complete the garment.

tip.text: Ensure the hem depth remains consistent all the way around the skirt.

items.techniques: Dressmaking, Box Pleat Construction, Dart Sewing, Yoke Construction, Lining Attachment, Concealed Zipper Insertion, Understitching, Seam Finishing, Topstitching, Hemming, Pressing

steps.title: Cut and Mark the Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Download, print, and cut out the pattern pieces. Cut the lower skirt sections, yoke pieces, lining pieces, and facings. Transfer all pleat and dart markings onto the fabric using tailor's chalk or clips.

tip.text: Missing pleat markings at this stage can cause uneven pleats later in the project.

steps.title: Prepare the Box Pleats
steps.content: Fold the lower skirt sections along the marked pleat lines to create four box pleats. Pin or baste the pleats in place and press thoroughly to establish crisp folds.

tip.text: Use steam and a pressing cloth to create sharp, professional-looking pleats.

steps.title: Sew the Back Yoke Darts
steps.content: Transfer the dart markings onto the two back yoke pieces. Stitch the darts and press them toward the centre back. Attach the side seam of the front yoke to one back yoke piece and repeat for the remaining side.

tip.text: Tie thread tails at the dart tips instead of backstitching to prevent puckering.

steps.title: Assemble the Lining Yoke
steps.content: Repeat the dart construction and yoke assembly process for the lining pieces. Press all seams neatly and ensure both lining and outer yoke sections match in size.

tip.text: Stack the lining and outer yokes together after sewing to verify they are identical.

steps.title: Join the Pleated Skirt Panels
steps.content: Place the pleated skirt sections right sides together and sew the side seams. Leave approximately 3.5 inches unstitched at the top of the left side seam for the zipper opening.

tip.text: Do not accidentally sew the zipper opening closed.

steps.title: Attach the Outer Yoke
steps.content: Match the skirt waist edge to the assembled outer yoke, aligning side seams and pleat positions. Stitch the yoke to the skirt and press the seam allowances upward toward the yoke.

tip.text: Distribute the pleats evenly before stitching to maintain skirt symmetry.

steps.title: Understitch the Waist Seam
steps.content: Understitch along the waist seam allowance, sewing close to the seam line through the yoke and seam allowances. This helps the lining remain inside the garment.

tip.text: A neat understitch dramatically improves the finished appearance of the waistline.

steps.title: Attach the Lining Yoke
steps.content: With right sides together, place the lining yoke over the outer yoke. Stitch around the top waist edge and side openings. Turn the lining to the inside and press carefully.

tip.text: Clip curves and grade seam allowances to reduce bulk around the waist.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zipper
steps.content: Install the concealed zipper into the left side seam opening. Stitch as close to the zipper teeth as possible using a concealed zipper foot.

tip.text: Check that the waist seam aligns perfectly across the zipper before final stitching.

steps.title: Secure the Lining
steps.content: Fold the seam allowances inside the zipper opening and hand stitch or slip stitch the lining yoke to cover the zipper tape and internal seams neatly.

tip.text: Invisible hand stitching creates a clean couture-style finish.

steps.title: Finish the Hem
steps.content: Turn up a 1.18 inch hem allowance along the lower edge of the skirt. Press, pin, and stitch the hem evenly around the entire skirt.

tip.text: Allow the skirt to hang for several hours before hemming if using fabric with significant drape.

steps.title: Final Pressing
steps.content: Give the completed skirt a thorough final press, sharpening the pleats and smoothing all seams. Inspect the zipper, hem, and waistline before wearing.

tip.text: Press pleats from the waist downward to maintain their crisp shape.

items.techniques: Dressmaking, Stretch Fabric Sewing, Four-Panel Skirt Construction, Handkerchief Hem Construction, Elastic Waistband Attachment, Overlocking, Stretch Stitching, Fusible Hemming Web Application, Hemming, Hand Sewing, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Panels
steps.content: Download and print the pattern, then cut two front panels and two back panels from jersey fabric according to the cutting layout. Transfer any notches and construction markings.

tip.text: Ensure the stretch direction of all panels is aligned before cutting.

steps.title: Sew the Panel Seams
steps.content: Match the panels right sides together and stitch the four seams to form the skirt shape. Finish the seam allowances with an overlocker or stretch stitch.

tip.text: Use a stretch stitch or narrow zigzag stitch to maintain fabric flexibility.

steps.title: Prepare the Pointed Hem Sections
steps.content: Finish the raw hem edges of each panel. At the lower point of every panel, fold the small straight edge in half, stitch across it, then turn and press to create a neat pointed corner.

tip.text: Take care when turning the points to avoid poking through delicate jersey fabric.

steps.title: Create the Handkerchief Hem
steps.content: Repeat the pointed corner construction on all four panels. Press the completed hem edges thoroughly to establish the handkerchief-style silhouette.

tip.text: Use a point turner or blunt knitting needle to sharpen corners neatly.

steps.title: Apply Fusible Hemming Web
steps.content: Position fusible hemming web along the hem allowance and press according to the manufacturer's instructions to stabilize the jersey fabric.

tip.text: Avoid stretching the fabric while pressing or the hem may become wavy.

steps.title: Stitch the Hem
steps.content: Machine stitch the hem close to the finished edge using a slightly longer stitch length suitable for stretch fabrics.

tip.text: A walking foot can help prevent stretching and puckering while hemming knit fabrics.

steps.title: Measure and Prepare the Elastic
steps.content: Measure the wearer's waist and cut elastic to fit comfortably, adding a small overlap allowance. Join the elastic ends securely to form a loop.

tip.text: Check the elastic fit before sewing permanently to avoid an uncomfortable waistband.

steps.title: Attach the Elastic Waistband
steps.content: Divide both the waistband edge and elastic loop into equal quarters. Match the markings and overlock or stretch stitch the elastic to the top edge of the skirt while gently stretching the elastic.

tip.text: Stretch only the elastic, not the fabric, while sewing.

steps.title: Fold and Conceal the Elastic
steps.content: Fold the waistband edge over the elastic toward the inside of the skirt. Press lightly and secure with stitching or a few discreet hand stitches at the seam positions.

tip.text: Hand tack the waistband at each seam to prevent the elastic from twisting during wear.

steps.title: Final Pressing
steps.content: Give the finished skirt a final press, smooth the waistband, and check that the handkerchief points hang evenly around the hem.

tip.text: Allow the skirt to hang for several hours before wearing so the hem can settle naturally.

items.techniques: Dressmaking, A-Line Skirt Construction, Dart Sewing, Pocket Flap Construction, Concealed Zipper Insertion, Waistband Construction, Belt Making, Belt Loop Construction, Buckram Reinforcement, Eyelet Installation, Edge Stitching, Topstitching, Hemming, Pressing

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Fabric
steps.content: Download and print the pattern. Cut all skirt pieces, waistband sections, pocket flaps, belt pieces, and belt loops according to the pattern layout. Transfer all notches and markings. Stitch the back darts and press them toward the centre back.

tip.text: Accurately transfer dart markings before removing the pattern pieces to ensure proper fit.

steps.title: Sew the Centre Back Seam
steps.content: Match the back skirt pieces right sides together and stitch the centre back seam, leaving the zipper opening unstitched. Finish seam allowances and press.

tip.text: Do not sew beyond the zipper notch or the zipper installation will be affected.

steps.title: Construct the Pocket Flaps
steps.content: Place each pair of flap pieces right sides together and stitch around the curved edges, leaving the top edge open. Trim seam allowances, turn right side out, and press. Position the flaps on the front skirt pieces and stitch them in place.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances before turning to achieve smoother flap edges.

steps.title: Join the Side Seams
steps.content: Place the front and back skirt pieces right sides together and sew the side seams. Finish the raw edges and press the seams open.

tip.text: Press every seam immediately after sewing for a cleaner professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare the Belt Loops
steps.content: Construct the belt loop strip by folding and stitching the long edges. Cut the strip into equal loop sections according to the pattern measurements.

tip.text: Uneven belt loops will be noticeable on the finished garment, so measure carefully.

steps.title: Attach the Waistband
steps.content: Fuse interfacing to the waistband pieces. Position the waistband along the skirt waistline with belt loops inserted at the marked locations. Stitch the waistband to the skirt and press the seam allowances upward.

tip.text: Check the placement of all belt loops before permanently stitching the waistband.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zipper
steps.content: Install the concealed zipper into the centre back opening. Stitch each side carefully and then close the remaining centre back seam below the zipper.

tip.text: Press the zipper coils flat before sewing to help create a nearly invisible zipper finish.

steps.title: Finish the Waistband
steps.content: Fold the waistband to the inside and hand stitch or edge stitch it neatly in place. Secure the upper ends of the belt loops into the waistband.

tip.text: Avoid twisting the waistband during folding and stitching.

steps.title: Hem the Skirt
steps.content: Turn up the hem allowance, press, and stitch around the skirt. Create the decorative pointed hem detail using buckram reinforcement according to the pattern instructions.

tip.text: Ensure the buckram remains fully enclosed to prevent visible edges on the finished skirt.

steps.title: Construct the Belt
steps.content: Fold and stitch the belt strip. Insert the buckram reinforcement and topstitch around the belt edges. Shape the pointed end as indicated by the pattern.

tip.text: Grade bulky seam allowances before turning the belt for sharper corners.

steps.title: Install Eyelets and Buckle
steps.content: Mark and install the eyelets in the belt. Create the buckle fastening and test the fit. Thread the completed belt through the belt loops.

tip.text: Practice eyelet installation on scrap fabric before working on the finished belt.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Give the entire skirt a thorough final press, checking the waistband, zipper, hem, and belt alignment before wearing.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth to protect delicate fabrics and prevent shine marks.

items.techniques: Dressmaking, Pleat Construction, In-Seam Pocket Construction, Pocket Bag Assembly, Concealed Zipper Insertion, Waistband Construction, Fusible Interfacing Application, Stitch-in-the-Ditch Sewing, Seam Finishing, Topstitching, Hemming, Pressing

steps.title: Create the Back Pleats
steps.content: Download and print the pattern. Form the pleats on the back skirt section using the marked notches. Fold the pleats toward the centre back, pin in place, and stitch across the upper edge to secure them temporarily.

tip.text: Check that all pleats are folded in the correct direction before stitching.

steps.title: Sew the Centre Back Seam
steps.content: Place the back skirt pieces right sides together and sew the centre back seam, leaving the zipper opening unstitched. Finish the seam allowances and press the seam open.

tip.text: Leave the exact zipper opening length marked on the pattern to ensure proper zipper installation.

steps.title: Prepare the Pocket Bags
steps.content: Fold and press the pocket bag sections following the pattern markings. Position the lower pocket pieces onto the upper pocket sections and stitch them together.

tip.text: Press each fold carefully to create crisp pocket edges.

steps.title: Attach the Pockets
steps.content: Align the completed pocket bags with the front skirt sections. Stitch the pocket pieces in place and finish the seam allowances.

tip.text: Ensure both pockets are positioned at identical heights before sewing.

steps.title: Join the Front and Side Sections
steps.content: Attach the side skirt sections to the front skirt pieces, enclosing the pocket bags within the seams. Press the seam allowances toward the centre front.

tip.text: Trim bulky seam allowances around the pocket area for a smoother finish.

steps.title: Form the Front Pleats
steps.content: Fold the front pleats according to the pattern markings and stitch across the upper edge to hold them in place.

tip.text: Measure the spacing between pleats to keep the skirt balanced.

steps.title: Sew the Side Seams
steps.content: Place the front and back skirt sections right sides together and stitch the side seams. Finish the raw edges and press the seams toward the back.

tip.text: Match pleat lines carefully across the seams for a professional appearance.

steps.title: Prepare the Waistband
steps.content: Fuse heavyweight interfacing to the waistband. Finish one long edge and press the waistband according to the pattern instructions.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth to avoid damaging the interfacing with excessive heat.

steps.title: Attach the Waistband
steps.content: Pin the waistband to the skirt waistline, matching centre back and side seam markings. Stitch in place and press the seam allowances upward.

tip.text: Distribute the pleats evenly before attaching the waistband.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zipper
steps.content: Install the concealed zipper into the centre back opening. Stitch each side carefully and close the remaining seam below the zipper.

tip.text: Press zipper coils flat before sewing to improve the invisible finish.

steps.title: Finish the Waistband Interior
steps.content: Fold the waistband to the inside, enclosing the raw edges. Secure it using stitch-in-the-ditch sewing or hand stitching.

tip.text: Check that the inner waistband edge is fully caught during stitching.

steps.title: Hem the Skirt
steps.content: Turn up a 1 inch hem allowance, press, and stitch in place. Give the entire garment a final press to complete the skirt.

tip.text: Allow the skirt to hang overnight before hemming if using heavier fabrics.

items.name: Classic Wool Panel A-Line Skirt
items.materialNeeded:

items.techniques: Dressmaking, Panel Skirt Construction, Concealed Zipper Insertion, Lining Construction, Understitching, Hand Stitching, Seam Finishing, Edge Stitching, Hemming, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the pattern. Cut all skirt panel pieces and lining sections according to the cutting guide. Transfer all notches and markings.

tip.text: Accurately transfer panel matching notches to ensure the seams align correctly.

steps.title: Assemble the Front Panels
steps.content: Place the front panel sections right sides together and stitch along the long panel seams. Press the seam allowances open and repeat for the remaining front panels.

tip.text: Press each seam as you sew to help the panels lie smoothly.

steps.title: Assemble the Back Panels
steps.content: Join the back panel sections in the same way, leaving the zipper opening unstitched in the designated seam.

tip.text: Leave the zipper opening exactly as marked on the pattern to avoid fitting issues later.

steps.title: Join the Side Seams
steps.content: Place the front and back skirt sections right sides together and sew the side seams. Press the seam allowances open.

tip.text: Match panel seam intersections carefully for a professional finish.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zipper
steps.content: Press the zipper seam allowances open and install the concealed zipper into the designated back opening. Stitch each side securely and test the zipper operation.

tip.text: Check that the waistband and panel seams align across the zipper before sewing permanently.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Assemble the lining pieces in the same order as the outer skirt, leaving the zipper opening unstitched. Finish the lower edge of the lining if required.

tip.text: Use a slightly shorter stitch length on lightweight lining fabrics for stronger seams.

steps.title: Attach the Lining
steps.content: With the skirt right side out and the lining wrong side out, place the lining over the skirt. Match all panel seams and stitch around the waistline.

tip.text: Ensure the lining is not twisted before stitching the waist seam.

steps.title: Understitch the Waistline
steps.content: Press the waist seam allowances toward the lining and understitch close to the seam line to keep the lining from rolling outward.

tip.text: Understitching greatly improves the appearance and durability of lined garments.

steps.title: Secure the Zipper Area
steps.content: Fold the lining seam allowances around the zipper and hand stitch neatly along both sides of the zipper opening.

tip.text: Keep hand stitches small and hidden to maintain a clean interior finish.

steps.title: Finish the Lining Hem
steps.content: Turn and stitch the lining hem allowance according to the pattern instructions. Press carefully.

tip.text: A shorter lining hem helps prevent it from showing beneath the skirt.

steps.title: Secure the Waistline Seams
steps.content: At each panel seam along the waistline, hand stitch the lining seam allowances to the skirt seam allowances to keep the lining positioned correctly.

tip.text: Avoid stitching through to the outer garment where the stitches may become visible.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Give the entire skirt a final press, ensuring all panel seams, zipper edges, and lining sections lie smoothly.

tip.text: Use plenty of steam when pressing wool felt to create crisp panel shaping.

items.name: Button-Front Floral A-Line Skirt
items.finishedSize:
Small: 28.3 inches
Medium: 30.7 inches
Large: 32.3 inches

19.7 inches contrast fabric
Lightweight fusible interfacing
6 buttons
Matching sewing thread
Dressmaking pins or clips
Fabric marker or tailor's chalk
Iron and pressing cloth
items.techniques: Dressmaking, A-Line Skirt Construction, Front Button Placket Construction, Pocket Construction, Pocket Flap Construction, Dart Sewing, Waistband Construction, Fusible Interfacing Application, Buttonhole Sewing, Button Attachment, Belt Loop Construction, Edge Stitching, Topstitching, Hemming, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the pattern. Cut all skirt pieces, pocket components, facings, waistband sections, and contrast fabric pieces according to the cutting layout. Transfer all markings and dart placements.

tip.text: Double-check the front opening pieces before cutting to avoid reversing left and right sections.

steps.title: Sew the Back Darts
steps.content: Stitch the back darts following the pattern markings and press them toward the center back.

tip.text: Press darts over a tailor’s ham to maintain the garment’s shape.

steps.title: Prepare the Front Pockets
steps.content: Attach the pocket linings to the front skirt pieces with right sides together. Stitch, clip curves where necessary, turn the pocket linings to the inside, and press flat.

tip.text: Avoid stretching curved pocket edges while pressing.

steps.title: Assemble the Pocket Bags
steps.content: Attach the pocket back pieces to complete the pocket bags. Stitch around the pocket edges and secure the pockets to the skirt front sections.

tip.text: Baste pocket openings temporarily to keep them aligned during later construction.

steps.title: Join the Side Seams
steps.content: Place the front and back skirt sections right sides together and sew the side seams. Finish the seam allowances and press them open.

tip.text: Ensure the pocket bags are fully enclosed within the side seams before stitching.

steps.title: Prepare the Front Opening Facings
steps.content: Fold and press the seam allowances on the front opening facings. Attach the facings to the skirt front opening edges and stitch in place.

tip.text: Trim seam allowance bulk at corners before turning for cleaner edges.

steps.title: Turn and Press the Facings
steps.content: Turn the facings to the inside of the skirt and press thoroughly. Edge stitch if desired for additional stability.

tip.text: Make sure both front opening edges remain symmetrical after pressing.

steps.title: Apply Interfacing to the Waistband
steps.content: Fuse interfacing to the waistband pieces according to the pattern instructions. Allow the pieces to cool completely before handling.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth to prevent shine marks on delicate fabrics.

steps.title: Construct the Waistband
steps.content: Sew the waistband ends together, fold the waistband in half lengthwise, and press. Attach the waistband to the skirt waistline.

tip.text: Match center points and side seams carefully to prevent twisting.

steps.title: Finish the Waistline
steps.content: Fold the inner waistband edge under and stitch in the ditch or hand sew it neatly to the inside of the skirt.

tip.text: Hand stitching the inner waistband provides a cleaner professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Button Front Closure
steps.content: Mark and sew the buttonholes along the waistband and front opening. Attach the six buttons to the corresponding positions.

tip.text: Test buttonhole size on a fabric scrap before sewing the actual garment.

steps.title: Make and Attach Belt Loops
steps.content: Cut narrow strips from the contrast fabric, fold and stitch them into belt loops, then attach them at the marked positions around the waistband.

tip.text: Backstitch securely at both ends of each belt loop for durability.

steps.title: Hem the Skirt
steps.content: Fold, press, and stitch the lower hem according to the pattern instructions to complete the skirt.

tip.text: Allow the skirt to hang overnight before hemming if using fabrics that may stretch.

Matching sewing thread
Dressmaking pins or clips

Iron and pressing cloth
items.techniques: Dressmaking, Stretch Fabric Sewing, Pencil Skirt Construction, Elastic Waistband Application, Overlocking, Stretch Stitching, Seam Finishing, Hemming, Twin Needle Topstitching, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Skirt Panels
steps.content: Download and print the pattern or draft your own using the provided measurements. Cut two front/back skirt panels on the fold according to your chosen size.

tip.text: Ensure the stretch direction runs around the body, not vertically.

steps.title: Shape the Waistline
steps.content: Mark the waist shaping measurements on the fabric and cut the waistline curves according to the drafting guide. Repeat for both front and back panels.

tip.text: Use a curved ruler to create a smooth waistline transition.

steps.title: Finish the Fabric Edges
steps.content: Neaten all raw edges using an overlocker or stretch-compatible zigzag stitch before assembly.

tip.text: Avoid stretching the fabric while overlocking or the edges may become wavy.

steps.title: Sew the Side Seams
steps.content: Place the front and back panels right sides together and stitch both side seams using a stretch stitch. Press the seams toward the back.

tip.text: Test your stitch length on a scrap of jersey fabric before sewing the garment.

steps.title: Prepare the Elastic Waistband
steps.content: Cut elastic to your waist measurement plus 0.8 inches. Overlap the ends and stitch securely to form a continuous loop.

tip.text: Check that the elastic is not twisted before joining the ends.

steps.title: Attach the Elastic
steps.content: Divide both the skirt waistline and elastic loop into quarters. Match the markings and pin evenly around the waist edge.

tip.text: Quarter-marking ensures the gathers are distributed evenly.

steps.title: Sew the Elastic to the Waistline
steps.content: Stretch the elastic gently as you sew it to the skirt waistline using a stretch stitch or overlocker, keeping the fabric flat between pins.

tip.text: Do not stretch the fabric itself—only the elastic.

steps.title: Fold the Elastic Inward
steps.content: Turn the elastic to the inside of the skirt and pin it in place, enclosing the waistband edge.

tip.text: Steam pressing lightly will help the waistband lie flat before stitching.

steps.title: Secure the Waistband
steps.content: Topstitch around the waistband using a stretch stitch or twin needle, securing the folded elastic inside.

tip.text: Avoid catching folds in the elastic while topstitching.

steps.title: Hem the Skirt
steps.content: Turn up a 1.2 inch hem allowance at the lower edge. Stabilize with hemming tape or fusible interfacing if desired, then stitch using a twin needle or stretch stitch.

tip.text: A twin needle creates a professional ready-to-wear finish on knit garments.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Give the skirt a final press using appropriate heat for jersey fabric and inspect all seams before wearing.

tip.text: Always test iron temperature on a scrap piece of knit fabric first.

items.techniques: Dressmaking, Wrap Skirt Construction, Denim Sewing, Dart Sewing, D-Ring Closure Construction, Tie Strap Construction, Waist Facing Construction, Hem Facing Construction, Understitching, Seam Grading, Hand Sewing, Button Loop Construction, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the pattern, then cut all skirt panels, facings, tie strap, D-ring tab, and waistband components according to the cutting guide. Apply interfacing to all waistband facing pieces.

tip.text: Transfer all markings before removing the pattern pieces to avoid confusion during assembly.

steps.title: Sew the Back Darts
steps.content: Stitch the darts on the back skirt panel and press them toward the center back.

tip.text: Press darts over a tailor’s ham for a smoother, more professional shape.

steps.title: Join the Back and Front Panels
steps.content: With right sides together, sew the front-right skirt panel to the back skirt panel at the side seam. Finish and press the seam allowances.

tip.text: Keep seam allowances consistent throughout the project for proper fit.

steps.title: Construct the D-Ring Tab
steps.content: Fold the D-ring tab right sides together and stitch along the long edges and tie edge, leaving the short edge open. Trim bulk, turn right side out, and press.

tip.text: Use a point turner to create crisp corners without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Attach the D-Rings
steps.content: Thread the tab through both D-rings, fold the raw edge inward, and stitch securely close to the rings.

tip.text: Reinforce the stitching because the D-ring tab carries most of the garment tension.

steps.title: Attach the D-Ring Tab to the Skirt
steps.content: Position the completed D-ring tab on the front-left skirt panel according to the pattern markings and stitch securely in place.

tip.text: Check the D-ring orientation before stitching to ensure the closure functions correctly.

steps.title: Attach the Tie Strap
steps.content: Position the tie strap on the front-right side seam approximately 0.8 inches below the waistline and baste it in place.

tip.text: Test the wrap closure before permanent stitching to confirm comfortable placement.

steps.title: Join Remaining Side Seams
steps.content: Sew the remaining front and back skirt seams, sandwiching the tie strap into the seam as indicated. Finish seam allowances and press.

tip.text: Make sure the tie strap is not twisted inside the seam.

steps.title: Attach the Waist Facing
steps.content: With right sides together, stitch the waist facing to the upper edge of the skirt. Match all notches and seam lines carefully.

tip.text: Grade seam allowances after stitching to reduce bulk around the waist.

steps.title: Construct and Attach the Hem Facing
steps.content: Join all hem facing pieces together and finish the inner edge. Attach the hem facing to the skirt hem with right sides together and stitch in place.

tip.text: Align the hem facing carefully around the asymmetrical hemline to prevent distortion.

steps.title: Understitch the Facings
steps.content: Trim and grade seam allowances, then understitch the waist and hem facings where possible. Turn the facings to the inside and press thoroughly.

tip.text: Understitching helps facings stay neatly inside the garment during wear.

steps.title: Secure the Facings
steps.content: Pin and edge stitch the hem facing in place, working carefully around the asymmetrical front overlap.

tip.text: Press the hem thoroughly before stitching to maintain a smooth finish.

steps.title: Add the Internal Button Closure
steps.content: Hand sew a thread loop to the upper edge of the front-right panel and attach the button inside the skirt to provide additional wrap security.

tip.text: Use doubled thread for the button loop to improve durability.

items.techniques: Dressmaking, Mini Skirt Construction, Patch Pocket Construction, Pocket Trim Application, Dart Sewing, Concealed Zipper Insertion, Waist Facing Construction, Full Lining Construction, Hand Hemming, Seam Finishing, Understitching, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the pattern. Cut all skirt, lining, pocket, and waistband facing pieces according to the cutting guide. Transfer dart, zipper, and pocket placement markings to the fabric.

tip.text: Pay attention to the nap direction of corduroy so all pieces run the same way.

steps.title: Apply Interfacing
steps.content: Fuse interfacing to the waistband facing pieces following the pattern instructions.

tip.text: Allow interfaced pieces to cool completely before moving them to prevent distortion.

steps.title: Prepare the Patch Pockets
steps.content: Attach ric rac trim to the pocket pieces. Place the pocket fabric and lining right sides together, stitch around the shape leaving an opening, trim bulk, turn right side out, and press.

tip.text: Trim seam allowances carefully around curves to achieve smooth pocket edges.

steps.title: Position and Attach the Pockets
steps.content: Mark the pocket placement on the front skirt panel. Pin both pockets evenly and topstitch around the edges to secure them.

tip.text: Measure pocket placement from the center and waistline to ensure both pockets are level.

steps.title: Sew the Back Darts
steps.content: Transfer dart markings and stitch the darts on both back skirt pieces. Press the darts toward the center back.

tip.text: Tie thread tails at dart points instead of backstitching to reduce puckering.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zipper
steps.content: Join the back skirt pieces up to the zipper marking and install the concealed zipper using a concealed zipper foot.

tip.text: Press the zipper coils flat before stitching for a more invisible finish.

steps.title: Join the Side Seams
steps.content: Place the front and back skirt pieces right sides together and sew the side seams. Finish seam allowances and press open.

tip.text: Press every seam immediately after sewing for a cleaner professional result.

steps.title: Attach the Waist Facing
steps.content: Pin the waistband facing to the skirt waistline, matching side seams and center back. Stitch and press the seam allowances upward.

tip.text: Check that the facing lies flat before stitching to avoid twisting.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Sew the lining darts and seams, leaving an opening at the zipper area. Press all seams neatly.

tip.text: Use a slightly shorter stitch length when sewing lightweight lining fabrics.

steps.title: Attach the Lining
steps.content: With right sides together, join the lining to the waistband facing. Turn the skirt right side out and press carefully.

tip.text: Keep the lining free from twists before attaching it to the skirt.

steps.title: Secure the Zipper Area
steps.content: Hand sew the lining around the zipper opening, ensuring the zipper remains fully functional and concealed.

tip.text: Use small slip stitches for an invisible finish.

steps.title: Hem the Skirt and Lining
steps.content: Press and stitch the skirt hem. Create a slightly shorter hem on the lining and hand sew it neatly in place.

tip.text: The lining should sit slightly above the outer hem so it remains hidden during wear.

items.name: Comfort Travel Pillow & Zipper Bag
items.finishedSize: Neck rest: 10.63 inches x 14.17 inches, Make-up bag: 6.69 inches x 9.06 inches
items.materialNeeded: Assorted cottons, fat quarters. Plain cotton scraps, mint, yellow, green. Pom pom trim, teal, yellow, pale pink. Fusible interfacing. Yellow zip, 9.84 inches. Polystyrene bead filling. Dried lavender.
items.techniques: Machine Embroidery, Hand Stitching, Zipper Insertion, Topstitching

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Cut fabric pieces
steps.content: Download the neck rest template. Cut two pieces from coordinating fabric, flipping the template for the second half. Repeat the same process for the back of the cushion.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Sew front and back pairs
steps.content: Pin each front and back pair right sides together. Stitch with a 0.2 inches seam across the short straight edge. Press the seams open and set aside.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Cut sleep pocket pieces
steps.content: For the yellow sleep pocket, cut two pieces from plain yellow fabric using the template.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Sew sleep pocket
steps.content: Pin the two yellow pieces together. Stitch with a 0.2 inches seam across the straight edge. Press the seam open.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Fold and topstitch pocket
steps.content: Fold back wrong sides together. Topstitch across the seam near the edge.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Embroider 'sleep' text
steps.content: Trace the word 'sleep' onto white tissue paper. Pin it in place on the pocket. Machine or hand embroider over the letters. Tear the tissue away carefully. Iron on the reverse side.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Clip curved edges
steps.content: Carefully make 0.2 inches snips all the way around the curved edge of the pocket. Iron the tabs onto the wrong side.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Attach sleep pocket
steps.content: Pin the pocket in place on the neck rest. Machine stitch around the curve just inside the edge, leaving the top open.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Make faux pocket
steps.content: Cut one piece of mint fabric. Apply interfacing to the back. Stitch the word 'repeat' onto it. Stitch all around the edges to attach.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Attach pom pom trim to front
steps.content: Pin teal pom pom trim right sides together around the right side of the black half. Start at the join and work around until reaching the curved end. Stitch in place.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Attach pom pom trim to back
steps.content: Repeat the process with yellow pom pom trim for the teal half of the neck rest.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Sew front and back together
steps.content: Pin the finished front and back pieces right sides together. Leave a 1.97 inches gap for turning. Stitch with a 0.2 inches seam allowance.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Snip curves and turn out
steps.content: Snip the curved edges carefully. Turn the pillow right side out through the gap.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Fill with beads
steps.content: Make a funnel from paper. Fill the pillow with polystyrene beads. Hand stitch the gap closed.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Cut lining pieces
steps.content: Using the make-up bag template, cut two pieces from plain lining fabric. Fuse interfacing to both pieces.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Cut back piece
steps.content: Cut one more piece from lining fabric for the back of the bag. Set all three lining pieces aside.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Cut and stitch front panels
steps.content: Trim the three pieces for the bag front. Stitch them together with a 0.2 inches seam. Press the seams open.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Cut palm tree shapes
steps.content: Back a piece of plain dark green fabric with interfacing. Use the template to draw two palm tree tops on the reverse side. Cut them out.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Cut tree trunks
steps.content: Back a fabric print with interfacing. Cut two tree trunks and trim.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Attach palm tree to bag front
steps.content: Pin the palm tree tops and trunks in place on the bag front. Machine stitch around the edges twice. Iron on the reverse side.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Cut zip tabs
steps.content: Cut two tab ends for the zip, measuring 1.46 inches x 4.41 inches. Turn the short ends in by 0.2 inches. Press.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Fold zip tabs
steps.content: Fold each tab in half, bringing the short ends together. Press again.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Prepare zip
steps.content: Slide the zip pull into the middle of the zip. Trim the metal ends from both sides so the zip measures 8.35 inches.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Attach tabs to zip ends
steps.content: Insert each end of the zip into the short open end of a fabric tab by 0.24 inches. Stitch across close to the fold edge several times to secure.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Attach zip to bag front and lining
steps.content: Lay the zip centrally, wrong side up, onto the top of the bag front (right side up). The top edge of the zip should be level with the raw edge. Lay a lining piece wrong side up on top. All edges should match, with the zip in the middle. Pin along the top. Use a zip foot to stitch. Trim away the tab overhang so all edges align.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Attach zip to bag back and second lining
steps.content: Fold everything back so the front of the zip and bag are facing you. Place the zip face down onto the right side of the remaining outer piece (bag back). Lay the last lining piece on top, right side down. Open the bag flat (right side up) and press both sides close to the zip. Topstitch along both sides of the zip.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Open zip and align pieces
steps.content: Open the zip completely. Align all the right sides together. Push the edge of the zip towards the lining. Match the topstitching seams. Pin in place. Repeat at the other end. Pin around all the raw edges.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Stitch around bag edges
steps.content: Machine stitch a 0.2 inches seam all the way around the raw edges of the bag. Leave the corner angles unstitched. Leave a 2.95 inches gap in one side of the lining. Keep the bag laid flat.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Stitch boxed corners left side
steps.content: Pinch the corners of the left side of the bag together so the seams match. Pin and stitch a 0.2 inches seam. Repeat on the other corner of the same side.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Stitch boxed corners right side
steps.content: Repeat the boxed corner process for the two corners on the right-hand side of the bag.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Turn bag right side out
steps.content: Pull the bag through the gap in the lining to turn it right side out.

steps.title: Make-up Bag - Close lining gap and finish
steps.content: Stitch the gap in the lining closed. Push the lining inside the bag. Press well.

items.name: Rainbow Cushion & Drawstring Bag Set
items.finishedSize: Travel pillow: approximately 11.81 inches x 9.84 inches, Drawstring backpack: approximately 15.75 inches x 12.60 inches
items.materialNeeded: Plain cottons in assorted pastel rainbow shades; plain white cotton; pastel coloured heavyweight cotton. Cotton cord, 0.28 inches. Fibre filling. Eyelets, 0.47 inches. Quilt wadding. Tape for aglets.
items.techniques: Patchwork, Curved piecing, Quilting, Topstitching, Channel sewing, Eyelet fitting

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Cut and mark arch pieces for rainbow
steps.content: Cut arch number one from white cotton and mark the guide points on the edge of the seam allowance. Cut arch number two from coloured cotton, then mark guide points on both outer and inner edges of the curve. Clip the inner curve of piece two to 0.08 inches from the traced seam line.

tip.text: When clipping the inner curve of arch two, stop short of the traced seam line to prevent weak spots or holes forming in the seam.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Sew inner curve and add arches three to five
steps.content: Pin, tack and sew the inside edge of piece two to the outside edge of piece two, matching the guide points and easing the fabric around the curve without overly stretching it. Open it out and press the seam allowance towards piece two. Cut arch numbers three, four, and five. Stitch to the outer curve of the work in turn, using the method detailed above.

tip.text: Pulling the fabric around the curve can distort the arch shape and create ripples in the finished rainbow.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Add arch six, press and pin to wadding
steps.content: Precise mark matching and tacking will result in smooth curves each time. Cut piece number six from white cotton but extend the vertical sides of piece six by 1.18 inches. Stitch to the outside of the rainbow in the same way as before. Press the whole block well and pin onto quilt wadding.

tip.text: Pressing each seam before adding the next arch helps maintain smooth, even rainbow curves.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Quilt, trim and sew pillow edges
steps.content: Quilt through the layers around each curve of the rainbow. Trim the outside edge of the rectangular block into a curve to match the rainbow, if a shaped pillow is desired. Lay the block face down onto plain cotton the same size and sew around the edges, leaving a small gap in the side for stuffing.

tip.text: Inconsistent spacing around the rainbow arches can make the quilting appear untidy and distract from the curved design.

steps.title: Travel Pillow - Turn, stuff and close pillow
steps.content: Turn out and push out the corners of the pillow well before filling. Use small pieces of fibre filling bit by bit to achieve a smooth pillow, pushing it well into the corners and filling to a medium squishiness. Turn in the raw edges of the gap and slip-stitch closed.

tip.text: Adding fibre filling gradually makes it easier to achieve a smooth shape without lumps or hollow spots.

steps.title: Drawtring Backpack - Make quilt block and topstitch panel
steps.content: Make a quilt block as detailed for the pillow but this time, use coloured cotton for pieces one and six. Trim the bottom of the block with a 0.98 inches x 12.60 inches strip the same colour. Place face down onto plain cotton, 9.45 inches x 12.60 inches, and sew all the way round, leaving a small gap in the bottom edge. Turn out through the gap and press the raw edges to the inside. Topstitch across the top of the panel only.

tip.text: Press the seam allowance inside before topstitching so the panel edges remain crisp and professional.

steps.title: Drawtring Backpack - Cut main pieces, attach panel and sew sides
steps.content: Cut two pieces of heavyweight cotton, 17.32 inches square, and bind the edges with a wide zig zag to prevent excessive fraying. Pin the panel to the centre of one piece, 2.17 inches up from the bottom edge and topstitch around the three sides to make a large pocket. Place the square panel right sides together and sew down the sides and across the bottom, leaving 3.94 inches unstitched at the top of each side. Turn out.

tip.text: Measure the placement carefully before topstitching because an uneven pocket is difficult to correct later.

steps.title: Drawtring Backpack - Create top channel and fit eyelets
steps.content: Fold under the seam allowance of the unstitched areas at the top of the bag and topstitch, sewing across the top of each side seam as you do so to strengthen it. Fold in 0.2 inches, then 1.18 inches along the top edges of the bag. Sew close to the edge of the hem to make a wide channel. Stitch diagonally across the bottom corners of the bag and fit a 0.47 inches eyelet into the centre of each resulting triangle, passing through both layers of the corners.

tip.text: The channel openings and eyelet corners take the most strain, so secure stitching is essential for durability.

steps.title: Drawtring Backpack - Thread cords and tie reef knot
steps.content: Take two 51.18 inches lengths of cotton cord and bind the ends with tape to make aglets. Pass one cord through the top of the bag channels from the left front, around the gap and return to the left back. Pass the other cord from the right front, around through the gap and return to the right back. Thread the ends of the cords through the corresponding eyelets at the bottom of the back and tie in a neat reef knot.

tip.text: Wrapping the cord ends with tape before threading makes them easier to guide through the channels and eyelets.

items.name: Winged Backpack & Coin Purse Set
items.finishedSize:
Backpack: 17.32 inches x 17.32 inches
Purse: Approximately 4.72 inches x 3.15 inches excluding frame
items.techniques:
Patchwork
Pocket Construction
Topstitching
Zip Installation
Quilting
Bag Construction
Strap Making
Stay Stitching
Gusset Construction
Snap Fastener Installation
Purse Frame Installation
Fabric Gluing
Hand Sewing
Slip Stitching
Turning and Pressing
items.materialNeeded:
Plain cotton: black, yellow, pale fabric
Patterned cotton: assorted prints (alternating black and yellow)
Lining fabric: plain cotton
Lightweight quilt wadding
Zip
Snap fasteners (plier fix type)
Purse frame
Fabric glue
Polyester thread (matching colours)

steps.title: Back Pack - Cut and layer pocket rectangles
steps.content: Download the templates, then print and cut out. Cut two rectangles of patterned and plain cotton to make the front and back of the backpack. Using the template, trim the top corners into a gentle curve. Cut 11.02 inches wide rectangles of other prints, alternating between black and yellow and making the largest 17.32 inches long, then 13.39 inches, 9.45 inches and 5.51 inches. Fold each of these rectangles in half, right sides out, and topstitch along the fold.

tip.text: Press each folded rectangle thoroughly before topstitching to create neat pocket openings.

steps.title: Back Pack - Topstitch pocket sections
steps.content: Layer the rectangles together, matching up the bottom edges starting with the second smallest and second largest. Topstitch down the centre to make two pocket sections. Layer the smallest rectangle over these and topstitch two vertical lines through the top two layers only to make three pocket sections. Layer the pieces onto the largest rectangle and the front panel of the bag. Zig zag stitch around the sides and bottom edge to hold them in place.

tip.text: Baste the layered rectangles before topstitching to prevent the pocket sections from becoming uneven.

steps.title: Back Pack - Make and attach wings
steps.content: Trace two wing templates onto right sides together pale fabric and layer onto lightweight quilt wadding. Sew along the traced line, leaving the straight edge unstitched, before cutting out the wings with a 0.20 inches seam allowance and clipping the angle. Turn the wings out and press well. Place a wing on either side of the pocketed front panel, matching up the raw edges, and stay stitch in place.

tip.text: Trim excess fabric at the wing points without cutting through the stitching to achieve sharp corners when turned.

steps.title: Back Pack - Sew zip gusset
steps.content: Cut two pieces of patterned and lining material, 1.97 inches x 19.69 inches. Place one plain and one patterned gusset piece right sides together and sandwich one edge of the zip between these, teeth facing the printed fabric and matching the edges. Stitch down the edge 0.20 inches from the teeth, turn the fabrics out so they lay next to the zip and topstitch 0.28 inches from the teeth. Repeat this action with the other two pieces of fabric on the other edge of the zip. Bind the raw edges of the gusset together with a zig zag stitch.

tip.text: Stitching too close to the teeth can prevent the zip from opening and closing smoothly.

steps.title: Back Pack - Attach zip gusset ends
steps.content: Snip two pieces of both patterned and plain fabric, 3.94 inches x 8.27 inches. Place one patterned and one plain piece right sides together and sandwich one end of the zip gusset between the two, matching up the raw edges. Sew across the short edge, taking care to not stitch through any metal parts. Turn the fabrics out and topstitch the seam before matching up the remaining raw edges and binding together with a zig zag stitch. Repeat for the other end of the zip gusset.

tip.text: Hitting metal parts with the needle may break the needle or damage the machine.

steps.title: Back Pack - Make shoulder straps
steps.content: Cut two strips of fabric, 3.15 inches x 17.72 inches and two strips of wadding, 1.18 inches x 17.72 inches. Fold each strip of fabric in half lengthways right sides together and stitch down the long seam. Turn out and press so the seam lays in the centre. Use a safety pin to thread the wadding strips into each tube, keeping it flat. Topstitch down both long sides of each strap to hold the wadding in place. Stay-stitch each strap to the top and bottom of the patterned bag back, matching raw edges.

tip.text: Feed the wadding through the straps gradually to avoid twisting or bunching inside the tubes.

steps.title: Back Pack - Sew gusset to bag front and lining
steps.content: Lay the pocketed bag front face up and pin one edge of the face down zip gusset around the top and sides, starting at the centre of each piece and working out in both directions. When happy with the fit, lay the bag lining piece over the top of the gusset and re-pin. Sew around the top and sides before turning out and check the seam encloses all the raw edges. Pin and stitch the back of the bag to the other edge of the gusset in the same way, unzipping the bag first to make it easier and taking care when stitching the seam to avoid other parts of the bag getting caught up.

tip.text: Unzip the bag before sewing the final gusset seam so the backpack can be turned right side out later.

steps.title: Back Pack - Sew bottom gusset and lining
steps.content: Cut a rectangle of patterned and a rectangle of plain fabric, 4.33 inches x 11.02 inches. Fold in 0.20 inches on one long edge of the lining piece and lightly glue or tack. Turn the unzipped backpack inside out, sandwich the bottom front of the bag between the unhemmed long sides of the two rectangles, right sides together and matching the raw edges. Stitch along this seam leaving 0.39 inches unsewn at each end. Manipulate the fabric to sandwich one side of the bag between one short edge of the fabric pieces and sew.

tip.text: Match the corners carefully before stitching to keep the backpack base symmetrical.

steps.title: Back Pack - Finish bottom and add snap fasteners
steps.content: Repeat for the remaining short side. Flip the lining fabric so the seams are enclosed. Sew the remaining long bottom edge of the front of the bag to the patterned fabric only before pinning and oversewing the hemmed edge of the lining over this raw seam. Turn the backpack out. Fit plier fix snap fasteners to the centres of the larger pockets if desired.

tip.text: Test snap placement before installing to ensure the pockets close securely and evenly.

steps.title: Purse - Make striped panel and strap
steps.content: Cut three strips of yellow and three strips of black fabric, 1.18 inches x 4.72 inches. Sew together to make a striped panel and press. Layer onto quilt wadding and topstitch along the seams. Using the template, snip one shape from the striped panel, one from a patterned fabric and two from lining material, marking the hinge position. Cut a strip of fabric, 1.18 inches x 3.15 inches. Fold in 0.20 inches on both long edges, then fold in half lengthways and topstitch to make a strap. Fold the strap in half and tack to one side of the striped front body, matching the raw edges so the loop sits on the bee.

tip.text: Arrange the yellow and black strips carefully before sewing to keep the bee-inspired stripes consistent.

steps.title: Purse - Sew flaps and lining
steps.content: Place the patterned and striped fabric and linings right sides together and sew around the top section from hinge position to hinge position. Turn out the resulting flaps and press. Match up the flaps right sides together and sew the patterned and striped pieces together around the curve of the purse from the ends of the previous seams, securing the strap as you do so. Fold the lining pieces up, tuck the flaps out of the way inside the purse, and sew the linings together, leaving a 1.57 inches gap in the bottom edge. Fold in and glue the edges of the gap before turning the purse right sides out. Slip-stitch the gap in the lining closed before tucking it inside the purse and pressing well.

tip.text: Leaving the lining gap open is essential for turning the purse right side out after assembly.

steps.title: Purse - Attach purse frame with glue
steps.content: Check the fit of the flaps inside the channels of the purse frame. Apply fabric glue into the channel on one side of the frame and tuck the edges of one flap into it, using a knitting needle if necessary and adjusting before the glue sets. Once this side is completely dry, fit the other flap in the same way.

tip.text: Moving the purse frame before the glue has dried can weaken the bond and affect alignment.

steps.title: Purse - Make and attach wings
steps.content: Trace out wing template onto right sides together pale fabric and layer it onto lightweight quilt wadding. Sew along the drawn line leaving a 1.18 inches gap in the lower edge. Cut out the wings leaving a 0.20 inches seam allowance and turn out. Fold in the raw edges of the gap and slip-stitch closed before pressing well. Fix the wings to the centre of the striped body with a few hand stitches.

tip.text: Press the wings thoroughly after turning to highlight the curves and create a cleaner finished appearance.

items.name: Lavender Sunburst Clutch
items.techniques:
Appliqué
Hand Sewing
Machine Sewing
Zip Installation
Quilting
Layering
Tacking
Boxed Corners
Tassel Making
Binding Seams
items.materialNeeded:
Plain cotton, 9.84 inches x 15.75 inches
Patterned fabric, 9.84 inches x 15.75 inches
Contrast cotton, 15.75 inches x 17.72 inches
Patterned fabric scraps
Quilt wadding, 9.84 inches x 15.75 inches
Embroidery thread
Zip, 9.84 inches
Seam allowance: 0.24 inches throughout unless stated otherwise

steps.title: Make a Card - Sew petals together
steps.content: Download the pattern. Using the petal template, cut nine shapes from patterned fabric. Fold each one in half lengthways with the printed side innermost, and stitch across the top diagonal edge. Turn right side out, ease out the point and press. Lay out the petals in the correct colour order. Pin the first two together along the long edges, with right sides together.

tip.text: Well-pressed petals create cleaner points and make it easier to align the colour sequence accurately.

steps.title: Make a Card - Applique lower centre piece
steps.content: Sew from the bottom to top, leaving the top 0.24 inches unstitched, and securing the seam with a few back stitches. Press the seam open and add the other petals in the same way. Pin the lower centre template to orange fabric and cut out 0.24 inches from the edge. Fold this margin over, along the curved edge and tack down over the paper. Press, then remove the template. Pin centrally over the raw edges at the centre of the applique.

tip.text: Leaving the top 0.24 inches unstitched is important because sewing all the way to the tip can create bulk in the centre of the appliqué.

steps.title: Make a Card - Assemble bag layers and hand stitch applique
steps.content: Using the bag template, cut a front, back, two linings and two pieces of wadding. Pin and tack the applique centrally to the bag front, lining up the bottom edges. Sandwich the bag front, a piece of wadding and a lining piece together. Tack round the outside edge. Hand stitch down the outline of the applique, sewing through all the layers. Layer a back, wadding and lining for the other side, and tack as before.

tip.text: Secure all layers thoroughly before hand stitching the appliqué or the wadding and lining may shift out of alignment.

steps.title: Stitch a keyring - Sew zip and assemble bag body
steps.content: Open out a zip and with right sides facing, sew one side of the tape to the front and the other to the back, between the points marked A. With right sides together, pin the front and back together along the side and bottom edges. Machine stitch, then refold the bottom corners so that points B align and stitch across the seam. Bind the seams with tape and turn through.

tip.text: Verify the zip opens in the correct direction before stitching both sides of the bag, as correcting it later requires seam removal.

steps.title: Stitch a keyring - Make tassel for zip pull
steps.content: Make a small tassel for the zip pull by sewing a loop of different embroidery threads through the hole and binding them together at the top. Trim the ends to finish.

tip.text: Combining several embroidery thread strands creates a fuller tassel that better complements the decorative peacock design.

items.name: Whimsical Fish Drawstring Bags
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fat quarters
White lining fabric
Medium-weight quilt wadding
Buttons
Matching sewing thread
items.techniques:
Machine Sewing
Topstitching
Bag Construction
Lining Construction
Turning and Pressing
Channel Construction
Drawstring Making
Slip Stitching
Appliqué Attachment

steps.title: Fish Bag - Cut and Sew the Fins and Tail
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut two body pieces and two top pieces from coordinating cotton fabrics. Cut two fin pieces and one tail piece from fabric folded right sides together. Layer the fin and tail pieces with quilt wadding and sew around the curved edges, leaving the straight edges open. Clip the curves, turn right side out and press flat.

tip.text: Clip seam allowances carefully around curves without cutting into the stitching to help the fins and tail turn smoothly.

steps.title: Fish Bag - Attach Fins and Prepare Body Panels
steps.content: Position a fin at the top centre of each body piece and baste in place. Sew a top section to each body piece with right sides together along the straight edge. Press the seam open and topstitch close to the seam line. Cut two lining pieces to match the completed body panels.

tip.text: Topstitching after pressing helps keep the seam flat and adds structure to the top of the bag.

steps.title: Fish Bag - Assemble Outer Bag and Lining
steps.content: Join each lining piece to the top edge of a body panel, sewing across the top and partially down each side. Clip the corners and turn the top flaps right side out. Baste the tail to the bottom of one body panel. Place the body panels right sides together, align all seams and sew around the perimeter, leaving a turning gap in the lining. Turn the bag right side out through the opening.

tip.text: Misaligned flap seams can cause uneven drawstring channels, so match the seams carefully before stitching.

steps.title: Fish Bag - Create the Drawstring Channels
steps.content: Close the turning gap with slip stitches and push the lining into the bag. Fold the top flaps down by half their depth and topstitch to form two drawstring channels around the bag opening.

tip.text: Press the flap folds before topstitching to create straighter and more consistent channels.

steps.title: Fish Bag - Sew Fabric Drawstrings
steps.content: Cut two fabric strips measuring 1.18 x 11.81 inches. Fold each strip lengthwise and stitch from the centre opening toward each end, tapering the stitching near the tips. Turn the tubes right side out through the centre opening.

tip.text: Leave the turning opening accessible in the middle of the tube or the drawstring cannot be turned right side out.

steps.title: Fish Bag - Insert Drawstrings and Add Eyes
steps.content: Slip-stitch the drawstring openings closed and press the tubes. Thread one drawstring through each channel and tie the ends together with neat reef knots. Sew buttons onto both sides of the fish to create the eyes and complete the project.

tip.text: Check both drawstrings gather evenly before tying the knots permanently.

items.name: Toy Storage Caddy Basket
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabric
Coordinating plain cotton fabric
Medium-weight quilt wadding
Stiff card insert
Buttons
Matching sewing thread
items.techniques:
Machine Sewing
Topstitching
Bag Construction
Lining Construction
Quilting
Box Construction
Slip Stitching
Turning and Pressing
Edge Finishing

steps.title: Project Bag - Prepare Handle and Side Panels
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut the basket panels from assorted printed cotton fabrics. Join the handle tops using the extension pieces and topstitch the seams to strengthen them. Attach the front side panels to each side of the handle panels to create an H-shaped basket body. Press the completed pieces onto quilt wadding, trim the wadding to match and secure around the edges with zigzag stitching.

tip.text: Topstitching the handle extension seams helps reinforce areas that will receive the most stress during use.

steps.title: Project Bag - Add Lining and Form Basket Sides
steps.content: Place each wadded basket piece onto coordinating lining fabric with right sides together and trim the lining to match. Sew around the curved handle sections and the top edges of the side panels. Turn the piece right side out by passing one side through the handle opening. Match the basket ends right sides together and sew both the outer fabric and lining sections. Fold the lining inside and topstitch around the basket opening before turning the basket inside out with the handle enclosed between the layers.

tip.text: Twisting the lining while turning can cause uneven basket sides and make the top edge difficult to align.

steps.title: Project Bag - Construct the Base Panel
steps.content: Cut the base panel from patterned fabric and secure it to quilt wadding. Match the centre of the long base edges with the front and back basket panels and sew them together, leaving a small gap at each end. Attach the short ends of the base panel to the basket sides to complete the rectangular base shape.

tip.text: Accurate centre matching is important because misaligned base seams can distort the finished basket shape.

steps.title: Project Bag - Attach Lining Base
steps.content: Cut a matching lining base panel and sew it to the basket lining in the same way as the outer base, leaving one short edge open for turning. Turn the basket right side out through the opening so the lining sits on the outside.

tip.text: Forgetting the turning opening will make it impossible to insert the base stiffener later.

steps.title: Project Bag - Insert Stiffener and Close Opening
steps.content: Cut a piece of stiff card measuring 7.48 x 15.35 inches to fit the basket base. Slide the card through the turning opening and position it flat inside the base. Fold the raw edges inward and slip stitch the opening closed.

tip.text: Make sure the card lies completely flat before closing the opening to avoid a warped basket base.

steps.title: Project Bag - Shape and Finish the Basket
steps.content: Turn the basket right side out, carefully guiding the handle over the card insert. Shape the corners by pressing the sides into position. Sew a coordinating button at the top of each corner to help maintain the basket structure and complete the project.

tip.text: Pressing the corners before attaching the buttons helps create crisp edges and a more structured finish.

items.name: Vintage Bow Frame Clutch
items.finishedSize:
Small Bag: 7.87 x 9.45 inches
Large Bag: 10.63 x 12.99 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton satin or chambray fabric
Coordinating lining fabric
Lightweight quilt wadding
Curved purse frame (3.54 x 5.12 inches)
Fabric glue
Matching sewing thread
items.techniques:
Machine Sewing
Curve Sewing
Bag Construction
Lining Construction
Turning and Pressing
Frame Purse Installation
Hand Stitching
Slip Stitching
Fabric Bow Making
Wadding Stabilization

steps.title: Clip Bag - Cut and Prepare All Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut all bag pieces according to the cutting guide, adding a 0.39 inches seam allowance around each piece. Transfer all template markings onto the fabric before construction begins.

tip.text: Transfer hinge placement and alignment marks before removing the paper template to improve assembly accuracy later.

steps.title: Clip Bag - Layer Wadding and Position the Bow Base
steps.content: Place each outer fabric piece onto lightweight quilt wadding and trim the wadding to match. If making the decorative bow, fold the longest rectangle lengthwise with right sides together and stitch along the long edge. Turn right side out, flatten with the seam centered and press lightly. Position the bow strip across the centre of one outer bag panel.

tip.text: Uneven wadding trimming can create bulky edges that are difficult to fit neatly into the purse frame.

steps.title: Clip Bag - Join Outer Fabric and Lining
steps.content: Place a lining piece and outer piece right sides together. Stitch around the upper curved edge between the marked points and clip the curve. Repeat for the remaining outer and lining pieces.

tip.text: Clip the curved seam allowance carefully without cutting into the stitching or the seam may weaken during turning.

steps.title: Clip Bag - Sew Bag Body and Lining
steps.content: Fold the lining upward and place the outer bag pieces right sides together. Stitch around the lower curved edges between the marked points and clip the curves. Fold the bag tops down and sew the lining pieces together in the same manner, leaving a turning opening along the lining base.

tip.text: Forgetting the turning gap in the lining means the bag cannot be turned right side out after assembly.

steps.title: Clip Bag - Turn and Install the Purse Frame
steps.content: Turn the bag right side out through the lining opening and check the fit of the bag top inside the purse frame channels. Close the turning gap. Apply fabric glue generously inside one frame channel and insert the top edge of the bag. Allow it to dry completely before repeating the process on the second side.

tip.text: Allow the first side of the frame to dry fully before fitting the second side to prevent shifting and misalignment.

steps.title: Clip Bag - Create the Decorative Bow
steps.content: Fold the medium rectangle in half and stitch as before. Turn right side out, flatten and press. Join the short ends to form a ring and position the seam at the back. Gather the centre using running stitches to create the bow shape.

tip.text: Hand gather the centre gradually to create balanced loops on both sides of the bow.

steps.title: Clip Bag - Finish and Attach the Bow
steps.content: Fold the smallest rectangle in half and stitch as before. Turn right side out and fold the raw edges inward. Wrap the strip around the centre of the bow and secure it at the back. Stitch the completed bow onto the centre of the bag to finish the project.

tip.text: Position the bow only after the purse frame is attached so it remains centered on the finished bag.

items.name: Sweetheart Pocket Shoulder Bag
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabric
Lightweight quilt wadding
Mini pom pom trim
Small jump rings
Bag charm clip
Matching sewing thread
Ribbon scraps
items.techniques:
Machine Sewing
Hand Sewing
Ladder Stitch
Bag Construction
Lining Construction
Curve Sewing
Turning and Pressing
Strap Making
Trim Application
Charm Attachment

steps.title: Heart Bag - Make the Pocket Panel
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut two small heart pieces for the pocket. Attach lightweight wadding to the wrong side of one heart and trim the excess. Baste mini pom pom trim around the perimeter with the pom poms facing inward. Place the second heart on top with right sides together and stitch around the edge, leaving a 1.97 inches turning gap along a straight section. Clip the inner point at the top of the heart, turn right side out and close the opening with ladder stitch.

tip.text: Clip only into the seam allowance at the heart point. Cutting through the stitching can weaken the seam and create holes.

steps.title: Heart Bag - Attach Pocket and Prepare Bag Panels
steps.content: Cut four large heart shapes and attach wadding to two of them to create the outer bag pieces. Position the completed pocket in the centre of one outer heart and secure it using ladder stitch around the sides and bottom, leaving the curved upper edge open. Pair each outer heart with a lining heart, right sides together. Mark the template points and stitch only along the upper curved sections between the marked points. Repeat for the remaining set.

tip.text: Accidentally stitching across the top of the pocket will prevent it from functioning as a usable pocket.

steps.title: Heart Bag - Assemble Outer Bag and Lining
steps.content: Turn both heart sections right side out and smooth the curved seams. Place the outer heart sections together with the wadded sides facing each other and stitch around the outer raw edges only. Keep the lining pieces folded away from the stitching. Turn the project so the lining pieces face each other with right sides together. Stitch around the lining edges, leaving a 2.36 inches opening for turning. Turn the entire bag right side out through the gap and shape the curves.

tip.text: Make sure the lining stays clear of the outer seam when sewing or unwanted layers may get caught in the stitching.

steps.title: Heart Bag - Make the Strap and Finish the Bag
steps.content: Close the lining opening and tuck the lining inside the bag. Cut a fabric strip measuring 1.57 x 15.75 inches for the strap. Fold it lengthwise, stitch along the long edge and leave a turning gap approximately 1.97 inches from one end. Move the seam to the centre, stitch both short ends closed and turn the strap right side out. Press and close the gap with ladder stitch. Sew a jump ring to each side of the bag and attach the strap ends. Create a decorative charm using ribbon scraps and pom pom trim, then attach it to a charm clip and secure it to the bag.

tip.text: Press the strap after turning it right side out to create a smoother finish and help it sit neatly on the shoulder.

items.name: Elegant Top Handle Handbag
items.materialNeeded:
Wool tweed fabric
Cotton lining fabric
Wadding
Upholstery-weight leather
Thick cord
Magnetic bag clasp
Snap fasteners
Matching sewing thread
items.techniques:
Bag Construction
Lining Construction
Boxed Corners
Topstitching
Leather Sewing
Hand Sewing
Magnetic Clasp Installation
Snap Fastener Installation
Turning and Pressing
Handle Construction

steps.title: Kelly Handbag - Prepare Outer Panels and Flap Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the template. Cut two outer bag pieces from tweed and attach them to wadding using zigzag stitching around the edges before trimming the excess. Cut two lining pieces from cotton lining fabric. Cut two flap pieces from tweed and one matching flap piece from wadding.

tip.text: Secure the wadding evenly around the edges before trimming to prevent shifting during bag assembly.

steps.title: Kelly Handbag - Construct the Flap and Install Clasp
steps.content: Place the tweed flap pieces right sides together with the wadding underneath. Stitch around the curved edge using a narrow seam allowance. Clip the curve, turn right side out and press. Topstitch around the curved edge and mark the clasp position approximately 1.18 inches above the curve. Install one half of the magnetic clasp with the backing hidden between the fabric layers.

tip.text: Clip the curved seam allowance carefully without cutting into the stitching to maintain a smooth flap edge.

steps.title: Kelly Handbag - Assemble the Outer Bag
steps.content: Baste the raw edge of the flap to the top of one outer bag panel with right sides together. Place the second outer panel on top and stitch the sides and bottom edge, leaving the corner notches open. Fold the corners so the side and bottom seams align, then stitch across the notches to create a flat base. Turn the bag right side out and install the second half of the magnetic clasp on the front panel.

tip.text: Misaligned corner seams can create an uneven base, causing the bag to lean when standing upright.

steps.title: Kelly Handbag - Make the Leather Handles
steps.content: Cut two leather strips measuring 1.97 x 17.72 inches. Round the ends and fold each strip around a length of thick cord. Secure with clips and stitch along the edges, leaving approximately 1.18 inches unsewn at both ends. Trim the cord, flatten the ends and create stitching holes around the circumference for hand sewing.

tip.text: Use clips instead of pins when working with leather because pin holes remain permanently visible.

steps.title: Kelly Handbag - Attach Handles and Sew the Lining
steps.content: Hand stitch the handles onto both sides of the bag. Construct the lining using the same method as the outer bag, but leave a 3.94 inches opening along the bottom edge for turning. With right sides together, place the lining over the outer bag and align the top edges, enclosing the handles and flap between the layers. Stitch around the top edge.

tip.text: Check that both handles are positioned at the same height before stitching, as uneven handles are difficult to correct later.

steps.title: Kelly Handbag - Turn and Finish the Bag
steps.content: Turn the handbag right side out through the opening in the lining. Fold the raw edges inward and stitch the opening closed. Push the lining inside the bag, press the upper edge and topstitch around the opening for a clean finish.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when pressing tweed to avoid flattening the fabric texture or causing shine marks.

steps.title: Kelly Handbag - Add Side Fasteners and Decorative Strap
steps.content: Hand sew snap fasteners near the upper corners of the bag so the sides can be fastened inward or released for additional capacity. Cut a narrow leather strip, add decorative charms and loop it around one handle to complete the handbag.

tip.text: Test the side snap positions before sewing permanently to ensure both sides fold evenly.

items.name: City Curve Sling Bag
items.materialNeeded:
Outer fabric fat quarters (2)
Lining fabric fat quarters (2)
Zip (17.32 inches)
Flanged piping (39.37 inches)
Webbing strap
Swivel clip fasteners (2)
D-rings (2)
Tri-glide bar adjuster
Matching sewing thread
items.techniques:
Zip Installation
Piping Insertion
Bag Construction
Lining Construction
Topstitching
Pocket Construction
Strap Construction
Hardware Installation
Hand Sewing
Turning and Pressing

steps.title: Crossbody Bag - Prepare Pattern Pieces and Interior Pocket
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut the top, front, back and zip cover pieces from the outer fabric. Cut the pocket, top, front and back pieces from the lining fabric. Press a 0.39 inches hem along both sides of the pocket. Position the pocket centrally on the lining back piece and stitch around three sides, leaving the top edge open. Reinforce the upper corners with extra stitching.

tip.text: Backstitch the pocket opening corners several times because these areas receive the most stress during daily use.

steps.title: Crossbody Bag - Add Fabric Covers to the Zip Ends
steps.content: Trim the zip to 17.32 inches. Press a 0.39 inches hem on one short edge of each zip cover. Place each cover right sides together with a zip end and hand stitch along the fold line. Fold the cover over the zip end and secure the raw edges with a few stitches. Repeat for the opposite end.

tip.text: Check that the zip slider remains accessible before stitching both zip covers permanently.
