items.techniques: Fabric applique, Fusible applique, Machine stitching, Topstitching, Zip insertion, Button sewing, Decorative embellishment, Hand tacking, Fabric painting, Zigzag stitching

steps.title: Cut the Cushion Panels
steps.content: Print and cut the templates. Cut a back panel measuring 43.1.97 inches x 42.36 inches from blue cotton and a center panel measuring 25.1.97 inches x 42.36 inches. Prepare all remaining fabric pieces according to the template markings.

tip.text: Press all fabrics before cutting to ensure accurate measurements and clean panel assembly.

steps.title: Create the Cat Appliques
steps.content: Apply fusible webbing to the wrong side of the butterfly and meadow print fabrics. Transfer the cat templates, cut out the shapes and fuse them onto the center panel. Machine stitch neatly around all applique edges to secure them.

tip.text: Leaving gaps between stitches around applique edges may cause the fabric to lift after repeated use.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zip
steps.content: Cut the upper and lower cushion sections. Join the zip between the center panel and adjoining panel using a 1.1.97 inches seam allowance. Press the seams flat and stitch as close to the zipper teeth as possible. Secure the second side of the zip and test its operation.

tip.text: Always test the zip before closing the cushion seams to avoid having to unpick completed seams later.

steps.title: Create the Sentiment Panel
steps.content: Use a sponge and fabric paint to apply your chosen wording with mini alphabet stamps onto cross-stitch print fabric. Heat-set the paint according to the manufacturer's instructions. Layer the printed fabric onto decorative felt rosettes and stitch it to the center panel.

tip.text: Practice the stamped wording on scrap fabric first to achieve even paint coverage and spacing.

steps.title: Make Felt Rosettes and Decorative Details
steps.content: Cut cream and blue felt circles and layer them to create rosettes. Sew buttons through the center of each rosette. Arrange additional buttons around the cat motifs and stitch them securely. Add white cotton lace trim to the upper and lower edges of the center panel.

tip.text: Ensure all buttons are sewn securely, especially if the cushion may be displayed where children can access it.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the front and back panels right sides together. Pin and machine stitch around the perimeter using a 1.1.97 inches seam allowance. Trim seam allowances where necessary and turn the cover right side out through the zip opening.

tip.text: Clip the corners diagonally before turning to achieve crisp, professional-looking corners.

steps.title: Insert the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Insert the cushion pad through the concealed zip opening. Smooth the corners and check that all embellishments are securely attached. Close the zip to complete the project.

tip.text: Overstuffing the cushion can distort the applique design and make the cover difficult to zip closed.

items.name: Floral Stitch Pillows
items.finishedSize: Rectangular cushion: 11.8 x 19.7 inches; Square cushion: 11.8 x 11.8 inches

Shell buttons, 3.
Tissue paper.

items.techniques: Free-motion embroidery, Machine sewing, Hand tacking, Seam pressing, Slip stitch, Decorative trim application, Button sewing, Turning and stuffing cushion covers

steps.title: Prepare the Front Panel
steps.content: Cut the green floral fabric, beige square print fabric, and cream felt according to the template measurements. Stitch the floral and beige fabrics together with right sides facing and press the seam open. Attach the cream felt section and press flat.

tip.text: Press every seam as you sew to keep the finished cushion front smooth and professional.

steps.title: Transfer the Embroidery Design
steps.content: Print the embroidery template and trace the floral design onto tissue paper. Position the tissue over the cream felt section and secure it with large hand tacking stitches. Place tearaway stabiliser behind the fabric.

tip.text: Skipping the stabiliser can cause puckering and uneven embroidery stitches.

steps.title: Stitch the Floral Embroidery
steps.content: Using free-motion machine embroidery, stitch over the traced lines twice. Add darker thread shades where desired to create depth and shadow. Carefully remove the tissue paper and tearaway stabiliser once the embroidery is complete.

tip.text: Remove tissue paper slowly to avoid pulling or damaging the embroidery stitches.

steps.title: Press the Embroidered Panel
steps.content: Press the embroidered section from the reverse side of the fabric to protect the stitched design and flatten the panel.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth to prevent flattening the embroidered texture.

steps.title: Create the Cushion Back
steps.content: Cut the backing fabric to match the finished front panel. Place the front and back pieces right sides together and pin securely around the edges.

tip.text: Misaligned edges can make the finished cushion appear uneven.

steps.title: Sew the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Machine stitch around the perimeter using a 3/8 inch seam allowance, leaving an opening for turning and inserting the cushion pad.

tip.text: Do not sew the turning opening closed, or you will not be able to insert the cushion pad.

steps.title: Trim and Turn
steps.content: Trim excess fabric from the corners to reduce bulk. Turn the cushion cover right side out and carefully shape the corners.

tip.text: Use a blunt point turner rather than scissors to avoid piercing the fabric.

steps.title: Insert the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Insert the appropriate cushion pad into the cover and distribute the filling evenly so the cushion keeps its shape.

tip.text: Fluff the cushion pad before inserting it for a fuller finish.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Hand sew the opening closed using an invisible slip stitch to create a neat finish.

tip.text: Large slip stitches may be visible from the outside and weaken the seam.

steps.title: Add Lace and Ribbon Trim
steps.content: For the square cushion variation, stitch lace across the front of the cushion and sew the tape measure ribbon beneath it. Press lightly from the reverse side.

tip.text: Pin trims in place before sewing to ensure they remain straight.

steps.title: Attach Decorative Buttons
steps.content: Hand sew three shell buttons evenly across the lower section of the cushion front to complete the design.

tip.text: Decorative buttons may present a choking hazard if the cushion is intended for young children.

items.name: Tree Applique Pillow
items.materialNeeded:
Mustard cotton fabric.
Plain linen fabric.
Cloud and floral printed fabric.
Various coloured fabric scraps.
Coordinating ribbon, approximately 18 inches.
Assorted buttons.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Fusible interfacing.
Square cushion pad, 17.5 x 17.5 inches.
Tissue paper.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Pins.
Iron and ironing board.
5mm seam allowance used unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Machine Sewing, Topstitching, Decorative Stitching, Envelope Cushion Construction, Pressing, Hand Finishing

steps.title: Cut the Cushion Front and Appliqué Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut a 44.1.97 inches square from plain linen fabric for the cushion front. Using the templates, cut the tree trunk, branches, clouds, leaves and decorative shapes from assorted printed fabrics. Apply fusible interfacing to the back of all appliqué pieces.

tip.text: Apply interfacing before cutting detailed shapes to prevent distortion and fraying.

steps.title: Arrange and Stitch the Appliqué Design
steps.content: Trace the layout onto tissue paper. Position the tree, clouds and decorative pieces on the cushion front, leaving space for the lettering. Pin everything in place and machine stitch around each shape. Press from the reverse side when finished.

tip.text: Do not place appliqué pieces inside the seam allowance area or they may be partially hidden after assembly.

steps.title: Add the Fabric Lettering
steps.content: Trace the lettering guide onto tissue paper. Position the fabric letters on the cushion front and stitch around each letter twice. Carefully remove the tissue paper and press the cushion front from the reverse side.

tip.text: Pull tissue paper away slowly to avoid stretching or breaking decorative stitches.

steps.title: Create Decorative Embellishments
steps.content: Stitch decorative birds and details around the appliqué scene. Add any extra decorative stitching shown in the design. Press the completed front panel flat.

tip.text: Use contrasting thread colours to make decorative stitching stand out.

steps.title: Add the Side Border Strips
steps.content: Cut two mustard cotton strips measuring approximately 2.1.97 inches x 44.1.97 inches. Stitch one strip to each side of the completed front panel using a narrow seam allowance. Trim excess fabric and press the seams open.

tip.text: Uneven seam allowances can cause the cushion front to become slightly skewed.

steps.title: Prepare the Envelope Back Panels
steps.content: Cut the required mustard cotton and floral print fabric pieces for the cushion back. Hem the overlapping edges and attach the coordinating ribbon as shown. Press all folds and seams flat.

tip.text: Press each hem before stitching to achieve cleaner and more accurate edges.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the cushion front right side up. Layer the back panels right side down, overlapping them to create the envelope opening. Align all raw edges, pin securely and stitch around the entire cushion using a 5mm seam allowance.

tip.text: Ensure the envelope overlap faces inward before sewing or the opening will be on the outside.

steps.title: Turn and Finish the Cushion
steps.content: Clip the corners to reduce bulk and turn the cushion cover right side out through the envelope opening. Carefully push out the corners, press the cover flat and insert the cushion pad. Tie the ribbon into a decorative bow if desired.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to shape corners neatly without damaging the fabric.

items.name: Leaf Pillow Set
items.finishedSize: Cushion: 12 x 12 inches; Hottie Cover: 8 x 13 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Cream wool fabric, 1m width.
White poly cotton fabric.
Concealed zip, 7 inches.
Beads and sequins, assorted.
Hand dyes: rosewood red, flamingo pink, woodland brown, terracotta brown.
Salt.
Toy stuffing.
Orange embroidery thread.
Leaf templates.
Fabric glue or pins.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Fabric dyeing, Applique, Machine sewing, Zip insertion, Embellishment, Blanket stitch, Cushion construction

steps.title: Cut and Dye the Cushion Fabrics
steps.content: Cut one 30.39 inches square for the cushion front, one 12.76 inches x 30.39 inches back piece and one 7.87 inches x 30.39 inches back piece from cream wool fabric. Prepare dye baths using Flamingo Pink, Rosewood Red, Woodland Brown and Terracotta Brown dyes. Dye the cushion pieces and several additional wool rectangles that will be used for the leaf appliqués. Allow all fabric pieces to dry completely.

tip.text: Do not sew dyed fabric until it is completely dry, as moisture can distort seams and transfer colour.

steps.title: Create and Attach the Leaf Appliqués
steps.content: Use the downloaded templates to cut nine leaf shapes from the dyed wool rectangles. Arrange the leaves on the cushion front until satisfied with the composition. Pin or lightly glue them in place, then machine stitch around each leaf to secure.

tip.text: Combine several dye shades across the leaves to create a more realistic autumn effect.

steps.title: Add Decorative Embellishments
steps.content: Sew coordinating beads and sequins onto selected leaves to add texture and detail. Distribute embellishments evenly across the design.

tip.text: Overloading one area with beads can make the cushion front bulky and difficult to sew.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zip
steps.content: Join the two cushion back sections with the concealed zip, using a 1.1.97 inches seam allowance. Press the zip area flat after stitching.

tip.text: Pressing before and after zip insertion helps achieve a professional finish.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the cushion front and completed back right sides together. Pin all edges and machine stitch with a 0.39 inches seam allowance. Clip the corners and trim excess bulk before turning.

tip.text: Avoid trimming too close to the stitching line, as this may weaken the seams.

steps.title: Create the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Cut two 30.39 inches squares from white poly cotton fabric. Stitch around the edges, leaving a small opening. Turn right side out, fill with toy stuffing and hand sew the opening closed.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually and distribute it evenly to avoid lumps.

steps.title: Finish the Cushion
steps.content: Turn the cushion cover right side out, insert the cushion pad through the zip opening and adjust the filling evenly inside the cover.

tip.text: Check that all embellishments are securely attached before regular use.

steps.title: Cut and Dye the Hottie Cover Pieces
steps.content: Cut the front and back pieces for the hot water bottle cover from cream wool fabric using the template. Prepare a Terracotta Brown dye bath and colour the cover pieces. Allow them to dry thoroughly.

tip.text: Rinse dyed wool thoroughly to prevent excess dye from transferring during use.

steps.title: Prepare the Leaf Decorations
steps.content: Cut six leaf shapes from leftover dyed wool. Arrange them on the front cover piece and pin or glue them temporarily in place.

tip.text: Take a photo of the arrangement before sewing so you can easily recreate the layout.

steps.title: Stitch the Appliqués
steps.content: Machine stitch around the edges of each leaf using matching or contrasting thread. Ensure all edges are securely attached.

tip.text: Pulling the wool while stitching may distort the shape of the appliqués.

steps.title: Decorate the Back Flaps
steps.content: Finish the edges of the back opening flaps with orange blanket stitch embroidery. Keep stitches evenly spaced for a decorative finish.

tip.text: Use embroidery thread of consistent tension to create uniform blanket stitches.

steps.title: Assemble the Hottie Cover
steps.content: Place the front and back pieces right sides together and stitch around the outer edges according to the template. Turn right side out and press carefully.

tip.text: Do not accidentally stitch the opening closed, or the hot water bottle will not fit inside.

steps.title: Finish the Hottie Cover
steps.content: Insert the hot water bottle into the finished cover and adjust the fabric so it sits smoothly around the bottle.

tip.text: Always ensure the hot water bottle is properly sealed before placing it inside the fabric cover.

items.name: Round Floor Pouf
items.materialNeeded:
Patterned cotton fabrics, eight coordinating prints.
White cotton fabric, 2m.
Polystyrene beans.
Zip, 11.81 inches.
Zip, 23.62 inches.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Iron and ironing board.
Funnel for filling.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Zip Insertion, Straight Stitch Sewing, Topstitching, Seam Matching, Pressing, Soft Furnishing Construction

steps.title: Create the Inner Pouffe Ring
steps.content: Download and print the pattern pieces. Cut eight 22.36 inches x 30.79 inches rectangles from white cotton fabric. Insert a 11.81 inches zip between two rectangles along one long edge. Sew the remaining rectangles together to create a continuous ring.

tip.text: Press every seam open immediately after stitching to reduce bulk and improve the final shape.

steps.title: Construct the Inner Top Panel
steps.content: Cut eight triangular panels from white cotton. Stitch the triangles together in pairs, then join the pairs into semicircles. Sew the semicircles together to form a complete octagonal top panel.

tip.text: Failing to align triangle points accurately can cause the top panel to become uneven.

steps.title: Assemble the Inner Beanbag
steps.content: Pin the top panel to the side ring with right sides together, matching seam lines carefully. Stitch around the edge. Construct the base panel in the same way and attach it to the ring, leaving the zip partially open.

tip.text: Always leave the zipper open before sewing the final seam so the beanbag can be turned right side out.

steps.title: Fill the Inner Beanbag
steps.content: Turn the beanbag right side out. Fill it with polystyrene beans through the zipper opening until comfortably full. Close the zipper securely.

tip.text: Polystyrene beads can scatter easily. Fill the beanbag in a controlled area and avoid overfilling.

steps.title: Create the Patchwork Top Cover
steps.content: Cut triangular sections from the eight patterned fabrics. Mix the prints and stitch sections together into full-sized triangles. Sew them into pairs, then semicircles, and finally into a complete octagonal top panel.

tip.text: Lay out all fabric pieces before sewing to achieve a balanced colour distribution.

steps.title: Make the Patchwork Side Panels
steps.content: Cut rectangular sections from each fabric print. Combine and stitch them together to form eight multi-coloured side panels. Join the panels together into a ring and press all seams flat.

tip.text: Skipping seam pressing can cause the patchwork sections to twist and distort.

steps.title: Install the Base Zip
steps.content: Construct the base from patchwork triangles in the same way as the top panel. Press under 0.39 inches along the straight edges. Attach a 23.62 inches zip between the folded edges and topstitch securely.

tip.text: Test the zip operation before final topstitching to ensure smooth opening and closing.

steps.title: Assemble the Outer Cover
steps.content: Attach the patchwork top panel to the side ring with right sides together. Stitch around the perimeter and press the seams. Repeat for the base panel, ensuring the zipper remains partially open.

tip.text: Mismatched seam intersections can make the finished pouffe appear uneven.

steps.title: Insert the Inner and Finish
steps.content: Turn the cover right side out through the zipper opening. Insert the filled beanbag inner. Adjust the filling so the seams align neatly and zip the cover closed.

tip.text: Massage the filling into the corners and edges for a smooth, professional finish.

items.name: Woodland Fox Pillow
items.materialNeeded:
Tweed fabric, blue, 19.69 inches.
Tweed fabric, beige, 19.69 inches.
Printed cotton fabric, orange floral.
Printed cotton fabric, orange.
Printed cotton fabric, blue floral.
Printed cotton fabric, red spot.
Printed cotton fabric, yellow spot.
Printed cotton fabric, blue spot.
Printed cotton fabric, green print.
Cream lining fabric, 19.69 inches.
Fusible webbing, 19.84 inches.
Zip, 11.81 inches.
Polyester fibre filling or cushion pad.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Carbon paper.
Pins.
Iron and ironing board.
items.techniques: Applique, Fusible Webbing Application, Zip Insertion, Straight Stitch Sewing, Edge Stitching, Pattern Matching, Cushion Construction

steps.title: Create the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Cut two 30.79 inches x 16.54 inches rectangles from cream lining fabric. Place right sides together and machine stitch around the edges, leaving a 3.94 inches opening for turning. Turn right side out, fill with fibre stuffing, and hand sew the opening closed.

tip.text: Distribute the filling evenly into the corners before closing the opening for a smooth cushion shape.

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Cut one 30.79 inches x 16.54 inches front panel from blue tweed fabric and two 12.76 inches x 16.54 inches back panels. Match the tweed pattern carefully so the checks align across the finished cushion cover.

tip.text: Ignoring pattern matching can make the zipper seam highly visible in the finished cushion.

steps.title: Insert the Back Zipper
steps.content: Place the two back panels right sides together and stitch 2.36 inches from each end along the long edge. Press the seam open and insert the 11.81 inches zipper into the opening. Topstitch securely on both sides of the zipper.

tip.text: Press the seam open before installing the zipper to avoid puckering around the zipper area.

steps.title: Cut the Woodland Appliqué Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the woodland animal template. Cut the fox body and face strips from orange floral fabric, the face, body strip and backing pieces from orange print fabric, the head from pink fabric, the eyes, ears and nose from red spot fabric, and the leaf from yellow spot fabric. Apply fusible webbing to the reverse side of all appliqué pieces.

tip.text: Label the appliqué pieces after cutting to keep track of their placement during assembly.

steps.title: Arrange the Appliqué Design
steps.content: Remove the backing paper from the fusible webbing and position the fox and leaf pieces in the center of the tweed front panel. Cover with a pressing cloth and iron to fuse all elements in place.

tip.text: Placing appliqué pieces too close to the seam allowance may cause parts of the design to be caught in the cushion seams.

steps.title: Stitch Around the Appliqué
steps.content: Using coordinating thread, machine stitch around each appliqué shape to secure it permanently. Trim thread ends neatly and pull loose thread tails to the back of the work before knotting.

tip.text: Use a shorter stitch length when sewing around curved appliqué sections for smoother edges.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Open the zipper halfway. Place the front and back cover pieces right sides together and pin around all four sides. Machine stitch using a consistent seam allowance and trim the corners.

tip.text: Always leave the zipper partially open before sewing the cover closed, otherwise turning the cushion right side out will be difficult.

steps.title: Finish the Cushion
steps.content: Turn the cover right side out through the zipper opening and press carefully. Insert the finished cushion pad and close the zipper to complete the woodland cushion.

tip.text: Push the corners out gently with a blunt tool to create crisp, professional-looking edges.

items.name: Elephant Pillow Set
items.finishedSize: Elephant toy and 12.36 inches x 12.36 inches mini cushion
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, Art Gallery Fabrics Littlest range.
Fabric, Floral Frolic Apricot.
Fabric, Heartfelt Snow.
Fabric, Cottontails Sand.
Calico, two 12.36 inches squares.
Felt, peach.
Sewing thread: cream, peach, green.
Embroidery thread: dark red.
Crochet lace trim.
Fusible webbing.
Lightweight fusible interfacing.
Buttons: white, 0.39 inches, four; dark red, 6mm, one; white, 6mm, two.
Toy stuffing.
Fabric glue.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Toy Making, Cushion Sewing, Applique, Embroidery, Stuffing

steps.title: Prepare the Elephant Body Pieces
steps.content: Print and cut the templates. Cut two body pieces and one head gusset from patterned fabric. Cut two saddle pieces (one reversed) from fusible webbing, fuse them onto spotty fabric, and cut out. Remove the backing paper and fuse the saddles onto both elephant body pieces. Zigzag stitch around the saddles, leaving the top edge open. Cut two pieces of lace the same width as the saddles and glue them along the lower edge.

tip.text: Check that both saddles are positioned symmetrically before fusing permanently.

steps.title: Create the Face and Attach the Gusset
steps.content: Mark the eye positions using a fabric pen. Using two strands of dark red embroidery thread, work French knots for the eyes. Pin and tack the head gusset to one body piece with right sides together, ensuring the template orientation is correct, then machine stitch.

tip.text: Incorrect gusset orientation can distort the elephant's head shape.

steps.title: Assemble the Elephant Body
steps.content: Pin the second body piece to the first with right sides together, matching the saddles on both sides. Stitch around the entire elephant shape, leaving an opening under the tummy for turning. Clip curved seams, turn right side out, and press.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances carefully to avoid bulky edges after turning.

steps.title: Make and Attach the Tail
steps.content: Cut a tail from peach felt and snip several times along the lower edge to create a tassel effect. Sew a small dark red button to the top of the tail. Attach the tail to the elephant using cream thread and secure it through the button.

tip.text: Use small stitches hidden behind the button for a cleaner finish.

steps.title: Construct the Legs
steps.content: Cut eight leg pieces from patterned fabric and eight foot pieces from spotty fabric. Stitch each leg to a foot section, then sew leg pairs together with right sides facing, leaving the top edges open.

tip.text: Ensure each foot is aligned correctly before stitching to avoid mismatched legs.

steps.title: Stuff and Attach the Legs
steps.content: Turn the legs right side out and stuff firmly. Close the openings. Position the legs using the template and attach them with green thread sewn through white buttons so the legs can move freely.

tip.text: Keep the stuffing firm but not overpacked to allow smooth leg movement.

steps.title: Stuff the Elephant
steps.content: Fill the elephant body with toy stuffing, making sure the trunk is firmly stuffed. Continue adding stuffing until the toy is plump and evenly shaped. Hand stitch the tummy opening closed.

tip.text: Uneven stuffing can cause the elephant to lean or lose its shape.

steps.title: Make the Ears
steps.content: Cut two outer ears from peach felt. Trace and cut two inner ears (one reversed) from fusible webbing and fuse them onto script-print fabric. Cut out and remove the backing paper. Fuse the inner ears onto the felt ears and zigzag stitch around the edges.

tip.text: Use a narrow zigzag stitch for a neat applique finish around the ear shapes.

steps.title: Attach the Ears and Finish the Toy
steps.content: Fold each ear slightly to create dimension and hand stitch in place. Position the ears on the elephant according to the template and sew securely. Bend the ears forward and stitch along the back edge to complete the toy.

tip.text: Attaching the ears too low can alter the overall appearance of the elephant.

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Front
steps.content: Cut a 4.72 inches x 12.76 inches rectangle of script fabric and a matching piece of lightweight interfacing. Fuse the interfacing to the wrong side. Cut two 3.1.97 inches x 12.76 inches strips of spotty fabric and stitch them along the top and bottom edges of the script fabric. Press the seams open.

tip.text: Press after every seam to achieve a professional-looking cushion front.

steps.title: Create the Elephant Applique
steps.content: Using the templates, trace and cut the elephant, saddle, inner ear, two legs, and two feet from fusible webbing. Fuse the pieces onto the appropriate fabrics and cut them out. Arrange the applique centrally on the cushion front and fuse into place.

tip.text: Check spacing carefully before fusing, as repositioning may be difficult afterwards.

steps.title: Decorate the Cushion Front
steps.content: Set the sewing machine to a wide zigzag stitch and sew around the elephant and saddle. Add lace beneath the saddle. Position the legs and feet slightly overlapping and stitch them in place. Sew a small white button at the top of each leg.

tip.text: Matching thread colors help the applique blend smoothly into the design.

steps.title: Add the Ear and Tail Details
steps.content: Cut an ear and tail from peach felt. Fuse the inner ear onto the outer ear and zigzag stitch around the edge. Mark the eye position and work a French knot using doubled dark red thread. Glue the ear and tail in place temporarily, then sew around them with peach thread.

tip.text: Use only a small amount of fabric glue to prevent visible residue.

steps.title: Construct the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Cut two 4.72 inches x 12.76 inches rectangles of script fabric for the back. Fold and stitch one edge of each piece twice by 5mm. Overlap the back pieces envelope-style behind the cushion front. Pin and stitch around the edge. Clip corners, turn right side out, and press.

tip.text: An envelope-style closure allows the cushion pad to be removed easily.

steps.title: Make the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Cut two 12.36 inches calico squares. Stitch around the edges, leaving an 3.15 inches opening. Clip corners, turn right side out, and fill with stuffing. Hand stitch the opening closed and insert the pad into the cushion cover.

tip.text: Avoid overstuffing the pad, as it may distort the cushion cover shape.

items.name: Bicycle Pillow Set
items.finishedSize: Cushion: 11.81 inches x 16.54 inches; Wallet: 11.1.97 inches x 9.84 inches (open)
items.materialNeeded:
Bike print fabric.
Grey fabric.
Jade fabric.
Natural linen.
Ribbon.
Cushion pad, 11.81 inches x 16.54 inches.
Pelmet interfacing.
Black thread.
Cream thread.
Bicycle template.
Pins.
Iron.
Sewing machine.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Sewing, Topstitching, Applique, Cushion Making, Wallet Construction

steps.title: Cut the Cushion Front Panels
steps.content: Cut a 41.18 inches strip of jade bike-print fabric, a 3.94 inches x 41.18 inches piece of linen, and a matching strip of grey bike-print fabric. Arrange the three sections to create the cushion front.

tip.text: Check the direction of the bicycle print before cutting to ensure all motifs face the same way.

steps.title: Join the Front Sections
steps.content: Place the fabric sections right sides together and stitch them into one panel. Press all seam allowances open on the reverse side.

tip.text: Poorly pressed seams can create bulky ridges that show through on the finished cushion.

steps.title: Add Decorative Ribbon
steps.content: Pin ribbon along both the upper and lower edges of the linen section and stitch into place. Press from the reverse side.

tip.text: Uneven ribbon placement can make the central panel appear crooked.

steps.title: Stitch the Bicycle Design
steps.content: Transfer the bicycle motif onto tissue paper and position it on the linen panel. Stitch over the design using black thread. Remove the tissue paper and embroider the wording as desired.

tip.text: Use a shorter stitch length when sewing around curved sections of the bicycle.

steps.title: Prepare the Cushion Back
steps.content: Cut two grey fabric sections for the cushion back. Insert ribbon between the layers and sew with a 5mm seam allowance. Press and topstitch neatly.

tip.text: Ensure ribbon ends are secured before assembling the back pieces.

steps.title: Create the Overlap Closure
steps.content: Cut a jade fabric section and hem one edge by folding it twice. Attach a ribbon tie approximately 3.54 inches from the hemmed edge and press flat.

tip.text: Press each fold before stitching for a cleaner finish.

steps.title: Assemble the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Place the cushion front right side up. Layer the back sections over the front, ensuring the overlap closure is positioned correctly. Stitch around all edges, trim corners, turn right side out and insert the cushion pad.

tip.text: Check that no ribbon is caught inside the seam before sewing around the cover.

steps.title: Cut Wallet Pieces
steps.content: Cut one grey and one jade fabric piece measuring 11.1.97 inches x 9.84 inches. Stitch a decorative ribbon across the grey piece and press.

tip.text: Placing the ribbon too high may interfere with the wallet fold.

steps.title: Construct the Wallet Body
steps.content: Place both wallet pieces right sides together and stitch around the edges, leaving an opening for turning. Trim corners, turn right side out and press.

tip.text: Trim seam allowances carefully to reduce bulk at the corners.

steps.title: Create the Wallet Folds
steps.content: Fold the wallet in half and press a centre crease. Fold each end inward by 1.97 inches and press again to form the future card pockets.

tip.text: Use a ruler to ensure both pocket folds are identical.

steps.title: Insert Interfacing
steps.content: Cut two pieces of pelmet interfacing and place them inside the wallet on either side of the centre fold. Pin securely.

tip.text: Keep a small gap between interfacing pieces to allow the wallet to fold smoothly.

steps.title: Topstitch the Wallet
steps.content: Topstitch around the entire wallet using black thread. Add a decorative folded ribbon above the fabric band if desired.

tip.text: Lengthen the stitch slightly when topstitching for a professional appearance.

steps.title: Form the Card Pockets
steps.content: Fold up both ends of the wallet to create pockets. Hand sew using ladder stitch so the stitches remain hidden and secure.

tip.text: Visible hand stitches can detract from the clean finish of the wallet.

items.name: Ice Cream Pillows
items.finishedSize: Ice cream cushion and ice lolly cushion (various sizes based on template)
items.materialNeeded:
Pink spotted cotton fabric, 81.97 inches x 17.87 inches.
Dark pink patterned fabric, 9.84 inches x 11.81 inches.
Yellow striped fabric, 15.75 inches x 25.59 inches.
Cream lining fabric, 31.50 inches x 111.81 inches.
Ric rac trim, pink, 17.72 inches.
Wide ric rac trim, dark pink, 1m.
Assorted buttons.
Red felt ball.
Felt flowers.
Polyester fibre filling.
Foam pad (for ice lolly cushion).
Beige fabric for lolly stick.
Cardboard tube or stiff insert.
Matching sewing thread.
Tracing paper.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Sewing, Cushion Construction, Applique, Decorative Trims, Stuffing

steps.title: Prepare the Ice Cream Template Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut the full ice cream shape from yellow striped fabric. Transfer the decorative cone lines and stitch over them using dark thread.

tip.text: Mark all template lines before cutting smaller decorative pieces to improve accuracy.

steps.title: Create the Ice Cream Scoops
steps.content: Cut the large scoop from pink spotted fabric and the smaller scoop from dark pink patterned fabric. Position the large scoop onto the cone section and stitch along the decorative edge. Attach the smaller scoop on top.

tip.text: Misaligned scoop edges can make the finished cushion appear uneven.

steps.title: Add Decorative Trims
steps.content: Sew wide pink ric rac across the join between the cone and scoop. Add narrow ric rac between the large and small scoop sections. Attach buttons evenly and sew felt flowers onto the trim.

tip.text: Pin trims in place before sewing to keep curves smooth and even.

steps.title: Construct the Cushion Cover
steps.content: Prepare the back sections by hemming the edges. Overlap the back pieces to create an envelope opening. Stitch the front and back together with right sides facing.

tip.text: Ensure the envelope overlap is deep enough to prevent the cushion insert from showing.

steps.title: Make the Cushion Pad
steps.content: Cut matching pieces from cream lining fabric. Sew around the shape leaving an opening for turning and stuffing. Turn right side out, fill with fibre stuffing, and close the opening.

tip.text: Distribute stuffing evenly into corners and curves for a professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the Ice Cream Cushion
steps.content: Insert the stuffed cushion pad into the cover. Sew a red felt ball to the top of the ice cream to represent a cherry.

tip.text: Overstuffing can distort the decorative applique details.

steps.title: Prepare the Ice Lolly Front
steps.content: Trim a foam pad into a rounded ice lolly shape. Cut spotted, patterned brown and plain brown fabric sections. Join the fabrics together and press seams flat.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when ironing patterned cottons to avoid shine marks.

steps.title: Decorate the Ice Lolly
steps.content: Sew ric rac trim across the colour-change seams. Add buttons and decorative details evenly across the front panel.

tip.text: Uneven button spacing can make the design appear unbalanced.

steps.title: Create the Back Panel
steps.content: Assemble the back panel using matching fabrics. Add ric rac trim and hem exposed edges before pressing all seams flat.

tip.text: Keep seam allowances consistent to ensure the front and back pieces match correctly.

steps.title: Construct the Cushion Body
steps.content: Join the side, top and base sections together. Attach the front panel, then sew the back panel into place, leaving an opening for turning.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances before turning for smoother edges.

steps.title: Insert the Foam Pad
steps.content: Turn the cover right side out and press. Insert the prepared foam pad into the cushion body and close the opening securely.

tip.text: Check that the foam fills the corners properly before sealing the opening.

steps.title: Make the Lolly Stick
steps.content: Cut two stick pieces from beige fabric. Stitch together, turn right side out and lightly stuff. Insert a cardboard tube or stiff support and close the opening.

tip.text: A firm insert helps the lolly stick maintain its shape during use.

steps.title: Attach the Stick and Finish
steps.content: Secure the stuffed stick to the base of the ice lolly cushion with strong hand stitches. Check all seams and decorative details before use.

tip.text: Reinforce the attachment point carefully, as it receives the most handling.

items.name: Stitch Cat Pillow
items.finishedSize: Cat-shaped cushion, approximately 7.87 inches x 11.81 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Grey wool fabric, 15.75 inches x 23.62 inches.
Embroidery needle.
Embroidery threads, assorted colours.
Embroidery hoop.
Polyester fibre filling.
Buttons, 2.
Sewing needle.
Matching sewing thread.
Fabric marker or transfer pencil.
1.0.79 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Embroidery, Running Stitch, Satin Stitch, Back Stitch, French Knots, Cushion Construction, Hand Sewing

steps.title: Transfer the Cat Design
steps.content: Download and print the template. Transfer the cat outline onto a piece of wool fabric measuring at least 7.87 inches x 11.81 inches. Place the fabric in an embroidery hoop and secure it tightly before stitching.

tip.text: Keep the fabric evenly tensioned in the hoop to prevent puckering during embroidery.

steps.title: Outline the Cat Shape
steps.content: Using orange embroidery thread, work running stitches around the entire outline of the cat. Continue around the body, tail, head and ears until the full silhouette is defined.

tip.text: Uneven stitch lengths can make the outline appear irregular.

steps.title: Embroider the Facial Features
steps.content: Outline the lower face using running stitch. Fill the triangular nose with black satin stitch. Add whiskers and the mouth using black back stitch. Create French knots for decorative facial details.

tip.text: Use short, neat satin stitches to create a smooth and solid nose shape.

steps.title: Decorate the Ears
steps.content: Fill the inner ears with yellow satin stitch. Outline each ear using rows of running stitch in contrasting colours following the triangular ear shape.

tip.text: Avoid pulling the thread too tightly, as this can distort the ear shape.

steps.title: Create Decorative Body Embroidery
steps.content: Use the remaining guide lines to decorate the cat with circles, spirals, zigzags, straight lines and curved motifs. Experiment with different thread colours and stitch patterns to create a unique design.

tip.text: Mix contrasting thread colours to make decorative motifs stand out against the grey fabric.

steps.title: Attach the Eyes
steps.content: Sew two buttons onto the face to create the cat's eyes. Position them evenly before stitching securely in place.

tip.text: Misaligned eyes can noticeably affect the cat's expression.

steps.title: Prepare the Back Piece
steps.content: Remove the embroidered fabric from the hoop and press lightly from the reverse side. Place it right sides together with a second piece of matching fabric.

tip.text: Always press embroidery from the reverse side to avoid flattening the stitches.

steps.title: Cut Out the Cushion Shape
steps.content: Cut around the cat shape through both fabric layers, leaving approximately 1.1.97 inches outside the embroidered outline for seam allowance.

tip.text: Use sharp embroidery scissors for smoother curved edges.

steps.title: Sew the Cushion Together
steps.content: Stitch around the cat shape using a 1.0.79 inches seam allowance. Leave a 1.97 inches opening along one straight section for turning and stuffing.

tip.text: Do not sew the turning gap closed at this stage.

steps.title: Turn and Stuff the Cushion
steps.content: Clip curved seam allowances carefully without cutting the stitches. Turn the cat right side out and fill evenly with fibre stuffing until plump.

tip.text: Insufficient stuffing can cause the cat shape to lose definition.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Fold the seam allowance of the opening inward and close the gap using small slip stitches. Secure the thread and trim any excess.

tip.text: Invisible slip stitches create a neat and professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the Cushion
steps.content: Check all embroidery details, remove any visible transfer marks and gently shape the cushion by hand. The embroidered cat cushion is now complete.

tip.text: Avoid excessive washing to preserve the decorative embroidery work.

items.name: Relaxed Batwing Dress
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 2m (59.06 inches wide).
Lightweight interfacing.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins.
Bias binding strip, 4.1.97 inches x 62.36 inches.
Dressmaker's chalk.
Paper scissors.
Fabric scissors.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout, except at the neckline where 0.39 inches is used.
items.techniques: Dressmaking, Bias Binding, Gathering, Slipstitching

steps.title: Cut Out the Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the template. Cut one front piece on the fold, one back piece on the fold, and two sleeve pieces. Cut the neckband from bias fabric according to the measurements provided.

tip.text: Check the fabric grainline before cutting to ensure the dress hangs correctly.

steps.title: Sew the Shoulder and Sleeve Seams
steps.content: Match the shoulder seams of the front and back pieces with right sides together and stitch. Open out the garment and sew the sleeves into the armholes. Press seams open.

tip.text: Press each seam immediately after stitching for a more professional finish.

steps.title: Prepare the Neck Binding
steps.content: Press one long edge of the bias strip to the wrong side. Pin the unfolded edge around the neckline, matching raw edges, and stitch in place.

tip.text: Avoid stretching the neckline while attaching the binding or it may become distorted.

steps.title: Finish the Neckline
steps.content: Fold the bias binding over the seam allowance to the inside of the garment. Turn under the raw edge and pin neatly. Slipstitch the folded edge in place by hand.

tip.text: Use small slipstitches and catch only a few threads of the outer fabric for an invisible finish.

steps.title: Sew the Side and Underarm Seams
steps.content: With right sides together, stitch from the sleeve hem through the underarm and down each side seam in one continuous line. Finish seam allowances and press.

tip.text: Misaligned underarm seams can cause twisting and discomfort when worn.

steps.title: Hem the Sleeves
steps.content: Turn the sleeve hems to the wrong side twice, press, and stitch close to the folded edge. Press the finished hems flat.

tip.text: A narrow double-fold hem reduces bulk and gives a cleaner appearance.

steps.title: Create the Waist Tie
steps.content: Cut and sew a matching fabric tie. Turn right side out, press flat, and topstitch if desired. Thread the tie through the waist casing or belt loops.

tip.text: Use a safety pin to easily turn narrow fabric ties right side out.

steps.title: Finish the Dress Hem
steps.content: Turn the lower hem twice to the inside, press carefully, and stitch around the entire hemline. Ensure the hem hangs evenly before sewing.

tip.text: Allow lightweight fabrics to hang for several hours before hemming to prevent uneven stretching.

steps.title: Final Press and Styling
steps.content: Give the dress a thorough final press. Tie the waist belt, check all seams, and trim any loose threads. The dress is now ready to wear.

tip.text: Steam pressing helps the dress drape beautifully and enhances the soft silhouette.

items.name: Drawstring Pocket Culottes
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 2.5m (59.06 inches wide).
Elastic, 1.18 inches wide.
Soft cord.
Lightweight fusible interfacing.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins.
Dressmaker's chalk.
Safety pin.
Fabric scissors.
Paper scissors.
Lightweight woven fabrics such as viscose or rayon challis are recommended.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Dressmaking, Pocket Construction, Elastic Waistband, Gathering

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Print and assemble the pattern. Cut one pair of front trousers, one pair of back trousers, one pair of pocket bags, and one waistband. Apply lightweight interfacing to the waistband piece.

tip.text: Transfer all notches and markings before removing the pattern pieces.

steps.title: Attach the Pocket Bags
steps.content: Position the pocket bags onto the front trouser pieces with right sides together. Stitch along the curved pocket opening, clip curves if necessary, and press the pocket bags away from the trousers.

tip.text: Misaligned pocket edges can result in uneven pocket openings.

steps.title: Construct the Pockets
steps.content: Fold the pocket bags to the inside of the trousers and topstitch if desired. Match the pocket layers together and sew around the pocket edges to secure them.

tip.text: Keep all pocket stitching within the seam allowance so it remains hidden after assembly.

steps.title: Sew the Front and Back Pieces
steps.content: Join the front trouser pieces at the front crotch seam and stitch. Repeat for the back trouser pieces along the back crotch seam. Press the seams open or toward one side.

tip.text: Reinforce the crotch seam with a second row of stitching for extra durability.

steps.title: Join the Leg Seams
steps.content: Place the front and back trouser sections right sides together. Stitch the side seams and inseams, matching notches carefully. Finish seam allowances and press.

tip.text: Twisting the leg pieces before sewing can result in mismatched seams.

steps.title: Create the Waistband Casing
steps.content: Join the short ends of the waistband to form a loop. Fold the waistband in half lengthwise with wrong sides together and press.

tip.text: Use plenty of steam to create a crisp fold along the waistband.

steps.title: Attach the Waistband
steps.content: Pin the raw edge of the folded waistband to the waist opening of the culottes. Stitch around the waistline, leaving an opening for inserting the elastic.

tip.text: Distribute the fabric evenly around the waistband to prevent unwanted gathers.

steps.title: Insert the Elastic and Cord
steps.content: Cut the elastic to the required length and thread it through the waistband casing using a safety pin. Insert the decorative cord through the front channel if desired. Join the elastic ends securely.

tip.text: Check that the elastic is not twisted before closing the opening.

steps.title: Hem the Legs
steps.content: Turn the lower edge of each leg to the inside twice, press, and stitch around both hems. Ensure both legs are the same finished length.

tip.text: Try on the garment before hemming to confirm the preferred finished length.

steps.title: Finish the Culottes
steps.content: Close the waistband opening, tie the cord into a bow, trim any loose threads, and give the garment a final press.

tip.text: A final steam press helps the culottes achieve their soft, flowing drape.

items.name: Soft Suede Open Jacket
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 17m (59.06 inches wide) lightweight to medium-weight woven fabric such as suede.
Hemming web.
Matching sewing thread.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Dressmaking, Sleeve Insertion, Understitching, Hemming, Facing Construction

steps.title: Cut Out the Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Print and assemble the pattern. Cut the front jacket panels, back panel, side front panels, sleeve pieces, and neck facing pieces from your chosen fabric. Transfer all notches and construction markings.

tip.text: Skipping pattern markings can make it difficult to align sleeves, facings, and side seams accurately.

steps.title: Construct the Jacket Body
steps.content: With right sides together, sew the front jacket panels to the side front panels. Press the seam allowances open. Attach the prepared front sections to the back panel, matching notches and stitching carefully.

tip.text: Press each seam immediately after stitching to achieve a professional finish and improve garment shape.

steps.title: Attach the Neck Facing
steps.content: Join the neck facing pieces at the shoulder seams and press open. With right sides together, pin the facing to the jacket neckline and front opening. Stitch around the neckline and front edges.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances before turning the facing to prevent puckering around the neckline.

steps.title: Understitch the Facing
steps.content: Trim excess bulk from the neckline seam allowances. Turn the facing to the inside and press. Understitch close to the seam through the facing and seam allowances to help the facing stay flat.

tip.text: Understitching is key to preventing the facing from rolling to the outside during wear.

steps.title: Prepare the Sleeves
steps.content: Sew the underarm seam of each sleeve and press the seams open. Stitch gathering rows around the sleeve cap between the marked notches and gently pull the threads to shape the sleeve head.

tip.text: Avoid over-gathering the sleeve cap, as excess fullness can create unwanted puckers.

steps.title: Insert the Sleeves
steps.content: With the jacket body turned right side out and sleeves inside out, insert each sleeve into the armhole. Match shoulder, front, and back notches, then stitch around the armhole. Press seam allowances toward the sleeve.

tip.text: Check sleeve orientation carefully before stitching, as reversing a sleeve is a common fitting error.

steps.title: Sew Side Seams
steps.content: Align the front and back side seams and stitch from the underarm to the hem. Press the seam allowances open for a smooth silhouette.

tip.text: Pin at key points such as the waist and hem to keep seams perfectly aligned.

steps.title: Finish Sleeve Hems
steps.content: Apply hemming web to the sleeve hems according to the manufacturer's instructions. Fold the hem allowance to the inside, press, and fuse in place.

tip.text: Applying too much heat can leave visible adhesive marks on delicate fabrics.

steps.title: Hem the Jacket
steps.content: Fold the jacket hem to the wrong side and secure using hemming web. Press thoroughly to create a crisp, professional finish along the lower edge.

tip.text: Allow the hem to cool completely before handling to ensure the adhesive sets properly.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Give the entire jacket a final press, shaping the collar and front edges carefully. Remove any visible markings and inspect all seams before wearing.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when working with suede or delicate fabrics to prevent shine marks.

items.name: Pleated Cord Trousers
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 2m (59.06 inches wide).
Fusible interfacing, 9.84 inches.
Button, 15mm.
Trouser zip, 7.87 inches.
Air-erasable fabric marker.
Matching sewing thread.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Dressmaking, Zip Insertion, Pocket Construction, Pleat Sewing, Topstitching, Buttonhole Sewing

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Print and assemble the pattern templates. Cut two front trouser pieces, two back trouser pieces, four pocket pieces, one waistband, and one belt carrier. Apply fusible interfacing to the marked waistband section and transfer all markings.

tip.text: Failing to transfer pleat and pocket markings accurately can cause fitting and alignment problems later.

steps.title: Sew the Centre Back Seam
steps.content: Place the back trouser pieces right sides together and stitch the centre-back seam. Finish the raw edges using an overlocker or zigzag stitch and press the seam allowance toward one side.

tip.text: Pressing the seam immediately after stitching helps create a cleaner, flatter finish.

steps.title: Construct the Fly Front
steps.content: Fold the fly extension along the marked fold line and press. Insert the trouser zip following the fly-front markings. Stitch the fly facing in place and secure the zip opening with neat topstitching.

tip.text: Check that the zip lies perfectly flat before topstitching, as uneven placement is difficult to correct later.

steps.title: Mark and Sew the Front Pleats
steps.content: Transfer the pleat markings to the front trouser pieces using an air-erasable fabric marker. Fold and pin each pleat toward the centre front and stitch or baste temporarily at the waist edge.

tip.text: Press the pleats sharply before stitching to create a professional tailored appearance.

steps.title: Construct the Pockets
steps.content: Attach the pocket bags to the front trouser pieces along the curved pocket opening. Clip the curves, understitch where necessary, and turn the pocket bags to the inside. Topstitch close to the pocket edge.

tip.text: Skipping understitching may cause the pocket lining to roll outward during wear.

steps.title: Secure the Pocket Bags
steps.content: Align the remaining pocket pieces and stitch around the pocket bag edges. Secure the pocket bags to the waistline and side seam areas to keep them flat during assembly.

tip.text: Baste the pocket bags to the waist edge to prevent shifting while constructing the trousers.

steps.title: Join Front and Back Trouser Sections
steps.content: Place the front and back trouser pieces right sides together. Stitch the side seams and inside leg seams, matching notches carefully. Finish the seam allowances and press open or toward the back.

tip.text: Double-check that left and right trouser legs are paired correctly before sewing the inseams.

steps.title: Sew the Crotch Seam
steps.content: Turn one trouser leg right side out and place it inside the other leg. Match the crotch seams and stitch the full front and back rise in one continuous seam. Finish and press the seam allowance.

tip.text: Misaligned crotch seams can affect both comfort and the overall fit of the finished trousers.

steps.title: Prepare and Attach the Waistband
steps.content: Join the waistband pieces if required and fold according to the pattern instructions. Attach the waistband to the trouser waist edge, matching centre and side seam markings.

tip.text: Grade bulky seam allowances before folding the waistband for a smoother finish.

steps.title: Create the Button Closure
steps.content: Fold the waistband to the inside and stitch neatly in place. Sew a buttonhole at the marked position and attach the button securely on the opposite side.

tip.text: Always test the buttonhole settings on a fabric scrap before sewing on the finished garment.

steps.title: Attach the Belt Carrier
steps.content: Create the belt carrier strip and stitch it securely to the waistband at the marked location. Reinforce the ends with extra stitching.

tip.text: Use a slightly shorter stitch length when securing belt carriers for added durability.

steps.title: Hem the Trouser Legs
steps.content: Try on the trousers and adjust the length if necessary. Fold the hems to the wrong side, press, and stitch neatly around each leg opening.

tip.text: Uneven hem measurements can make the trouser legs appear different lengths when worn.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Remove any temporary markings, trim loose threads, and give the trousers a final press. Shape the pleats, pockets, and waistband for a crisp tailored finish.

tip.text: Pressing with a pressing cloth helps prevent shine marks on cotton corduroy and similar fabrics.

items.name: Button-Front Utility Playsuit
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 2.5m (59.06 inches wide).
Fusible interfacing.
Buttons, 0.79 inches, 5 pieces.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins or clips.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Dressmaking, Collar Construction, Pocket Sewing, Buttonholes, Topstitching

steps.title: Prepare and Cut the Fabric
steps.content: Download and print the template, then cut all bodice, shorts, pocket, collar, sleeve, facing, belt and belt carrier pieces according to the cutting guide. Transfer all notches and markings.

tip.text: Pre-wash and press your fabric before cutting to prevent future shrinkage.

steps.title: Sew the Bodice
steps.content: Join the front and back bodice pieces at the shoulder seams. Press the seams open and attach the sleeves to the armholes.

tip.text: Misaligned shoulder seams can affect the fit of the collar and front opening.

steps.title: Construct the Collar
steps.content: Apply interfacing to the collar pieces, sew them together, trim seam allowances, turn right side out and press. Attach the collar to the neckline.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances before turning the collar for a crisp finish.

steps.title: Attach the Front Facings
steps.content: Fuse interfacing to the facing pieces and stitch them to the front edges of the bodice. Trim, turn to the inside and press flat.

tip.text: Uneven facings can cause the front opening to twist after buttonholes are added.

steps.title: Make the Pockets
steps.content: Attach the pocket bags to the front shorts pieces, understitch where required, then press the pockets towards the inside.

tip.text: Understitching helps keep the pocket lining hidden inside the garment.

steps.title: Assemble the Shorts
steps.content: Join the front and back shorts pieces at the side seams and inseams. Press all seams neatly.

tip.text: Check that inseams match before stitching to avoid twisting legs.

steps.title: Join Bodice and Shorts
steps.content: Match the waistline seams and stitch the bodice to the shorts section. Finish and press the waist seam.

tip.text: Carefully align side seams to keep the garment balanced.

steps.title: Create and Attach Belt Carriers
steps.content: Fold and stitch the belt carrier strips, then secure them evenly around the waistline according to the pattern markings.

tip.text: Reinforce both ends of each belt carrier for durability.

steps.title: Make the Belt
steps.content: Fold the belt piece lengthwise, stitch, turn right side out and press. Topstitch around the edges for a professional finish.

tip.text: Use a loop turner or safety pin to make turning the belt easier.

steps.title: Sew Buttonholes and Buttons
steps.content: Mark the button positions, sew the buttonholes along the front opening, and attach the buttons securely.

tip.text: Always test buttonholes on a fabric scrap before sewing on the garment.

steps.title: Hem the Garment
steps.content: Turn up and stitch the sleeve hems and shorts hems. Press carefully for a clean finish.

tip.text: Use a seam gauge to keep all hems even.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Trim loose threads, give the playsuit a final press, insert the belt through the carriers and inspect all seams before wearing.

tip.text: Allow the garment to cool completely after pressing so the seams set properly.

items.name: Flowing Drawstring Pants
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 59.06 inches wide x 1.3m (or 2.6m if mirrored).
Piping cord or ribbon.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Bias binding.
Pins.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Dressmaking, Custom Fitting, Bias Binding, Waist Channel Construction, Hemming

steps.title: Print and Adjust the Pattern
steps.content: Download and print the pattern template. Extend the leg length to suit your height and adjust the rise measurement if needed by measuring from the lower back waist, through the legs, to the front waistline.

tip.text: Check all custom measurements before cutting to ensure a comfortable fit.

steps.title: Prepare the Fabric Layout
steps.content: Lay the pattern pieces on the fabric with the required extensions added. Ensure the grainline is straight and place the back waist slightly higher than the front waist.

tip.text: Ignoring the grainline can cause the trousers to twist after sewing.

steps.title: Cut the Trouser Pieces
steps.content: Cut the fabric according to the pattern. If the fabric is not wide enough for a double layer, cut one side first, then flip the pattern to create a mirrored pair.

tip.text: Transfer all notches and markings before removing the pattern pieces.

steps.title: Sew the Front and Back Seams
steps.content: Match the two front trouser pieces and stitch the front crotch seam. Repeat for the back pieces, then neaten and press the seam allowances.

tip.text: Accurate crotch seam stitching is essential for comfort and durability.

steps.title: Join the Legs
steps.content: Match front and back pieces with right sides together. Sew the inside leg seams from crotch to hem, then neaten and press.

tip.text: Misaligned inseams can cause the trouser legs to hang unevenly.

steps.title: Sew the Side Seams
steps.content: Stitch the outer leg seams from waist to hem. Finish the raw edges using an overlocker or zigzag stitch and press the seams open.

tip.text: Press each seam as you sew for a more professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Waistband Channel
steps.content: Open one side of the folded bias binding and stitch it to the top edge of the trousers. Fold the binding to the inside, enclosing the raw edge and creating a channel around the waistline.

tip.text: Keep the channel width consistent so the drawstring feeds through easily.

steps.title: Insert the Drawstring
steps.content: Leave a small opening at the front waist and thread piping cord or ribbon through the waistband channel. Secure the ends to prevent fraying.

tip.text: Attach a safety pin to the cord to make threading easier.

steps.title: Hem the Trouser Legs
steps.content: Press up the hem allowance at the bottom of each leg and fold again for a clean finish. Stitch close to the folded edge.

tip.text: Uneven hems are very noticeable on wide-leg trousers, so measure carefully.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Trim all loose threads, give the trousers a final press, and adjust the drawstring length. Your custom-fit palazzo pants are ready to wear.

tip.text: Hang the trousers overnight before hemming if using drapey fabric to allow the fabric to settle.

items.name: Belted Lounge Robe
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, lightweight woven fabric such as cotton sateen, poplin or lawn, 3m (59.06 inches wide).
Matching thread.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout except around the belt, side pockets, front facing and collar where a 0.39 inches seam allowance is used.
items.techniques: Dressmaking, Collar Construction, Sleeve Setting, Pocket Insertion, Edge Stitching

steps.title: Prepare and Attach the Breast Pocket
steps.content: Print and cut the template pieces. Finish the top edge of the breast pocket and fold the marked section. Stitch, trim corners, turn right side out and press. Finish the remaining edges and position the pocket on the left front dressing gown piece before edge-stitching it in place.

tip.text: Press the pocket carefully before attaching to achieve sharp, professional-looking edges.

steps.title: Join Fronts and Back
steps.content: Place the front dressing gown pieces together and sew the centre-back collar seam. Press the seam open. Position the back piece onto the fronts and sew the shoulder seams. Clip neckline markings and sew the neckline seam in one continuous line.

tip.text: Failing to match neckline markings accurately can cause the collar to sit unevenly.

steps.title: Construct the Collar Facing
steps.content: Join the front facings and collar at the centre back. Press the seam open and finish the outer edge. Position the facing around the collar and neckline, matching all notches and edges before stitching in place.

tip.text: Take extra care around curved sections to prevent puckering when the facing is turned.

steps.title: Turn and Secure the Facing
steps.content: Sew across the hem edges of the facings, trim excess bulk and clip corners. Turn the facing to the inside and press thoroughly. Secure the folded edge around the neckline and shoulder areas using hand or machine stitching.

tip.text: Trim seam allowances before turning to reduce bulk and create smoother edges.

steps.title: Attach the Side Pockets
steps.content: Finish the side seam edges and pocket edges. Sew pocket pieces to the gown fronts and backs at the marked points. Join each pocket pair, stitch around the curved edge and press the pockets toward the front.

tip.text: Check that both pockets are positioned at exactly the same height before sewing.

steps.title: Set the Sleeves
steps.content: Finish the sleeve edges and sew gathering stitches around the sleeve heads. Stitch the underarm seams, turn the sleeves right side out and ease them into the armholes. Match seam points carefully and stitch in place.

tip.text: Distribute the ease evenly around the sleeve cap for a smooth, wrinkle-free finish.

steps.title: Hem the Sleeves and Gown
steps.content: Neaten and press the sleeve hems before edge-stitching them in place. Finish the lower edge of the gown and turn up the hem. Create the belt loops from folded strips and stitch them securely at the side seams above the pocket openings.

tip.text: Measure both sleeves before hemming to ensure they are exactly the same length.

steps.title: Make and Finish the Belt
steps.content: Sew the belt pieces together, press the seam open and fold the belt lengthwise. Stitch around the shape, leaving a turning gap. Trim corners, turn right side out and press flat. Close the opening with hand slip-stitching.

tip.text: Use a blunt point turner to create crisp corners without damaging the fabric.

items.name: Button-Front Flare Pants
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 2.5m (115.75 inches wide).
Fusible interfacing, 19.69 inches.
Six buttons, 20mm.
Matching thread.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Trouser Construction, Waistband Insertion, Side Seam Pockets, Buttonholes, Topstitching

steps.title: Prepare the Trouser Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the template, then cut all pattern pieces. Sew the centre-back seam on the back trouser pieces and stitch the darts. Match the front pieces at the centre front and sew according to the markings. Press all seams carefully.

tip.text: Press each seam immediately after sewing for a cleaner and more professional finish.

steps.title: Apply Interfacing to Front Facings
steps.content: Fuse interfacing to the wrong side of the front flap facings. Position the facings right sides together on the front trousers and stitch along the marked V-shaped lines. Transfer all markings accurately before sewing.

tip.text: Inaccurate marking of the V-shape can cause uneven front openings later.

steps.title: Create the Front Openings
steps.content: Cut along the marked opening lines and carefully clip into the point. Turn the facings to the wrong side and press neatly. Understitch the facings and finish the raw edges before pressing again.

tip.text: Avoid cutting beyond the marked pivot point, as this may weaken the fabric and create holes.

steps.title: Construct the Button Facing
steps.content: Finish the button facings and fold them according to the pattern instructions. Sew them into position beneath the buttonhole section, ensuring both sides align evenly. Press thoroughly.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when working with interfaced sections to avoid shine marks.

steps.title: Prepare and Attach the Front Waistband
steps.content: Apply interfacing to one front waistband piece. Sew the waistband sections together, trim corners and turn right side out. Attach the waistband to the front trouser section and press the seam.

tip.text: Check that both waistband ends are symmetrical before attaching them to the trousers.

steps.title: Create the Buttonholes
steps.content: Finish the remaining waistband edge and secure it in place. Mark the buttonhole positions carefully and sew the buttonholes evenly across both front waistband sections.

tip.text: Always test buttonhole settings on a fabric scrap before sewing them on the garment.

steps.title: Attach the Pocket Pieces
steps.content: Match each pocket piece to the side edges of the front and back trousers. Sew the pockets in place and press them away from the garment body.

tip.text: Ensure the top of each pocket aligns exactly with the waist edge for a balanced appearance.

steps.title: Sew the Side Seams and Pocket Bags
steps.content: Join the front and back trouser pieces together at the side seams, sewing around the pocket bags. Finish the seam allowances and press the completed seams.

tip.text: Twisted pocket bags can cause bulk and make the pockets uncomfortable to use.

steps.title: Secure the Pockets and Prepare the Waistband
steps.content: Clip the seam allowance around the pocket area and press the pockets toward the front. Tack the pocket openings to the waistband edge. Fuse interfacing to the remaining waistband pieces and construct the waistband.

tip.text: Tacking the pocket tops prevents them from shifting during waistband installation.

steps.title: Attach the Waistband
steps.content: Pin the waistband to the trouser waistline, matching all seam points and notches. Stitch one side of the waistband in place and check that the front edges align correctly.

tip.text: An uneven waistband will affect the placement of the buttons and overall fit.

steps.title: Finish the Trousers
steps.content: Complete the inner leg seams and finish all seam allowances. Turn up the hems and stitch them in place. Sew the six buttons securely onto the waistband and front facing to correspond with the buttonholes.

tip.text: Reinforce button stitching with several extra passes of thread for long-lasting durability.

items.name: Belted Long Vest
items.materialNeeded:
Boiled wool fabric, 2.2m.
Fusible interfacing, 1m.
Coordinating thread.
Use a 1.1.97 inches seam allowance throughout, excluding the belt and belt carriers.
items.techniques: Garment Construction, Facing Application, Collar Insertion, Topstitching, Hemming

steps.title: Prepare and Interface the Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the template. Cut all jacket, collar and facing pieces according to the cutting guide. Fuse interfacing to the front facings and one collar piece. Sew the collar pieces together, leaving the neckline edge open. Trim corners, turn right side out and press.

tip.text: Trim excess bulk from the collar corners before turning to achieve crisp, professional points.

steps.title: Join the Shoulder Seams
steps.content: With right sides together, stitch the front jacket pieces to the back jacket at the shoulder seams. Press the seam allowances open. Align the collar with the neckline, matching all notches and centre-back markings, then stitch in place.

tip.text: Misaligned notches can cause the collar to sit unevenly along the neckline.

steps.title: Construct the Facing
steps.content: Join the front facing pieces to the back facing at the shoulder seams and press open. Position the completed facing over the jacket with right sides together, sandwiching the collar between the layers. Stitch around the neckline and front edges.

tip.text: Pin generously around curved areas to keep the facing and collar perfectly aligned.

steps.title: Finish the Armholes
steps.content: Clip the curved seam allowances around the neckline and armholes. Turn the jacket right side out through the shoulders and carefully press all edges flat. Match the armhole edges and secure the facing neatly inside.

tip.text: Avoid clipping into the stitching line when trimming seam allowances around curves.

steps.title: Sew the Side Seams and Splits
steps.content: Match the side seams and stitch from the underarm down to the marked split points. Continue stitching through the facing edges as indicated. Press the seam allowances open and press the split edges flat.

tip.text: Pressing the splits unevenly can cause the jacket fronts to hang incorrectly.

steps.title: Hem the Jacket
steps.content: Fold and press the hem allowance around the lower edge of the jacket and the side splits. Stitch or hand-finish the hems according to your preferred method and secure the facing where necessary.

tip.text: Use plenty of steam when pressing boiled wool to achieve sharp, smooth hems.

steps.title: Create the Belt Carriers
steps.content: Cut fabric strips for the belt carriers. Fold lengthwise and stitch with a narrow seam allowance. Turn right side out, press flat and fold the raw ends under. Stitch the carriers securely at the marked waist positions.

tip.text: Check both carrier positions before sewing to ensure the belt sits level around the waist.

steps.title: Make the Belt
steps.content: Cut a belt strip to the required length. Fold lengthwise with right sides together and stitch, leaving an opening for turning. Trim corners, turn right side out, press thoroughly and slip-stitch the opening closed.

tip.text: Bulky seam allowances inside the belt can create visible ridges after pressing.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Give the entire jacket a final press, ensuring the collar, front edges and armholes lie flat. Thread the belt through the carriers and check that all seams, hems and facings are secure.

tip.text: A thorough final pressing step greatly improves the professional appearance of the finished garment.

items.name: Zip-Back Tapered Pants
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 1.5m (59.06 inches).
Concealed zip, 15.75 inches.
Fusible interfacing.
Matching thread.
Air-erasable fabric marker.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Trouser Construction, In-Seam Pockets, Concealed Zip Insertion, Waistband Construction

steps.title: Prepare the Pocket Openings
steps.content: Download and print the template. Position the pocket bags along the slanted edges of the front trouser pieces, right sides facing. Match the notches and stitch across the pocket opening. Turn the pocket bags to the wrong side and press.

tip.text: Press the pocket opening carefully after turning to achieve a crisp, professional edge.

steps.title: Secure the Pocket Bags
steps.content: Position the back pocket pieces behind the pocket bags, matching the curved edges. Stitch around the pocket bags, then neaten the edges. Press the pockets flat and secure them to the waistline and side seam within the seam allowance.

tip.text: If the pocket layers shift during stitching, the pocket opening may appear uneven from the right side.

steps.title: Sew the Front and Back Crotch Seams
steps.content: Match the two front trouser pieces together and stitch the centre-front crotch seam. Sew the darts in the back trouser pieces, then stitch the centre-back crotch seam. Neaten and press the seam allowances.

tip.text: Accurate dart placement is essential for achieving the correct fit through the waist and hips.

steps.title: Join the Side and Inner Leg Seams
steps.content: Lay the front trousers over the back trousers with right sides facing. Stitch the side seams and press them toward the back. Match and sew the inner leg seams in one continuous seam.

tip.text: Check that all notches align before sewing to prevent twisting in the finished trouser legs.

steps.title: Prepare and Attach the Waistband
steps.content: Fuse interfacing to the waistband piece and neaten one long edge. Align the waistband with the trouser waistline, matching the centre-back edges and zip opening. Pin and stitch in place.

tip.text: Stretching the waistband while sewing can cause rippling around the waist.

steps.title: Insert the Concealed Zip
steps.content: Sew the concealed zip into the centre-back opening using a concealed zip foot. Fold and press the seam allowance and align the zip teeth with the crease line before stitching.

tip.text: Keep the zip perfectly aligned on both sides to ensure the waistband matches at the opening.

steps.title: Finish the Zip Installation
steps.content: Continue stitching both sides of the zip close to the teeth. Remove excess zip tape and secure the lower end to prevent the zip pull from catching.

tip.text: Press the zip area lightly after insertion to help it lie flat without creating impressions on the fabric.

steps.title: Complete the Waistband
steps.content: Fold the waistband to the inside of the trousers, enclosing the seam allowance. Pin in place and stitch neatly in the ditch from the right side or hand stitch for an invisible finish.

tip.text: Uneven folding along the waistband edge can be noticeable on the finished garment.

steps.title: Hem the Trouser Legs
steps.content: Neaten the lower edge of each leg. Fold up a 1.18 inches hem, press thoroughly, and hand stitch or machine stitch in place.

tip.text: Try on the trousers before hemming to confirm the final leg length.

steps.title: Final Press and Finishing
steps.content: Give the trousers a final press, shaping the waistband, pockets, and leg seams. Check the zip operation and remove any visible marking lines.

tip.text: Always test the iron temperature on a fabric scrap first to avoid shine marks or heat damage.

items.name: Faux Fur Lined Vest
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, 1m (123.62 inches) faux fur or similar heavyweight fabric.
Lining fabric, 1m.
Matching thread.
1.1.97 inches seam allowance used throughout.
items.techniques: Garment Construction, Lining Insertion, Armhole Finishing, Slip Stitching

steps.title: Prepare and Join the Shoulder Seams
steps.content: Download and cut out the pattern pieces. Pin and sew the shoulder seams of both the outer fabric and lining, right sides together, matching all notches. Stitch to the marked dot at the back shoulder edge and press the seams open.

tip.text: Press shoulder seams open after stitching to reduce bulk and help the garment sit smoothly.

steps.title: Attach the Neckline and Front Edges
steps.content: Match the neckline lining to the gilet neckline with right sides facing. Pin the centre front edges and stitch continuously from the lower front edge, around the neckline, and down the opposite front edge.

tip.text: Uneven pinning around the neckline can cause twisting when the gilet is turned right side out.

steps.title: Sew the Armholes
steps.content: Match the armholes of the gilet and lining with right sides together. Pin carefully and stitch around each armhole. Clip the curved seam allowances and trim excess bulk from curved corners.

tip.text: Avoid cutting into the stitching line when clipping curves, as this can weaken the seam.

steps.title: Turn and Press the Gilet
