Embroidery thread
Multi-colored yarn
Matching sewing thread
Pins or sewing clips
Fabric scissors
Iron and ironing board
items.techniques: Template Cutting, Fusible Applique, Machine Applique, Embroidery, Soft Toy Construction, Turning, Stuffing, Pleat Formation, Decorative Stitching, Hemming, Bag Construction, Boxed Corners, Lining Construction, Topstitching, Pressing

steps.title: Cut the Template Pieces
steps.content: Print and assemble the templates. Apply fusible webbing to the back of the glitter felt and printed cotton fabrics. Cut two nostrils, one scalloped nose piece, two inner ears, two outer ears, one horn, one head piece, one outer front body piece, one outer back body piece, one lining front body piece, and one lining main body piece.

tip.text: Label each cut piece immediately after cutting to avoid confusion during assembly.

steps.title: Create the Unicorn Face Applique
steps.content: Fuse the inner ear pieces onto the outer ears. Fuse the nostrils onto the scalloped nose piece. Position and fuse the completed nose applique onto the head piece near the lower edge.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when fusing glitter felt to prevent damage from excessive heat.

steps.title: Add Facial Details
steps.content: Attach the doll eyes securely to the head piece. Embroider the mouth and any additional facial details using embroidery thread.

tip.text: Avoid placing the eyes too close together, as it can distort the unicorn's facial proportions.

steps.title: Construct the Horn and Ears
steps.content: Fold the horn piece right sides together and sew along the long edges, leaving the bottom open. Turn right side out and lightly stuff with wadding. Create a small pleat at the base of each ear and secure with a few stitches.

tip.text: Use only a small amount of stuffing so the horn remains soft and easy to sew into the seam.

steps.title: Attach the Horn and Ears
steps.content: Place the head piece right side up. Position the horn at the center of the top edge with the tip pointing downward. Place one ear on each side of the horn. Baste all pieces in place.

tip.text: Ensure the ears and horn point inward toward the fabric so they will face outward after turning.

steps.title: Join the Head and Back Body
steps.content: Place the outer back body piece right sides together with the head piece. Stitch along the top edge, securing the horn and ears within the seam. Remove any temporary basting stitches.

tip.text: Backstitch over the horn and ear bases to reinforce these high-stress areas.

steps.title: Add the Unicorn Mane
steps.content: Thread strands of multi-colored yarn around the horn area to create a decorative mane. Wrap embroidery thread around the horn in a spiral pattern and secure at both ends.

tip.text: Mix several yarn colors and lengths to create a fuller, more playful mane.

steps.title: Prepare the Outer Pocket
steps.content: Create a 0.4" hem along the top edge of the outer front body piece. Place the pocket piece onto the lower portion of the outer body with right sides facing outward. Stitch around the sides and bottom, leaving the hemmed top edge open.

tip.text: Do not accidentally stitch the pocket opening closed.

steps.title: Create the Outer Storage Body
steps.content: Sew the outer body pieces together according to the template shape. Box both lower corners by measuring approximately 2.4" from each corner point, stitching across, and trimming the excess fabric. Turn the outer body right side out.

tip.text: Measure both boxed corners carefully so the organizer stands evenly.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Hem the upper edge of the lining front body piece by 0.4". Sew it onto the lower section of the lining body to create the lining pocket. Stitch around the sides and bottom, leaving the top open. Box the lining corners the same way as the outer body but leave the lining wrong side out.

tip.text: Do not turn the lining right side out before inserting it into the organizer.

steps.title: Insert Wadding and Assemble
steps.content: Place the lining inside the outer organizer. Match the pocket openings and baste them together. Cut a slightly smaller head-shaped piece of wadding and insert it between the outer fabric and lining at the unicorn head section.

tip.text: Trim the wadding slightly smaller than the template to reduce seam bulk.

steps.title: Finish the Organizer
steps.content: Fold the raw edges of both the lining and outer fabric inward. Press neatly, pin together, and topstitch all around the opening to secure the layers and complete the unicorn storage organizer.

tip.text: Check that all raw edges are fully enclosed before topstitching to prevent fraying during use.

items.name: Felt Glasses Sleeve
items.materialNeeded:
Violet felt
Lavender felt
Aqua felt
Primrose felt
Cream felt
Matching sewing thread
Ribbon, 13"
2 buttons
Embroidery thread
Pins or sewing clips
Fabric scissors
Pinking shears
Hand sewing needle
Template printout
items.techniques: Template Cutting, Felt Applique, Hand Applique, Oversewing, Decorative Embroidery, Button Attachment, Topstitching, Hemming, Soft Case Construction, Edge Finishing

steps.title: Cut the Main Felt Pieces
steps.content: Cut two violet felt rectangles measuring 4" x 4.75" and two lavender felt rectangles measuring 2.4" x 4". Pair one small rectangle with one large rectangle for the front and back sections.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors to keep the felt edges clean and smooth.

steps.title: Join the Front and Back Panels
steps.content: Pin a long edge of each lavender rectangle to a short edge of each violet rectangle. Sew using a seam allowance of approximately 0.4". Press the seams flat and topstitch along both joined seams.

tip.text: Topstitch close to the seam line to help the felt lie flat and prevent bulky edges.

steps.title: Shape the Case Opening
steps.content: With the lavender section positioned at the top of the front panel, cut a smooth semi-circular notch at the center of the upper edge to create the opening for the case.

tip.text: Avoid cutting the notch too deep or it may weaken the opening edge.

steps.title: Prepare the Owl Applique Pieces
steps.content: Using the template, cut a pale pink circle for the owl body, aqua pieces for the head and wings, primrose pieces for the feet, beak, moon, and stars, and cream circles for the eyes.

tip.text: Keep all applique pieces organized by color before beginning assembly.

steps.title: Attach the Owl Body
steps.content: Position the owl body on the lower front section of the case. Stitch around the edges using matching thread and an oversewing stitch. Tuck the feet beneath the lower edge of the body while sewing.

tip.text: Pin the applique pieces in place before stitching to maintain accurate positioning.

steps.title: Add the Owl Features
steps.content: Stitch the owl head, wings, beak, and eye pieces onto the body. Secure each piece with small, even oversewing stitches around the edges.

tip.text: Large uneven stitches can make the applique look untidy and less secure.

steps.title: Attach Buttons and Embroider Details
steps.content: Sew one button onto each eye using violet thread. Embroider eyebrows and any decorative facial details with embroidery thread.

tip.text: Ensure the buttons are sewn securely so they do not loosen with regular use.

steps.title: Decorate the Upper Panel
steps.content: Stitch the moon and star applique pieces onto the upper lavender section of the front panel using matching thread and oversewing stitches.

tip.text: Arrange the moon and stars before sewing to create a balanced composition.

steps.title: Create the Ribbon Closure
steps.content: Attach one end of the ribbon to the center top of the back panel. Cut a lavender felt rectangle measuring 0.6" x 0.8" and stitch it over the ribbon end to conceal and reinforce the attachment point.

tip.text: Stitch several times through the ribbon attachment area for added durability.

steps.title: Make the Ribbon Tab
steps.content: Cut two lavender felt circles approximately 0.8" in diameter. Sandwich the free ribbon end between the circles and oversew around the edges to create a decorative pull tab.

tip.text: Keep the ribbon centered between the circles before stitching.

steps.title: Assemble the Case
steps.content: Place the front and back pieces wrong sides together. Stitch around both long sides and across the bottom edge approximately 0.3" from the edge using an oversewing stitch.

tip.text: Do not sew across the top opening or the case will not be usable.

steps.title: Finish the Edges
steps.content: Trim the outer edges carefully with pinking shears to create a decorative finish and reduce fraying. Inspect all seams and trim any loose threads.

tip.text: Trim slowly with pinking shears to maintain an even decorative edge around the entire case.

items.name: Floral Gift Duo
items.finishedSize: Glasses case: approx. 7.1 inches x 3.5 inches; Nail scissors pouch: approx. 3.9 inches x 2.4 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric: floral print, 7.9 inches square; small print, 7.9 inches square.
Felt: thick felt, 15.7 inches square; dark orange, orange, yellow, blue, red and green scraps.
Sequins: orange.
Seed beads.
Embroidery thread, coordinating.
Fusible webbing.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Embroidery scissors.
0.4 inches seam allowance used where stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Embroidery, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Beadwork

steps.title: Prepare the Glasses Case Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the template. Cut the glasses case shape from thick felt and a mirrored lining piece from green floral fabric. Cut small squares of dark orange, orange, yellow and green felt and apply fusible webbing to the reverse side of each piece.

tip.text: Fuse the webbing before cutting detailed motifs to prevent distortion.

steps.title: Cut and Arrange the Flower Motifs
steps.content: Trace the flower, oval and stem motifs onto the appropriate felt colours and cut them out carefully. Arrange the stem and flower pieces on the front of the glasses case and press them into position.

tip.text: Check motif placement before pressing, as fused pieces are difficult to reposition.

steps.title: Decorate the Flower Design
steps.content: Stitch around the flower motifs using straight stitch and zigzag stitch. Layer the oval shapes to create the flower centre and embellish with orange sequins and seed beads. Add extra decorative stitching around the design.

tip.text: Mix hand embroidery with machine stitching for added texture and detail.

steps.title: Add the Embroidered Message
steps.content: Write 'Mum' or a personalised message onto the fabric with an air-erasable pen. Embroider over the lettering using backstitch and coordinating embroidery thread.

tip.text: Test the pen on a fabric scrap first to ensure the markings disappear completely.

steps.title: Construct the Glasses Case
steps.content: Place the felt outer and floral lining right sides together. Stitch around the curved edges, leaving the opening edge free. Turn right side out, press carefully and tuck the lining inside. Fold the lining edge under and secure with neat hand stitches.

tip.text: Trim bulky seam allowances around curves before turning for a smoother finish.

steps.title: Prepare the Nail Scissors Pouch
steps.content: Cut the pouch front and back pieces from green wool felt and matching lining fabric. Apply fusible webbing to blue, green and red felt scraps and transfer the flower template shapes.

tip.text: Keep appliqué pieces organised in colour groups to speed up assembly.

steps.title: Decorate the Pouch Front
steps.content: Fuse the flower and stem motifs onto the front pouch section. Secure them using zigzag and straight stitching, then embellish with sequins and beads around the flower centre and outer edges.

tip.text: Avoid placing bulky beads too close to seam allowances.

steps.title: Assemble the Pouch
steps.content: With right sides together, sew the front and back pouch pieces. Turn right side out and press. Stitch the lining separately, insert it into the pouch and fold the raw edges inward. Hand stitch the lining securely into place.

tip.text: Ensure the lining sits flat inside the pouch before securing it permanently.

steps.title: Finish the Gift Set
steps.content: Inspect all stitching, trim loose threads and give both projects a final press. Pair the glasses case and scissors pouch together to create a coordinated handmade Mother's Day gift set.

tip.text: Matching embroidery colours across both projects creates a professional coordinated look.

items.name: Snow Globe Sweater
items.finishedSize: Adult jumper with custom snow globe appliqué
items.materialNeeded:
Paper globe templates.
Scissors.
Felt or other non-fraying fabric in two colours.
Fabric marker pen.
Sewing machine with darning foot.
A4 plastic craft sheet.
Selection of Christmas-themed appliqué pieces.
Bean-bag filling.
Glitter.
Dress pins.
items.techniques: Applique, Machine Sewing, Decorative Embellishment

steps.title: Prepare the Jumper
steps.content: Iron the Christmas appliqué pieces centrally onto the jumper according to the desired design layout.

tip.text: Arrange all appliqué elements before ironing to ensure the composition is balanced.

steps.title: Create the Globe Template
steps.content: Using an A4 plastic craft sheet and the snow globe template, cut out the snow globe shape, enlarging the template if required to fit the design.

tip.text: A template that is too small may not fully cover the appliqué scene.

steps.title: Cut the Felt Pieces
steps.content: Cut the outer globe circle and the globe base from felt using the prepared template as a guide.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors to achieve smooth, accurate curves.

steps.title: Add the Snow Effect
steps.content: Place a small amount of bean-bag filling along with glitter, stars, or other festive embellishments over the appliqué design.

tip.text: Avoid overfilling the globe or the contents may restrict movement inside the design.

steps.title: Assemble the Globe
steps.content: Position the plastic craft sheet over the embellishments. Place the felt globe ring on top, followed by the felt base, ensuring all layers align correctly.

tip.text: Misaligned layers can leave parts of the plastic sheet exposed.

steps.title: Pin Everything in Place
steps.content: Secure all layers with dress pins, keeping the decorative filling evenly distributed within the globe area.

tip.text: Use plenty of pins around curves to prevent shifting during sewing.

steps.title: Sew the Snow Globe
steps.content: Fit a darning foot to your sewing machine and sew around the outer edge of the felt globe using a straight stitch close to the edge to secure all layers.

tip.text: Sew slowly around curved sections to maintain an even seam and avoid puckering.

items.name: Bunny Bow Scrunchie
items.finishedSize: One adult or child-sized scrunchie with removable bunny ears
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric, assorted fat quarters.
Round elastic cord.
Fabric glue.
items.techniques: Basic Sewing, Gathering, Slip Stitching, Knot Tying

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Choose a cotton fat quarter and cut the required strips for the scrunchie band and bunny ear tie according to the template measurements.

tip.text: Lightweight cotton creates softer gathers and is easier to tie into neat bunny ears.

steps.title: Sew the Scrunchie Tube
steps.content: Fold the scrunchie strip lengthwise with right sides together and stitch along the long edge to create a fabric tube.

tip.text: Keep the seam allowance consistent to ensure the scrunchie gathers evenly.

steps.title: Turn and Insert Elastic
steps.content: Turn the tube right side out and thread the round elastic through the casing. Secure the elastic ends together with a strong knot.

tip.text: Hold both ends of the elastic firmly while threading to prevent it slipping back into the tube.

steps.title: Close the Scrunchie
steps.content: Tuck the raw fabric edges inside and close the opening using slip stitches or a small amount of fabric glue.

tip.text: Hand stitching gives a cleaner finish than glue and creates a more durable scrunchie.

steps.title: Create the Bunny Ears
steps.content: Cut and sew the bunny ear piece, then turn it right side out and press it flat. Shape the pointed ends neatly.

tip.text: Trim excess seam allowance at the points before turning to reduce bulk.

steps.title: Tie the Bunny Ears
steps.content: Wrap the ear piece around the scrunchie and tie it into an overhand knot, positioning the ears upright.

tip.text: Tighten the knot gradually while shaping it with your fingers to keep it neat and symmetrical.

steps.title: Adjust and Finish
steps.content: Arrange the gathers evenly around the scrunchie and adjust the ears until both sides match. Trim any loose threads.

tip.text: Avoid pulling the knot too tightly at once, as it can twist the fabric and distort the bunny ears.

items.name: Dressed Bunny Dolls
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, cotton: patterned, plain and floral prints for body, ears and clothing.
Toy stuffing.
Lightweight quilt wadding.
Buttons: 0.4 inches and 0.6 inches.
Embroidery thread.
Embroidery needle.
Doll-making needle.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Sewing machine.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Toy Making, Stuffing, Hand Sewing, Garment Construction, Embroidery

steps.title: Prepare the Bunny Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Print and cut out all bunny templates. Trace the body, head, arms, legs, ears and foot pad pieces onto the chosen fabrics. Cut out all pieces carefully, transferring any markings from the template.

tip.text: Keep paired pieces together and label mirrored parts to avoid assembly mistakes later.

steps.title: Sew the Head and Body
steps.content: With right sides together, stitch the head and body sections along the marked seam lines. Leave the turning and stuffing openings indicated on the pattern. Clip curved seams and turn right side out.

tip.text: Skipping seam clipping on curved areas can create puckering after turning.

steps.title: Construct the Legs and Feet
steps.content: Attach the sole pieces to the leg sections, matching all markings carefully. Stitch around the curved foot seams, turn right side out and lightly stuff the feet to maintain their shape.

tip.text: Take extra care when sewing tight curves to prevent distortion around the feet.

steps.title: Make the Arms
steps.content: Sew each pair of arm pieces together, leaving the top edge open. Turn right side out and add a small amount of stuffing, keeping the upper section soft for easier attachment.

tip.text: Using a turning tool helps achieve smooth, neatly shaped arms.

steps.title: Stuff and Shape the Bunny
steps.content: Firmly stuff the head and body, paying special attention to the neck area. Add stuffing gradually to the limbs until the bunny feels evenly filled and holds its shape.

tip.text: Uneven stuffing can cause the finished bunny to lean or sit awkwardly.

steps.title: Attach the Limbs
steps.content: Use a doll-making needle and buttons to attach the arms and legs through the body. Tighten the thread securely while allowing the limbs to remain movable.

tip.text: Check all button joints carefully, as loose joints may weaken with repeated play.

steps.title: Create the Face and Attach the Ears
steps.content: Position and attach the eyes, embroider the nose and mouth, then sew the ears securely to the head. Adjust the ear placement until the expression looks balanced.

tip.text: Pin the ears temporarily and view the bunny from multiple angles before sewing.

steps.title: Sew the Pinafore Dress
steps.content: Cut the pinafore pieces and sew the bodice sections together. Gather the skirt, attach it to the bodice and complete the straps. Finish all edges neatly and press before dressing the bunny.

tip.text: Uneven gathering can make the skirt hang unevenly around the bunny.

steps.title: Make the Dungarees
steps.content: Sew the trouser sections together, create the crotch seam and form the leg openings. Attach the bib, waistband and shoulder straps, then finish all raw edges.

tip.text: Press each seam as you sew for a cleaner and more professional finish.

steps.title: Dress and Finish the Bunny Duo
steps.content: Dress each bunny in its completed outfit and make any final adjustments. Trim loose threads, reshape the stuffing if necessary and ensure all details are securely attached.

tip.text: For toys intended for young children, replace small embellishments with child-safe alternatives.

items.name: Mum & Baby Animals
items.finishedSize: Mum: approx. 11.8 inches tall, Baby: approx. 5.9 inches tall
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric: plain and patterned prints.
Embroidery thread.
Toy stuffing.
Lightweight quilt wadding.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Sewing machine.
Hand sewing needle.
0.4 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Toy Making, Stuffing, Embroidery, Hand Sewing

steps.title: Cut Out the Body Pieces
steps.content: Print and cut out the templates. Trace all body, arm, leg, ear and pocket pieces onto the chosen fabrics. Cut out the pieces carefully, transferring any markings needed for assembly.

tip.text: Keep pattern pieces grouped together to avoid mixing up mum and baby sizes.

steps.title: Make the Head and Body
steps.content: Fold the body fabric right sides together and stitch around the traced outline, leaving the turning gap marked on the pattern. Clip curves where necessary and turn right side out.

tip.text: Forgetting to leave the turning gap will make stuffing impossible later.

steps.title: Create the Arms and Legs
steps.content: Sew each pair of arm and leg pieces together with right sides facing. Leave a small opening for turning. Turn the pieces right side out and press flat.

tip.text: A chopstick or turning tool helps push out narrow limbs neatly.

steps.title: Make the Ears
steps.content: Pair the ear fabrics with right sides together and stitch around the edges. Trim the seams, turn right side out and press before attaching them to the head.

tip.text: Bulky seam allowances can make the ears look uneven after turning.

steps.title: Attach Limbs and Ears
steps.content: Position the arms, legs and ears on the body according to the markings. Pin securely and stitch in place before completing the body seams.

tip.text: Check that both sides are symmetrical before sewing permanently.

steps.title: Stuff the Toy
steps.content: Fill the body, arms and legs firmly with toy stuffing. Shape the limbs as you work and ensure the stuffing is distributed evenly throughout the toy.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually in small amounts for a smoother finish.

steps.title: Close the Openings
steps.content: Fold in the seam allowances around each opening and close them using neat slip stitches. Secure all thread ends firmly.

tip.text: Large hand stitches may remain visible on the finished toy.

steps.title: Embroider the Face
steps.content: Using embroidery thread, stitch the eyes, nose, whiskers and mouth onto the face. Keep the features centered and evenly spaced.

tip.text: Draw the facial features lightly with a removable fabric marker before stitching.

steps.title: Add the Pocket or Apron Detail
steps.content: Create the decorative tummy pocket or apron piece from contrasting fabric. Attach it securely to the front of the toy using small machine or hand stitches.

tip.text: Make sure decorative pieces are firmly attached if the toy will be handled frequently.

steps.title: Make the Baby Animal
steps.content: Repeat the same construction process using the smaller pattern pieces to create the matching baby bear or bunny companion.

tip.text: Use coordinating fabrics so the mum and baby animals look like a matching set.

steps.title: Finish and Shape
steps.content: Check all seams, reshape the stuffing if necessary and trim any loose threads. Gently press the fabric if needed and make final adjustments to the facial features.

tip.text: Avoid overheating stuffed toys when pressing, as synthetic fillings can be damaged by excessive heat.

items.name: Hanging Heart Garland
items.materialNeeded:
Floral fabric, three different prints.
Plain cotton fabric, three colours.
White nylon cord.
Bamboo garden cane, 18.5 inches.
Toy filling.
Pinking shears.
Tapestry needle.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Hand Sewing, Stuffing, Decorative Hanging Construction

steps.title: Prepare the Heart Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the heart template. Cut out 30 heart pieces to make 15 stuffed hearts, using a mixture of floral and plain fabrics. Cut two matching heart shapes for each ornament.

tip.text: Mix prints and solid fabrics evenly throughout the project to create a balanced design.

steps.title: Sew the Hearts
steps.content: Place matching heart pieces right sides together and pin around the edges. Sew around each heart, leaving a 3–1.6 inches opening for turning and stuffing.

tip.text: Leaving too small an opening can make turning and stuffing difficult.

steps.title: Clip and Turn
steps.content: Trim the seam allowances and clip curves where necessary. Turn each heart right side out through the opening and gently shape the curves with a blunt tool.

tip.text: Use a knitting needle or chopstick carefully to push out the heart point neatly.

steps.title: Stuff the Hearts
steps.content: Fill each heart with toy stuffing until plump but not overly firm. Ensure the stuffing is distributed evenly throughout the shape.

tip.text: Overstuffing may distort the heart shape and strain the seams.

steps.title: Close the Openings
steps.content: Hand sew the openings closed using small ladder stitches. Repeat the process until all 15 hearts are completed.

tip.text: Matching thread colour will help hide the closing stitches.

steps.title: Prepare the Hanging Cords
steps.content: Cut three lengths of white cord. Thread a tapestry needle and pass the cord through the bottom point of a heart, bringing the needle out at the top centre. Repeat to create three vertical strands of hearts.

tip.text: Uneven spacing between hearts can make the finished wall hanging look unbalanced.

steps.title: Secure the Hearts
steps.content: Tie knots above and below each heart to keep them in position on the cord. Continue threading and securing hearts until all strands are assembled.

tip.text: Measure the spacing before knotting to ensure all strands hang evenly.

steps.title: Attach to the Bamboo Cane
steps.content: Drill or create small holes near each end and the centre of the bamboo cane. Thread the three heart strands through the holes and secure them with knots.

tip.text: Check that all knots are tight enough to support the weight of the stuffed hearts.

steps.title: Create the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Cut an additional piece of cord and tie it to both ends of the bamboo cane to form a hanging loop.

tip.text: Adjust the loop length to suit the wall space where the decoration will be displayed.

steps.title: Finish the Wall Hanging
steps.content: Trim excess cord, check the alignment of the hearts, and make final adjustments to spacing and positioning. Hang the decoration and enjoy your finished project.

tip.text: Keep the wall hanging away from direct sunlight to help preserve fabric colours.

items.name: Bug Shadow Box
items.finishedSize: Sized to fit a square shadow box frame
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric: pink deep-pile plush, pink spotted cotton, sparkly plush.
Felt: white and assorted colours.
Medium-weight interfacing.
Embroidery thread: pink, blue, purple, yellow, red, black.
Sewing needle.
Fabric scissors.
4-inch embroidery hoop.
Square box frame.
Pins.
Fabric glue.
Acrylic glue.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Embroidery, Appliqué, Hand Sewing, Machine Sewing, Framing

steps.title: Prepare the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the butterfly, moth, spotted beetle and striped beetle templates. Transfer the designs onto the appropriate fabrics and cut out all required pieces.

tip.text: Use a removable fabric marker to transfer details accurately without leaving permanent marks.

steps.title: Create the Butterfly
steps.content: Place the butterfly fabrics in an embroidery hoop and stitch around the template outlines. Add decorative embroidery details to the wings, trim the pieces neatly and assemble the butterfly body and wings.

tip.text: Avoid pulling embroidery stitches too tightly as this can distort the wing shape.

steps.title: Make the Moth
steps.content: Transfer the moth design onto fabric and stitch the decorative details. Cut out the moth pieces, secure the layers together and finish the edges neatly.

tip.text: Keep the fabric taut in the embroidery hoop to achieve smooth and even stitching.

steps.title: Construct the Spotted Beetle
steps.content: Layer the spotted beetle fabrics and embroider the decorative markings. Stitch around the outline, create the antennae and trim away excess fabric.

tip.text: Take extra care when trimming around small curves to avoid cutting through the stitching.

steps.title: Construct the Striped Beetle
steps.content: Transfer the striped beetle pattern onto fabric and add the embroidered stripes and decorative details. Stitch the pieces together and trim neatly around the finished shape.

tip.text: Use contrasting thread colours to make the beetle markings stand out.

steps.title: Create the Ladybird
steps.content: Cut out the ladybird pieces, stitch around the body shape and add embroidered spots, facial details and antennae before trimming the finished design.

tip.text: Check the placement of facial details before stitching permanently to keep the design symmetrical.

steps.title: Arrange the Display
steps.content: Position the completed insects inside the square box frame. Experiment with the arrangement until balanced and visually appealing.

tip.text: Take a photo of your preferred layout before securing the insects in place.

steps.title: Mount and Finish
steps.content: Secure the insects inside the frame using fabric glue or hand stitches. Allow everything to dry completely before closing the shadow box and displaying the finished artwork.

tip.text: Ensure all glued pieces are fully dry before closing the frame to prevent shifting.

items.name: Kids’ Art Cosy Set
items.finishedSize:
Tea Cosy: 9.1 inches x 13.8 inches
Mug Cosy: 3.1 inches x 9.8 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, cotton, printed.
Fleece, cream, 18.5 inches square.
Cream cotton fabric, 9.1 inches square.
Embroidery thread.
Ribbon, 18.1 inches.
Tracing paper or tissue paper.
Children's drawing.
Pins.
Scissors.
Sewing machine.
0.4 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Appliqué, Embroidery, Machine Sewing, Topstitching

steps.title: Prepare the Artwork Panel
steps.content: Choose a child's drawing and resize it using a photocopier. Trace the drawing onto tracing paper and transfer it to a piece of cream cotton fabric. Embroider over the lines using embroidery thread to recreate the artwork.

tip.text: Use a light box or hold the fabric against a window to make tracing easier.

steps.title: Create the Tea Cosy Front Panel
steps.content: Cut a 6.3 inches x 7.1 inches patch of embroidered cotton. Trim the completed artwork panel and appliqué it onto a larger piece of patterned cotton fabric using zigzag stitching or buttonhole stitch.

tip.text: Make sure the artwork panel is centred before stitching permanently.

steps.title: Cut the Tea Cosy Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the tea cosy template. Cut two outer fabric pieces and two fleece lining pieces using the template.

tip.text: Cut both lining pieces together to ensure they are perfectly symmetrical.

steps.title: Assemble the Tea Cosy Outer
steps.content: Place the outer fabric pieces right sides together and stitch around the curved edge, leaving the bottom edge open. Trim the seam allowance and press.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances carefully to reduce bulk when turning.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Sew the fleece lining pieces together in the same way, leaving the bottom edge open. Turn the outer fabric right side out but leave the lining wrong side out.

tip.text: Press the seams flat before assembling the layers for a smoother finish.

steps.title: Finish the Tea Cosy
steps.content: Insert the outer tea cosy into the fleece lining with right sides facing. Stitch around the bottom edge, leaving a small opening. Turn right side out through the opening and close it with neat hand stitches.

tip.text: Do not forget to leave a turning gap or the project cannot be turned right side out.

steps.title: Prepare the Mug Cosy Artwork
steps.content: Resize and trace a child's drawing onto cream cotton fabric. Embroider the design and appliqué the finished panel onto patterned cotton fabric.

tip.text: Simple line drawings work best and are quicker to embroider.

steps.title: Make the Mug Cosy Body
steps.content: Cut a 3.9 inches x 7.1 inches rectangle of patterned cotton and a matching fleece lining piece. Stitch the ribbon ties to the outer fabric and layer with the fleece.

tip.text: Check that the ribbon ends are aligned evenly before sewing.

steps.title: Sew and Turn the Mug Cosy
steps.content: Place the fleece and outer fabric right sides together. Stitch around the edges leaving a turning gap. Trim the corners, turn right side out and press carefully.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to push out the corners neatly.

steps.title: Close and Finish
steps.content: Hand stitch the opening closed and topstitch around the mug cosy if desired. Tie the ribbon around a mug and enjoy the finished personalised gift set.

tip.text: Keep the cosy clear of direct flames or stovetop heat sources.

items.name: Felt Tree Trio
items.finishedSize: Tall tree: 8.7 inches high; Medium tree: 6.3 inches high; Small tree: 4.7 inches high
items.materialNeeded:
Felt, three shades of green.
Trims: lace, green.
Printed tape measure.
Toy stuffing.
Embroidery thread, brown.
Wooden bobbins.
White tissue paper.
PVA glue.
0.4 inches seam allowance used throughout.
items.techniques: Machine Embroidery, Stuffing, Hand Sewing, Decorative Trims

steps.title: Prepare the Tall Tree Templates
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut two pieces from dark green felt for the tree body. Cut a 8.7 inches wooden skewer and secure it inside a wooden bobbin using glue. Leave the glue to dry completely before continuing.

tip.text: Allow the glue to dry fully before assembling the tree to prevent the trunk from becoming unstable.

steps.title: Transfer the Embroidery Design
steps.content: Use the provided template to trace decorative embroidery motifs onto the felt pieces. Machine or hand embroider the designs before cutting out the final tree shapes.

tip.text: Attach white tissue paper beneath the felt while stitching to help stabilize the embroidery.

steps.title: Create the Layered Branch Sections
steps.content: Cut the embroidered felt sections and arrange them in overlapping layers to form the tree silhouette. Position the upper layers slightly above the lower ones to create dimension.

tip.text: Misaligned layers can make the finished tree appear uneven, so check symmetry before stitching.

steps.title: Attach Decorative Trim
steps.content: Cut strips of lace trim and stitch them between the overlapping tree sections. Secure the trim neatly while maintaining the tree shape.

tip.text: Pin the trim in place before sewing to keep the decorative edges evenly spaced.

steps.title: Assemble and Stuff the Tall Tree
steps.content: Place the front and back tree pieces right sides together and stitch around the edges, leaving a small opening. Turn right side out, insert stuffing, then close the opening with hand stitches.

tip.text: Avoid overstuffing, as excessive filling can distort the embroidered details.

steps.title: Insert the Trunk
steps.content: Push the prepared skewer trunk into the base of the stuffed tree. Add extra glue if needed to secure the trunk firmly inside the tree body.

tip.text: Insert the trunk centrally to keep the tree standing upright.

steps.title: Create the Medium Tree
steps.content: Repeat the same process using the medium-sized template and a shorter trunk. Embroider, assemble, stuff, and attach decorative trims as before.

tip.text: Using the wrong template size can result in mismatched proportions within the trio.

steps.title: Create the Small Tree
steps.content: Follow the same construction method using the smallest template. Embroider the design, assemble the layers, stuff lightly, and secure the trunk in place.

tip.text: Smaller pieces can shift while sewing, so use plenty of pins to maintain alignment.

steps.title: Finish the Trio Display
steps.content: Arrange the three completed trees together. Check that each tree stands securely and trim any loose threads before displaying.

tip.text: Using slightly different embroidery patterns on each tree creates a more attractive coordinated display.

items.name: Gingerbread Ornament Set
items.finishedSize: Gingerbread man: 9.4 inches x 11 inches; Christmas tree: 5.1 inches x 8.9 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Jumper, wool, pale or beige, 19.7 inches.
Felt: lime green, 9.1 inches x 11.8 inches.
Fabric scraps: white, turquoise, pink.
Dye.
Woodland Brown Fimo clay.
Embroidery thread.
Lime green ribbon, 0.4 inches wide.
Buttons: 0.7 inches, 0.9 inches blossom, 0.6 inches, 1 inches.
Ric rac trim: pink, baby pink, turquoise.
Ribbon: satin green, 0.2 inches; white; blue, 0.4 inches.
Flat-backed crystals.
Glue.
Toy stuffing.
Embroidery tool.
Polymer clay: black, turquoise, white, lime, pink, purple.
Glitter.
0.4 inches seam allowance used throughout.
items.techniques: Applique, Hand Sewing, Embroidery, Stuffing, Polymer Clay Crafting

steps.title: Prepare the Gingerbread Man Template
steps.content: Print and cut out the gingerbread man template. Transfer the shape onto the wool jumper fabric and cut two matching body pieces.

tip.text: Use a felted wool jumper for a sturdier ornament that resists fraying.

steps.title: Add Decorative Ric Rac Trim
steps.content: Pin pink ric rac around the outer edge of the front body piece. Stitch neatly in place to create the icing effect before assembling the ornament.

tip.text: Stretching the trim while sewing can cause the finished decoration to ripple.

steps.title: Create the Face Features
steps.content: Cut a smile from white felt and sew or glue it to the face. Attach black polymer clay or button eyes and secure firmly.

tip.text: Ensure all small embellishments are attached securely to prevent them from becoming loose.

steps.title: Make the Decorative Buttons
steps.content: Roll polymer clay into small discs and decorate with patterns using an embossing tool. Bake according to the manufacturer's instructions and allow to cool completely.

tip.text: Apply a light dusting of glitter before baking for extra sparkle.

steps.title: Attach Buttons and Bow
steps.content: Sew the decorative buttons onto the front of the gingerbread man. Tie a small ribbon bow and attach it at the neckline.

tip.text: Position the buttons evenly down the center to keep the design balanced.

steps.title: Assemble the Gingerbread Man
steps.content: Place the front and back body pieces right sides together. Stitch around the edge, leaving a small opening for stuffing.

tip.text: Clip curved areas carefully before turning to help the shape lie smoothly.

steps.title: Stuff and Close the Ornament
steps.content: Turn the ornament right side out and fill firmly with toy stuffing. Hand-stitch the opening closed using small invisible stitches.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually to avoid lumps and maintain an even shape.

steps.title: Cut the Christmas Tree Pieces
steps.content: Trace the Christmas tree template onto lime green felt and cut two tree shapes. Prepare additional felt pieces for layered decorations.

tip.text: Cutting without transferring markings first can make decoration placement difficult.

steps.title: Decorate the Tree Front
steps.content: Attach ric rac trims, buttons, blossoms, crystals, and clay decorations to the front tree piece. Arrange them in horizontal rows to resemble garlands.

tip.text: Lay out all embellishments before stitching to achieve a balanced design.

steps.title: Construct and Stuff the Tree
steps.content: Place the front and back tree pieces together and stitch around the edge, leaving an opening. Turn right side out, stuff lightly, and close the gap.

tip.text: Avoid overstuffing the tree or the embellishments may distort.

steps.title: Add Hanging Loops
steps.content: Create hanging loops from ribbon and sew them securely into the top of both the gingerbread man and tree decorations.

tip.text: Double-stitch the ribbon ends for extra durability when hanging.

steps.title: Finish the Decorations
steps.content: Trim any loose threads, check all embellishments are secure, and display the gingerbread man and Christmas tree as festive holiday ornaments.

tip.text: Inspect glued embellishments after drying and reinforce any pieces that feel loose.

items.name: Winter Dress-Up Doll
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric: cotton, various prints.
Faux fur.
Fibre filling.
Quilt wadding.
DK yarn, black.
Embroidery thread.
Narrow flat elastic.
Decorative buttons.
Snap fasteners.
0.4 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Toy Making, Stuffing, Embroidery, Hand Sewing, Garment Construction

steps.title: Cut Out the Doll Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut the body, head, arms, legs, foot pieces, and clothing pieces from the selected fabrics, transferring all markings and notches.

tip.text: Label each cut piece immediately to avoid confusion during assembly.

steps.title: Sew and Stuff the Head
steps.content: Sew the head pieces together, leaving the neck opening free. Clip curved seams, turn right side out, and lightly stuff. Embroider the facial features before closing the opening.

tip.text: Overstuffing the head too early can make facial embroidery difficult and uneven.

steps.title: Construct the Body
steps.content: Place the body pieces right sides together and stitch around the edges, leaving turning and stuffing openings. Turn right side out and fill firmly with fibre stuffing.

tip.text: Ensure the neck area is stuffed firmly enough to support the finished head.

steps.title: Prepare the Legs
steps.content: Sew each leg section together, attach the feet, and turn right side out. Insert stuffing evenly from the ankle upward before closing the openings.

tip.text: Use a stuffing tool or chopstick to fill narrow ankle sections neatly.

steps.title: Prepare the Arms
steps.content: Sew the arm pieces together and turn them right side out. Stuff lightly, leaving the upper ends soft for easier attachment to the body.

tip.text: Stuffing the shoulder area too firmly can restrict arm movement.

steps.title: Attach Limbs with Buttons
steps.content: Join the arms and legs to the body using strong thread and decorative buttons, creating movable joints. Secure all knots firmly.

tip.text: Pull the thread tightly enough to secure the joints, but not so tightly that the limbs cannot move.

steps.title: Attach the Head
steps.content: Position the completed head on the neck opening and hand sew securely around the neckline. Add extra stuffing if necessary before closing.

tip.text: Use ladder stitch around the neckline for a neat and almost invisible finish.

steps.title: Create the Hair
steps.content: Cut lengths of black DK yarn and sew them onto the head in rows. Gather sections into pigtails and secure with elastic bands.

tip.text: Spacing the yarn rows too far apart may leave visible gaps on the scalp.

steps.title: Sew the Tunic
steps.content: Cut the tunic front and back pieces. Sew shoulder seams, finish edges, and stitch the side seams while leaving openings where indicated. Add snap fasteners at the back.

tip.text: Press each seam as you sew to keep the garment crisp and professional.

steps.title: Make the Skirt
steps.content: Join the skirt panels, hem the lower edge, gather the upper edge, and attach it to a waistband casing. Insert elastic through the casing and secure.

tip.text: Check the elastic length before stitching the casing closed to ensure a comfortable fit.

steps.title: Create the Faux Fur Coat
steps.content: Cut the coat pieces from fabric and faux fur. Sew the outer coat and lining separately, join them together, and turn right side out. Finish the edges carefully.

tip.text: Trim excess bulk from faux fur seam allowances to reduce thickness around curves.

steps.title: Dress the Snow Dolly
steps.content: Put the skirt, tunic, leggings, and coat onto the doll. Arrange the clothing neatly and fasten all closures.

tip.text: Add decorative trims, buttons, or ribbon details to personalize the finished doll.

steps.title: Finish and Inspect
steps.content: Check all seams, joints, and embellishments. Trim loose threads and ensure the doll stands or sits properly before display.

tip.text: Reinforce any loose buttons or decorative pieces before gifting the doll.

items.name: Fabric Reindeer
items.materialNeeded:
Patterned fabric.
Coordinating fabric.
Black embroidery thread.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Toy filling.
Ribbon.
Bell.
Wooden bead, 0.2 inches.
0.2 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Soft Toy Sewing, Stuffing, Embroidery, Hand Sewing

steps.title: Prepare the Template Pieces
steps.content: Download, print and cut out the reindeer templates. Cut one front and one back body piece from your chosen fabrics. Transfer all markings, including eye placement and opening guides, before sewing.

tip.text: Print templates at 100% scale to ensure the finished toy matches the intended size.

steps.title: Embroider the Eyes
steps.content: Mark the eye positions on the front body piece and embroider them using doubled black embroidery thread. Keep the stitches small and even for a neat expression.

tip.text: Embroidering the eyes after assembling the toy is much harder and can distort the fabric.

steps.title: Sew the Body
steps.content: Place the front and back body pieces right sides together and stitch around the outer edge, leaving a gap for turning and stuffing. Reinforce the stitching around the antlers and legs.

tip.text: Do not stitch the turning gap closed or the toy cannot be turned right side out.

steps.title: Trim and Turn
steps.content: Trim the seam allowance and clip into curved areas. Turn the reindeer right side out through the opening and carefully shape the antlers, legs and tail.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to push out corners without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Stuff the Reindeer
steps.content: Fill the reindeer firmly with toy stuffing, working small amounts into the antlers, legs and neck before filling the body. Continue until the toy feels evenly padded.

tip.text: Overstuffing can stretch the seams and distort the reindeer's shape.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Fold the raw edges of the opening inward and close the gap using a neat ladder stitch or slip stitch.

tip.text: Small hand stitches help create an almost invisible finish.

steps.title: Create the Neck Bow
steps.content: Cut a length of ribbon and tie it around the reindeer's neck. Form a neat bow and adjust the loops until they are even.

tip.text: Press the ribbon lightly before tying to achieve a crisp, professional bow.

steps.title: Attach the Bell
steps.content: Secure a small bell to the centre of the ribbon bow using matching thread. Stitch several times to ensure it is firmly attached.

tip.text: Decorative bells should be omitted when making toys for babies or very young children.

steps.title: Add the Wooden Bead Nose
steps.content: Sew a 0.2 inches wooden bead onto the face to create the reindeer's nose. Position it centrally below the eyes and secure with multiple stitches.

tip.text: Small beads may present a choking hazard and should not be used on toys intended for infants.

steps.title: Finish the Reindeer
steps.content: Check all seams, trim loose threads and adjust the stuffing if necessary. Shape the toy by hand and ensure all decorative elements are securely attached before displaying or gifting.

tip.text: Give the finished toy a gentle squeeze and reshape the stuffing to achieve a smooth, even appearance.

items.name: Snowman Pair
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, cotton: cream floral print, red print, blue floral print.
Embroidery thread.
Toy filling.
Pom-poms, cream.
Buttons, assorted.
Orange button for nose.
Ribbon scraps for scarf.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
General sewing supplies.
Use seam allowance of 0.2 inches unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Stuffing, Hand Sewing, Gathering, Embroidery

steps.title: Cut Out the Template Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the snowman templates. Cut the body, head, arm, hat, and scarf pieces from the selected fabrics. Transfer any markings to the fabric using an air-erasable pen.

tip.text: Forgetting to transfer placement markings can make facial features and accessories difficult to position accurately.

steps.title: Create the Body Shape
steps.content: Sew the body pieces together with right sides facing, leaving an opening for turning. Clip curved seams, turn right side out, and begin stuffing evenly from the base upward.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually in small amounts to create a smooth rounded shape without lumps.

steps.title: Form the Head
steps.content: Gather the head opening using a running stitch and pull gently to create a rounded shape. Add stuffing until the head feels firm and symmetrical.

tip.text: Overfilling the head may distort the facial area and make embroidery more difficult.

steps.title: Attach the Arms
steps.content: Sew each arm pair together, leaving the top edge open. Turn right side out, lightly stuff, and hand stitch the arms securely to each side of the body.

tip.text: Leave the upper section of each arm slightly unstuffed for easier attachment and a softer appearance.

steps.title: Add the Face Details
steps.content: Attach button eyes and an orange button nose. Embroider the smile and any additional facial features using embroidery thread.

tip.text: Always check eye placement before sewing permanently to avoid an uneven expression.

steps.title: Make and Attach the Hat
steps.content: Sew the hat pieces together, turn right side out, and fit over the snowman's head. Add a cream pom-pom to the top and secure the hat with hand stitches.

tip.text: Tack the hat at several hidden points to prevent it from shifting during display.

steps.title: Create the Scarf
steps.content: Cut a fabric strip for the scarf, fold and press the edges if desired, then wrap it around the neck and secure with a few hidden stitches.

tip.text: Avoid making the scarf too tight, as it can distort the shape of the neck area.

steps.title: Finish the Snowman
steps.content: Close all remaining openings with slip stitches. Adjust the stuffing, shape the body, and add decorative buttons or embellishments as desired.

tip.text: Gently massage the stuffing into place before closing the final seam for a smoother finish.

items.name: Winter Cabin Hoop
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, thin polycotton, white, 12 inches circle.
Felt: white, green glitter, aqua, mint, brown, dark green, cream, and red.
Embroidery thread: white, green, dark brown, blue, pale blue, black, grey.
Embroidery hoop, 8.1 inches.
Pearl buttons, 0.4 inches x 0.8 inches.
Silver net fabric.
Fabric glue.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
General sewing supplies.
items.techniques: Embroidery, Applique, Hand Sewing, Decorative Stitching

steps.title: Prepare the Hoop Background
steps.content: Download and print the template. Cut a 12 inches circle from white polycotton and place it in the embroidery hoop. Tighten the fabric evenly so the surface remains smooth and taut.

tip.text: Loose fabric can cause puckering and uneven embroidery stitches.

steps.title: Create the Snowy Hills
steps.content: Cut the hillside shapes from white glitter felt using the template. Position the layers on the hoop background and secure them with small hand stitches along the edges.

tip.text: Layer the hills slightly to create depth and a more realistic winter landscape.

steps.title: Make the Trees
steps.content: Cut tree pieces from green and dark green felt. Add embroidered branch details using contrasting thread, then stitch the trees onto the background in the desired arrangement.

tip.text: Placing trees too close together can hide decorative embroidery details.

steps.title: Construct the Cabin
steps.content: Cut the cabin walls, roof, door, and window pieces from felt. Layer the pieces together and secure with hand stitching to create the cabin structure.

tip.text: Use small, neat stitches around the roof and door edges for a clean finished appearance.

steps.title: Add Cabin Details
steps.content: Attach pearl buttons as decorative window features and embroider additional details around the cabin, including outlines and wood texture effects.

tip.text: Uneven button placement can make the cabin appear asymmetrical.

steps.title: Create the Snow Effects
steps.content: Use white embroidery thread to stitch snow drifts, snowy roof accents, and decorative winter details across the scene.

tip.text: Vary stitch lengths slightly to create a more natural snow-covered texture.

steps.title: Attach the Net Fabric
steps.content: Cut a small piece of silver net fabric and place it over selected snowy areas to create a frosty sparkle effect. Secure with tiny hidden stitches.

tip.text: Avoid stretching the net fabric while sewing, as it can distort the design.

steps.title: Finish the Embroidery
steps.content: Check all applique pieces and embroidery details, trimming any loose threads. Add extra decorative stitches where needed to enhance the winter scene.

tip.text: Step back and view the design from a distance before finishing to ensure the composition feels balanced.

steps.title: Secure the Back of the Hoop
steps.content: Trim excess fabric around the hoop, gather the edges at the back with strong thread, and secure neatly. Cover the back if desired for a professional finish.

tip.text: Pull the gathering thread gradually to avoid tearing the fabric around the hoop edge.

items.name: Holiday Baby Bibs
items.materialNeeded:
Brown striped cotton fabric.
Red Christmas print cotton fabric.
Cream square cotton fabric.
White fleece fabric.
Cotton batting.
Felt: white and mustard yellow.
Brown embroidery thread.
Sewing thread.
Press studs or hook-and-loop tape.
Iron-on fusible webbing.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
General sewing supplies.
items.techniques: Applique, Machine Sewing, Embroidery, Topstitching

steps.title: Print and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the bib templates. Cut out the main bib shape and all applique pieces including the beard, face, hat, nose, and decorative details. Transfer any markings to the fabric.

tip.text: Cutting applique pieces without transferring placement marks can make assembly difficult later.

steps.title: Prepare the Main Bib Pieces
steps.content: Cut the front and back bib sections from the selected fabrics. Cut matching fleece or batting pieces for added softness and absorbency.

tip.text: Use fleece as the backing layer to create a softer and more comfortable bib for babies.

steps.title: Create the Robin Bib Applique
steps.content: Fuse the applique pieces onto the bib front using fusible webbing. Position the robin body, wing, beak, and facial details according to the template before stitching them in place.

tip.text: Allow the ironed applique to cool completely before moving it to prevent shifting.

steps.title: Make the Santa Face
steps.content: Cut the Santa face, beard, hat, and nose pieces from the appropriate fabrics and felt. Layer the pieces according to the template and secure them with machine stitching.

tip.text: Use a narrow zigzag stitch around applique edges for durability and a neat finish.

steps.title: Embroider the Facial Features
steps.content: Embroider the eyes, smile, and any additional facial details using brown embroidery thread. Add decorative stitching where desired.

tip.text: Check facial feature placement carefully before stitching, as small adjustments greatly affect the final expression.

steps.title: Attach the Nose and Hat Details
steps.content: Secure the felt nose and any hat embellishments to the Santa design. Topstitch around the hat and beard to add dimension and definition.

tip.text: Lightly stuff the nose before attaching if you want a slightly raised three-dimensional effect.

steps.title: Assemble the Bib
steps.content: Place the bib front and back pieces right sides together with the batting or fleece layer included. Stitch around the edge, leaving an opening for turning.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances before turning to reduce bulk and help the bib lie flat.

steps.title: Turn and Topstitch
steps.content: Turn the bib right side out through the opening. Press carefully, then topstitch around the entire edge to close the opening and secure all layers.

tip.text: A matching thread color creates a clean professional finish around the bib edge.

steps.title: Add the Fastening
steps.content: Attach press studs according to the manufacturer's instructions, or use hook-and-loop tape as an alternative fastening method.

tip.text: Ensure fasteners are securely attached because baby accessories receive frequent washing and use.

steps.title: Finish and Inspect
steps.content: Trim any loose threads, check that all applique pieces are securely stitched, and give the bib a final press before use or gifting.

tip.text: Skipping the final inspection may leave loose threads that can unravel with repeated washing.

items.name: Animal Mug Cosies
items.finishedSize: Sized to fit a small child's mug (adjustable to other mug sizes)
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, cotton print, plain felt, various colours.
Quilt wadding.
Medium weight embroidery thread.
Fabric glue.
Snap fasteners.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Embroidery, Hand Sewing, Machine Sewing

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Mug Wrap
steps.content: Download and print the template. Measure the mug circumference and adjust the template if needed. Cut one wrap piece from cotton fabric and one from quilt wadding, adding a 0.2 inches seam allowance. Place right sides together with the wadding on the outside. Sew around the curved edges, leaving a 1.6 inches gap along one straight edge. Clip curves, turn right side out and press.

tip.text: Clipping the curved seam allowance helps the wrap sit smoothly around the mug.

steps.title: Topstitch and Add Fasteners
steps.content: Fold the raw edges of the opening inward and slip-stitch the gap closed. Topstitch around the wrap approximately 0.1 inches from the edge. Attach snap fasteners to both ends of the wrap to secure it around the mug.

tip.text: Check the snap orientation before attaching to ensure the cosy fastens correctly.

steps.title: Cut the Polar Bear Pieces
steps.content: Using the template, cut the bear face pieces from felt. Use darker felt for the body details and lighter felt for the head and mask sections. Remove the paper template after cutting.

tip.text: Keep all felt pieces grouped together to avoid mixing up similar shapes.

steps.title: Create the Eyes and Nose
steps.content: Cut small circles from black and white felt for the eyes and sew them together. Position the eyes on the face and secure with small stitches. Add a felt nose and embroider any additional facial details.

tip.text: Take time to align both eyes evenly before stitching permanently.

steps.title: Attach the Face Applique
steps.content: Arrange the face pieces on the front of the mug wrap. Sew around the edges using small neat stitches and secure all layers firmly in place.

tip.text: Use matching thread for invisible stitching or contrasting thread for a decorative finish.

steps.title: Make the Ears
steps.content: Place an ear piece over a second ear piece and stitch around the curved edge, leaving the bottom edge open. Repeat for the second ear. Lightly fill each ear with toy stuffing before closing.

tip.text: Overstuffing the ears can make them bulky and difficult to attach neatly.

steps.title: Attach the Ears
steps.content: Position the ears at the top of the bear face and secure them with small hand stitches. Ensure both ears are symmetrical before final stitching.

tip.text: Pin the ears in place first and check their position from the front before sewing.

steps.title: Finish the Mug Cosy
steps.content: Trim any loose threads and inspect all stitching. Wrap the cosy around the mug and fasten the snaps. Adjust the fit if necessary and enjoy your finished polar bear mug warmer.

tip.text: This design can be customised with different animal faces using the same cosy base pattern.

items.name: Nordic Gnome
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, printed cotton, 9.8 inches square.
Faux sheepskin, 3.1 inches x 4.7 inches.
Felt, 9.8 inches x 11.8 inches.
Iron-on interfacing.
Toy stuffing.
Tailor's chalk.
Pom poms, white.
Pipe cleaners.
Dry rice.
0.2 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Soft Toy Construction, Hand Sewing, Machine Sewing, Stuffing

steps.title: Cut Out the Template Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Transfer the body, leg, arm and hat templates onto the fabric and felt. Cut out all required pieces, including two hats, one beard, one nose, two arms and two legs.

tip.text: Mark all notches and placement points before removing the paper templates.

steps.title: Prepare the Legs
steps.content: With right sides facing, fold the leg pieces in half lengthwise and stitch along the long raw edges. Turn right side out and press. Repeat for both legs, then thread a pipe cleaner through each leg and add a small amount of stuffing.

tip.text: The pipe cleaner allows the legs to be gently posed after assembly.

steps.title: Create the Arms and Boots
steps.content: Cut rectangles of fabric for the arms. Stitch together with right sides facing and turn through. Sew the mitten and boot pieces, leaving openings for turning. Turn right side out and attach the arms and boots to their corresponding leg sections.

tip.text: Make sure left and right boot pieces are aligned correctly before sewing.

steps.title: Make the Hat
steps.content: Sew the two hat pieces together with right sides facing. Turn right side out and add a white pom-pom to the tip. Press lightly to shape the hat.

tip.text: A small amount of stuffing near the hat tip helps the pom-pom sit neatly.

steps.title: Assemble the Body
steps.content: Place the arm and leg pieces onto the body template following the markings. Position the second body piece on top with right sides together. Sew around the body, leaving an opening at the bottom for turning.

tip.text: Ensure the arms and legs are tucked inside the body before stitching the perimeter.

steps.title: Turn and Stuff
steps.content: Turn the body right side out through the opening. Fill the body with toy stuffing and add dry rice to create weight and stability. Close the opening with hand stitching.

tip.text: Adding rice or sand makes the gnomes ideal as decorative doorstops.

steps.title: Attach the Beard
steps.content: Cut the beard shape from faux sheepskin and trim as required. Position it on the front of the body and stitch securely in place beneath the hat line.

tip.text: Brush the faux fur away from the stitching line to avoid trapping fibres in the seam.

steps.title: Add the Mittens and Boots
steps.content: Attach the finished mittens and boots to the arm and leg ends. Secure with strong hand stitches to withstand handling.

tip.text: Double-check symmetry before permanently attaching the pieces.

steps.title: Attach the Nose
steps.content: Sew a small pom-pom or fabric-covered nose onto the face so it peeks out beneath the beard and hat.

tip.text: Position the nose slightly higher than expected, as the beard will cover part of it.

steps.title: Finish the Nordic Gnomes
steps.content: Place the hat over the head and secure with a few hidden stitches if desired. Adjust the beard, arms and legs, trim loose threads and shape the gnome for display.

tip.text: Create a matching pair using contrasting fabrics for a charming Scandinavian-inspired display.

items.name: Christmas Elf Plush
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric, plain pink.
Assorted patterned cotton fabrics in red, green and festive prints.
Felt, green and red.
Fiber filling.
Embroidery thread.
Buttons, assorted sizes.
Small jingle bell.
Heart-shaped button.
Tracing paper.
Pins.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Stuffing, Sewing, Embroidery, Soft Toy Construction

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Arms
steps.content: Print and cut the templates. Cut four arm pieces from patterned fabric. Sew each pair together leaving the top edge open. Clip curves, turn right side out and stuff lightly.

tip.text: Use a turning tool to shape the mitten-like hands neatly.

steps.title: Create the Head and Neck
steps.content: Cut two head and neck pieces from pink cotton fabric. Sew around the edges leaving the neck opening free. Clip curves, turn right side out and stuff firmly.

tip.text: Overstuffing the head can distort the embroidered facial features.

steps.title: Construct the Body
steps.content: Cut two body pieces from patterned fabric. Stitch the pieces together, leaving openings for attaching the limbs and turning.

tip.text: Press the seams before turning to achieve a smoother finished body.

steps.title: Make the Legs
steps.content: Cut four leg pieces from striped fabric. Sew each pair together, turn right side out and lightly stuff. Attach the legs securely to the lower body.

tip.text: Leave the legs slightly soft to maintain the elf's floppy appearance.

steps.title: Attach Arms and Head
steps.content: Position the arms between the body layers and stitch securely. Insert the neck into the body opening and hand-sew in place.

tip.text: Reinforce the shoulder and neck seams as these areas receive the most stress.

steps.title: Add Facial Features
steps.content: Embroider the eyes and smile. Add rosy cheeks using fabric circles or embroidery details. Secure all thread ends inside the head.

tip.text: Check the placement of the eyes before stitching permanently to keep the face symmetrical.

steps.title: Create the Collar and Belt
steps.content: Cut a collar from red felt and gather it around the neck. Sew a decorative belt around the waist and attach a large button at the center.

tip.text: A contrasting button creates a cheerful focal point on the costume.

steps.title: Make the Hat
steps.content: Cut the hat pieces from patterned fabric and sew together. Turn right side out and fit onto the elf's head. Add a jingle bell to the tip.

tip.text: Omit small bells and buttons if the toy is intended for young children.

steps.title: Finish the Elf
steps.content: Stuff the body firmly, close all openings, and add decorative buttons or embellishments. Shape the limbs and give the elf a final press.

tip.text: Hand-sew decorative details after stuffing to ensure accurate placement.

items.name: Spring Fabric Wreath
items.materialNeeded:
Plain yellow cotton fabric, approximately 12" x 12"
Patterned green cotton fabric, approximately 12" x 12"
Fusible webbing
Craft wire
Wreath base
4 buttons
Pliers
Pinking shears
Iron and ironing board
Leaf and flower templates
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Fusible Applique, Ironing, Fabric Cutting, Decorative Edge Cutting, Wire Twisting, Wire Wrapping, Fabric Flower Construction, Button Attachment, Wreath Assembly
items.keyTechniques: Fusible Webbing Applique, Wire Twisting, Decorative Edge Finishing

steps.title: Prepare the Wire Stems
steps.content: Cut 10 pieces of craft wire, each measuring approximately 8" long. Fold each wire in half and twist the center to create a small loop at the top of each stem.

tip.text: Use pliers to twist the wire neatly and keep the loop small enough to fit inside the fabric leaf.

steps.title: Trace the Leaf Templates
steps.content: Trace the leaf template 10 times onto fusible webbing. Roughly cut out each traced leaf shape, leaving a small border around the lines.

tip.text: Do not cut directly on the template line yet, as the final trimming is easier after the fabric layers are fused together.

steps.title: Fuse the Leaf Fabric Layers
steps.content: Iron the fusible webbing leaf shapes onto the wrong side of the patterned green fabric. Peel away the paper backing. Place the green fabric onto the yellow fabric with the adhesive side facing down.

tip.text: Use the correct iron temperature for your fabric and fusible webbing to avoid scorching the fabric or damaging the adhesive.

steps.title: Insert the Wire into the Leaves
steps.content: Place one prepared wire stem between the green and yellow fabric layers for each leaf. Position the looped end near the top of the leaf shape and keep the twisted wire centered along the leaf.

tip.text: Make sure the wire does not extend beyond the leaf edge before cutting the shape.

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Leaves
steps.content: Use pinking shears to cut out each leaf shape, trimming just inside the traced outline. The wire should remain enclosed between the two fused fabric layers.

tip.text: Avoid cutting too close to the wire, as this may expose the wire or weaken the edge of the leaf.

steps.title: Create the Leaf Garland
steps.content: Hold two leaves together so the second leaf sits approximately 0.8" below the first leaf. Starting approximately 0.8" below the second leaf, twist the wire stems together. Continue adding the remaining leaves in the same way to create a garland.

tip.text: Alternate the direction of the leaves as you add them to create a fuller and more natural-looking garland.

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Flowers
steps.content: Use the flower templates to cut two small and two large double-sided flowers. Prepare them in the same way as the leaves by fusing the fabric layers together before cutting with pinking shears.

tip.text: Mix patterned and plain fabrics for the flowers to create more contrast on the wreath.

steps.title: Prepare the Button Flower Centers
steps.content: Cut four pieces of craft wire, each measuring approximately 6" long. Thread one or two wire pieces through the holes of each button and twist the wire ends together to create a flower stem.

tip.text: Twist the wire ends securely so the button does not loosen while attaching it to the flower.

steps.title: Attach the Buttons to the Flowers
steps.content: Cut a small slit in the center of each fabric flower. Push the wire stem attached to the button through the center opening. Position the button at the front of the flower and twist the wire behind it to secure.

tip.text: Keep the button centered on the flower before tightening the wire for a balanced finish.

steps.title: Attach the Garland to the Wreath
steps.content: Lay the leaf garland around the wreath base. Use short pieces of craft wire to wrap and secure the garland to the wreath in several places.

tip.text: Do not secure the garland in only one or two places, as it may shift or sag when the wreath is hung.

steps.title: Add the Fabric Flowers
steps.content: Arrange the fabric flowers along the leaf garland. Wrap each flower wire stem around the garland wire to hold it in place. Adjust the leaves and flowers until the wreath looks balanced.

tip.text: Place the larger flowers first, then fill the empty areas with smaller flowers and leaves.

steps.title: Finish the Wreath
steps.content: Check that all wire ends are tightly twisted and tucked behind the wreath. Adjust the leaves and flowers one final time, then hang the finished spring wreath as a decorative home accessory.

tip.text: Fold sharp wire ends inward or cover them with fabric to prevent scratches when handling the finished wreath.

items.notions: Craft wire, Fusible webbing, Buttons, Pliers, Pinking shears, Iron
items.mainFabric:
Plain yellow cotton fabric
Patterned green cotton fabric
Approximate fabric required: 12" x 12" of each fabric
items.patternCutList:
10 leaf templates
2 small flower templates
2 large flower templates

items.name: Joy Reindeer Embroidery Hoop
items.materialNeeded:
White or cream cotton or linen fabric, at least 12" x 12"
Embroidery hoop, 9"
Six-strand embroidery floss in red, yellow, blue, green, cream, ochre, brown, medium brown, and white
Assorted cup sequins
Ribbon for hanging
Crewel embroidery needle
Pen or pencil
Printed reindeer and lettering template
Light box or window for tracing
Scissors
items.techniques: Fabric Preparation, Template Tracing, Hoop Mounting, Backstitch, Running Stitch, Satin Stitch, Padded Satin Stitch, Split Stitch, Sequin Attachment, Hand Embroidery, Fabric Trimming, Ribbon Hanging Finish
items.keyTechniques: Padded Satin Stitch, Backstitch, Running Stitch, Satin Stitch

steps.title: Prepare the Fabric
steps.content: Choose a piece of white or cream cotton or linen fabric measuring at least 12" x 12". Wash and press the fabric before starting to soften it and remove any creases.

tip.text: Natural cotton or linen fabrics are easier to embroider than slippery synthetic fabrics.

steps.title: Trace the Design
steps.content: Print the reindeer and lettering template. Tape the template to a light box or a bright window, place the fabric over it, and trace the design lightly with a pen or pencil.

tip.text: Use a light touch when tracing so the guide lines do not remain visible outside the embroidery stitches.

steps.title: Place the Fabric in the Hoop
steps.content: Place the traced fabric into a 9" embroidery hoop. Position the design in the center and tighten the hoop screw until the fabric is smooth and firm.

tip.text: The fabric should be taut like a drum, but avoid pulling it so tightly that the design becomes distorted.

steps.title: Thread the Needle
steps.content: Thread a crewel needle with two strands separated from six-strand embroidery floss. Choose thread colors that match the reindeer, trees, lettering, and decorative details.

tip.text: Separate the floss strands one at a time, then place them together again to reduce tangling.

steps.title: Outline the Main Shapes
steps.content: Use backstitch with two strands of embroidery floss to outline the larger parts of the reindeer, including the body, legs, antlers, head, and blanket. Outline the trees, stars, and lettering in their chosen colors.

tip.text: Keep the backstitches short and even, especially around curved areas such as the reindeer head and antlers.

steps.title: Add Running Stitch Padding
steps.content: Fill the areas that will use padded satin stitch with evenly spaced running stitches. Work the foundation stitches inside the drawn outlines and keep them in one direction.

tip.text: For narrow embroidered areas, a single layer of running stitch is enough to create a subtle raised effect.

steps.title: Embroider the Reindeer with Satin Stitch
steps.content: Use three strands of embroidery floss to cover the padded areas with satin stitch. Stitch over the running stitch foundation to fill the reindeer body, legs, neck, head, antlers, and decorative blanket sections.

tip.text: Do not make satin stitches too long, as long stitches can snag or loosen during use.

steps.title: Stitch the Lettering and Stars
steps.content: Embroider the words "Joy to the World" using satin stitch or padded satin stitch. Add the decorative stars using satin stitch in yellow embroidery floss.

tip.text: Work one letter at a time and keep the satin stitches close together for smooth, solid lettering.

steps.title: Embroider the Trees and Ground Lines
steps.content: Use green embroidery floss to stitch the trees and brown floss to stitch the curved ground lines. Use backstitch or split stitch for the fine outlines and details.

tip.text: Split stitch is useful for small curves because it creates a smooth continuous line.

steps.title: Add the Reindeer Facial Details
steps.content: Use black or dark brown embroidery floss to add the eye, nostril, mouth, and other small facial details. Add small decorative stitches to the reindeer blanket if desired.

tip.text: Use only one or two strands of floss for facial details so they do not overpower the embroidered design.

steps.title: Attach the Sequins
steps.content: Sew assorted cup sequins onto the reindeer antlers to create Christmas baubles. Use a single strand of embroidery floss and secure each sequin with a small stitch through its center.

tip.text: Keep the sequins spaced apart so the antlers remain visible and the decoration does not look crowded.

steps.title: Add the Bauble Strings
steps.content: Use backstitch or split stitch to embroider straight lines from the antlers to the sequins, creating the appearance of hanging Christmas baubles.

tip.text: Use a fine thread color that contrasts gently with the antlers so the hanging lines remain visible.

steps.title: Trim the Excess Fabric
steps.content: Keep the embroidery inside the hoop. Trim away the excess fabric around the outer edge, leaving an even margin of approximately 1.2" around the hoop.

tip.text: Cut slowly around the hoop and keep the scissors away from the embroidery stitches.

steps.title: Finish the Back of the Hoop
steps.content: Fold the remaining fabric margin toward the back of the hoop. Tuck the fabric neatly behind the frame and secure it with a few hand stitches if needed.

tip.text: Distribute the folded fabric evenly around the back so the hoop sits flat against the wall.

steps.title: Add the Hanging Ribbon
steps.content: Tie a ribbon around the brass screw at the top of the embroidery hoop. Make a neat bow or loop for hanging, then adjust the fabric and embroidery one final time.

tip.text: Choose a ribbon color that matches the embroidered lettering for a coordinated Christmas display.

items.notions: Embroidery hoop, Crewel needle, Six-strand embroidery floss, Cup sequins, Ribbon, Pen or pencil, Scissors
items.mainFabric:
White or cream cotton or linen fabric
Minimum size: 12" x 12"
items.patternCutList:
1 reindeer embroidery template
1 "Joy to the World" lettering template
2 tree motifs
3 star motifs

items.name: Floral Hexagon Tea Cosy
items.materialNeeded: Fabric, cotton: turquoise floral, 18in square; yellow floral, 18in square; bright pink, 10in square. Lining fabric, 12in x 14in. Wadding (batting), 12in x 14in. Bias binding, 40in. Paper hexagon templates: 24 yellow, 28 turquoise, 23 pink. Hand-sewing thread. Sewing thread. Air-erasable fabric pen. Pins. Iron and pressing cloth. 5mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: English Paper Piecing, Hand Sewing, Quilting, Machine Sewing, Binding, Pressing

steps.title: Prepare and Cut the Hexagons
steps.content: Print the template sheet and cut out all paper hexagons. Cut 28 turquoise fabric hexagons, 24 yellow fabric hexagons and 23 pink fabric hexagons, leaving a 5mm seam allowance around each shape. Wrap the fabric around the paper templates and secure with tacking stitches.

tip.text: Inaccurate seam allowances will affect the final alignment of the patchwork and make the tea cosy difficult to assemble.

steps.title: Arrange the Patchwork Layout
