steps.content: Lay out the covered hexagons according to the template diagram, creating the floral pattern with turquoise centres, yellow petals and pink flower sections. Check the arrangement carefully before sewing.

tip.text: Mixing up the colour placement can disrupt the flower motifs and make the finished design look uneven.

steps.title: Join the Hexagons
steps.content: Place two hexagons right sides together and stitch along the edges using ladder stitch or whip stitch. Continue joining the pieces until the complete patchwork panel is formed.

tip.text: Use small, evenly spaced stitches to create nearly invisible joins between the hexagons.

steps.title: Remove Templates and Press
steps.content: Once the patchwork panel is complete, carefully remove all paper templates and any tacking stitches. Press the patchwork from the wrong side to flatten the seams.

tip.text: Do not remove the paper templates before all adjoining seams are sewn, or the shapes may distort.

steps.title: Quilt the Front Panel
steps.content: Place the patchwork panel on top of the wadding. Pin the layers together and trace around the design using an air-erasable pen. Hand quilt around the flower centres, petals and outer edges of the motifs.

tip.text: Quilting close to the patchwork seams helps define the floral design and adds structure to the tea cosy.

steps.title: Trim and Bind the Patchwork Edge
steps.content: Trim the quilted panel to the tea cosy shape. Attach bias binding around the curved outer edge to conceal the raw fabric edges.

tip.text: Pulling the binding too tightly around curves can cause puckering along the finished edge.

steps.title: Prepare the Lining
steps.content: Cut matching lining pieces using the tea cosy template. Place the lining pieces right sides together and stitch along the curved top edge. Press the seam open.

tip.text: Pressing the seam open reduces bulk and helps the tea cosy sit smoothly over the teapot.

steps.title: Assemble the Tea Cosy
steps.content: Place the quilted outer section and lining right sides together. Position the wadding between the layers and align all edges. Stitch around the curved upper edge with a 0.39 inches seam allowance.

tip.text: Ensure the wadding remains flat and does not shift during sewing, or the tea cosy may become lumpy.

steps.title: Turn and Press
steps.content: Turn the tea cosy right side out through the bottom opening. Carefully shape the curved top seam and press the entire project.

tip.text: Use a point turner or blunt tool to smooth the curved edges without stretching the fabric.

steps.title: Finish the Lower Edge
steps.content: Fold the seam allowance at the lower opening inward. Attach bias binding around the bottom edge of the tea cosy, enclosing all raw edges and securing the lining neatly inside.

tip.text: If the lining is not evenly distributed before binding, it may bunch up inside the finished tea cosy.

steps.title: Final Press and Shape
steps.content: Give the tea cosy a final press and place it over a teapot to shape. Check all seams and bindings for security before use.

tip.text: A final steam press helps the quilted patchwork settle into its finished shape and improves the overall appearance.

items.name: Fruit Quilt Coasters
items.finishedSize: Pear coaster: 5.9 x 7.9 inches; Apple coaster: 5.9 x 5.9 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Plain cotton fabric, 6 inches x 8 inches (pear), 6 inches square (apple).
Patterned cotton fabric, 6 inches square.
Contrast patterned cotton fabric, 3¼ inches x 8¼ inches (pear), 3¼ inches x 6¼ inches (apple).
Cotton fabric scraps for patchwork hexagons.
Wadding/Batting, 6 inches x 8 inches (pear), 6 inches square (apple).
Paper hexagon templates.
Bias binding strips.
Sewing thread, matching colors.
Pins.
Fabric marking pen.
Iron.
Hand sewing needle.
5mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: English Paper Piecing (EPP), Patchwork, Applique, Hand Sewing, Quilting, Bias Binding, Topstitching

steps.title: Prepare the Hexagon Templates
steps.content: Download and print the fruit coaster templates. Cut out all paper hexagons and wrap fabric around each template, folding the seam allowance neatly to the back. Secure with temporary stitches or fabric glue.

tip.text: Keep seam allowances evenly folded on every hexagon to ensure the patchwork fits together accurately.

steps.title: Assemble the Fruit Patchwork
steps.content: Arrange the covered hexagons according to the pear or apple layout shown on the template. Hand stitch neighboring hexagons together using small whip stitches or ladder stitches until the complete fruit motif is formed.

tip.text: Pulling stitches too tightly can distort the hexagon shapes and cause the patchwork to pucker.

steps.title: Remove Templates and Press
steps.content: Carefully remove the paper templates from the finished patchwork sections. Press the patchwork flat from the wrong side to set the seams and smooth the surface.

tip.text: Use steam sparingly when pressing to avoid stretching the patchwork.

steps.title: Prepare the Background Fabric
steps.content: Cut the background fabric according to the coaster dimensions. Position the completed fruit motif in the center of the background fabric and pin it securely in place.

tip.text: Measure equal spacing around the motif before pinning to keep the design centered.

steps.title: Applique the Fruit Motif
steps.content: Attach the fruit patchwork to the background fabric using small slip stitches or applique stitches around the outer edges. Add any leaf pieces and decorative details as required.

tip.text: Check that all paper templates have been removed before stitching the applique permanently.

steps.title: Layer the Coaster
steps.content: Place the background fabric, wadding, and backing fabric together to create a quilt sandwich. Pin or baste all layers securely to prevent shifting.

tip.text: Insufficient pinning can cause the layers to move during quilting, resulting in uneven edges.

steps.title: Quilt the Design
steps.content: Quilt around the fruit motif and along selected patchwork seams to secure all layers together. Maintain consistent stitch length for a professional finish.

tip.text: Stitch close to the patchwork seams to highlight the fruit design while keeping quilting subtle.

steps.title: Trim the Coaster
steps.content: Trim away excess wadding and backing fabric so all layers are even and the coaster matches the finished dimensions.

tip.text: Avoid trimming too close to the stitching line, which can weaken the finished edge.

steps.title: Apply Bias Binding
steps.content: Attach bias binding around the entire edge of the coaster. Fold neatly around corners and curves, then stitch the binding securely in place.

tip.text: Press the bias binding before sewing to achieve smoother curves around the pear-shaped coaster.

steps.title: Finish and Press
steps.content: Inspect all stitching, trim loose threads, and give the coaster a final press. Ensure the binding is secure and the coaster lies flat.

tip.text: Skipping the final press can make even a well-made coaster look unfinished.

items.name: Pocketed Machine Cover
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, cotton: plain orange; plain turquoise; floral print pink; floral print blue; blue crosshatch print.
Cotton wadding.
Lightweight iron-on interfacing.
Cotton tape: royal blue, 3/8 inch wide.
Decorative tape, 3/4 inch wide.
Embroidery thread.
Matching sewing thread.
Tissue paper.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Iron and pressing tools.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork, Machine Sewing, Topstitching, Free-Motion Embroidery, Pocket Construction, Quilting, Turning and Pressing, Edge Stitching

steps.title: Prepare the Main Cover Front
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut one square of plain orange cotton and one rectangle of plain turquoise cotton according to the pattern measurements. Stitch the turquoise section to the lower edge of the orange panel with right sides together. Press the seam open and position decorative tape over the seam line. Topstitch both sides of the tape to secure it.

tip.text: Press the seam open thoroughly before attaching the decorative tape to achieve a smooth professional finish.

steps.title: Construct the Large Front Pocket
steps.content: Cut the floral print and blue crosshatch pocket sections according to the pattern. Join the fabric sections with right sides together and press the seams open. Fold the upper edge twice to create a narrow hem and stitch in place. Add decorative tape across the pocket front and secure with topstitching.

tip.text: Failing to press the seams open can create bulky areas that make the pocket sit unevenly on the cover.

steps.title: Create the Small Tool Pocket
steps.content: Cut the smaller floral pocket piece and finish the upper edge with a double-fold hem. Apply decorative tape near the top edge and stitch securely. Press flat and set aside for assembly.

tip.text: Use a shorter stitch length when sewing pocket openings to improve durability during daily use.

steps.title: Attach the Pocket Sections
steps.content: Position the large pocket on the lower section of the main cover front. Stitch along both sides and the bottom edge. Place the smaller pocket on top of the large pocket and stitch vertical divisions to create separate storage compartments for sewing tools and accessories.

tip.text: Measure the width of your tools before sewing the pocket dividers to ensure each compartment is usable.

steps.title: Transfer and Stitch the Embroidery Design
steps.content: Trace the decorative lettering design onto tissue paper and position it across the turquoise panel. Stitch over the design using free-motion embroidery. Carefully remove the tissue paper and add decorative floral motifs around the lettering as desired.

tip.text: Pulling the tissue paper away too quickly can distort delicate embroidery stitches.

steps.title: Create the Outer Cover
steps.content: Place the completed front panel and the back cover panel right sides together. Layer cotton wadding behind the outer fabric if required by the pattern. Stitch around the sides and top edge, leaving the lower edge open. Trim the seam allowances and clip the corners.

tip.text: Trim excess bulk from corners before turning to achieve sharper and cleaner edges.

steps.title: Turn and Press the Cover
steps.content: Turn the cover right side out through the opening. Carefully push out all corners and curved edges. Press the entire cover flat and topstitch around the outer edges for additional structure and durability.

tip.text: Avoid using sharp scissors to push corners out, as they can accidentally pierce the fabric.

steps.title: Construct the Fabric Storage Bin
steps.content: Cut two square pieces of fabric and one matching piece of interfacing. Fuse the interfacing to the lining fabric. Stitch the pieces together, leaving an opening for turning. Turn right side out, press, and create boxed corners by sewing across each corner according to the pattern markings.

tip.text: Accurate boxed corners will help the storage bin maintain its shape when filled.

steps.title: Attach the Storage Bin
steps.content: Position the finished fabric bin on the lower left section of the machine cover. Stitch securely along the sides and bottom edges while leaving the upper edge open. Reinforce stress points with additional stitching.

tip.text: This pocket may hold heavier items, so reinforce the upper corners with extra backstitching.

steps.title: Add the Side Ties
steps.content: Cut two lengths of royal blue cotton tape. Fold each length in half and insert them into the side seams of the cover at the marked positions. Stitch securely so they can be tied around the sewing machine to hold the cover in place.

tip.text: Uneven tie placement can cause the cover to sit crookedly on the sewing machine.

steps.title: Prepare and Insert the Lining
steps.content: Cut the lining pieces from orange cotton and lightweight interfacing. Assemble the lining, leaving a turning gap. Insert the lining into the outer cover with right sides together and stitch around the lower edge. Turn the cover through the opening and press thoroughly.

tip.text: Grade bulky seam allowances before turning to reduce thickness along the lower edge.

steps.title: Finish the Machine Cover
steps.content: Close the turning gap with topstitching or hand stitching. Give the cover a final press and check all pocket seams, embroidery, and ties for security. Fit the cover over the sewing machine and adjust the ties as needed.

tip.text: A final press using steam helps the cover hold its shape and gives the project a polished appearance.

items.name: Coffee Pot Table Quilt
items.finishedSize: Coffee Pot Block: 8 inches square; Table Centrepiece: 24 inches square
items.materialNeeded: Fabric, assorted cotton prints for coffee pot and background. Freezer paper. Wax-coated baking paper. White cotton fabric, 24 inches x 24 inches. Quilt batting/wadding, 24 inches x 24 inches. Sewing thread. Embroidery thread. Fabric marking pencil. Pins. Iron and ironing board. Rotary cutter or fabric scissors. Quilting ruler.
items.techniques: Foundation Piecing, Patchwork, Machine Sewing, Pressing, Quilting, Topstitching, Binding

steps.title: Prepare the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the coffee pot block template. Trace each template piece onto freezer paper and cut out accurately. Label all sections according to the pattern guide.

tip.text: Keep template pieces organized in sewing order to make assembly much easier.

steps.title: Cut Fabric for Foundation Piecing
steps.content: Choose coordinating fabrics for the coffee pot, lid, handle and background sections. Cut each fabric piece approximately 1/2 inch larger than its corresponding template piece.

tip.text: Cutting fabric too close to the template can leave insufficient seam allowance after trimming.

steps.title: Create the Coffee Pot Unit
steps.content: Using the freezer paper piecing method, stitch the coffee pot sections together in sequence. Press each seam before adding the next section and trim excess fabric as required.

tip.text: Press seams immediately after stitching to keep the block flat and accurate.

steps.title: Complete the Background Sections
steps.content: Attach the surrounding background pieces to the coffee pot unit. Continue piecing until the entire block is assembled according to the template layout.

tip.text: Check the orientation of each background piece before sewing, as reversing a section can distort the design.

steps.title: Remove the Freezer Paper
steps.content: Carefully peel away the freezer paper from the back of the block. Press the completed block flat and square it to 8 inches.

tip.text: Pulling freezer paper too quickly may stretch seams or distort the block shape.

steps.title: Make Additional Blocks
steps.content: Repeat the process until you have four matching coffee pot blocks for the table centrepiece arrangement.

tip.text: Measure every finished block before assembly to ensure consistent sizing.

steps.title: Arrange the Centrepiece Layout
steps.content: Lay out the coffee pot blocks together with the background squares shown in the design. Adjust placement until all blocks are aligned and evenly spaced.

tip.text: Take a reference photo before sewing so the arrangement remains consistent.

steps.title: Join the Patchwork Top
steps.content: Sew the blocks and background sections into rows. Join the rows together to create the complete table centrepiece top. Press all seams flat.

tip.text: Uneven seam allowances can cause points and corners to misalign.

steps.title: Add the Border Sections
steps.content: Construct the decorative border units and stitch them around the outer edges of the patchwork centrepiece. Press seams carefully after each addition.

tip.text: Measure opposite sides before attaching borders to prevent rippling along the edges.

steps.title: Create the Quilt Sandwich
steps.content: Layer the backing fabric right side down, place the batting on top, and position the patchwork top right side up. Smooth all layers and secure with pins or basting stitches.

tip.text: Work from the center outward when pinning to eliminate wrinkles between layers.

steps.title: Quilt the Centrepiece
steps.content: Machine quilt around the coffee pot motifs and along selected patchwork seams to secure all layers together. Remove basting pins as you sew.

tip.text: A walking foot helps feed multiple layers evenly and reduces fabric shifting.

steps.title: Bind and Finish
steps.content: Trim the excess batting and backing. Prepare binding strips and stitch them around the outer edge. Fold the binding to the back and hand stitch or machine stitch in place to finish the centrepiece.

tip.text: Take extra care when forming mitered corners to achieve a professional finish.

items.name: Stag Head Wall Decor
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric, assorted prints.
Felt: brown, orange and peach.
Polyester fibre filling.
Quilt wadding, scrap.
Embroidery hoop, 8 inches diameter.
Thick mount board.
Buttons, ¼ inch diameter, two.
Sewing thread, matching colours.
Embroidery thread, black.
Fabric marker or chalk.
5mm seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Template Tracing, Machine Sewing, Curved Seam Sewing, Stuffing, Hand Sewing, Ladder Stitch, Slip Stitch, Embroidery, Button Attachment, Soft Sculpture Construction

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Print and cut out the stag head templates. Cut the head, neck and body sections from assorted cotton fabrics, adding a 5mm seam allowance. Ensure mirrored pieces are cut where required and transfer all markings.

tip.text: Label mirrored pieces immediately after cutting to prevent assembly errors later.

steps.title: Construct the Head
steps.content: Pin the head sections right sides together and stitch along the marked seam lines, leaving the neck opening unstitched. Clip curved seams, turn right side out and press lightly.

tip.text: Skipping seam clipping can cause puckering around the curved areas of the head.

steps.title: Prepare the Mounting Base
steps.content: Separate the embroidery hoop and trace the inner ring onto thick mount board. Cut out the circle and cover it with a layer of quilt wadding to create a padded mounting surface.

tip.text: Keep the wadding smooth and wrinkle-free for a cleaner finished appearance.

steps.title: Attach the Neck to the Base
steps.content: Position the padded mount board inside the neck section. Fold the raw fabric edges inward and hand stitch neatly around the board to secure the neck structure.

tip.text: Do not pull the fabric too tightly around the board or the neck shape may become distorted.

steps.title: Create the Main Body
steps.content: Place the body pieces right sides together with a layer of wadding between them. Stitch around the curved edges, leaving the bottom edge open. Clip curves, turn right side out and press.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to shape curves without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Stuff the Body and Head
steps.content: Fill the body and head firmly with polyester fibre filling. Add stuffing gradually and shape the contours evenly, paying special attention to the neck and shoulder areas.

tip.text: Uneven stuffing can make the finished stag head appear lopsided when mounted.

steps.title: Make and Attach the Antlers
steps.content: Cut mirrored antler pieces from felt. Stitch around each pair, leaving an opening for turning and stuffing. Turn right side out, lightly stuff and sew closed. Attach the antlers securely to the top of the head.

tip.text: Check symmetry carefully before permanently sewing the antlers in place.

steps.title: Construct and Insert the Ears
steps.content: Cut ear pieces from contrasting fabrics and sew each pair right sides together. Turn, press and position the ears between the antlers. Secure them with hand stitching.

tip.text: Pressing the ears before attachment helps them hold their shape neatly.

steps.title: Create the Facial Features
steps.content: Cut felt pieces for the muzzle and nose. Stitch them to the face and embroider eyelashes and decorative details using embroidery thread.

tip.text: Pin all facial features first and step back to check balance before sewing.

steps.title: Attach the Eyes
steps.content: Sew two buttons onto the face to create the eyes. Pull the thread firmly through the stuffing to create slight indentations and add dimension to the expression.

tip.text: Ensure both eyes are aligned evenly to avoid an uneven appearance.

steps.title: Close the Openings
steps.content: Use ladder stitch or slip stitch to close all remaining openings. Check the shape and add additional stuffing if required before fully closing.

tip.text: Visible closing stitches can reduce the professional appearance of the finished project.

steps.title: Mount and Finish
steps.content: Attach the completed stag head securely to the embroidery hoop mount. Position the hoop screw at the top so it can be used as a hanging point. Trim loose threads and perform a final inspection.

tip.text: Center the hanging screw at the top of the hoop to keep the stag head balanced on the wall.

items.name: Honeycomb Play Mat
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric, assorted prints, approximately 1 yard total.
Cotton fabric, navy contrast, approximately 1 yard.
Quilt wadding, 36 inches x 31 inches.
Cotton backing fabric, 36 inches x 31 inches.
Matching sewing thread.
Paper templates (hexagon and triangle).
Fabric marker or chalk.
Pins or fabric clips.
 ⅜ inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Template Tracing, English Paper Piecing, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Patchwork Assembly, Quilting, Topstitching, Edge Binding, Pressing

steps.title: Prepare and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Print and cut out the hexagon and triangle templates. Transfer the shapes onto cardstock or template plastic for durability. Use the templates to cut the required number of hexagon and triangle fabric pieces from the assorted cotton prints and navy contrast fabric.

tip.text: Create reusable template pieces from thick cardstock to maintain consistent shape sizes throughout the project.

steps.title: Sew the First Hexagon Units
steps.content: Place two triangle pieces right sides together and stitch along one side of the first hexagon section using a 5mm seam allowance. Continue adding triangle pieces around the curved edge of the hexagon shape until the first unit is complete.

tip.text: Misaligned triangle points can distort the honeycomb pattern and make later assembly difficult.

steps.title: Press and Shape the Units
steps.content: Open out each stitched unit and finger-press the seam allowances toward the darker fabric. Repeat the construction process until all hexagon units have been completed.

tip.text: Consistent pressing direction helps seams nest neatly when joining multiple units together.

steps.title: Arrange the Honeycomb Layout
steps.content: Lay all completed hexagon units on a flat surface. Arrange the colours evenly across the playmat and position the rows so the honeycomb pattern flows naturally from edge to edge.

tip.text: Take a photo of the arrangement before sewing so you can easily recreate the layout if pieces get moved.

steps.title: Join Hexagons into Rows
steps.content: Sew adjacent hexagon units together to form horizontal rows. Match seam intersections carefully and pin generously before stitching to maintain alignment.

tip.text: Stretching bias edges while sewing can cause rows to become uneven and difficult to join.

steps.title: Connect the Rows
steps.content: Join the completed rows together, ensuring the points of the hexagons align accurately. Press all seam allowances flat after each row is attached.

tip.text: Skipping pressing between rows can create bulky seam intersections that affect the finished appearance.

steps.title: Prepare the Quilt Sandwich
steps.content: Lay the backing fabric wrong side up on a flat surface. Position the quilt wadding on top, then place the honeycomb patchwork right side up. Smooth all layers carefully to remove wrinkles.

tip.text: Use temporary spray adhesive or quilting pins to keep the layers from shifting during quilting.

steps.title: Baste the Layers Together
steps.content: Secure all three layers using quilting pins or long hand-basting stitches. Start in the center and work outward to keep the layers evenly distributed.

tip.text: Poor basting can cause puckers or folds to appear in the backing fabric during quilting.

steps.title: Quilt the Playmat
steps.content: Stitch around the edges of the hexagons or follow selected seam lines to quilt the layers together. Continue quilting evenly across the entire surface of the playmat.

tip.text: Stitching in the ditch along existing seams creates a clean finish and hides quilting lines.

steps.title: Trim and Shape the Edges
steps.content: Trim away excess wadding and backing fabric, following the outline of the honeycomb shape. Ensure all sides remain symmetrical and smooth.

tip.text: Cutting too close to the patchwork edge can leave insufficient seam allowance for binding.

steps.title: Apply the Binding
steps.content: Cut binding strips from navy fabric and join them together. Sew the binding around the entire perimeter of the playmat, easing it carefully around corners and angled edges.

tip.text: Pulling the binding too tightly can cause the finished edge to ripple or curl.

steps.title: Finish the Playmat
steps.content: Fold the binding to the back and hand stitch or machine stitch it in place. Give the playmat a final press and inspect all seams to ensure everything is securely finished.

tip.text: A final steam press helps flatten seam bulk and gives the playmat a professional finish.

items.name: Christmas Tree Block
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric A (background): 4 rectangles, 2.36 inches x 3.54 inches.
Fabric B (border fabric): 2 mirrored trapeziums and 5 strips, 0.59 inch x 9.06 inches.
Fabric C (tree center): 1 triangle.
Fabric D (tree top): 1 triangle.
Fabric E (tree trunk): 2 rectangles.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins or sewing clips.
Rotary cutter or fabric scissors.
Quilting ruler.
Iron and ironing board.
Sewing machine.
¼ inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Quilt Block Construction, Straight Stitch Sewing, Pressing Seams, Strip Piecing, Squaring Up Blocks

steps.title: Prepare and Arrange the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Print the template at actual size and cut all fabric pieces according to the pattern. Arrange the tree, trunk, background, and border pieces in their correct positions before sewing.

tip.text: Printing the template with scaling enabled can affect the final block size and cause assembly issues later.

steps.title: Sew the Tree Center Unit
steps.content: Attach the two mirrored trapezium pieces to the left and right sides of the center triangle. Press the seams flat after stitching to maintain accurate block dimensions.

tip.text: Reversing the trapezium orientation can distort the tree shape and prevent the pieces from aligning correctly.

steps.title: Add the Tree Top
steps.content: Position the top triangle above the center unit and sew along the matching edge. Press the seam toward the darker fabric to reduce bulk.

tip.text: Press after every seam instead of waiting until the end to improve piecing accuracy.

steps.title: Construct the Tree Trunk
steps.content: Sew the trunk rectangles together to create the trunk section. Press the seam flat and verify that the trunk width matches the tree center.

tip.text: Uneven trunk pieces can shift the tree off-center within the finished block.

steps.title: Join the Tree and Trunk Sections
steps.content: Attach the trunk unit to the bottom of the tree section. Match center points carefully and sew using a consistent seam allowance.

tip.text: Pin the center points before sewing to keep the trunk perfectly aligned beneath the tree.

steps.title: Attach the Background Pieces
steps.content: Sew the background rectangles around the tree unit according to the layout shown in the pattern. Press each seam before adding the next section.

tip.text: Pulling or stretching the fabric while sewing can cause the quilt block to become skewed.

steps.title: Add the Border Strips
steps.content: Sew the border strips around the completed tree block. Attach each strip with right sides together and press after every seam.

tip.text: Measure the block before cutting border strips to ensure the finished block remains square.

steps.title: Square Up the Block
steps.content: Trim the completed block to approximately 8.7 inches square. Check that all corners are square and the tree motif remains centered.

tip.text: Trim small amounts at a time, as removing too much fabric cannot be corrected later.

items.name: Patchwork Deer Plushy
items.materialNeeded:
Pink patterned cotton, 2 prints.
Mustard patterned cotton, 2 prints.
Turquoise patterned cotton.
Black stranded cotton.
Toy filling.
Crewel needle.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Sewing machine.
Matching sewing thread.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Soft Toy Construction, Curved Seam Sewing, Stuffing, Hand Embroidery, Ladder Stitch, Turning and Clipping Curves

steps.title: Cut and Organize the Patchwork Pieces
steps.content: Cut eight 2.95 inches squares from pink fabrics, eight from mustard fabrics, and six from turquoise fabric. Arrange the squares according to the layout diagram to form the patchwork body design.

tip.text: Lay out all squares before sewing and take a photo for reference so the patchwork pattern stays consistent during assembly.

steps.title: Sew the Patchwork Panels
steps.content: Join the squares into rows, pressing the seams after each row. Sew the rows together to create the complete patchwork panel. Repeat for the required body sections and press all seams flat.

tip.text: Skipping pressing between rows can cause bulky seam intersections and make matching corners difficult.

steps.title: Cut the Body Pieces
steps.content: Pin the template pieces onto the completed patchwork fabric and cut out the deer body sections. Cut any additional body pieces from coordinating fabrics according to the pattern requirements.

tip.text: Ensure the patchwork seams are fully pressed before cutting or the template pieces may become distorted.

steps.title: Embroider the Facial Features
steps.content: Transfer the eye markings onto the head pieces. Satin stitch the eyelashes and embroider the eye details using black stranded cotton. Add any decorative embroidery details shown on the pattern.

tip.text: Use a removable fabric marker for facial placement and check both sides before stitching permanently.

steps.title: Construct the Head Section
steps.content: Match the head pieces with right sides together and sew the facial seams. Clip the curved seam allowances where necessary and turn the head section right side out.

tip.text: Failing to clip curved seams can cause puckering and prevent the head from maintaining its intended shape.

steps.title: Attach the Body Panels
steps.content: Join the body pieces together following the pattern markings. Match patchwork seams carefully and sew around the body, leaving the designated turning opening.

tip.text: Misaligned patchwork seams are highly visible on the finished toy, so pin seam intersections before stitching.

steps.title: Prepare and Attach the Legs
steps.content: Sew the leg pieces together in pairs, matching the seam lines accurately. Attach the completed legs to the body sections according to the pattern instructions.

tip.text: Reduce stitch length slightly around tight curves to achieve smoother leg shapes.

steps.title: Make and Attach the Ears
steps.content: Sew the ear pieces together with right sides facing. Turn the ears right side out, press lightly, and position them between the head layers before completing the head assembly.

tip.text: Placing the ears at different heights can make the finished deer appear uneven.

steps.title: Create the Antlers
steps.content: Sew the antler pieces together, clip the curves, and turn them right side out. Position the antlers according to the pattern and secure them within the head seams.

tip.text: Trim seam allowances carefully around the antler points to avoid excess bulk after turning.

steps.title: Add the Tail
steps.content: Sew the tail pieces together along the outer edge, turn right side out, and lightly stuff. Position the tail between the body layers and secure it during final body assembly.

tip.text: A small amount of stuffing is enough for the tail; overstuffing can make it difficult to sew into the body seam.

steps.title: Turn and Stuff the Deer
steps.content: Turn the deer right side out through the opening. Gradually stuff the legs, body, neck, and head until the toy is evenly filled and holds its shape.

tip.text: Uneven stuffing can cause the deer to lean or become unstable when standing.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Use a ladder stitch or small hand stitches to close the turning opening neatly. Secure the thread and hide the knot inside the toy.

tip.text: A ladder stitch creates an almost invisible closure and gives the toy a professional finish.

steps.title: Finish and Shape the Deer
steps.content: Check all seams, adjust the stuffing where needed, and gently shape the neck, body, and legs. Add any final embroidered details and trim loose threads.

tip.text: Leaving loose threads around embroidered features can become noticeable on the finished toy.

items.name: Aardvark Plush
items.materialNeeded:
Tilda Birdpond collection fabric.
Coloured felt.
Small black beads for eyes.
Toy filling.
Fabric pencil.
Matching sewing thread.
Needle and thread.
Pins.
Tape measure.
Rotary cutter or fabric scissors.
Iron and ironing board.
Acrylic ruler.
5mm seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Soft Toy Construction, Curved Seam Sewing, Gusset Insertion, Hand Sewing, Stuffing, Turning and Clipping Curves, Felt Applique, Bead Attachment

steps.title: Print and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Print the templates at actual size and trace all pattern pieces onto the fabric. Cut thirteen fabric pieces and five felt pieces, transferring all markings accurately.

tip.text: Printing with page scaling enabled will alter the finished size and may prevent matching pieces from fitting together correctly.

steps.title: Prepare the Leg Pieces
steps.content: Fold the gusset and side-leg pieces along the marked centre lines. Transfer all guide marks and fold lines before sewing.

tip.text: Mark notches and fold lines clearly with a removable fabric pencil to make alignment easier during assembly.

steps.title: Attach the Head Gusset
steps.content: Sew one side of the head gusset to one head piece, beginning at the tail and working toward the snout. Repeat for the second head piece, matching the markings carefully.

tip.text: Stretching the gusset while sewing can distort the snout shape and make the head asymmetrical.

steps.title: Join the Body Sections
steps.content: Sew the main body pieces together, leaving the bottom of each leg open. Match all seam intersections carefully and reinforce the seams at stress points.

tip.text: Pin seam intersections before stitching to keep the patchwork design aligned.

steps.title: Sew the Remaining Head Seams
steps.content: Continue sewing the remaining head seams together, starting from the end of the snout and working around the head. Stop at the designated opening.

tip.text: Sewing past the turning opening can make it difficult to turn and stuff the toy later.

steps.title: Clip Curves and Turn Right Side Out
steps.content: Trim seam allowances and clip all curves and corners before turning the toy right side out through the opening.

tip.text: Failing to clip curved seams may cause puckering and prevent the snout from forming smoothly.

steps.title: Prepare the Felt Snout Details
steps.content: Firmly stuff the leg holes. Cut the felt circles required for the snout and prepare the decorative facial details according to the template markings.

tip.text: Cut felt pieces with sharp scissors to achieve clean edges and a neater finished appearance.

steps.title: Attach the Snout Circles
steps.content: Position the felt circles on the snout and hand stitch them securely in place. Ensure both sides are symmetrical before securing the final stitches.

tip.text: Uneven placement of the snout circles can make the facial features appear crooked.

steps.title: Construct the Ears
steps.content: Layer the ear pieces with right sides together and sew around the curved edges. Clip the seam allowances, turn right side out, and press lightly.

tip.text: Avoid trimming too close to the stitching when clipping curves, as this may weaken the seam.

steps.title: Attach the Ears to the Head
steps.content: Fold the ear bases and position them on either side of the head according to the markings. Stitch securely through the head seams.

tip.text: Baste the ears in place before final stitching to ensure both ears are positioned evenly.

steps.title: Stuff the Aardvark
steps.content: Fill the body, legs, neck, and head firmly with toy stuffing. Add small amounts gradually to achieve a smooth and even shape.

tip.text: Overstuffing the snout can distort its shape and place unnecessary strain on the seams.

steps.title: Close the Openings
steps.content: Hand sew all remaining openings using small, neat stitches. Secure the thread ends and hide the knots inside the toy.

tip.text: A ladder stitch provides a nearly invisible finish and is ideal for closing stuffing openings.

steps.title: Add the Eyes
steps.content: Sew the black beads onto the marked eye positions. Pull the thread slightly while securing the beads to create a gentle indentation in the head.

tip.text: Ensure bead eyes are stitched securely with multiple passes to prevent them from loosening over time.

steps.title: Finish the Toy
steps.content: Check all seams, shape the stuffing evenly throughout the body, trim any loose threads, and make final adjustments to the ears and snout.

tip.text: Skipping the final shaping step can leave lumps or uneven areas visible in the finished toy.

items.name: Red Riding Hood Set
items.finishedSize: Quilt: 30 x 30 inches, Doll: 8 inches tall
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric for fairytale prints, 5 pieces, 16.93 inches x 18.90 inches.
Four coordinating prints, 9.84 inches x 13.78 inches.
Pink fabric.
White fabric.
Wadding (batting).
Felt: brown, red, purple, and turquoise.
Toy filling.
Embroidery thread.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Rotary cutter and ruler.
Iron and ironing board.
Sewing machine.
Templates printed at actual size.
0.39 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Quilt Construction, Layering and Quilting, Felt Applique, Hand Embroidery, Soft Doll Construction, Stuffing, Ladder Stitch

steps.title: Print and Prepare the Templates
steps.content: Print all template pieces at 100% or actual size. Cut out the doll templates and prepare all quilt and doll fabrics according to the cutting guide.

tip.text: Printing at an incorrect scale will affect both the doll proportions and the quilt block measurements.

steps.title: Cut the Quilt Fabrics
steps.content: Cut six 3.35 inches squares from the fairytale prints and coordinating fabrics. Cut the required triangles and border strips according to the cutting guide.

tip.text: Use a rotary cutter and quilting ruler for cleaner cuts and more accurate patchwork assembly.

steps.title: Create the Triangle Units
steps.content: Place fairytale-print squares and coordinating fabric squares right sides together. Mark a diagonal line, stitch on both sides, cut along the marked line, and press open to create half-square triangle units.

tip.text: Pressing instead of ironing carefully can stretch the bias edges and distort the triangles.

steps.title: Assemble the Quilt Blocks
steps.content: Arrange the half-square triangle units and remaining squares according to the quilt layout. Sew pieces into rows and then join the rows together.

tip.text: Take a photo of the layout before sewing to avoid accidentally rotating a block during assembly.

steps.title: Add the Quilt Borders
steps.content: Attach the coordinating border strips around the quilt center. Press all seams flat and check that the quilt remains square.

tip.text: Pulling the border strips while sewing can cause the quilt edges to wave.

steps.title: Layer the Quilt Sandwich
steps.content: Lay the backing fabric right side down, place the batting on top, and position the quilt top right side up. Smooth all layers and secure them with pins.

tip.text: Wrinkles trapped inside the layers will remain visible after quilting.

steps.title: Quilt the Layers Together
steps.content: Quilt along selected seam lines or around block shapes to secure the layers. Remove pins as you sew.

tip.text: Start quilting from the center and work outward to minimize puckering.

steps.title: Bind the Quilt
steps.content: Trim the excess batting and backing. Attach binding around the quilt edges and finish by hand or machine.

tip.text: Uneven trimming before binding can make the finished quilt appear crooked.

steps.title: Cut the Doll Pieces
steps.content: Using the printed templates, cut the doll body, dress, hood, legs, shoes, and hair pieces from the specified felt and fabric colors.

tip.text: Keep small felt pieces labeled to avoid mixing similar shapes during assembly.

steps.title: Embroider the Face
steps.content: Transfer the facial markings and embroider the eyes, eyebrows, nose, and mouth using embroidery thread before assembling the head.

tip.text: Completing facial embroidery after assembly is much more difficult and may distort the finished face.

steps.title: Assemble the Head and Hair
steps.content: Attach the hair pieces to the head sections and sew the head components together. Turn right side out if required by the pattern.

tip.text: Use small, even stitches around facial curves for a smoother finished appearance.

steps.title: Construct the Body and Legs
steps.content: Sew the body and leg pieces together according to the template markings. Leave a small opening for stuffing.

tip.text: Do not over-trim seam allowances around narrow areas such as the ankles and neck.

steps.title: Stuff the Doll
steps.content: Turn the doll right side out and fill the body, arms, and legs evenly with toy stuffing until the shape is smooth and firm.

tip.text: Overstuffing can stretch seams and distort the shape of the dress and hood.

steps.title: Attach the Hood and Cape
steps.content: Sew the hood pieces together and attach them to the doll. Position the cape around the shoulders and secure it neatly.

tip.text: Pin the hood in place before stitching to ensure the face opening remains centered.

steps.title: Finish the Doll
steps.content: Close the stuffing opening with ladder stitch, add the shoes and final decorative details, and trim any loose threads.

tip.text: Secure all decorative elements firmly, especially if the doll will be handled frequently.

items.name: Umbrella Drawstring Bag
items.materialNeeded:
Nine cotton print fabrics, 8 squares of each colourway.
Lightweight iron-on interfacing.
Heavyweight cotton or blackout lining, 19.69 inches.
Cotton cord, 59.06 inches.
Coloured felt.
Coloured embroidery thread.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Rotary cutter and ruler.
Iron and ironing board.
Sewing machine.
0.20 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Hand Embroidery, Patchwork Piecing, Quilt Block Construction, Drawstring Channel Construction, Topstitching, Bag Making

steps.title: Print and Prepare the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the umbrella templates at actual size. Cut out the umbrella sections and transfer the shapes onto interfacing and felt as required.

tip.text: Printing templates at the wrong scale will affect the size and alignment of the umbrella applique.

steps.title: Create the Umbrella Applique Pieces
steps.content: Fuse lightweight interfacing to the wrong side of the selected fabrics. Cut the umbrella canopy sections from different fabric prints and prepare the felt umbrella handles.

tip.text: Choose contrasting fabric colours for each umbrella panel to make the applique design stand out.

steps.title: Assemble the Umbrella Canopies
steps.content: Arrange the canopy sections according to the template and stitch them together. Press the seams flat and trim any excess fabric before applying to the background squares.

tip.text: Failing to press seams between sections can make curved umbrella edges appear uneven.

steps.title: Attach the Felt Handles
steps.content: Position a felt umbrella handle beneath each canopy section and secure it with matching embroidery thread using small oversewn stitches.

tip.text: Start the handle from the center of the block to help keep all umbrella motifs aligned.

steps.title: Create Four Umbrella Blocks
steps.content: Arrange and stitch the umbrella motifs onto four background squares. Ensure the umbrellas are positioned consistently to create a balanced overall design.

tip.text: Uneven spacing between umbrellas can make the finished panel appear unbalanced.

steps.title: Construct the Patchwork Panel
steps.content: Cut the remaining print fabrics into squares and arrange them around the umbrella blocks. Sew the pieces into rows and then join the rows together to complete the front panel.

tip.text: Photograph the layout before sewing to avoid accidentally rotating a block during assembly.

steps.title: Prepare the Bag Borders
steps.content: Cut darker print fabric strips and sew them around the completed patchwork panel to create a border. Press all seams carefully.

tip.text: Stretching border strips while sewing can cause the finished bag front to become distorted.

steps.title: Layer the Bag Front
steps.content: Place the patchwork panel right side up on top of the heavyweight lining fabric. Smooth the layers and pin them together securely.

tip.text: Pin at regular intervals across the panel to prevent shifting while quilting.

steps.title: Quilt the Front Panel
steps.content: Quilt around the umbrella motifs and selected seam lines to secure the patchwork panel to the lining layer. Remove pins as you sew.

tip.text: Beginning quilting near the edge can push excess fabric toward the center and create puckers.

steps.title: Trim and Square the Panel
steps.content: Trim the quilted panel to the required size. Ensure all edges are straight and corners are square before constructing the bag.

tip.text: Uneven trimming can cause problems when joining the bag seams later.

steps.title: Sew the Bag Seams
steps.content: Fold the panel right sides together and stitch the side seams. Press the seams open or to one side as preferred.

tip.text: Backstitch at the beginning and end of each seam to strengthen the bag structure.

steps.title: Create the Drawstring Channel
steps.content: Fold the top edge to the inside and stitch to form a channel for the cord. Leave openings for inserting the drawstring.

tip.text: A channel that is too narrow may prevent the cord from moving freely.

steps.title: Insert the Drawstring Cord
steps.content: Thread the cotton cord through the channel using a safety pin or bodkin. Join the cord ends securely to create the drawstring closure.

tip.text: Seal or knot the cord ends immediately after threading to prevent fraying.

steps.title: Finish the Bag
steps.content: Check all seams, remove loose threads, test the drawstring action, and give the finished bag a final press.

tip.text: Avoid pressing directly onto felt applique pieces with high heat, as they may lose their shape.

items.name: Patchwork Turtle Plush
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabric, 15.75 inches x 18.11 inches.
Plain cotton fabric, 18.11 inches.
Toy filling.
Small beads, 2.
Matching sewing thread.
Embroidery thread.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Hand sewing needle.
Sewing machine.
Templates printed at actual size.
0.39 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Soft Toy Construction, Curved Seam Sewing, Hand Embroidery, Stuffing, Turning and Clipping Curves, Ladder Stitch

steps.title: Print and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Print the turtle templates at 100% or actual size. Cut out all template pieces and transfer any markings onto the fabric before cutting.

tip.text: Printing at an incorrect scale will affect the size of the shell pieces and make assembly difficult.

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Cut one shell centre piece, one shell centre panel, one pair of head pieces, one head top piece, four pairs of legs, and one pair of tail pieces from the printed fabric. Cut all remaining pieces from the plain fabric.

tip.text: Cutting paired pieces without checking fabric orientation can result in mismatched legs or tail sections.

steps.title: Construct the Legs
steps.content: Place each pair of leg pieces right sides together and sew around the curved edges, leaving the straight edge open. Clip curves, turn right side out, and lightly stuff.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to gently push out curved areas without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Construct the Tail
steps.content: Sew the tail pieces together around the curved edges, leaving the opening unstitched. Clip the seam allowance, turn right side out, and lightly stuff.

tip.text: Overstuffing the tail can make it difficult to insert neatly into the body seam later.

steps.title: Assemble the Head
steps.content: Place the head pieces right sides together and pin the head top piece between them. Sew along both sides of the head, starting at the forehead and ending at the neck. Clip the curves and turn the head right side out.

tip.text: Clip curved seam allowances carefully to prevent puckering around the turtle's face.

steps.title: Stuff and Finish the Head
steps.content: Lightly stuff the head with toy filling. Sew on two small beads for the eyes or embroider the eyes if preferred.

tip.text: Attach bead eyes securely with multiple passes of thread to prevent them from loosening.

steps.title: Prepare the Shell Top
steps.content: Arrange the shell centre and shell panel pieces according to the template layout. Sew the patchwork shell sections together and press the seams flat.

tip.text: Press each seam as it is sewn to keep the shell shape accurate and reduce bulk.

steps.title: Attach the Head to the Shell
steps.content: Position the completed head at the front opening of the shell top with raw edges aligned. Baste in place to prevent shifting during assembly.

tip.text: Failing to secure the head before assembly can cause it to twist out of position.

steps.title: Attach the Legs and Tail
steps.content: Pin the legs and tail to the shell top, matching the placement marks. Ensure all pieces face inward toward the center of the shell before sewing.

tip.text: Check the orientation of every limb before stitching, as they will be enclosed inside the seams.

steps.title: Join the Shell Top and Bottom
steps.content: Place the shell top and shell underside right sides together. Match all seams and curves carefully, then sew around the entire turtle, leaving a turning gap.

tip.text: Pin frequently around curved sections to maintain smooth shell edges.

steps.title: Clip Curves and Turn Right Side Out
steps.content: Trim excess seam allowance and clip all curved areas before turning the turtle through the opening. Carefully shape the shell and limbs.

tip.text: Do not clip through the stitching line when trimming curved seam allowances.

steps.title: Stuff the Turtle
steps.content: Fill the turtle evenly with toy stuffing, shaping the shell, head, legs, and tail as you work until the toy feels firm but not overstretched.

tip.text: Uneven stuffing can cause the turtle to sit lopsided and distort the shell shape.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Hand stitch the turning gap closed using a ladder stitch or small slip stitches. Secure the thread and hide the knot inside the toy.

tip.text: A ladder stitch creates an almost invisible closure and gives the toy a professional finish.

steps.title: Finish the Turtle
steps.content: Check all seams, adjust the stuffing if necessary, trim loose threads, and shape the shell and limbs to complete the project.

tip.text: Skipping the final shaping step can leave visible lumps and uneven areas in the finished toy.

items.name: Liberty Heart Quilt
items.materialNeeded:
Seven printed fabrics (Liberty Bloomsbury collection or equivalent), cut into 2.17 inch squares.
White cotton sheeting, 47.24 inches x 94.49 inches.
Vilene iron-on quilt wadding, medium weight, 15.75 inch square.
Quilt wadding, 2oz, 47.24 inch square.
Matching sewing thread.
Pins.
Rotary cutter.
Quilting ruler.
Fabric scissors.
Iron and ironing board.
Sewing machine.
Heart template printed at actual size.
0.25 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Half-Square Triangle Construction, Applique, Quilt Top Assembly, Quilting, Border Construction, Binding

steps.title: Cut the Patchwork Squares
steps.content: Select seven printed fabrics and cut ten 2.17 inches squares from each fabric. Mix the fabrics and arrange them so that no identical prints touch when sewn together.

tip.text: Lay out all squares before sewing and adjust the colour balance across the quilt for a more cohesive design.

steps.title: Create the Patchwork Blocks
steps.content: Sew the squares together using a 5mm seam allowance to form patchwork blocks. Press each block and cut into quarters measuring approximately 2.85 inches square.

tip.text: Skipping pressing before cutting can result in uneven quarter blocks and inaccurate measurements.

steps.title: Make the Heart Units
steps.content: Arrange seven patchwork squares and turn them to create a heart shape. Cut one square diagonally from the bottom right to the top left and stitch the sections together to form a complete heart block. Repeat to make four heart units.

tip.text: Keep track of triangle orientation during assembly, as reversing a piece can distort the heart shape.

steps.title: Prepare the Applique Hearts
steps.content: Print the heart template and cut four hearts from iron-on quilt wadding. Position them on the wrong side of the patchwork heart panels, baste around the edges, and press into place.

tip.text: Press from the fabric side using a pressing cloth to avoid shifting the applique layers.

steps.title: Trim and Finish the Heart Panels
steps.content: Trim away excess fabric close to the wadding, clip curved seam allowances, fold under the edges, and tack securely to create neat heart appliques.

tip.text: Large untrimmed seam allowances can create bulky edges that show through the finished quilt.

steps.title: Prepare the Quilt Background Squares
steps.content: Cut four 9.45 inches squares from the white cotton sheeting. Pin a heart applique to the center of each square and oversew it in place.

tip.text: Fold each square in half both ways to locate the exact center before positioning the heart.

steps.title: Create the Patchwork Strips
steps.content: Cut four strips measuring 1.97 inches x 21.65 inches from each of the seven printed fabrics. Sew strips together along the long edges and press seams flat to create patchwork strip panels.

tip.text: Failing to keep seam allowances consistent can cause strip panels to become uneven.

steps.title: Assemble the Quilt Center
steps.content: Join pairs of heart squares using patchwork strips between them. Repeat with the remaining heart squares, then join the sections together to create the quilt center.

tip.text: Pin every seam intersection to keep the patchwork strips aligned across the quilt.

steps.title: Add the First Border
steps.content: Cut white sheeting strips measuring 1.57 inches x 47.24 inches and sew them around the quilt center. Miter or overlap the corners neatly.

tip.text: Stretching long border strips while sewing can cause the quilt edges to ripple.

steps.title: Create the Outer Patchwork Border
steps.content: Cut additional 3.94 inch squares from the printed fabrics and assemble them into patchwork strips. Sew the shorter strips to the sides of the quilt and the longer strips to the top and bottom.

tip.text: Mixing up the side and top border lengths may cause the quilt to lose its square shape.

steps.title: Add the Final Border
steps.content: Construct another border using remaining patchwork squares and attach it around the quilt perimeter. Press all seams carefully after stitching.

tip.text: Measure the quilt through the center before cutting border pieces for a flatter finished result.

steps.title: Prepare the Quilt Sandwich
steps.content: Lay the backing fabric on a flat surface, place the quilt wadding on top, and position the quilt top right side up. Smooth all layers and pin thoroughly from the center outward.

tip.text: Wrinkles trapped inside the quilt sandwich will remain visible after quilting.

steps.title: Quilt the Layers
steps.content: Quilt along seam lines beginning at the center and working outward. Continue until all layers are secured and the quilt surface lies flat.

tip.text: Starting in the center helps distribute excess fabric evenly and reduces puckering.

steps.title: Trim the Quilt
steps.content: Trim away excess backing fabric and batting after quilting. Square the corners and ensure all edges are even.

tip.text: Trimming before quilting is complete can leave insufficient fabric for final finishing.

steps.title: Prepare and Attach the Binding
steps.content: Cut the remaining white sheeting into 1.57 inches-wide strips and join them end to end to create a continuous binding strip. Sew the binding to the front of the quilt.

tip.text: Join binding strips with diagonal seams to reduce bulk at the joins.

steps.title: Finish the Quilt
steps.content: Fold the binding over to the back of the quilt, miter the corners neatly, and hand stitch the binding in place. Give the quilt a final press and remove any loose threads.

tip.text: Take extra care when mitering corners, as uneven folds are highly noticeable on a finished quilt.

items.name: Happy Hexagon Quilt
items.materialNeeded:
Honeycomb Moda Happy-Go-Lucky by Bonnie & Camille fabric pack (40 hexagons).
Green gingham fabric, 27.56 inches x 59.06 inches.
White cotton sheeting, 59.06 inches x 90.55 inches.
Vilene Quickscreen Triangle interfacing, 1m.
Lightweight quilt wadding, 43.31 inches x 59.06 inches.
Matching sewing thread.
Hand sewing needle.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Rotary cutter and ruler.
Iron and ironing board.
Sewing machine.
Printed hexagon templates (optional alternative to interfacing method).
5mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: English Paper Piecing, Hexagon Piecing, Hand Stitching, Quilt Top Assembly, Quilting, Border Construction, Mitred Corners, Binding

steps.title: Prepare the Hexagon Templates
steps.content: Use Vilene Quickscreen Triangle interfacing or printed templates to create hexagons measuring approximately 4.72 inches across and matching white triangles with 6.1.97 inches sides.

tip.text: Prepare all templates before cutting fabric to keep the piecing process organized and efficient.

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Hexagons
steps.content: Cut fabric hexagons from the Honeycomb Moda prints, leaving approximately 0.39 inches seam allowance around each shape for wrapping and tacking.

tip.text: Insufficient seam allowance makes it difficult to fold fabric neatly around the template edges.

steps.title: Cover the Templates
steps.content: Fold the fabric seam allowances over the template edges and tack them securely in place. Trim excess bulk from the corners where necessary.

tip.text: Use fine tacking stitches that are easy to remove later without distorting the fabric.

steps.title: Prepare the White Triangle Units
steps.content: Cut white sheeting strips and attach interfacing triangles, leaving a small gap between each shape. Fold the seam allowances over the templates and tack in place.

tip.text: Ensure all triangle points remain sharp and accurate, as small errors become noticeable across the quilt.

steps.title: Plan the Quilt Layout
steps.content: Arrange the hexagons in alternating rows of six and seven shapes. Distribute colours and prints evenly so that identical fabrics do not touch.

tip.text: Take a photo of the layout before sewing to preserve the arrangement.

steps.title: Insert the White Triangles
steps.content: Place a white triangle between each hexagon and along the quilt edges to create the distinctive honeycomb pattern and zigzag outline.

tip.text: Misplacing a triangle can interrupt the geometric flow of the honeycomb design.

steps.title: Hand Stitch the First Section
steps.content: Place adjacent shapes right sides together and join them using small oversewing stitches along the template edges.

tip.text: Use short, consistent stitches to create nearly invisible joins on the front of the quilt.

steps.title: Continue Joining the Hexagons
steps.content: Sew additional rows together until the entire central honeycomb panel is completed. Remove tacking stitches and templates as sections are finished.

tip.text: Do not remove templates too early, as the shapes may lose their accuracy before surrounding seams are sewn.

steps.title: Create the Zigzag Border Units
steps.content: Cut rhomboid interfacing pieces and cover them with green gingham fabric. Assemble them into alternating angled sections to form the quilt's zigzag edge.

tip.text: Incorrect rhomboid orientation can disrupt the alternating zigzag effect.

steps.title: Construct the Top and Bottom Borders
steps.content: Fold and prepare long gingham strips, then attach them to the top and bottom edges of the quilt to create straight finishing borders.

tip.text: Measure the quilt center before cutting border strips to prevent waviness.

steps.title: Press and Remove Templates
steps.content: Remove all remaining interfacing pieces and tacking stitches. Press the completed patchwork panel from the reverse side using steam.

tip.text: Avoid excessive heat on unfinished seams, which may stretch bias edges.

steps.title: Prepare the Quilt Sandwich
steps.content: Lay the backing fabric flat, position the quilt wadding on top, and center the patchwork quilt top over the batting. Pin all layers securely.

tip.text: Work from the center outward when pinning to eliminate wrinkles between layers.

steps.title: Trim the Batting and Backing
steps.content: Trim the quilt batting to match the quilt top dimensions. Cut the backing fabric approximately 0.79 inches larger around all edges.

tip.text: Cutting the backing too small can leave insufficient fabric for the final finishing step.

steps.title: Quilt the Layers Together
steps.content: Hand quilt through all layers using the edges of the hexagons as stitching guides. Continue quilting around the inside edge of the gingham border.

tip.text: Mark quilting lines lightly before stitching to maintain consistent spacing.

steps.title: Finish the Quilt Edges
steps.content: Fold the backing fabric over the quilt edge, mitering and clipping corners where required. Pin and stitch the folded backing securely around the quilt.

tip.text: Take extra care when mitering corners, as bulky folds are difficult to correct once stitched.

steps.title: Final Press and Inspection
steps.content: Remove loose threads, inspect all quilting stitches, and give the completed quilt a final press to flatten seams and borders.

tip.text: Allow the quilt to cool completely after pressing before folding or storing.

items.name: Embroidered Memory Album
items.materialNeeded:
Grey linen fabric, 11.42 inches x 31.97 inches.
Embroidery threads: black, dark grey, grey, mid-grey, light grey, very light grey, white, and red.
Blumenthal PhotoFabric cotton poplin, A4 sheet.
Red wool felt scraps.
Embroidery hoop.
Crewel embroidery needle.
Hand sewing needle.
White sewing thread.
Thick cardboard: one piece 20.39 inches x 27.0.79 inches, one piece 20.39 inches x 9.45 inches, and one piece 1.18 inches x 20.39 inches.
Thick paper, 25-30 sheets.
Lightweight quilt wadding, 20.39 inches x 27.0.79 inches.
Carpet tape, 1.97 inches wide.
Fabric glue (optional).
Three brass bookbinding posts, 1.18 inches.
Three-hole punch.
Erasable fabric pen or pencil.
Pins.
Fabric scissors.
Iron and ironing board.
items.techniques: Photo Embroidery, Split Stitch Embroidery, Satin Stitch, Running Stitch, Back Stitch, Felt Applique, Bookbinding, Fabric Cover Construction, Hand Sewing

steps.title: Prepare the Photograph
steps.content: Photocopy or print the selected photograph onto PhotoFabric following the manufacturer's instructions. Aim for an image approximately 3.54 inches x 10.39 inches including a narrow border.

tip.text: Always print a test image first to ensure the photograph fits correctly within the album cover layout.

steps.title: Mount the Photo in an Embroidery Hoop
steps.content: Remove the backing paper from the PhotoFabric and place it securely in an embroidery hoop so the image remains taut while stitching.

tip.text: Keep the fabric evenly tensioned to prevent puckering while embroidering detailed areas.

steps.title: Embroider the Photograph
steps.content: Using split stitch, embroider over the printed photograph. Use two strands of embroidery floss for larger shaded areas and a single strand for fine details and facial features.

tip.text: Using thick thread for small facial details can obscure important features and reduce image clarity.

steps.title: Add a Satin Stitch Border
steps.content: Work a narrow satin stitch border around the edge of the embroidered image using two strands of white embroidery thread.

tip.text: Keep satin stitches parallel and evenly tensioned for a clean framed appearance.

steps.title: Trim and Press the Embroidered Photo
steps.content: Remove the photograph from the hoop and lightly press it from the reverse side. Trim the fabric, leaving approximately 0.39 inches border around the image.

tip.text: Press from the wrong side only to avoid flattening the embroidery texture.

steps.title: Transfer the Title Design
steps.content: Download and trace the 'Photographs' template onto the linen fabric. Position the design near the lower section of the cover according to the layout.

tip.text: Use an erasable fabric marker so any visible guidelines can be removed later.

steps.title: Embroider the Album Title
steps.content: Place the linen fabric in an embroidery hoop and stitch the lettering using split stitch with two strands of red embroidery thread.

tip.text: Pulling stitches too tightly can cause the linen fabric to distort around the lettering.

steps.title: Attach the Embroidered Photograph
steps.content: Position the embroidered photograph on the linen cover fabric. Turn under the raw edges and slip stitch it securely into place using white sewing thread.

tip.text: Pin all four corners first to keep the photograph square while stitching.

steps.title: Decorate the Background
steps.content: Work lines of running stitch around the embroidered photograph to enhance the design and secure all fabric layers together.

tip.text: Uneven running stitches can distract from the embroidered photograph instead of complementing it.

steps.title: Create Felt Photo Corners
steps.content: Cut four right-angled triangles from red felt. Cut a small notch in each piece to form decorative photo corners and edge them with slip stitching.

tip.text: Use sharp embroidery scissors to create crisp, symmetrical felt corners.

steps.title: Add Decorative Back Stitching
steps.content: Sew a line of back stitch down the center of each felt corner using red embroidery thread to mimic traditional photo album corners.

tip.text: Mark the center line lightly before stitching to keep the design symmetrical.

steps.title: Prepare the Album Covers
steps.content: Cut thick cardboard into one 20.39 inches x 27.0.79 inches piece, one 20.39 inches x 9.45 inches piece, and one 1.18 inches x 20.39 inches spine piece. Punch three evenly spaced holes through the spine section.

tip.text: Accurate hole placement is essential to ensure the pages align correctly after assembly.

steps.title: Create the Album Hinge
steps.content: Align the cardboard sections with a small gap between them and apply carpet tape across the gap to create a flexible hinge for the album cover.

tip.text: A hinge gap that is too narrow can prevent the album from opening smoothly.

steps.title: Cover the Cardboard Structure
steps.content: Place quilt wadding over the cover board and position the embroidered linen on top. Fold the fabric edges to the reverse side and secure them with glue or stitching.

tip.text: Smooth the linen carefully before securing to avoid visible wrinkles on the finished cover.

steps.title: Add the Inner Lining
steps.content: Cut thick paper to size and glue it neatly inside the cover to conceal the folded fabric edges and create a clean interior finish.

tip.text: Applying too much glue may cause the paper lining to wrinkle.

steps.title: Prepare the Album Pages
steps.content: Cut the album pages from thick paper and punch matching holes in each sheet. Prepare additional spacer strips if extra room is needed for photographs.

tip.text: Punch all pages together in small stacks to keep the holes perfectly aligned.

steps.title: Install the Binding Posts
steps.content: Insert the brass bookbinding posts through the back cover and stack the album pages onto the posts. Add spacer strips if desired.

tip.text: Do not overfill the album, as excessive thickness may strain the binding posts.

steps.title: Attach the Front Cover
steps.content: Position the embroidered front cover over the binding posts and secure it with the screw caps.

tip.text: Tighten all posts evenly to keep the album cover sitting flat.

steps.title: Finish the Album
steps.content: Check page movement, trim any loose threads, remove visible markings, and lightly press the fabric cover if needed.

tip.text: Avoid pressing directly over raised embroidery or brass hardware.

items.name: Rainbow Dolly Quilt
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, plain cotton, assorted pastel rainbow shades.
Fabric, white cotton.
Fibre filling.
Zip.
Embroidery thread.
Acrylic yarn, assorted colours.
Soft-coloured pencil.
Lightweight fusible applique sheet.
Narrow elastic.
Press stud fasteners.
Card for templates.
Matching sewing thread.
5mm seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Sewing, Embroidery, Stuffing, Hand Stitching

steps.title: Prepare the Rainbow Applique
steps.content: Trace each rainbow arch onto lightweight fusible applique sheet. Fuse the pieces onto the selected cotton fabrics and cut them out accurately. Arrange the arches on the background fabric and secure them with a narrow zigzag stitch.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth while fusing applique pieces to protect delicate cotton prints.

steps.title: Cut the Doll Pieces
steps.content: Using the templates, cut the body, head, arm and leg pieces from cotton fabric. Transfer all notches and matching marks before removing the pattern pieces.

tip.text: Skipping transfer marks can cause alignment problems when attaching the limbs.

steps.title: Sew and Stuff the Legs
steps.content: Place each leg pair right sides together and stitch around the edges, leaving the upper edge open. Turn right side out and stuff firmly with fibre filling before closing the openings.

tip.text: Overstuffing the legs can create bulky seams and distort the finished shape.

steps.title: Sew and Stuff the Arms
steps.content: Stitch each arm pair together, leaving an opening for turning. Clip curves, turn right side out and stuff lightly before closing the openings.

tip.text: Leave the upper section of the arms slightly softer so they sit naturally against the body.

steps.title: Assemble the Body
steps.content: Position the arms and legs between the front and back body pieces. Stitch around the body and head, leaving an opening for turning. Turn right side out and stuff the body firmly.

tip.text: Always check that the limbs are facing inward before stitching the body seam.

steps.title: Close the Body Opening
steps.content: Add additional stuffing where necessary to create an even shape. Close the turning opening using small hand stitches.

tip.text: Use ladder stitch for a nearly invisible closure.

steps.title: Embroider the Face
steps.content: Transfer the facial markings using the template. Embroider the eyes, eyelashes, eyebrows and mouth with embroidery thread. Add soft cheek colour using a coloured pencil if desired.

tip.text: Check the symmetry of the eyes before stitching permanently.

steps.title: Create the Hair
steps.content: Wrap acrylic yarn around a card and cut the loops. Stitch the yarn strands securely to the head and continue adding rows until the desired fullness is achieved.

tip.text: Sew multiple rows of yarn close together to create fuller hair coverage.

steps.title: Make the Tunic
steps.content: Cut the tunic pieces from cotton fabric. Stitch the shoulder seams and side seams, finish the neckline and hem, then press all seams flat.

tip.text: Unpressed seams can make the tunic appear twisted when worn.

steps.title: Create the Rainbow Skirt
steps.content: Apply the rainbow motif to the skirt fabric. Stitch the skirt seam, finish the lower hem and create a casing along the waistband.

tip.text: Check that the rainbow motif is centred before sewing the skirt seam.

steps.title: Insert the Elastic
steps.content: Thread narrow elastic through the waistband casing. Adjust the fit, stitch the elastic ends together and close the casing opening.

tip.text: Make sure the elastic is not twisted inside the casing before securing it.

steps.title: Construct the Backpack
steps.content: Cut the backpack pieces, sew the straps and attach them to the bag. Install the zipper and stitch the backpack together before turning right side out.

tip.text: Misaligned zipper tape can cause the backpack opening to sit unevenly.

steps.title: Finish the Doll
steps.content: Dress the doll in the tunic and skirt. Fit the backpack, attach any press studs and trim all loose threads to complete the project.

tip.text: Give the clothing a final press before dressing the doll for a professional finish.

items.name: Star Quilt Blocks
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric scraps in assorted prints.
Plain cotton fabric for backing.
Toy stuffing.
Embroidery thread.
Sewing thread.
Buttons.
Ribbon for hanging loop.
Printed template.
Pins.
5mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Straight Stitching, Seam Pressing, Clipping Curves, Turning, Stuffing, Running Stitch Quilting, Hand Sewing, Button Attachment

steps.title: Cut the Patchwork Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the template. Use it to cut five diamond-shaped pieces from assorted patterned fabrics. Arrange the pieces to form a star shape before sewing.

tip.text: Choose fabrics with contrasting colors and prints to make each star point stand out.

steps.title: Sew the Star Front
steps.content: Stitch the diamond pieces together along their edges to create the patchwork star front. Press all seam allowances neatly to one side.

tip.text: Inconsistent seam allowances can distort the points and make the star uneven.

steps.title: Prepare the Backing
steps.content: Place the completed patchwork star right side down onto plain cotton fabric. Cut the backing fabric to match the exact shape of the star front.

tip.text: Pin the layers together before trimming to prevent shifting.

steps.title: Join Front and Back
steps.content: Place the patchwork front and backing right sides together. Sew around the star, leaving a 1.57 inches opening on one straight edge for turning.

tip.text: Do not accidentally stitch the turning gap closed.

steps.title: Clip and Turn
steps.content: Trim the seam allowance and carefully clip around the points and curves. Turn the star right side out through the opening and gently push out the points.

tip.text: Cutting too close to the stitching line may weaken the seams and cause holes.

steps.title: Stuff the Star
steps.content: Lightly fill the star with toy stuffing, making sure the filling reaches all points evenly while maintaining a flat decorative shape.

tip.text: Add stuffing gradually to avoid lumps and overfilling.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Fold the raw edges of the turning gap inward and hand stitch the opening closed using matching thread.

tip.text: Use a ladder stitch for an almost invisible finish.

steps.title: Quilt the Decoration
steps.content: Using two strands of embroidery thread, work running stitches along the patchwork seams and around the star points to create texture and secure the stuffing.

tip.text: Pulling the embroidery thread too tightly can cause puckering.

steps.title: Add Decorative Details
steps.content: Sew a button securely in the center of the star. Attach a ribbon loop to one point so the decoration can be hung.

tip.text: Secure the button and ribbon firmly to prevent them from coming loose during use.

items.name: Tortoise Sewing Weights
items.materialNeeded:
Hand-dyed batik cotton, assorted shades.
Cream cotton, 20.79 inches x 11.81 inches.
Toy filling.
Split yellow lentils.
Embroidery thread: green, yellow, lilac, pink, black.
Flower buttons: green and pink.
Scrap of pink felt.
Sewing thread.
PVA glue.
Printed template.
Pins.
5mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Machine Sewing, Stuffing, Hand Sewing, Backstitch, French Knots, Embroidery, Whip Stitch, Button Attachment

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the template. Cut six shell segments from assorted batik cotton fabrics. Cut two head pieces, eight leg pieces, two tail pieces, and one belly piece from the specified fabrics.

tip.text: Vary the fabric shades between shell segments to create a more interesting patchwork effect.

steps.title: Create the Shell Halves
steps.content: Place two shell segments right sides together and stitch along one side. Continue adding segments until three pieces form one shell half. Repeat to create a second shell half.

tip.text: Misaligned points between segments can affect the round shape of the finished shell.

steps.title: Assemble the Shell
steps.content: Place the two shell halves right sides together and stitch around the curved edges, leaving a 1.18 inches opening at the base for turning. Press seams open and turn right side out.

tip.text: Leave the turning gap unstitched or the shell cannot be filled properly.

steps.title: Fill and Weight the Shell
steps.content: Stuff the shell with toy filling and add a handful of split yellow lentils to give the tortoise extra weight. Close the opening securely with small hand stitches.

tip.text: Distribute the lentils evenly inside the shell so the tortoise sits flat and balanced.

steps.title: Make the Legs
steps.content: Sew each pair of leg pieces right sides together, leaving a small opening. Trim seam allowances, turn right side out, and stuff lightly with toy filling.
