tip.text: Overstuffing the legs can make them difficult to attach neatly to the body.

steps.title: Construct the Head and Tail
steps.content: Sew the head pieces together and repeat for the tail pieces. Trim curves, turn right side out, and stuff lightly before closing the openings.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to shape the nose and tail without damaging the seams.

steps.title: Attach Limbs to the Shell
steps.content: Position the legs, head, and tail evenly around the underside of the shell. Hand sew each piece firmly in place using strong backstitches.

tip.text: Check the placement before sewing permanently to ensure the tortoise sits evenly.

steps.title: Add the Belly Panel
steps.content: Turn under the raw edges of the belly piece by 5mm. Pin it to the underside of the tortoise, covering the attachment points, and sew it in place with neat whip stitches.

tip.text: Press the folded seam allowance before stitching for a cleaner finish.

steps.title: Decorate the Shell
steps.content: Sew a flower button to the center of the shell. Embroider decorative star stitches on each shell segment using contrasting embroidery thread.

tip.text: Pulling embroidery stitches too tightly may cause the shell fabric to pucker.

steps.title: Embroider the Face
steps.content: Create the eyes using French knots and embroider the mouth with backstitch. Cut two small circles from pink felt and attach them as rosy cheeks using PVA glue.

tip.text: Allow the glue to dry completely before handling the tortoise to prevent the felt pieces from shifting.

items.name: Doll Bed Set
items.finishedSize: Doll: 5.5 x 14 inches; Patchwork Bed: 10 x 15.5 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Five coordinating cotton fabrics.
Cream fabric, fat quarter.
Yellow fabric scraps.
Pink fabric scraps.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Quilt wadding.
Toy stuffing.
White cotton lace.
Fabric marker.
Pins.
Hand sewing needle.
Sewing machine.
Iron and ironing board.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Toy Making, Patchwork Piecing, Quilting, Stuffing, Hand Sewing, Machine Sewing, Embroidery

steps.title: Cut the Doll Body Pieces
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut four body pieces from cream fabric, ensuring that two pieces are mirrored. Transfer all template markings before sewing.

tip.text: Cutting all body pieces in the same orientation instead of mirrored pairs will prevent accurate assembly.

steps.title: Sew and Stuff the Doll Body
steps.content: Place body pieces right sides together and sew using a narrow seam allowance. Clip curves, turn right side out and press carefully. Stuff the body firmly with toy filling through the neck opening.

tip.text: Avoid overstuffing around the neck area, as bulky seams can make attaching the head difficult.

steps.title: Create the Hair and Face
steps.content: Cut the hair piece from yellow fabric using the template. Position and stitch it to the head. Embroider facial details such as eyes, eyelashes, cheeks and mouth according to the pattern markings.

tip.text: Lightly draw facial features with a removable fabric marker before embroidering for better symmetry.

steps.title: Make the Doll Dress
steps.content: Cut dress pieces from printed fabric. Sew shoulder seams and side seams, finish the raw edges and hem the dress. Leave an opening at the back so the dress can be fitted onto the doll.

tip.text: Sewing the back opening closed will make dressing the doll difficult.

steps.title: Prepare the Patchwork Squares
steps.content: Cut multiple 3.54 inches squares from the selected fabrics. Arrange the fabrics into a balanced patchwork layout before sewing.

tip.text: Take a photo of the layout before stitching to preserve the planned color arrangement.

steps.title: Piece the Patchwork Top
steps.content: Join squares into rows and then sew the rows together to create the patchwork quilt top. Press seam allowances after each row is completed.

tip.text: Unpressed seams can cause misaligned intersections and a distorted patchwork design.

steps.title: Create the Quilt Sandwich
steps.content: Layer the patchwork top, quilt wadding and backing fabric. Smooth all layers and pin thoroughly to prevent shifting during quilting.

tip.text: Insufficient pinning may cause wrinkles or puckers while quilting.

steps.title: Quilt the Bed Cover
steps.content: Quilt through all layers by stitching along selected seam lines and patchwork sections. Trim away excess backing and wadding after quilting.

tip.text: Start quilting from the center and work outward to keep the layers evenly distributed.

steps.title: Add the Lace Trim
steps.content: Attach white cotton lace along the upper edge of the quilt. Stitch carefully so the lace remains evenly spaced and flat.

tip.text: Baste the lace in place before final stitching for more accurate positioning.

steps.title: Make the Pillow
steps.content: Cut and sew the pillow pieces. Leave a small opening for turning, stuff lightly and hand stitch the opening closed.

tip.text: Overstuffing the pillow can make it look oversized compared to the doll bed.

steps.title: Bind and Finish the Bed
steps.content: Attach binding strips around the outer edges of the quilt. Fold neatly around corners and hand stitch the binding to the back for a clean finish.

tip.text: Uneven binding width is highly visible on small quilts, so measure carefully while sewing.

steps.title: Complete the Doll and Bed Set
steps.content: Dress the doll, place the pillow and quilt on the miniature bed, and inspect all seams. Trim loose threads and give the finished set a final press.

tip.text: A final pressing improves the appearance of both the patchwork quilt and the doll clothing.

items.name: Cuddly Bunny Plush
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, mock mohair, 71.97 inches.
Fabric, patterned cotton, 11.42 inches x 23.62 inches.
Wool felt, purple, 5.12 inches square.
Buttons, blue, 2.
Buttons, pink, 4.
Embroidery thread: pale pink, black, brown.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Strong thread for jointed limbs.
Toy filling.
Fusible webbing, 3.15 inches x 12.36 inches.
Long sewing needle.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Soft Toy Construction, Machine Sewing, Hand Sewing, Stuffing, Button Jointing, Embroidery, Applique, Gathering, Whip Stitch

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Pieces
steps.content: Print and cut the template pieces. Cut one head piece, one centre head piece, one front body, one back body, two legs, four ears and two arms from mock mohair fabric following the nap direction. Cut two middle ear pieces and two middle paw pieces from patterned fabric. Cut two paw pieces from wool felt.

tip.text: Keep all mohair pieces aligned with the same nap direction or the finished bunny will have uneven fur shading.

steps.title: Construct the Body and Arms
steps.content: Pin and stitch the back body pieces together at the marked centre point. Repeat for the front body pieces. Place the front and back sections right sides together and stitch around the body, leaving an opening on the underside for turning and stuffing. Sew the arm sections in pairs, leaving a small gap for turning. Turn right side out and lightly stuff the arms before closing the openings.

tip.text: Overstuffing the arms can make later jointing difficult and reduce arm movement.

steps.title: Make the Legs and Paw Pads
steps.content: Sew the leg sections together, leaving a gap on the underside. Apply fusible webbing to the back of the middle paw fabric and press the wool felt paw pad into position. Stitch around the paw pad and attach it to the leg opening. Turn the leg right side out, stuff firmly and sew the opening closed. Repeat for the second leg.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to shape the foot neatly before stuffing for a more professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Ears
steps.content: Fold the middle ear inserts and stitch the darts. Press under a 5mm hem around the inserts and attach them to the fur ear pieces. Place ear pieces right sides together and stitch around the edges, leaving a turning gap. Turn right side out, lightly stuff and close the opening. Repeat for the second ear.

tip.text: Do not overstuff the ears or they may not fold naturally when attached to the head.

steps.title: Assemble and Stuff the Head
steps.content: Join the side head sections from the nose to the neckline. Attach the centre head panel and stitch securely. Cut the marked slits and insert the ears in the indicated positions. Turn the head right side out and stuff firmly until smooth and rounded. Sew a line of running stitches around the neck opening and gently pull to shape the neck.

tip.text: Check ear placement carefully before stitching permanently, as uneven ears are difficult to correct later.

steps.title: Joint the Arms and Legs
steps.content: Position the arms against the body and attach them using strong thread and buttons to create movable joints. Repeat the process for the legs, ensuring both sides are aligned evenly and move freely.

tip.text: Tighten the jointing thread gradually and test movement frequently to avoid restricting the limbs.

steps.title: Attach the Head and Add Facial Features
steps.content: Position the head on the body and tack it in place. Stitch securely around the neckline using strong thread. Embroider the nose with pale pink satin stitch, create the mouth using black chain stitch, sew the buttons in place for the eyes and embroider claws on the paws using brown embroidery thread.

tip.text: Mark the facial features lightly with an air-erasable pen before stitching to keep the face symmetrical.

steps.title: Make and Attach the Tail
steps.content: Cut a 4.72 inches circle from fur fabric. Sew running stitches around the edge and pull the thread to gather the fabric into a ball shape. Add toy filling, tighten the stitches and secure the opening. Sew the completed tail firmly to the back of the bunny.

tip.text: Adding too much stuffing can make the tail difficult to close into a neat round shape.

steps.title: Create the Decorative Neck Bow
steps.content: Cut a 3.54 inches x 15.75 inches strip of patterned fabric. Fold lengthwise with right sides together and stitch along the long edge, leaving a turning gap. Turn right side out, press flat and hand sew the opening closed. Tie the strip into a bow and place it around the bunny's neck.

tip.text: Press the fabric tube before tying the bow to achieve crisp edges and a cleaner appearance.

items.name: Christmas Fat Quarter Set
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabrics, assorted festive prints.
Background cotton fabric, approximately 12 inches x 16 inches.
Plain cotton backing fabric, 12 inches x 16 inches.
Lightweight quilt wadding/batting, 12 inches x 16 inches.
Fusible adhesive web.
Coordinating sewing thread.
Decorative binding fabric.
Hanging loop fabric strip.
Paper templates.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Iron and ironing board.
3mm seam allowance for appliqué edges where stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Fusible Adhesive Application, Quilting, Topstitching, Edge Stitching, Binding, Hand Finishing

steps.title: Prepare and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the Christmas tree templates at 100% scale. Carefully cut out all template pieces and label them according to the layout diagram.

tip.text: Incorrect printer scaling will affect the size and alignment of the finished tree design.

steps.title: Trace Shapes onto Fusible Web
steps.content: Trace each tree segment onto the paper side of the fusible adhesive web. Leave a small gap between shapes to make cutting easier.

tip.text: Keep template labels visible on the fusible web to avoid mixing up similar pieces.

steps.title: Fuse Shapes to Fabric
steps.content: Iron the traced fusible web pieces onto the wrong side of the selected festive fabrics. Allow them to cool completely before cutting out the shapes.

tip.text: Cutting while the adhesive is still warm may cause edges to distort.

steps.title: Cut Out the Applique Pieces
steps.content: Cut accurately around each fused template shape using sharp fabric scissors. Remove the paper backing from all pieces.

tip.text: Small embroidery scissors provide better control around curved areas.

steps.title: Arrange the Christmas Tree Design
steps.content: Position the tree segments onto the background fabric according to the template guide. Ensure adjacent pieces do not use the same print for visual contrast.

tip.text: Take a reference photo before fusing so you can recreate the arrangement if needed.

steps.title: Fuse the Applique in Place
steps.content: Once satisfied with the arrangement, press the applique pieces onto the background fabric following the fusible web manufacturer's instructions.

tip.text: Avoid sliding the iron across the fabric as this can shift the applique pieces.

steps.title: Secure the Applique Edges
steps.content: Using coordinating thread, stitch around each applique shape with a narrow zigzag stitch or decorative applique stitch to permanently secure the pieces.

tip.text: Wide zigzag stitches can overpower small applique details and reduce definition.

steps.title: Create the Quilt Sandwich
steps.content: Layer the completed applique front, quilt wadding, and backing fabric with wrong sides together. Smooth all layers and pin or baste securely.

tip.text: Start pinning from the center and work outward to prevent wrinkles.

steps.title: Quilt the Wall Hanging
steps.content: Quilt around the tree design and across the background as desired. Use simple straight-line quilting to add texture and secure the layers.

tip.text: Echo quilting around the tree helps the applique design stand out.

steps.title: Trim and Bind the Edges
steps.content: Trim excess batting and backing fabric. Attach binding around all four edges, folding neatly at the corners and securing with stitching.

tip.text: Uneven trimming before binding can result in wavy edges on the finished wall hanging.

steps.title: Add the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Create a fabric hanging loop and stitch it securely to the top back edge of the wall hanging before completing the final section of binding.

tip.text: Reinforce the hanging loop with multiple rows of stitching to support the project's weight.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Give the completed wall hanging a final press, trim loose threads, and check all applique and binding stitches before displaying.

tip.text: Press from the back using a pressing cloth to protect decorative stitching and fabric prints.

items.name: Felt Egg Ornaments
items.materialNeeded:
Wool felt, assorted colours.
Polyester sewing thread.
Embroidery thread, coordinating colours.
Ric-rac trim.
Decorative ribbons.
Toy stuffing.
Tracing paper.
Fabric scissors.
Pins.
Hand sewing needle.
Fabric marker.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Hand Sewing, Blanket Stitch, Applique, Embroidery, Stuffing, Decorative Trim Attachment, Ribbon Attachment

steps.title: Print and Cut the Template
steps.content: Print the egg template at full size and cut it out. Trace the template onto the chosen felt colour and cut two matching egg-shaped pieces.

tip.text: Use sharp fabric scissors to achieve smooth curves around the egg shape.

steps.title: Plan the Decoration Layout
steps.content: Arrange ribbons, ric-rac trims, appliqué shapes, and embroidery designs on the front egg piece before sewing. Experiment with placement until you are satisfied with the design.

tip.text: Take a photo of the layout before sewing so you can easily recreate the arrangement.

steps.title: Attach Decorative Trims
steps.content: Hand stitch or machine stitch ric-rac trims, ribbons, and decorative bands onto the front egg piece following the planned design.

tip.text: Stretching the ribbon while sewing can cause the decoration to pucker.

steps.title: Create Appliqué Details
steps.content: Cut decorative shapes such as flowers, rabbits, or seasonal motifs from contrasting felt. Position them on the front panel and secure with small appliqué stitches.

tip.text: Use matching thread colours to make appliqué stitches nearly invisible.

steps.title: Add Embroidery Details
steps.content: Embroider decorative elements such as flower centres, stems, leaves, dots, or borders using coordinating embroidery thread.

tip.text: Pulling embroidery stitches too tightly can distort the felt surface.

steps.title: Prepare the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Cut a ribbon approximately 6 inches long and fold it in half to create a hanging loop. Position the loop at the top of the ornament between the felt layers.

tip.text: Ensure the ribbon ends are securely enclosed between the felt layers to prevent the ornament from falling when hung.

steps.title: Sew the Egg Together
steps.content: Place the front and back egg pieces with wrong sides facing inward. Stitch around the edge using blanket stitch, leaving a small opening for stuffing.

tip.text: Keep blanket stitches evenly spaced for a neat handmade finish.

steps.title: Stuff the Ornament
steps.content: Fill the egg lightly with toy stuffing until it holds its shape while remaining soft and lightweight.

tip.text: Overstuffing can stretch the felt and make the seams appear uneven.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Continue the blanket stitch around the remaining opening to fully close the ornament and secure the thread.

tip.text: Loose finishing knots may come undone after repeated handling.

steps.title: Final Shaping and Inspection
steps.content: Gently shape the ornament by hand, trim any loose threads, and check that all decorative elements are firmly attached before displaying it.

tip.text: Lightly steam the finished ornament from a distance to smooth minor wrinkles in the felt.

items.name: Curly Elf Stocking
items.finishedSize: 7.5 inches wide × 15.75 inches high (including hanging loop)
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric A (main stocking leg), 23.15 inches x 7.48 inches.
Fabric B (contrast cuff and foot), 5.91 inches x 23.15 inches.
Fabric C (lining), 5.91 inches x 23.15 inches.
Ribbon, 22 inches.
Pom-pom trim, 12 inches.
Ric-rac braid, 12 inches.
Cotton tape for hanging loop, 12 inches.
Small metal bell.
Polyester sewing thread.
Coordinating embroidery thread (optional).
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Iron.
Toy stuffing.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Machine Sewing, Gathering Trim, Topstitching, Seam Clipping, Stuffing, Decorative Trim Attachment, Hanging Loop Construction

steps.title: Print and Cut the Pattern Pieces
steps.content: Print the stocking template at full size and cut out all pattern pieces. Cut the stocking leg, elf foot, cuff, and lining pieces from the selected fabrics. Transfer any alignment marks to the fabric.

tip.text: Use contrasting fabrics for the leg and foot sections to highlight the elf-inspired design.

steps.title: Construct the Elf Foot
steps.content: Place the two elf foot pieces right sides together and stitch around the curved edges, leaving the straight upper edge open. Clip the curved seam allowances and turn right side out.

tip.text: Clip curved seams carefully without cutting through the stitching line to prevent puckering.

steps.title: Attach Decorative Trims
steps.content: Sew the pom-pom trim and ric-rac braid along the upper edge of the elf foot section. Ensure the decorative edge faces outward and remains visible after assembly.

tip.text: Placing trim too close to the seam allowance may hide the decoration inside the finished seam.

steps.title: Join the Foot to the Stocking Leg
steps.content: Align the upper edge of the elf foot with the lower edge of the stocking leg. Stitch securely and press the seam allowance toward the leg section.

tip.text: Pressing after each seam helps create a crisp, professional-looking finish.

steps.title: Prepare the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Fold the cotton tape in half to create a loop. Position the raw ends at the upper corner of the stocking, aligning them with the seam allowance.

tip.text: Check that the loop is facing inward before sewing so it turns correctly after assembly.

steps.title: Assemble the Outer Stocking
steps.content: Place the outer stocking pieces right sides together and stitch around the entire shape, leaving the top opening unsewn. Reinforce the area where the hanging loop is attached.

tip.text: Failing to match the foot seam accurately can result in an uneven stocking shape.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Sew the lining pieces together using the same method as the outer stocking, leaving a small turning gap along one side seam.

tip.text: Mark the turning gap with pins to avoid accidentally stitching it closed.

steps.title: Join the Outer Stocking and Lining
steps.content: Insert the outer stocking into the lining with right sides facing. Match side seams and top edges, then stitch completely around the top opening.

tip.text: Ensure the hanging loop remains tucked between the layers before sewing.

steps.title: Turn the Stocking Right Side Out
steps.content: Pull the stocking through the opening left in the lining. Carefully push out the toe and heel curves, then close the lining opening with a slip stitch or machine stitch.

tip.text: Using sharp tools to push corners may pierce the fabric and damage the finished stocking.

steps.title: Create the Decorative Cuff
steps.content: Fold the cuff fabric in half, stitch the short ends together, and press. Attach the cuff around the top opening of the stocking and topstitch in place.

tip.text: A narrow topstitch helps keep the cuff flat and prevents the lining from rolling outward.

steps.title: Stuff the Elf Foot
steps.content: Add a small amount of toy stuffing into the curved elf foot section to create the characteristic three-dimensional shape.

tip.text: Do not overstuff the foot, as excessive bulk can distort the stocking silhouette.

steps.title: Attach the Bell and Ribbon
steps.content: Tie a decorative ribbon bow near the cuff and attach the small bell securely to the bow or hanging loop area.

tip.text: Hand-sew the bell rather than relying solely on knots for better durability.

steps.title: Personalize the Stocking
steps.content: Optional: embroider a name, initials, or festive motif onto the stocking front before filling it with treats or gifts.

tip.text: Embroidery is easiest to complete before the lining is attached.

steps.title: Final Press and Inspection
steps.content: Press the finished stocking lightly, trim loose threads, and check all seams, trims, and embellishments before hanging.

tip.text: Avoid applying a hot iron directly onto pom-poms or synthetic trims, as they may melt or become misshapen.

items.name: Festive Table Mats
items.finishedSize: Centrepiece: 16.5 inches square | Coasters: 5 inches square
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric, assorted coordinating Christmas prints.
Background fabric, four contrasting prints.
Applique fabric scraps.
Cotton batting/wadding.
Lightweight fusible interfacing.
Matching polyester sewing thread.
Embroidery thread.
Fabric marking pen.
Pins or sewing clips.
Iron.
Fabric scissors.
Rotary cutter and cutting mat.
Quilting ruler.
Temporary fabric adhesive spray (optional).
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork Piecing, Applique, Machine Quilting, Topstitching, Layering Quilt Sandwich, Binding, Embroidery, Edge Stitching

steps.title: Prepare and Cut Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Cut four equal squares for the centrepiece background and additional fabric pieces for the appliqué motif. Cut matching batting and backing fabric according to the finished dimensions.

tip.text: Label each fabric section after cutting to keep the layout organized during assembly.

steps.title: Arrange the Patchwork Layout
steps.content: Lay the four background squares on a flat surface and arrange them into the desired design. Check colour balance and pattern direction before sewing.

tip.text: Ignoring fabric direction can cause prints to appear upside down in the finished centrepiece.

steps.title: Join the Background Squares
steps.content: Sew the squares together in rows using a 1/4 inch seam allowance. Press seams open or to one side, then join the rows to form the complete centrepiece background.

tip.text: Press after every seam to improve accuracy and reduce bulk.

steps.title: Prepare the Applique Templates
steps.content: Trace the Christmas motif template onto lightweight interfacing or template paper. Cut out the shapes and transfer them onto the chosen fabrics.

tip.text: Mirror any directional appliqué pieces if using fusible appliqué techniques.

steps.title: Fuse or Position the Applique
steps.content: Arrange the appliqué pieces on the centre of the patchwork background. Fuse them in place or pin securely before stitching.

tip.text: Applying the iron before checking placement may make repositioning difficult.

steps.title: Stitch the Applique Motif
steps.content: Secure all appliqué pieces using a satin stitch, blanket stitch, or narrow zigzag stitch around the edges. Add decorative embroidery details if desired.

tip.text: Reduce machine speed when sewing around tight curves for smoother appliqué edges.

steps.title: Create the Quilt Sandwich
steps.content: Layer the backing fabric wrong side up, batting in the middle, and the decorated patchwork top right side up. Smooth all layers and pin or baste them together.

tip.text: Wrinkles trapped in the backing layer can permanently affect the finished project.

steps.title: Quilt the Centrepiece
steps.content: Machine quilt around the appliqué design and through selected patchwork sections. Use coordinating thread to enhance the decorative elements.

tip.text: Start quilting from the centre and work outward to minimize fabric shifting.

steps.title: Trim and Square the Centrepiece
steps.content: Trim excess batting and backing fabric. Use a quilting ruler to square all edges and ensure the finished piece remains symmetrical.

tip.text: Uneven trimming can make binding difficult and affect the final appearance.

steps.title: Prepare the Binding
steps.content: Cut binding strips, join them together, and press lengthwise with wrong sides facing. Align the raw edges of the binding with the centrepiece edge.

tip.text: Pressing the binding before attaching creates cleaner corners and smoother edges.

steps.title: Attach the Binding
steps.content: Sew the binding around all four sides of the centrepiece, mitering the corners neatly. Fold the binding to the back and stitch it in place.

tip.text: Bulky corners can occur if excess seam allowance is not trimmed before folding.

steps.title: Cut the Coaster Pieces
steps.content: Cut fabric squares, batting, and backing pieces for four matching coasters. Coordinate fabrics with the centrepiece design.

tip.text: Use leftover patchwork fabrics from the centrepiece for a cohesive table set.

steps.title: Assemble the Coasters
steps.content: Layer the coaster top, batting, and backing fabric. Stitch around the edges, leaving a small turning gap. Turn right side out and press flat.

tip.text: Insufficient clipping at corners can prevent the coaster from lying flat after turning.

steps.title: Quilt and Topstitch the Coasters
steps.content: Topstitch around the coaster edges and add simple quilting lines to secure all layers together.

tip.text: Keep topstitching an equal distance from the edge for a professional finish.

steps.title: Final Press and Inspection
steps.content: Give both the centrepiece and coasters a final press. Trim loose threads and inspect all seams, quilting lines, and appliqué stitching before use.

tip.text: Avoid excessive heat on synthetic batting, as it may lose loft or become distorted.

items.name: Hanging Elf Stocking
items.materialNeeded:
