White cotton fabric, 1/4 yard.
Polka dot coordinating fabric, 1/4 yard.
Pom-pom trim, 1 yard.
Decorative Christmas ribbon trim, 2 yards.
Bias binding, 1 yard.
Red buttons, 2 large.
Silver ribbon scraps.
Red ribbon, 12 inches.
White glitter felt.
Toy stuffing.
Matching polyester thread.
Embroidery thread.
Fabric marker.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Iron.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Machine Sewing, Decorative Trim Attachment, Applique, Embroidery, Stuffing, Topstitching, Bias Binding Application, Gathering, Edge Finishing

steps.title: Print and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Print the Elf Legs Stocking templates at full size. Cut all pattern pieces including the tunic front and back, trouser legs, shoes, cuffs, and decorative elements. Transfer all markings to the fabric.

tip.text: Verify printer settings are set to Actual Size before cutting fabric to maintain the correct proportions.

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Cut all required pieces from the selected fabrics. Cut matching pairs for the trouser legs and shoes, and separate pieces for the tunic front, tunic back, cuffs, and decorative panels.

tip.text: Cutting mirrored pieces incorrectly can result in two identical legs instead of a matching pair.

steps.title: Construct the Elf Shoes
steps.content: Place each pair of shoe pieces right sides together and stitch around the curved edges, leaving the upper edge open. Clip curves, turn right side out, and lightly stuff the toe area.

tip.text: Avoid overstuffing the curved toe or the shoe may lose its characteristic elf shape.

steps.title: Sew the Trouser Legs
steps.content: Join each pair of trouser leg pieces with right sides together. Stitch the side seams and inseams, then turn right side out and press.

tip.text: Pressing seams immediately after sewing produces cleaner leg shapes and easier assembly.

steps.title: Attach the Shoes to the Legs
steps.content: Align the shoe openings with the lower edge of each trouser leg. Stitch securely and reinforce the seam because this area may support the weight of stocking fillers.

tip.text: Weak seams between the shoe and leg sections may split when the stocking is filled.

steps.title: Apply Decorative Leg Trims
steps.content: Sew ribbon trims and pom-pom braid around the lower trouser sections above the shoes. Keep the trim level on both legs for symmetry.

tip.text: Uneven trim placement makes the finished legs appear mismatched.

steps.title: Create the Tunic Front
steps.content: Position the contrasting centre panel on the tunic front and stitch it in place. Attach decorative ribbon trims along both edges of the panel.

tip.text: Use an edge-stitch foot if available to maintain even spacing alongside decorative trims.

steps.title: Add the Decorative Buttons
steps.content: Mark the button positions on the centre panel and sew the decorative buttons securely to the tunic front.

tip.text: Use strong thread and multiple passes when attaching large buttons.

steps.title: Create the Personalized Band
steps.content: Cut the white fabric band that runs across the lower tunic. Embroider a festive greeting or a recipient's name using embroidery thread.

tip.text: Complete all embroidery before attaching the band to the stocking for easier stitching.

steps.title: Attach the Personalized Band
steps.content: Sew the embroidered band across the lower tunic front. Add decorative ribbon trim above the band and pom-pom trim below it.

tip.text: Failing to align the band horizontally can make the finished stocking appear crooked.

steps.title: Join the Tunic Front and Back
steps.content: Place the tunic front and back pieces right sides together and stitch around the sides and lower edge, leaving the top opening unsewn.

tip.text: Check that all trims and pom-poms are tucked inward before sewing to avoid catching them in the seam.

steps.title: Attach the Trouser Section
steps.content: Position the completed trouser section beneath the tunic and stitch securely along the waist seam. Press the seam allowance upward toward the tunic.

tip.text: Topstitch the waist seam for additional strength and a professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Hanging Straps
steps.content: Cut two lengths of decorative ribbon and fold the ends under. Attach one strap to each upper corner of the tunic to create hanging loops.

tip.text: Reinforce the strap attachment points because they will carry the full weight of the stocking.

steps.title: Finish the Top Opening
steps.content: Apply bias binding around the top opening of the stocking. Fold neatly to the inside and topstitch in place.

tip.text: Stretching the bias binding while sewing can cause the opening to ripple.

steps.title: Shape and Lightly Stuff the Legs
steps.content: Add a small amount of stuffing into the elf shoes and lower leg area if desired to help maintain the decorative shape while hanging.

tip.text: Use only enough stuffing to support the shape without reducing storage space inside the stocking.

steps.title: Final Press and Inspection
steps.content: Press the completed stocking carefully, trim loose threads, and inspect all decorative elements, trims, and hanging loops before use.

tip.text: Avoid direct contact between a hot iron and pom-pom trim, ribbon, or synthetic embellishments.

items.name: Pudding Door Holder
items.materialNeeded:
Brown corduroy fabric, 1/2 yard.
White cotton fabric for icing, 1/4 yard.
Green cotton fabric for holly leaves, fabric scraps.
Red cotton fabric for berry, fabric scrap.
Polyester sewing thread.
Embroidery thread.
Toy stuffing.
Polyester wadding/batting.
Small stones, gravel, rice, or weighted filling for doorstop base.
Black and brown buttons, assorted sizes.
Fabric marker.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Hand sewing needle.
Iron.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Machine Sewing, Free-Motion Embroidery, Applique, Quilting, Stuffing, Weighted Filling Construction, Hand Sewing, Decorative Button Attachment

steps.title: Print and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Print the Christmas Pudding Doorstop templates at full size. Cut the pudding body sections, icing top, holly leaves, and berry templates. Transfer all markings onto the fabric.

tip.text: Check that your printer is set to Actual Size to ensure all pieces fit together correctly.

steps.title: Cut the Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Cut six pudding body segments from brown corduroy, one icing piece from white fabric, two holly leaf pieces from green fabric, and one berry strip from red fabric.

tip.text: Ignoring the nap direction of corduroy can cause the finished pudding sections to appear different shades.

steps.title: Join the Pudding Body Panels
steps.content: Sew the corduroy body segments together one at a time with right sides facing. Continue until all sections form a rounded pudding shape, leaving an opening for turning and filling.

tip.text: Match the top and bottom points carefully to maintain a symmetrical shape.

steps.title: Turn and Shape the Pudding
steps.content: Turn the pudding body right side out and gently push out all seam lines. Press lightly if necessary and check that the shape is evenly rounded.

tip.text: Avoid excessive ironing on corduroy, as it can flatten the pile permanently.

steps.title: Add Decorative Buttons
steps.content: Sew assorted buttons around the pudding body to resemble raisins. Space them evenly across all visible sections.

tip.text: Placing all buttons on one side can make the decoration look unbalanced.

steps.title: Fill the Doorstop Base
steps.content: Add weighted filling such as stones, rice, or gravel into the lower portion of the pudding. Add polyester stuffing above the weight layer to create a rounded shape.

tip.text: Place weighted filling inside a sealed fabric bag before inserting it to prevent leakage over time.

steps.title: Close the Opening
steps.content: Hand stitch the remaining opening closed using a ladder stitch or slip stitch. Shape the pudding evenly while tightening the final stitches.

tip.text: Add small amounts of stuffing gradually to avoid lumps and uneven areas.

steps.title: Create the Icing Top
steps.content: Cut the icing shape from white fabric and a matching piece of wadding. Layer them together and quilt free-motion swirls across the surface to create a realistic icing texture.

tip.text: Practice free-motion stitching on scrap fabric before quilting the final icing piece.

steps.title: Trim and Finish the Icing
steps.content: Trim excess wadding from the edges and finish the icing shape neatly. Lightly press from the wrong side if required.

tip.text: Cutting too close to the stitching line may weaken the edge of the appliqué.

steps.title: Create the Holly Leaves
steps.content: Trace the holly template onto the wrong side of green fabric and cut four leaf pieces. Layer pairs together with a small piece of wadding inside.

tip.text: Using a thin layer of wadding helps the leaves hold their shape and adds dimension.

steps.title: Quilt the Holly Veins
steps.content: Stitch around each leaf and sew vein details using contrasting embroidery thread or machine stitching. Trim carefully around the leaf edges.

tip.text: Sharp inner leaf points can be difficult to turn neatly if seam allowances are not clipped.

steps.title: Make the Berry
steps.content: Cut a small circle from red fabric. Sew a gathering stitch around the edge, add a small amount of stuffing, pull the thread tight, and secure to form a round berry.

tip.text: Overstuffing the berry can make it difficult to close smoothly.

steps.title: Attach the Icing
steps.content: Position the icing piece on top of the pudding and hand stitch around the edges using small hidden stitches.

tip.text: Pin the icing in several places first to ensure the drips are evenly distributed around the pudding.

steps.title: Attach the Holly Decoration
steps.content: Arrange the holly leaves on top of the icing and secure them with hand stitching through the center. Attach the berry where the leaves overlap.

tip.text: Make sure all decorative pieces are firmly secured since the doorstop may be moved frequently.

steps.title: Shape and Balance the Doorstop
steps.content: Stand the pudding upright and adjust the weighted filling and stuffing by hand until it sits evenly and remains stable.

tip.text: A heavier weight concentrated at the bottom improves stability without increasing the overall size.

steps.title: Final Inspection
steps.content: Trim loose threads, check all seams, buttons, and decorative elements, and ensure the weighted filling is securely contained before use.

tip.text: Inspect the closure seam carefully, as it bears the greatest stress from the doorstop's weight.

items.name: Penguin Gift Stocking
items.materialNeeded:
Silver-grey linen fabric, 1/2 yard.
Navy cotton fabric, 1/2 yard.
White cotton fabric, 1/4 yard.
Pink felt, fabric scraps.
Yellow felt, fabric scrap.
Black felt, fabric scrap.
Spot-print cotton fabric, 1/4 yard.
Fusible interfacing.
Polyester wadding/batting.
Pom-pom trim, 12 inches.
Decorative ribbon, 12 inches.
Matching polyester sewing thread.
Embroidery thread.
Fabric marker.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Iron.
Blush crayon or fabric-safe coloring pencil (optional).
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Machine Sewing, Applique, Machine Embroidery, Quilting, Topstitching, Lining Construction, Edge Stitching, Decorative Pocket Construction

steps.title: Print and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Print the Penguin Stocking templates at full size and cut out all pattern pieces. Transfer markings to the fabric and identify the stocking body, face, wings, beak, cheeks, pocket, cuff, and lining pieces.

tip.text: Keep paper templates organized in labeled envelopes to avoid mixing up similarly shaped pieces.

steps.title: Cut All Fabric Components
steps.content: Cut the stocking front and back, lining pieces, face panel, wings, pocket sections, cuff, and appliqué shapes from the appropriate fabrics. Cut matching batting pieces where required.

tip.text: Forgetting to cut mirrored wing pieces can result in both wings facing the same direction.

steps.title: Prepare the Penguin Face
steps.content: Fuse interfacing to the back of the white face fabric if needed. Position the face panel onto the stocking front and secure it temporarily with pins or fabric adhesive.

tip.text: Lightweight fusible web helps keep curved appliqué pieces stable during stitching.

steps.title: Create the Eyes
steps.content: Cut the eye whites and pupils from felt. Layer the pieces and stitch them onto the face panel using small appliqué stitches or a narrow zigzag stitch.

tip.text: Check eye placement carefully before stitching permanently, as small differences can dramatically change the expression.

steps.title: Attach the Beak
steps.content: Cut the beak shape from yellow felt and position it between the eyes. Secure it with appliqué stitching around the edge.

tip.text: A small amount of temporary fabric glue can help hold tiny felt pieces in place while sewing.

steps.title: Add the Cheeks
steps.content: Cut circular cheek shapes from pink felt and stitch them onto the face panel. Alternatively, use fabric-safe blush to softly tint the cheek area.

tip.text: Overly bright cheek coloring can distract from the rest of the penguin design.

steps.title: Construct the Wings
steps.content: Layer the wing fabric with batting and quilt decorative stitching lines across the surface. Trim neatly and topstitch around the edges.

tip.text: Quilting before trimming produces cleaner and more consistent wing shapes.

steps.title: Attach the Wings
steps.content: Position the wings on either side of the face panel and stitch them securely to the stocking front.

tip.text: Make sure both wings are aligned evenly before sewing.

steps.title: Create the Present Pocket
steps.content: Cut pocket pieces from contrasting fabrics and stitch them together. Add ribbon trim to resemble a wrapped gift and secure the bow detail.

tip.text: Topstitching around the ribbon edges helps prevent them from lifting over time.

steps.title: Attach the Pocket
steps.content: Position the gift pocket on the lower front of the stocking and stitch around the sides and bottom, leaving the top edge open.

tip.text: Accidentally stitching the pocket opening closed will make it unusable.

steps.title: Add Decorative Machine Embroidery
steps.content: Sew decorative quilting or embroidery lines around the face, wings, and stocking body to add texture and visual interest.

tip.text: Practice decorative stitches on fabric scraps before stitching on the final project.

steps.title: Prepare the Cuff
steps.content: Fold the cuff fabric in half and stitch the short ends together. Press the seam open and fold the cuff with wrong sides together.

tip.text: Uneven cuff folding can make the top opening appear twisted.

steps.title: Attach Pom-Pom Trim
steps.content: Sew pom-pom trim around the lower edge of the cuff or at the cuff-body seam according to the design.

tip.text: Keep pom-poms clear of the stitching line to avoid accidentally sewing through them.

steps.title: Construct the Outer Stocking
steps.content: Place the stocking front and back right sides together and stitch around the outer edges, leaving the top opening unsewn. Clip curves and trim bulk where necessary.

tip.text: Clip the heel and toe curves to help them turn smoothly.

steps.title: Construct the Lining
steps.content: Sew the lining pieces together using the same method as the outer stocking, leaving a turning gap in one side seam.

tip.text: Do not forget the turning gap or the stocking cannot be turned right side out later.

steps.title: Create the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Fold the decorative ribbon in half and position it at the upper edge of the stocking between the outer fabric and cuff.

tip.text: Reinforce the hanging loop with multiple rows of stitching for added strength.

steps.title: Join the Outer Stocking, Cuff, and Lining
steps.content: Insert the cuff into the outer stocking, then place the lining over both layers with right sides together. Stitch completely around the top edge.

tip.text: Misaligned side seams can cause the cuff to twist after turning.

steps.title: Turn and Finish
steps.content: Pull the stocking through the turning gap in the lining. Push out all curves, close the lining opening with a ladder stitch, and tuck the lining neatly inside.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to avoid puncturing the fabric while shaping the toe.

steps.title: Final Press and Inspection
steps.content: Press the finished stocking lightly, trim loose threads, and inspect all appliqué, embroidery, pocket, and hanging loop attachments.

tip.text: A final pressing helps define the cuff edge and gives the stocking a polished appearance.

items.name: Gingerbread Gift Box
items.finishedSize: 6 inches wide × 6 inches deep × 8 inches high
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabric (gingerbread walls), 1 fat quarter.
Cream cotton fabric (house lining), 1 fat quarter.
Brown felt, fabric scraps.
Pink felt, fabric scraps.
Green felt, fabric scraps.
Red felt, fabric scraps.
Ric-rac trim, 1 yard.
Decorative lace trim, 1 yard.
Candy-cane ribbon or striped ribbon, 2 yards.
Medium-weight card stock.
Heavy-weight cardboard.
Iron-on interfacing.
Polyester thread.
Fabric glue.
Hand sewing needle.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Craft knife.
Ruler.
Water-soluble fabric marker.
Iron.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Machine Sewing, Applique, Box Construction, Cardboard Reinforcement, Decorative Trim Application, Hand Sewing, Topstitching, Fabric Glue Application

steps.title: Print and Prepare Templates
steps.content: Print the Gingerbread House templates at 100% scale. Cut out all pattern pieces for the walls, roof panels, base, lid, door, tree, and decorative elements.

tip.text: Label each template piece before cutting fabric to avoid confusion during assembly.

steps.title: Cut Fabric Pieces
steps.content: Cut all house wall panels, roof sections, lining pieces, and decorative appliqué shapes from the appropriate fabrics and felt.

tip.text: Forgetting seam allowances when cutting manually can cause the finished box to be too small.

steps.title: Prepare Cardboard Supports
steps.content: Use the templates to cut reinforcement panels from card stock and heavy cardboard. These inserts will provide structure to the house walls and roof.

tip.text: Measure carefully before cutting cardboard because incorrectly sized inserts can distort the final shape.

steps.title: Create the Front Door Appliqué
steps.content: Layer felt pieces to form the decorative front door. Stitch or glue the heart motif onto the door and add any additional embellishments.

tip.text: Use a narrow zigzag stitch around felt appliqué pieces for a clean and durable finish.

steps.title: Create the Christmas Tree Decoration
steps.content: Cut tree shapes from green felt and layer contrasting strips to create the decorated tree. Stitch securely and add decorative trims if desired.

tip.text: Decorative ribbon scraps are perfect for creating miniature garlands on the tree.

steps.title: Attach Front Decorations
steps.content: Position the tree and door on the front wall panel. Stitch all appliqué pieces in place before assembling the house structure.

tip.text: Adding appliqué after construction is much more difficult because of limited access inside the house.

steps.title: Apply Decorative Roof and Wall Trims
steps.content: Sew lace trim and ric-rac along the roofline and wall edges to imitate icing details found on traditional gingerbread houses.

tip.text: Pin trims first and check symmetry before sewing permanently.

steps.title: Construct the House Walls
steps.content: Join the wall panels together with right sides facing. Stitch each seam carefully and press open after sewing.

tip.text: Keep corners accurately aligned to ensure the finished house sits square.

steps.title: Insert Structural Panels
steps.content: Slide the prepared card stock or cardboard inserts into the wall sections. Secure them according to the template instructions.

tip.text: Do not force oversized inserts into the fabric shell, as they may stretch or warp the seams.

steps.title: Create the House Base
steps.content: Construct the fabric-covered base section and insert the cardboard support. Attach the base securely to the wall structure.

tip.text: A firm base helps the house remain upright when filled with gifts or treats.

steps.title: Construct the Roof Panels
steps.content: Sew the roof fabric pieces together and insert the matching support panels. Finish the roof edges neatly with decorative trims.

tip.text: Uneven roof seams can cause the lid to fit poorly on the completed house.

steps.title: Create the Removable Lid
steps.content: Assemble the roof and upper wall sections into a removable lid. Check that it slides comfortably over the house body.

tip.text: Test the lid fit repeatedly during construction instead of waiting until final assembly.

steps.title: Prepare the Carry Ribbon
steps.content: Cut the striped ribbon and position it inside the lid structure to create a decorative carrying handle.

tip.text: Reinforce the ribbon attachment points because they will support the weight of the filled house.

steps.title: Attach the Ribbon Handle
steps.content: Secure the ribbon handle between the roof layers and stitch firmly through all required layers.

tip.text: Use multiple rows of stitching to increase durability.

steps.title: Assemble the House and Lid
steps.content: Place the lid onto the completed house body and verify that all edges align neatly. Make any necessary adjustments before finishing.

tip.text: Skipping a fit check may result in a lid that sits unevenly or does not close properly.

steps.title: Finish Interior Edges
steps.content: Hand stitch any remaining openings closed and tidy all visible seams inside the house.

tip.text: Neat interior finishing gives the project a professional handmade appearance.

steps.title: Add Final Decorative Details
steps.content: Attach any remaining trims, buttons, ribbon accents, or festive embellishments to personalize the gingerbread house.

tip.text: Avoid placing bulky embellishments near the lid edges where they could interfere with opening and closing.

steps.title: Final Inspection
steps.content: Check all seams, trims, appliqué pieces, cardboard supports, and ribbon attachments. Remove loose threads and ensure the house holds its shape correctly.

tip.text: A gentle steam press on the exterior fabric can help sharpen edges and improve the finished presentation.

items.name: Halloween Wall Banner
items.materialNeeded:
Green cotton fabric, 21.7" x 43.3"
Orange cotton fabric, 18.1" x 22"
Fusible webbing, 19.7" square
Bias binding, 1" x 98.4"
White embroidery thread
Embroidery needle
Clear glass beads
Carbon paper or chalk pencil
Large embroidery hoop (optional)
items.techniques: Double Hemming, Bias Binding Application, Fusible Applique, Zigzag Stitching, Template Transfer, Twisted Chain Embroidery Stitch, Detached Chain Stitch, Hand Embroidery, Bead Embellishment

steps.title: Prepare the Banner Fabric
steps.content: Cut a piece of green cotton fabric measuring 21.7" x 43.3". Fold the two short edges twice by 0.4" to create double hems, press, and stitch in place.

tip.text: Press each fold before stitching to achieve crisp and even hems.

steps.title: Bind the Top Edge
steps.content: Cut the bias binding into two equal lengths. Apply one piece to the top long edge of the banner, centering it so the excess extends beyond both corners to create hanging ties.

tip.text: Keep the bias binding evenly aligned along the edge to prevent twisting after stitching.

steps.title: Shape and Bind the Bottom Edge
steps.content: Draw a gentle wavy line along the lower edge of the banner and carefully cut along the marked line. Apply the second piece of bias binding along the curved edge in the same manner.

tip.text: Clip the seam allowance slightly when binding curved edges to help the binding lie flat.

steps.title: Prepare the Letter Applique Templates
steps.content: Trace the letters for 'Happy Halloween' onto the paper side of the fusible webbing. Remember that all letters must be traced in reverse.

tip.text: Forgetting to reverse the letters will result in mirrored text on the finished banner.

steps.title: Fuse the Letter Shapes
steps.content: Place the fusible webbing onto the orange fabric with the paper side facing up. Fuse according to the manufacturer's instructions using a hot iron.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth if needed to protect delicate fabrics from excessive heat.

steps.title: Cut Out the Letters
steps.content: Carefully cut out all letter shapes. Remove the backing paper from each piece after cutting.

tip.text: Use small sharp scissors for clean curves and detailed letter shapes.

steps.title: Position the Letter Applique
steps.content: Arrange the letters on the green banner fabric to spell 'Happy Halloween'. Adjust spacing and alignment until satisfied with the layout.

tip.text: Check spacing carefully before fusing, as repositioning afterward can be difficult.

steps.title: Fuse the Letters to the Banner
steps.content: Press the letters in place with a hot iron to permanently bond them to the banner fabric.

tip.text: Apply firm, even pressure without sliding the iron to prevent shifting.

steps.title: Secure the Applique
steps.content: Use a zigzag stitch around the edge of each letter to secure the applique and prevent fraying.

tip.text: Reduce stitch width slightly when sewing around tight curves for a smoother finish.

steps.title: Transfer the Spider Web Design
steps.content: Use carbon paper or a chalk pencil to transfer the spider web motif onto the banner fabric.

tip.text: Test transfer markings on a fabric scrap first to ensure they can be removed later.

steps.title: Prepare for Embroidery
steps.content: Thread an embroidery needle with three strands of white embroidery floss. Place the fabric in an embroidery hoop if desired.

tip.text: Keeping the fabric taut helps maintain consistent embroidery stitches.

steps.title: Embroider the Spider Web
steps.content: Work along each marked line using twisted chain stitch. Begin from the center and gradually embroider outward, following the web design.

tip.text: Avoid pulling the thread too tightly, as it can distort the banner fabric.

steps.title: Create Detached Chain Details
steps.content: Finish each embroidered line with a small securing stitch. Add detached chain stitches where needed to enhance decorative details.

tip.text: Keep stitch sizes consistent for a neat and professional appearance.

steps.title: Add Bead Embellishments
steps.content: Hand sew clear glass beads at selected points on the spider web to create decorative highlights.

tip.text: Do not overcrowd the design with beads; a few well-placed embellishments create a cleaner result.

steps.title: Final Press and Finish
steps.content: Give the banner a final press from the wrong side, trim any loose threads, and prepare the ties for hanging.

tip.text: Press from the wrong side of the embroidery to protect the stitched texture and beadwork.

items.name: Festive Cactus Plush
items.materialNeeded:
Green felt, 2 sheets (9in × 12in).
Brown felt, 2 sheets (9in × 12in).
Yellow felt, scrap for star.
Black felt scraps for eyes.
Red embroidery thread.
Polyester toy stuffing.
Heavy card stock or thin cardboard.
Fabric glue.
Embroidery needle.
Sewing needle.
Matching polyester thread.
Twisted baker's twine or striped cord, 16 inches.
Assorted felt scraps (red, pink, orange, blue, purple, yellow) for bulb covers.
Small amount of toy stuffing for bulbs.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Water-soluble fabric marker.
6mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Soft Toy Construction, Hand Sewing, Machine Sewing, Felt Applique, Embroidery, Stuffing, Decorative Garland Making, Fabric Gluing, Dimensional Plush Construction

steps.title: Print and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Print the Christmas Cactus templates at 100% scale. Cut out the cactus body, flower pot, star topper, light bulbs, and decorative elements.

tip.text: Store the smaller bulb templates separately so they don't get lost during cutting.

steps.title: Cut the Felt Pieces
steps.content: Cut all cactus body sections from green felt, pot pieces from brown felt, and decorative shapes from the remaining felt colors.

tip.text: Cutting multiple felt layers at once can create uneven edges on curved pieces.

steps.title: Construct the Main Cactus Body
steps.content: Place the cactus body pieces right sides together and stitch around the outer edges, leaving an opening at the base for turning and stuffing.

tip.text: Use shorter stitch lengths around tight curves to achieve smoother contours.

steps.title: Clip Curves and Turn
steps.content: Clip the seam allowance around all curved areas and turn the cactus right side out through the opening.

tip.text: Avoid clipping through the stitching line, as this can weaken the seam.

steps.title: Stuff the Cactus
steps.content: Fill the cactus evenly with toy stuffing, paying particular attention to the arms and upper body to maintain a rounded shape.

tip.text: Understuffed cactus arms may collapse and fail to support the decorative garland.

steps.title: Close the Base Opening
steps.content: Hand stitch the opening closed using a ladder stitch, ensuring the base remains flat.

tip.text: Pull the ladder stitch tight gradually for an almost invisible finish.

steps.title: Create the Facial Features
steps.content: Cut two circles from black felt for the eyes and stitch or glue them to the front of the cactus. Embroider a smiling mouth using red embroidery thread.

tip.text: Lightly sketch the face placement first using a removable fabric marker.

steps.title: Make the Star Topper
steps.content: Cut two star shapes from yellow felt. Stitch around the edges, leaving a small opening, lightly stuff, then close the opening.

tip.text: Overstuffing the star can distort the points and make it appear rounded.

steps.title: Attach the Star
steps.content: Position the star at the top center of the cactus and secure it firmly with hand stitching.

tip.text: Use several reinforcement stitches because the topper is exposed and can be easily pulled.

steps.title: Create the Flower Pot Outer Layer
steps.content: Join the brown felt pot sections together and stitch the side seam. Fold and press the upper rim to create the rolled pot appearance.

tip.text: Topstitch around the folded rim for a cleaner and more realistic flower pot finish.

steps.title: Add Structural Support
steps.content: Cut a circular support piece from heavy card stock and place it inside the base of the pot to help maintain its shape.

tip.text: Ensure the support piece fits snugly without forcing the felt outward.

steps.title: Insert and Secure the Cactus
steps.content: Place the stuffed cactus inside the flower pot and stitch the base securely to the pot opening.

tip.text: Distribute stuffing evenly before attaching the cactus permanently.

steps.title: Make the Christmas Light Bulbs
steps.content: Cut bulb shapes from assorted felt colors. Sew around each bulb, lightly stuff, and close the openings.

tip.text: Bulbs that are stuffed too firmly may look oversized compared to the cactus.

steps.title: Prepare the Garland Cord
steps.content: Cut a length of striped baker's twine or decorative cord long enough to wrap between both cactus arms.

tip.text: Test the drape of the garland before attaching the bulbs.

steps.title: Attach the Bulbs to the Garland
steps.content: Space the felt bulbs evenly along the cord and secure them using hand stitches or fabric glue.

tip.text: Allow fabric glue to dry completely before handling the garland.

steps.title: Attach the Garland to the Cactus
steps.content: Wrap the finished garland across the cactus arms and secure the cord at both ends with hidden stitches.

tip.text: Attaching the garland too tightly can pull the arms inward and distort the cactus shape.

steps.title: Shape and Adjust the Plush
steps.content: Massage the stuffing gently to smooth any lumps and adjust the arms so the garland hangs naturally.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to redistribute stuffing into narrow areas if needed.

steps.title: Final Inspection
steps.content: Trim loose threads, inspect all decorative attachments, and verify that the cactus stands securely in the flower pot.

tip.text: Check all glued and stitched decorations carefully before gifting or displaying the plush.

items.name: Teacher Stationery Set
items.finishedSize:
Notebook Cover: 9 inches × 13 inches (fits A5 notebook)
Pencil Pouch: 10 inches × 4 inches
items.materialNeeded:
Printed cotton fabric, 1/2 yard.
Pink felt.
White felt.
Green felt.
Brown felt.
Lightweight fusible interfacing.
Wadding/batting.
Gingham ribbon.
Zipper, 10 inches.
Embroidery thread (brown, black, coordinating colors).
Sewing thread.
Notebook (A5 size).
Key ring.
Craft knife.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Iron.
Water-soluble fabric marker.
6mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Machine Sewing, Zipper Installation, Basic Embroidery, Quilted Construction, Topstitching, Felt Decoration, Hand Finishing

steps.title: Print and Prepare Templates
steps.content: Print the apple applique templates at 100% scale and cut out all paper pattern pieces for the notebook cover and pencil pouch decorations.

tip.text: Keep template pieces organized in labeled envelopes to avoid mixing small applique shapes.

steps.title: Cut Fabric for the Notebook Cover
steps.content: Cut the outer fabric, lining fabric, interfacing, and batting pieces according to the notebook cover measurements. Fuse interfacing to the wrong side of the outer fabric.

tip.text: Failing to fuse interfacing evenly can create bubbles or wrinkles in the finished cover.

steps.title: Prepare the Apple Applique
steps.content: Cut the apple body from pink felt, leaves from green felt, stem from brown felt, and worm details from white felt using the templates.

tip.text: Sharp embroidery scissors produce cleaner felt edges and reduce fraying.

steps.title: Position the Applique Design
steps.content: Arrange the apple, worm, leaves, and stem on the notebook cover front. Adjust placement until balanced and visually centered.

tip.text: Take a quick photo before stitching so you can recreate the arrangement if pieces shift.

steps.title: Secure the Applique Pieces
steps.content: Stitch around the felt applique pieces using a narrow zigzag stitch or hand blanket stitch. Secure all decorative elements firmly.

tip.text: Avoid stitching too close to the felt edge, as the material may stretch or distort.

steps.title: Embroider Decorative Details
steps.content: Add embroidered details to the worm face, apple accents, and leaf veins using coordinating embroidery thread.

tip.text: Use a single strand of embroidery floss for finer facial details.

steps.title: Assemble the Notebook Cover Exterior
steps.content: Layer the outer fabric, batting, and lining. Quilt or stitch as desired to stabilize the layers before assembly.

tip.text: Skipping stabilization may cause the cover to shift during final construction.

steps.title: Create the Notebook Flaps
steps.content: Fold and stitch the inner flap sections that will hold the notebook covers. Press all folds carefully.

tip.text: A well-pressed flap gives the notebook cover a professional appearance.

steps.title: Construct the Notebook Cover
steps.content: Join the cover layers, stitch around the perimeter, turn right side out, and topstitch close to the edge.

tip.text: Leave a sufficient turning gap and avoid trimming corners too aggressively.

steps.title: Insert the Notebook
steps.content: Slide the notebook covers into the interior flaps and check the fit. Make any adjustments before final pressing.

tip.text: A cover that is too tight may strain seams when the notebook is opened.

steps.title: Cut Fabric for the Pencil Pouch
steps.content: Cut outer fabric, lining fabric, and interfacing pieces for the pencil pouch according to the pattern dimensions.

tip.text: Fuse interfacing before cutting smaller pieces for easier handling.

steps.title: Prepare the Zipper
steps.content: Press the zipper tape flat and verify that the zipper length matches the pouch opening.

tip.text: Always open the zipper halfway before closing the pouch seams.

steps.title: Install the Zipper
steps.content: Attach the zipper between the outer fabric and lining pieces using a zipper foot. Topstitch along both zipper edges.

tip.text: Basting the zipper first helps prevent shifting during final stitching.

steps.title: Create the Pencil Pouch Body
steps.content: Join the pouch outer and lining sections, stitch around the perimeter, and box the corners if desired for extra depth.

tip.text: Forgetting to open the zipper before sewing the pouch shut makes turning impossible.

steps.title: Turn and Shape the Pouch
steps.content: Turn the pouch right side out through the lining opening. Push out corners carefully and press the finished pouch.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to shape corners without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Create the Matching Apple Charm
steps.content: Cut miniature apple applique pieces from felt and stitch them together. Add embroidered facial details and decorative stitching.

tip.text: Use contrasting embroidery thread to make small decorative features stand out.

steps.title: Attach the Key Ring
steps.content: Secure the finished apple charm to a ribbon loop or fabric tab and attach it to the key ring.

tip.text: Reinforce attachment points with multiple stitches because they receive frequent handling.

steps.title: Attach the Charm to the Pouch
steps.content: Connect the apple charm to the zipper pull using the key ring for a coordinated decorative finish.

tip.text: A removable charm allows the pouch to be washed more easily.

steps.title: Final Press and Inspection
steps.content: Press both projects carefully, trim loose threads, and inspect all applique, embroidery, and zipper stitching.

tip.text: Avoid pressing directly over felt applique; use a pressing cloth to protect the texture.

items.name: Sunny Peg Holder
items.materialNeeded:
Main cotton fabric, 21in × 13in.
Printed cotton fabrics (assorted coordinating prints).
Neutral cotton fabric for background panel.
Lightweight fusible interfacing.
Wadding/batting.
Bias binding, approximately 2 yards.
Decorative lace trim, 13 inches.
2 large buttons.
Ribbon or fabric strips for hanging loops.
Fabric glue (optional).
Embroidery thread (optional).
Coordinating sewing thread.
Pins or sewing clips.
Fabric scissors.
Iron.
Water-soluble fabric marker.
6mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Machine Sewing, Bias Binding Application, Decorative Topstitching, Layered Construction, Fabric Embellishment, Button Loop Closure, Basic Quilting

steps.title: Print and Prepare Templates
steps.content: Print the peg bag templates at 100% scale and cut out all applique shapes, including the sunshine rays and miniature clothing pieces.

tip.text: Keep applique templates grouped by section to speed up assembly later.

steps.title: Cut the Main Bag Pieces
steps.content: Cut the front, back, lining, and batting pieces according to the pattern dimensions. Press all fabrics before construction.

tip.text: Cutting wrinkled fabric can result in uneven panel sizes.

steps.title: Prepare the Background Panel
steps.content: Fuse lightweight interfacing to the wrong side of the main front panel to provide stability for the applique work.

tip.text: Allow the fabric to cool completely after fusing before handling.

steps.title: Create the Sunshine Applique
steps.content: Cut the sun circle and sunray shapes from yellow fabric. Position them across the upper section of the front panel.

tip.text: Arrange the rays before stitching to ensure even spacing around the sun.

steps.title: Secure the Sunshine Design
steps.content: Stitch around the sun and rays using a narrow zigzag stitch or applique stitch to permanently secure the shapes.

tip.text: Sharp corners on the rays require slower stitching to maintain clean points.

steps.title: Create the Mini Clothesline Pieces
steps.content: Cut miniature clothing shapes including shirts, dresses, and trousers from coordinating printed fabrics.

tip.text: Use small sharp scissors for neat edges on tiny applique shapes.

steps.title: Arrange the Clothesline Scene
steps.content: Position the miniature clothing pieces across the middle section of the bag to resemble laundry hanging on a line.

tip.text: Uneven spacing between garments can make the scene appear cluttered.

steps.title: Attach the Clothing Applique
steps.content: Secure all miniature clothing pieces with applique stitching around each shape.

tip.text: A matching thread color helps small applique stitches disappear into the design.

steps.title: Add Decorative Embroidery
steps.content: Optional embroidered outlines or decorative details can be added to the clothing pieces to enhance the handmade appearance.

tip.text: Simple backstitch embroidery works well for tiny garment details.

steps.title: Attach the Lace Trim
steps.content: Position the decorative lace trim above the clothesline applique section and stitch it securely across the width of the bag.

tip.text: Stretching lace while sewing may cause rippling after pressing.

steps.title: Layer the Bag Front
steps.content: Place the decorated front panel over the batting and baste the layers together to prevent shifting.

tip.text: Temporary spray adhesive can help stabilize multiple layers.

steps.title: Prepare the Hanging Loops
steps.content: Fold ribbon or fabric strips to create two hanging loops. Stitch securely and position them at the top corners.

tip.text: These loops support the full weight of the peg bag, so reinforce the stitching well.

steps.title: Assemble the Bag Exterior
steps.content: Place the front and back pieces right sides together and stitch around the sides and bottom, leaving the top edge open.

tip.text: Check that hanging loops are tucked safely inside before stitching.

steps.title: Create the Peg Storage Pocket
steps.content: Form the front storage section by folding and stitching according to the template, creating a large pocket for clothes pegs.

tip.text: Topstitching the pocket edge improves durability during daily use.

steps.title: Apply Bias Binding
steps.content: Finish all exposed edges using bias binding, stitching neatly around curves and corners.

tip.text: Avoid pulling the bias binding while sewing, as it can distort the bag shape.

steps.title: Add Button Closures
steps.content: Sew two large decorative buttons onto the upper section of the peg bag and create corresponding button loops.

tip.text: Test the loop size around each button before permanently stitching.

steps.title: Turn and Shape the Bag
steps.content: Turn the bag right side out, push out corners carefully, and shape the pocket section evenly.

tip.text: Sharp tools can poke through seams when pushing out corners.

steps.title: Final Pressing
steps.content: Press the entire peg bag carefully, paying special attention to the applique and lace sections.

tip.text: Use a pressing cloth when ironing over lace or decorative embellishments.

steps.title: Final Inspection
steps.content: Trim loose threads, verify that all applique pieces are secure, and test the hanging loops and button closures before use.

tip.text: A final strength check ensures the peg bag will withstand regular outdoor use.

items.name: Festive Felt Props
items.finishedSize: Various sizes depending on prop design
items.materialNeeded:
Felt: red, white, brown, green.
Sequins: red and silver.
Iron-on interfacing.
Wooden skewers or craft sticks.
Fabric glue.
Embroidery thread.
Tissue paper.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Scissors.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Felt Craft, Embroidery, Applique, Hand Sewing

steps.title: Prepare the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut out all pattern pieces for the Santa hat, beard, reindeer antlers, Rudolph nose and speech bubble.

tip.text: Ensure templates are printed at 100% scale for accurate sizing.

steps.title: Make the Santa Beard
steps.content: Cut the beard layers from white felt and interfacing. Layer the pieces together and stitch decorative swirls following the template markings.

tip.text: Use matching thread to create a clean and professional finish.

steps.title: Create the Santa Hat
steps.content: Cut the hat shape from red felt and sew the side seam. Add the white brim and decorative holly leaves before attaching the wooden stick.

tip.text: Attaching embellishments after assembly can be more difficult and less neat.

steps.title: Sew the Reindeer Antlers
steps.content: Cut two antler shapes from brown felt. Stitch together around the edges, leaving a small opening if additional stability is required.

tip.text: Lightly stiffen the antlers with interfacing to help them keep their shape.

steps.title: Make Rudolph's Nose
steps.content: Cut circular pieces from red felt and red sequined fabric. Stitch around the edge and secure onto a wooden skewer.

tip.text: Check that sequins are firmly attached to avoid shedding during use.

steps.title: Create the Speech Bubble
steps.content: Cut the speech bubble pieces from white felt and interfacing. Embroider or write a festive message before assembling.

tip.text: Personalised messages make the props more memorable for party guests.

steps.title: Attach the Handles
steps.content: Secure each completed felt design to a wooden skewer using fabric glue and additional stitching where necessary.

tip.text: Allow glue to dry completely before handling the props.

steps.title: Finish and Display
steps.content: Trim any loose threads and check all embellishments. Arrange the props together ready for festive photographs and celebrations.

tip.text: Skipping the final quality check may result in decorations loosening during use.

items.name: Fabric Advent Calendar
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, cotton: red fat quarter; skin tone fat quarter; metallic gold fat quarter; white 17.72 inches x 19.69 inches.
Faux sheepskin: 17.72 inches x 19.69 inches.
Faux fur: 5.91 inches x 15.75 inches.
Felt: glitter, 4.72 inches x 9.84 inches; pink, brown and cream scraps.
Ribbon: red, 5mm wide, 19.69 inches.
Embroidery thread.
Fusible webbing.
Toy stuffing.
5mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Embroidery, Soft Toy Construction, Decorative Sewing

steps.title: Prepare and Cut the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the templates. Cut the beard pieces from faux sheepskin, facial features from felt, and the hat sections from red cotton fabric. Transfer all markings and placement guides.

tip.text: Print templates at actual size to ensure the numbered pockets fit correctly.

steps.title: Create Santa's Face
steps.content: Embroider the eyes, eyebrows and smile using embroidery thread. Attach the cheeks, nose and moustache pieces using applique techniques.

tip.text: Use small embroidery stitches to create expressive facial features.

steps.title: Construct the Beard
steps.content: Cut the beard shape from faux sheepskin and attach it beneath the face. Position carefully so all facial features remain visible.

tip.text: Placing the beard too high can cover the mouth and reduce facial expression.

steps.title: Make the Numbered Pockets
steps.content: Cut twelve pocket shapes in assorted geometric designs. Apply glitter numbers from 1 to 12 using fusible webbing and secure with stitching.

tip.text: Mix different pocket shapes to create a more playful and festive layout.

steps.title: Attach the Pockets
steps.content: Arrange the numbered pockets across Santa's beard. Stitch around the sides and lower edges, leaving the tops open to hold treats or small gifts.

tip.text: Ensure pocket openings remain unobstructed before final stitching.

steps.title: Sew the Santa Hat
steps.content: Join the hat pieces together and add the faux fur brim. Create and attach a pom-pom to the tip of the hat.

tip.text: A lightly stuffed pom-pom creates a fuller and softer appearance.

steps.title: Assemble the Main Body
steps.content: Attach the completed face, beard and hat sections to the main backing fabric. Stitch securely and smooth all layers before quilting or finishing.

tip.text: Uneven placement of the face can make the calendar appear asymmetrical.

steps.title: Add Hanging Loop
steps.content: Fold the ribbon in half to create a hanging loop and stitch it securely into the top seam of the calendar.

tip.text: Reinforce the hanging loop with extra stitching to support the weight of filled pockets.

steps.title: Finish the Edges
steps.content: Place the backing fabric behind the calendar and stitch around the outer edge. Turn right side out if required and close any remaining openings.

tip.text: Topstitch around the outer edge for a neat professional finish.

steps.title: Decorate and Fill
steps.content: Fill the numbered pockets with sweets, notes or small surprises. Hang the finished Advent Calendar ready for the Christmas countdown.

tip.text: Overfilling pockets may stretch the fabric and distort the design.

items.name: Santa Door Sign
items.materialNeeded:
Red cotton fabric.
Fusible interfacing.
Embroidery thread: white, green.
Wooden dowel, 4.72 inches.
Hanging cord.
Embroidery needle.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Iron.
Scissors.
items.techniques: Embroidery, Sewing, Turning, Topstitching

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Fabric
steps.content: Print and cut the template. Cut the banner shape from red cotton fabric and fusible interfacing. Fuse the interfacing to the wrong side of the fabric according to the manufacturer's instructions.

tip.text: Using interfacing helps the banner hold its shape and prevents puckering during embroidery.

steps.title: Transfer and Embroider the Design
steps.content: Transfer the lettering and leaf wreath motif onto the fabric using an air-erasable fabric pen. Embroider the text with white embroidery thread and stitch the leaves with green thread.

tip.text: Check the lettering placement before stitching to ensure the design remains centered.

steps.title: Sew the Banner
steps.content: Fold the fabric with right sides together and stitch around the banner shape, leaving the top edge open. Trim the corners and clip the point at the bottom.

tip.text: Avoid cutting through the stitching when trimming the seam allowances.

steps.title: Turn and Press
steps.content: Turn the banner right side out through the opening. Carefully push out the corners and bottom point. Press flat with an iron.

tip.text: Use a blunt turning tool to achieve crisp corners without damaging the fabric.

steps.title: Create the Hanging Channel
steps.content: Fold the top edge to the wrong side and stitch across to create a channel for the wooden dowel.

tip.text: Make sure the channel is wide enough for the dowel to slide through easily.

steps.title: Add the Dowel and Cord
steps.content: Insert the wooden dowel through the channel. Tie a length of cord to both ends of the dowel to create a hanging loop.

tip.text: Adjust the cord length before knotting permanently so the banner hangs evenly.

steps.title: Finish the Banner
steps.content: Give the banner a final press and trim any loose threads. Hang it on a door, wall, or festive display area.

tip.text: Keep embroidery threads neatly secured on the reverse side to prevent unraveling over time.

items.name: Reindeer Treat Bag
items.materialNeeded:
Cotton fabric, cream, 7.09 inches x 23.62 inches.
Cotton fabric, red print.
Ribbon, red and white, narrow, 20mm.
Sequins, cream.
Acid-free glue stick.
Embroidery thread, red.
Embroidery needle.
Air-erasable fabric pen.
Pins.
Iron.
Sewing machine.
0.39 inches seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Free-motion Embroidery, Machine Sewing, Hand Embroidery

steps.title: Cut and Prepare the Main Fabric
steps.content: Print and cut the template pieces. Cut one rectangle measuring 7.09 inches x 23.62 inches from cream cotton fabric. Fold the fabric in half with right sides together and press. Mark the centre line and template placement using an air-erasable fabric pen.

tip.text: Press the fabric thoroughly before marking to ensure accurate template placement.

steps.title: Create the Reindeer Applique
steps.content: Trace the reindeer template onto the paper side of fusible webbing and iron it onto the wrong side of the red fabric. Cut out the shape carefully, remove the backing paper and position the reindeer onto the front of the bag. Fuse in place following the manufacturer's instructions.

tip.text: Allow the fused applique to cool completely before handling to prevent lifting edges.

steps.title: Add the Lettering
steps.content: Using the template as a guide, draw the words 'Magic Reindeer Food' around the applique with an air-erasable fabric pen. Check spacing and alignment before stitching.

tip.text: Uneven spacing between words can make the finished design appear unbalanced.

steps.title: Free-motion Stitch the Design
steps.content: Using red embroidery thread, stitch around the reindeer applique with free-motion embroidery. Continue stitching over the lettering to create a handwritten effect. Reinforce any areas that need additional definition.

tip.text: Practice free-motion stitching on a fabric scrap before sewing the final design.

steps.title: Attach Decorative Sequins
steps.content: Sew cream sequins around the reindeer motif to resemble falling snow. Secure each sequin with small stitches and knot the thread neatly on the reverse side.

tip.text: Scatter the sequins irregularly for a more natural snowfall effect.

steps.title: Construct the Bag
steps.content: Fold the fabric with right sides together. Stitch both side seams using a 0.39 inches seam allowance. Finish seam allowances as desired and turn the bag right side out.

tip.text: Backstitch at the beginning and end of each seam to strengthen the bag.

steps.title: Create the Ribbon Casing
steps.content: Fold the top raw edge down twice to form a neat casing. Stitch around the upper edge, leaving openings on both sides for the ribbon drawstring.

tip.text: Ensure the casing is wide enough for the ribbon to pass through smoothly.

steps.title: Insert the Drawstring
steps.content: Cut two lengths of narrow ribbon. Using a safety pin, thread one ribbon through one side of the casing and the second ribbon through the opposite side. Tie knots at the ribbon ends.

tip.text: Sealing ribbon ends with a small amount of fray-check will prevent unraveling.

steps.title: Finish the Bag
steps.content: Give the bag a final press, remove any visible fabric markings, and check all stitching. Fill with reindeer food or festive treats and pull the ribbons closed.

tip.text: Remove all temporary fabric markings before gifting or displaying the finished bag.

items.name: Holiday Wreath Set
items.finishedSize: Wreath: approximately 11.81 inches diameter; Ornaments: 10–4.72 inches tall
items.materialNeeded:
Assorted Christmas cotton fabrics.
Felt or fusible fleece.
Ribbon for hanging loops and bow.
Toy stuffing.
Matching sewing thread.
Fabric glue (optional).
Pins and fabric scissors.
Sewing machine.
Printed templates.
5mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Patchwork, Applique, Stuffing, Machine Sewing

steps.title: Prepare the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the templates at 100% scale. Cut out all wreath and ornament pattern pieces and transfer markings onto the chosen fabrics.

tip.text: Printing at the wrong scale can cause ornaments to appear too large or too small for the wreath.

steps.title: Make the Ornaments
steps.content: Cut two fabric pieces for each ornament shape. Stitch around the edges leaving a turning gap. Clip curves, turn right side out and lightly stuff before closing the opening.

tip.text: Use a chopstick or turning tool to push out corners neatly without stretching the fabric.

steps.title: Add Decorative Details
steps.content: Apply applique pieces and stitch decorative elements such as windows, wheels, foliage or festive motifs before assembling the ornaments.

tip.text: Secure small applique pieces carefully to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Create Hanging Loops
steps.content: Fold ribbon lengths in half and stitch securely into the top of each ornament. Test the strength of the loop before final assembly.

tip.text: Double-stitch the ribbon attachment point for extra durability.

steps.title: Construct the Wreath Base
steps.content: Join fabric strips to create a patchwork panel. Wrap the panel around the wreath form, keeping the fabric smooth and evenly tensioned. Stitch the opening closed.

tip.text: Pulling the fabric too tightly can distort patchwork seams and patterns.

steps.title: Add the Bow
steps.content: Cut and fold fabric strips into a large decorative bow. Stitch or hand-sew the bow securely to the top centre of the wreath.

tip.text: Press the bow pieces before assembly for crisp, professional-looking folds.

steps.title: Attach the Ornaments
steps.content: Position the completed ornaments in the centre of the wreath. Secure them with ribbon, thread or hidden stitches until balanced and symmetrical.

tip.text: Check the arrangement before sewing permanently, as repositioning can leave visible stitch holes.

steps.title: Finish the Wreath
steps.content: Trim loose threads, fluff the ornaments and add a hanging loop to the back. Give the wreath a final press where appropriate.

tip.text: A gentle steam press helps patchwork sections lie flat and enhances the finished appearance.

items.name: Felt Jumper Ornaments
items.materialNeeded:
Felt: cotton, pink, orange scraps.
Felt, three neutral shades.
Assorted scraps of grey, white glitter, dark blue, light blue, mustard, red.
Embroidery thread: mustard, white, pink, purple, violet.
Wadding.
Ribbon, narrow silver.
Acid-free glue stick.
Printed templates.
5mm seam allowance used throughout unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Embroidery, Hand Sewing, Machine Sewing

steps.title: Prepare the Templates
steps.content: Download and print the jumper templates at 100% scale. Cut out all pattern pieces and transfer the shapes onto the selected felt colours.

tip.text: Incorrect print scaling can affect the proportions of the jumper decorations.

steps.title: Cut the Jumper Shapes
steps.content: Cut front and back jumper pieces from felt. Cut cuffs, collars, waistbands and decorative applique pieces from coordinating colours.

tip.text: Use sharp embroidery scissors for clean edges and more accurate applique pieces.

steps.title: Attach Decorative Panels
steps.content: Position cuffs, collars and hem bands onto the jumper front. Stitch in place using machine stitching or decorative embroidery stitches.

tip.text: Pin small pieces securely before stitching to prevent them shifting out of position.

steps.title: Create the Snowman Design
steps.content: Cut the snowman face, nose and facial features from felt. Glue lightly in place and stitch around the edges. Add embroidered eyes and mouth details.

tip.text: Short embroidery stitches create smoother facial features and a neater finish.

steps.title: Embroider Festive Details
steps.content: Add decorative embroidery including snowflakes, knitted-effect patterns, zigzags, French knots and festive lettering using contrasting thread colours.

tip.text: Pulling embroidery thread too tightly may cause the felt to pucker.

steps.title: Insert the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Cut a length of narrow silver ribbon and fold it in half. Position the loop between the front and back jumper pieces at the neckline.

tip.text: Test the loop length before stitching to ensure the ornament hangs correctly.

steps.title: Assemble the Ornament
steps.content: Place the front and back pieces right sides together. Stitch around the outer edge, leaving a small opening if adding light stuffing.

tip.text: Take extra care around sleeve corners and neckline curves for a smooth finish.

steps.title: Finish and Decorate
steps.content: Turn right side out if required, add a small amount of stuffing and close the opening. Trim loose threads and add any final embroidery accents.

tip.text: Mix embroidery colours and stitch styles to give each jumper its own unique personality.

items.name: Stocking Garland Set
items.finishedSize: Approximately 4.5 inches tall x 4 inches wide each ornament
items.materialNeeded:
Felt, wool-blend: cream, light blue, grey, mustard, orange, pink, dark blue and white scraps.
Embroidery thread: white, navy, blue, purple, pink and black.
Ribbon, narrow metallic silver, 18 inches.
Acid-free fabric glue.
Printable templates.
Tracing paper.
Pins.
Polyester stuffing (small amount, optional).
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Embroidery, Machine Sewing, Topstitching, Decorative Stitching, Ornament Construction

steps.title: Print and Cut the Template Pieces
steps.content: Print the jumper templates at 100% scale and cut out all pattern pieces. Trace the required shapes onto the corresponding felt colours and cut them neatly. Prepare front and back jumper pieces along with decorative appliqué components.

tip.text: Always print templates at actual size. Scaling errors will affect the symmetry of the finished ornament.

steps.title: Create the Jumper Cuffs and Hem
steps.content: Position the cuff and hem bands onto the front jumper piece. Secure with fabric glue or pins and stitch in place using a straight stitch or decorative machine stitch.

tip.text: Use a slightly shorter stitch length when sewing small felt pieces to achieve cleaner edges.

steps.title: Add Decorative Sweater Details
steps.content: Sew decorative bands, zigzags, diamonds or festive motifs onto the front of the jumper according to the chosen design. Use contrasting embroidery thread to create traditional knitted-sweater effects.

tip.text: Overcrowding the design can make small ornaments look messy. Leave some open space between decorative elements.

steps.title: Create the Snowman Applique
steps.content: For the snowman version, attach the white circular face, orange carrot nose and facial features to the front jumper. Stitch around the appliqué pieces and embroider the mouth and eyes.

tip.text: Glue small pieces temporarily before stitching to prevent shifting while sewing.

steps.title: Add Embroidery Embellishments
steps.content: Embroider snowflakes, lettering, French knots and decorative accents using coordinating embroidery threads. Follow the design markings and keep stitches even throughout.

tip.text: Use three strands of embroidery floss for decorative details that need to stand out on felt.

steps.title: Attach the Hanging Loop
steps.content: Cut a length of metallic ribbon and fold it into a loop. Position the raw ends at the centre top of the jumper back piece and secure with a few stitches.

tip.text: Make sure the loop is centered before sewing or the ornament may hang unevenly.

steps.title: Assemble the Ornament
steps.content: Place the front and back jumper pieces together with right sides facing outward. Stitch around the entire outer edge, leaving a small opening if stuffing is desired.

tip.text: Sewing too close to the edge may cause the felt layers to separate with use.

steps.title: Add Optional Stuffing
steps.content: Insert a small amount of polyester stuffing through the opening to give the ornament dimension. Distribute the filling evenly before closing the gap.

tip.text: Use only a tiny amount of stuffing to maintain the flat ornament shape.

steps.title: Close and Finish the Ornament
steps.content: Stitch the remaining opening closed and check all embroidery and appliqué details. Trim loose threads and reshape the ornament if necessary.

tip.text: Inspect all hanging loops and seams before displaying the ornament on a tree.

steps.title: Display on the Christmas Tree
steps.content: Hang the finished jumper decorations on the tree using the attached ribbon loops. Mix different jumper styles and colours for a coordinated festive display.

tip.text: Create multiple ornaments with personalized names to make thoughtful handmade gifts.

items.name: Wine Gift Bags
items.finishedSize: Approximately 13 inches tall x 5 inches diameter (fits a standard 750ml wine bottle)
items.materialNeeded:
Fabric, cotton: foliage print, green, red and gold spot print, 2 fat quarters.
Felt: white, black, brown, orange, red and cream scraps.
Ribbon: red star-print ribbon, 18 inches.
Sequins: assorted colours, 8–12 pieces.
Embroidery thread: black, brown, white and red.
Acid-free fabric glue.
Printable templates.
Tracing paper.
Pins.
Fabric marking pen.
1/4 inch seam allowance used throughout, unless otherwise stated.
items.techniques: Applique, Embroidery, Machine Sewing, Topstitching, Decorative Stitching, Felt Applique, Channel Casing Construction, Ribbon Closure

steps.title: Print and Prepare the Templates
steps.content: Print all templates at 100% scale and cut out the required pattern pieces. Choose either the snowman or reindeer design and trace the applique pieces onto the corresponding felt colours.

tip.text: Printing templates at any scale other than 100% may prevent the appliqué design from fitting correctly on the bottle cover.

steps.title: Cut the Main Fabric Panel
steps.content: Cut one rectangle from the chosen cotton fabric measuring approximately 10.83 inches x 16.54 inches. Fold the rectangle with right sides together and stitch the side seam to form a fabric tube.

tip.text: Press the seam allowance open before continuing to reduce bulk and create a smoother finished cover.

steps.title: Create the Base of the Bag
steps.content: Fold and stitch the lower edge of the tube closed to form the bottle bag base. Trim excess seam allowance and box the corners slightly if a wider base is desired.

tip.text: Uneven seam allowances can cause the bottle bag to lean or sit unevenly when standing upright.

steps.title: Prepare the Applique Pieces
steps.content: Cut all felt pieces for the chosen design. For the snowman, prepare the body circles, hat, scarf, carrot nose and buttons. For the reindeer, prepare the body, antlers, ears, muzzle, nose and hooves.

tip.text: Keep applique pieces organized in small trays or envelopes to avoid losing tiny components.

steps.title: Assemble the Snowman or Reindeer Design
steps.content: Layer the felt pieces according to the template and secure them temporarily with fabric glue. Position the completed design on the front of the bag and stitch around all applique edges.

tip.text: Applying too much glue may gum up the sewing machine needle during stitching.

steps.title: Add Facial Features and Decorative Details
steps.content: Embroider facial details using embroidery thread. Attach sequins, buttons and additional embellishments to create festive character details.

tip.text: Use hand embroidery for facial features to achieve cleaner details than machine stitching on small shapes.

steps.title: Create the Ribbon Casing
steps.content: Fold the top edge of the fabric toward the wrong side to create a channel casing. Stitch around the bag, leaving a small opening through which the ribbon can be threaded.

tip.text: Make sure the casing width is consistent all the way around or the ribbon may not slide smoothly.

steps.title: Thread the Drawstring Ribbon
steps.content: Attach a safety pin to one end of the ribbon and guide it through the casing until it emerges from the opening. Knot the ends together securely.

tip.text: Folding the ribbon tip and securing it with a pin makes threading through the casing much easier.

steps.title: Press and Shape the Finished Bag
steps.content: Give the entire project a final press, avoiding direct heat on sequins or glued embellishments. Insert a wine bottle to shape the cover naturally.

tip.text: High heat can melt sequins or weaken fabric glue, so always use a pressing cloth when needed.

steps.title: Close and Decorate for Gift Giving
steps.content: Place the wine bottle inside the finished bag and pull the ribbon tight around the neck. Tie a decorative bow and adjust the appliqué design so it faces forward.

tip.text: Coordinate ribbon colours with the appliqué theme to create a more polished handmade gift presentation.
